A Man’s Guide to Women

 

A guide to understanding women and finding the girl of your dreams

 

 

By

T. Knud Carter

Introduction

Tania had protested when I got out of the car.  “Oh, that’s okay.  You don’t have to walk me to the door.”

 

Now she was reaching for her keys.  She said she had a lot of things to do for work tomorrow so that’s probably why she seemed to be in a hurry to get inside.  “Think Robert…say something clever.”  I fell back on the only thing I’d planned on saying, “Would you like to go out again sometime?”

 

Now she had her key in the lock and was turning the tumblers.  “Uhhh…I’m really busy for the next few weeks…I’ll call you.”  With that she opened the door, moved into the opening, thanked me for dinner, turned and closed the door.

 

I turned and walked back to my car.  “Wow, my first date with Tania.  She’s gorgeous, classy, intelligent….She’s perfect.  She’s my ideal woman.”

 

I spent the next day reviewing my dream date.  When I’d arrived to pick her up, Tania was talking on her cell phone.  She smiled at me as she opened the door.  What a smile she has.  I melted.  Now in the car she finished her call and asked where we were going to eat.  I’d done my homework and found a restaurant that would impress her.  “I thought we’d go to Chez Michel.”

 

“Oh…that’ll be fine.”  I thought there’d be more of a reaction but then I realized this is Tania.  She’s used to fine dining.  I’ve moved up to the “A” group.  As we drove to the restaurant I told Tania I’d never been to Chez Michel before but I’d heard good things about it.  Now there was an awkward silence.  I was feeling a little insecure so I started telling her about my work and my family.  She was content to listen so I began to tell her how much I enjoy hiking and then her phone rang.  The rest of the drive to the restaurant she was talking to a friend.  I heard her refer to Chez Michel so I suppose her friend called to ask how the date was going.

 

Chez Michel is a restaurant with an ambiance of fine dining.  I’d picked well and was feeling more secure about how the date was going.  We were seated and Tania asked if we could have a glass of wine to start.  The waiter left the menus and went off to get our wine.   I looked at the menu and everything was expensive…gulp.  “But hey, you only live once.  Live it up, Robert.  You’re out with Tania, the girl of your dreams, and no matter what the night costs it’ll be worth it…Hey, maybe you’ll end up in bed with her.”  I started getting a hard on just thinking about that.  I adjusted my napkin.

 

We ordered, the waiter left again and another awkward silence set in.  “Think Robert.  Ask her a question.”  “How was your day?”

 

Tania took a sip of wine, adjusted her napkin and said, “Oh…fine.”

 

I asked if she liked her job and Tania said it was okay.  I began telling her more about my job and what a looser my boss was…Then her cell phone rang.  It must have been her friend again.  Tania was quite animated on the phone and began laughing.  As they became engrossed in the conversation I watched her face go from one expression to another.  She really was beautiful.  I could fall in love with her so easily.

 

Her conversation went on for a long time but I didn’t care.  It took the pressure off of me and Tania was certainly enjoying herself.  The food came so Tania finished her call.  We ate with some occasional chit chat.  She enjoyed her meal and so did I.  When the waiter came to clear I asked if she’d like some more wine but she declined.  I asked for the check.  Another awkward silence set in so I began to tell her about a hike I was going on with friends that weekend.  Tania seemed interested to hear about it.  Then the check came.  I glanced at it and then did a double take.  It was $93.00.  The salads, appetizer and glasses of wine had added up.  I pulled out my credit card.

 

I asked if she’d like to go somewhere for a drink and chat but she needed to get home to take care of things around the house.  I certainly understood how that went.  As we pulled out of the parking lot I asked if she likes to go to movies.  I was watching the road and didn’t notice she had her cell phone out and was making a call.  She began talking and I suppose it was her friend again.  She glanced at me and told her friend the dinner was very nice.  The pain of the expensive meal melted away.  I started picturing Tania leading me into her bedroom.

 

She ended the call a few blocks from her apartment and thanked me for a nice evening.  I began telling her what a wonderful time I had and I hoped we could do it again.  She didn’t respond but she was busy putting her phone away and finding her keys in her purse so she may not have heard me.  We pulled up in front of her apartment and I got out to walk her to the door.  Just walking next to her and catching the scent of her perfume was amazing.

 

I called Tania maybe 5 times over the next several weeks to ask her out again.  She was always busy.  I spent those weeks totally fixated on her.  My friends began to roll their eyes every time I brought up her name.  Now I look back on that first date and I’m not sure whether to be amused or embarrassed…maybe grateful. 

 

Fast forward 6 months…

 

I remember the first time I noticed Michael.  Actually, I didn’t notice him.  I noticed the girl with him.  I hadn’t had a date in a long while and she was quite attractive.  So when they walked by, I noticed her.  Michael lived in my apartment complex so we’d pass each other going to work or run into each other at the small gym in the apartment “rec” room.

 

But I never paid much attention to Michael until that day I saw him with his date.  You see Michael wasn’t extraordinary in outward ways…so he wasn’t the kind of guy who caught your attention.  He was average looking and average height and build.  He had a good job but lots of people do.  So after I saw him with that attractive girl, I started to pay attention.

 

I introduced myself to Michael one day at the gym.  We had a few things in common and a friendship began.  We both played tennis and we both enjoyed college sports.  Soon our friendship went beyond workout partners in the gym.  We’d go out for a beer or something to eat during the week and often we got out for a run or played tennis on weekends.  I started to find out Michael was actually quite an extraordinary guy.  He had a nice, easy going and positive personality.  I saw that immediately but also I quickly learned he had his life in order.  He had a good job.  He had no debt.  He had free time and enough money to go out when he wanted to.  He traveled some.  He had a common sense about him and I think that was one of the reasons he always enjoyed himself.  I guess you could say he had his priorities straight.  He was confident and secure…but not because he was the second coming of…like…George Clooney.  He was secure in himself because he knew himself.  He knew his strengths and weaknesses and wasn’t trying to be more than he was.  When he played tennis he wasn’t trying to beat his opponent.  He simply focused on playing as well as he could.  He played and practiced to improve…to reach his potentials.  But win or lose, he was gracious and enthusiastic.

 

One Saturday evening we went to an Applebee’s to get something to eat and watch a college game on the TV.  There were a lot of good looking girls there…both in groups and out on dates.  We were having fun checking them out.  The subject moved from basketball to women.   Michael noticed one of the waitresses and started wondering out loud if he should try to meet her.  “I thought you had a girlfriend?”  I asked him.

 

“Yeah, I’m dating someone.”  He said and then smiled.  I got his meaning…It doesn’t hurt to meet new people.

 

I started asking Michael about his dating life…and I mentioned I wasn’t happy with mine.  In fact, it had been 6 months since my “big” date with Tania and I hadn’t been out since.  He started asking me questions about past girlfriends, failed relationships and dating experiences.  That was his nature…to ask questions and focus a conversation on the person he was talking to.  We stayed late that night talking and laughing. 

 

Thus began a friendship and an education which changed my life.  Of course, I regret I didn’t meet Michael when I was 14 years old but I’m so grateful we met when I was 27.  He was 32 when we met but was wise way beyond his years.

 

I’ve written this book to pass on all I leaned from Michael, my friend and mentor.  I should add here I don’t have a degree in psychology.  In fact, I have nothing on my resume that makes me an authority on this topic.  I guess you could say I’m able to write this book because I’m a good listener and I have one hell of a memory.

 

Michael was an amazing teacher because I never even thought of it as teaching.  He’d pose questions and we’d explore the answers together.  Best of all, he loved telling stories which he used to illustrate an idea.  I think a story well told is the best way to learn.  So I’ve written this book, in many ways, as I heard it from Michael.  I only hope I can do justice to the wit and wisdom of my friend.

 

 

*************************************

 





Chapter 1  What Women Really Want

That evening at Applebee’s was a great time.  I told Michael a few stories of my dating successes and failures and went on about a relationship I’d had right after college.  It had been nothing but confusion and frustration for me.  That one still bothered me because I never did understand her.  At a point, I sighed and said, “I have no idea what women want.”

 

Michael kind of came to attention when I said that and responded, “That’s a good question…What do women want?”

 

“Exactly,” I said.

 

“No, really.  Tell me.  What do women want?”

 

I think it was that moment I realized I was totally clueless about women.  On the surface, it seemed like a straightforward and simple question but my mind drew a blank.  I started fumbling with my words and mentioning vague things like love, romance and security.  But, I was totally winging it.

 

Michael was smiling at my efforts.  “Let’s be more specific:  What do women look for in a man?”

 

That did seem to make the question easier…After all, I’d seen daytime talk shows a few times when I was home from work.  “Well,” I said, “Women are looking for a guy who has a sensitive side, someone who respects her, someone who’s stable and faithful…Women are looking for a nice guy.”  I was feeling a little more confident with that response.

 

“Is that your final answer?”  Michael was imitating the quiz show.

 

“Yes, my final answer.”  I continued the joke.

 

“Well, your answer certainly fits with conventional wisdom, but I don’t think that’s the right answer…If it were true, men wouldn’t be so confused.  Consider that relationship of yours.  Did being a nice, stable, respectful guy spell success with that girl?”

 

Boy, he had me there but I defended my answer.  “But there’re books and articles on the subject.  I remember seeing an author on a daytime talk show.  The author had interviewed hundreds of women asking the question, What do you want from the man you love?  The book concluded women are looking for sensitive, caring, loving, romantic men above all else.  They want candlelit dinners, love notes and flowers.” 

 

“Ah,” Michael said, “The author asked, What does she want…from…the man she loves  …From…the man she loves?  What we need to know is how…to be…the man she loves.  The candlelit dinners, the surprises, the gifts and the flowers are just frosting on the cake after she’s chosen that guy to be in love with.  We need to know what it is about that guy that attracts her in the first place.  Why does a woman want that particular guy to understand her and care for her?”

 

Wow…the simplicity of that distinction was brilliance.  “Oh my God,” I said, “You’re totally right.”  I pondered this for a moment as the light bulb went off and said, “What you just said goes to the whole women say one thing and do another concept.  We have to focus on what women do.  No wonder men are so confused.”

 

Michael picked up on this.  “Most women are confused about themselves too.  Their motivations are happening on a subconscious level and they don’t understand it…As they’re speaking feminist talking points, their actions and gut instincts aren’t listening.”

 

“Okay, I’m listening.  What do women want?”  I settled into my seat wishing I had my laptop with me to take notes.  I mean if Michael’s going to unlock this mystery, then what next?   The Da Vinci Code?  The Kennedy Assassination?  Creation?

 

“Well, it’s many things but I’d say one of the most important things a woman looks for in a man is strength.”  Michael pondered and then added, “Strength and Leadership.”  He collected his thoughts for a moment and then went on.  “Let’s look at the traits and instincts developed in pre-historic times by modern women’s ancestors.  Since the dawn of man’s time on earth a woman’s choice of mate was the most important decision she had to make.  Which man she picked could mean the difference between survival and extinction…and in her quest for survival a woman was drawn to the strong male for many reasons.”

 

“First, the prehistoric woman depended on the man for food and protection.  Before she mated a woman had to ask herself, Is this man strong enough to survive?  She, and eventually her child, will need shelter from the elements and food and protection.  All of these will come from the man she mates with and she knows only the strong will survive and be able to provide the things she needs to survive.”

 

“Secondly, it’s important for a woman to produce offspring strong and capable of survival.  In order to do this her egg must be fertilized by the right genes.  Consider the investment a woman has in this regard.  Her eggs are precious since only one is produced each month…and then once fertilized she has nine months of pregnancy and years of child rearing ahead of her.  The cycle of ovulation, pregnancy and child rearing takes years and so it’s no wonder a woman is choosy and searches for a male who, by his own strength and ability to survive, promises to possess genes with the same traits.  Just any man will not do.  She doesn’t want to risk her egg with anything but the strongest genes she can find.”

 

“So a woman’s drive to seek out the stronger male became instinct.  This instinct, strengthened and reinforced by natural selection over 250,000 years of man’s time on earth, became firmly entrenched in the makeup of a woman.  And, though times have changed and the way a man expresses his role in society is different now than it was 250,000 years ago…or even 100 years ago, this instinct is still strong in women today.”

I couldn’t argue with anything Michael had just presented but I probed for clarification.  “Does this mean all women are looking for a linebacker?”

 

Michael pondered for a few seconds.  “I’m sure there are some women who are looking for a linebacker…and they probably won’t be happy until they find one.  But, those are the exception.  Now I would say women are attracted to men who are physically strong but, today the concept of strength and leadership is also interpreted by women in other ways.  Today men can express strength by being decisive, by being knowledgeable, by having the ability to influence others, by being in a position of authority, by being accomplished in one’s profession or in a sport and certainly by being financially successful.  After all, money is power.”

 

“Ah, money and women…they do seem to mix well together.”  I was doing a bit of venting and Michael picked up on it.

 

“But think about it.  If everything we just talked about is true, then of course a woman will be attracted to money.  It means security, shelter, food and all of that for her children too.”  Michael was on a roll.  “And here’s the beauty of a woman’s attraction for money.  You may not be born the size of a linebacker but the size of your financial position is something you do have control over.”

 

“There’s more to it than just your resume’ or your balance sheet though.”  Michael continued.  “You’ll see strength in subtle everyday words and actions.  For example, women notice a man speaking directly in a clear, strong voice without hesitation instead of interrupting his train of thought with uhs, not looking people in the eye or his voice trailing off at the end of a sentence as though he lacks conviction.”

 

“Filling the man’s role often amounts to no more than awareness of the situation, making the decisions or expending a little energy to take the lead.  Another example:  four male coworkers of equal position in a company approach a restaurant door for lunch.  One of them breaks from the formation to open and hold the door for the other three.  To the casual observer, who’s taken charge?  In fact, I believe in the Middle East the last man to enter a room is the one with the highest status.  Women pick up on these subtleties…you can believe that.”

 

I completely understood what Michael was saying but I guess the brainwashing the feminists had done still had hold of me.  “But can’t strength and leadership be taken too far?  I’m often hearing a woman complain about being smothered by her ex.  I think one called it stifling dominance.  She had no voice in anything…she wasn’t allowed to make the smallest decision.”

 

Michael smiled.  “Well, of course it can be carried too far and what you’re talking about will drive a woman away just as being a weakling will.  But actually, the kind of man you’re talking about, that over-the-top, dominant male, is probably not as strong as he’d like everyone to think.  Usually those guys are the most insecure men out there.  That’s why they have a need to handcuff a woman.  They live in fear she’ll get away.  We should add then, a strong man also means he’s secure enough to let a woman be herself and reach for her potentials…not trying to step on her spirit.”

 

I was really enjoying this conversation.  It was like a fog lifting.  I realized how rhetoric, labels and extreme stereotypes had dominated the topic until everyone was confused and polarized.  Michael’s common sense was refreshing.

 

As if he read my mind, he continued with a story.  “Recently, I saw one of those audience participation talk shows and the topic was the male role…How women want men to act.  One woman from the audience made the comment, I want my man to be strong and take charge.  The host, a female, responded to this comment, You want macho?  You like men to act macho?  She said macho with a derogatory tone.”

 

“Now a little unsure, the woman replied, Well, I don’t want a wimp.  The host turned to another woman in the audience and asked if she wanted some macho tough guy.”

 

“Also a bit intimidated by the term macho, the woman replied, Well, I want a man to be strong.”

 

“This went on for a while.  The audience continued to describe their preference in men with terms like strong and take the lead.  The host continued to label any mention of a man being strong, in charge or leading the way as macho and, therefore, a tyrant and undesirable.  She kept trying to sell the audience on what she referred to as the sensitive type but they weren’t buying it.  Every time the sensitive type was mentioned someone in the audience was quick to say they weren’t attracted to a wimp.”

“I became frustrated watching because the host kept talking in extremes.  If a man was strong then he was labeled a macho tyrant.   I couldn’t tell if she was baiting the audience for a lively discussion or just stupid.  The problem, of course, was both the audience and the host were throwing out labels and no one was defining terms.”

 

“Finally, a woman came to the microphone and said, Look, I want my man to be a leader, make decisions and be strong enough to follow through.  I want him to plan the evening, hold the door, choose the wine and drive the car.  I want him to help me when I need it and lend advice and support when I need it and be a father figure when I need it.  I want him to deal with the car mechanic and be shrewd with the salesman.  I don’t want him to let people walk all over him.  I want him to be in charge of his affairs and take care of his business.  I also want him to be strong enough and secure enough in his own masculinity to let me make my own decisions when I need to.  And if that’s what you call macho then I want macho.  This was greeted by explosive applause.  The woman had obviously captured the mood of the audience.  It was refreshing to hear a woman’s opinion fly in the face of fashionable feminist rhetoric.”

 

“So, what am I to conclude about the feminists?” I asked.  “Is the entire movement misguided or was it just hijacked by a bunch of men hating extremists and that’s all we hear?  Does that extremist voice truly represent women?”

 

“I think you’ve hit on it.”  Michael said.  “Those extremist attitudes aren’t representative of women in general but, they did hijack the movement and became the voice of it.  That’s caused a lot of confusion for men and women alike.” 

 

Michael continued, “The spirit behind the movement was simple.  Women wanted a stronger voice in their own lives.  They wanted to be stronger and more self-sufficient.  That doesn’t mean they wanted weaker men.  Unfortunately, many made that interpretation.  Strong, anti-male rhetoric developed.  The domineering male was seen as the cause of women’s subjugation.  A strong male was portrayed as completely undesirable.  He was the devil, the enemy of all womanhood.  Certainly, a woman could never be attracted to this type of man.  A woman would be accused of turning her back on the sisterhood were she attracted to a strong male or were she comfortable in her reliance on him.”

 

I pondered this and said, “So women came to think some passive milquetoast was the ideal man but they found themselves unhappy.  Men started deferring to women and found themselves unsure, uncomfortable, confused and, at the end of the day, failing to satisfy women.  What a mess.”

 

“Let me ask you,” Michael probed, “Have you ever made a decision not based on what your experience and common sense tell you, but rather on trying to please your girlfriend?  And, when that decision went bad, whose fault was it?”

 

“Oh my God, Yes.”  I started remembering an old girlfriend.  “I called this girl I’d been dating and asked if she’d like to see a movie and then go out for something to eat.  My plan was to avoid the crowded restaurants between 6pm and 8pm on a Friday night by seeing an early movie.  It was a simple plan.  Then she started making changes.  All the details are too long and agonizing to go through but here’s the short version.  We arrive to the Multiplex and she changes which movie she wants to see and the next showing begins in over an hour.  So that’s fine but then we should eat before the movie and near the theater so we’re not pressed for time.  There were plenty of restaurant options for that.  No, she wanted to go to Olive Garden.  I love the food there but with the drive and the typical wait on a Friday night I doubted we could get back in time for the movie she wanted to see.  I explained all that to her but she was determined we follow her plan…”

 

“…Sure enough, with traffic and the wait for a table I pretty much wrote off the movie and decided to just enjoy the meal and an evening out.  So as our plates are about empty she asks what time it is.  I look at my watch and tell her the movie had just started.  So get this, she says, Oh, I wanted to go to the movie…Boy, you didn’t plan this out very well.”

 

Michael started laughing as I finished with details of the argument which developed after I told her the problem had been her changes to a simple and workable plan.

 

“Perfect example,” Michael said, “Against your better judgment of time management, traffic and restaurant crowds you bend over backwards to please your girlfriend.  You give control of the evening over to her and when the decisions go bad she sees you as a loser who can’t plan a simple date.”

 

I asked Michael how I should’ve handled that evening.  Should I have demanded to follow my plan to show strength and leadership?  “No, that’d be silly and make you look uptight.  The idea of a date is to simply have a nice evening without stress…and that’s what you did.  You had a nice dinner.  You can see the movie another night.”  Michael thought for a moment.  “The only thing I’d say in a situation like that would be to make sure she understands she has ownership for the plan and it sounds like you did that.  You explained you might not get back in time for the movie she wanted to see…”

 

“…That night you did learn something important about that girl.  She won’t take responsibility for her decisions.  And, if she won’t take responsibility for something minor like which movie to see, what happens when it’s a major decision with major consequences?  I have a friend whose wife got caught up in the housing boom.  Every day she was hearing stories of people making easy money in real estate so she decided she had to get in on the action.  She spent every day at work analyzing the multiple listings online.  She spent every weekend and many evenings dragging her husband around to look at properties.  He was skeptical because real estate values were at ridiculous levels and it all looked like a bubble about to burst.  This was a dangerous situation.  You have a woman who thinks she’s a genius and she’s spending other people’s money…in this case the bank’s money and her husband’s nest egg which he came into the marriage with.  She saw no possibility for bad consequences.  Finally, the husband put his foot down and told his wife her scheme was exactly how people get over-extended and in a position to be wiped out if the market turns.”

 

“Well, she was furious.  She chastised him for being negative.  She accused him of lacking ambition.  But, he stuck to his guns.  Within a year the market collapsed.  The $300,000 downtown condos she claimed would be fantastic rental properties and do nothing but increase in value were now listed for $150,000…and no one was buying at that price.  My friend told me the scariest part has been watching his wife refuse to admit she was wrong.  He sees no indication she learned anything from the experience.  She still thinks she’s a genius and still thinks her husband lacks ambition.”

 

“So we have two examples of strength and leadership.  In your case, it was appropriate to be flexible with plans for an evening out and in the end resulted in a nice meal.  When she blamed you for missing the movie…Well, laugh it off.  You just learned a lot about that girl.  My friend with the wife who wanted to get in on the real estate boom is a different story.  The consequences were huge.  He had to stand strong.  If he’d given his wife control over his finances then he’d be wiped out.  In addition, she wouldn’t see the result of her bad decision as her failure.  She’d see her husband as a loser who can’t manage his money or his life.  And, in a way I’d agree with her.  Okay, hear me out.  The blame would fall on him.  He would’ve failed to maintain control over his finances and his life and she would’ve seen him as weak.  Never mind how unjust that is.  He would’ve failed in his role as the strong male.”

 

“Boy, that story is frightening.  In hindsight his decision to say no to his wife looks like a no brainer but at the time everyone was making a fortune in real estate.  It would’ve been easy to ignore his misgivings about the market and give in to his wife…with disastrous consequences.”

Michael nodded in agreement and then continued.  “His story is a valuable lesson.  But, let’s get back to the dating example because that’s what you and I deal with.  Dating should be a fun time out and a way to get to know a girl.  Being flexible with the plans is a good thing but if you find yourself trying to please her every whim and desire, you’ve lost control.  So at some point you have to take charge.  For example on your date you could say, Okay, I love Olive Garden too but if we go there we probably won’t have time to get back for the movie and I don’t want to rush through dinner trying to make it.  So either we see the movie now and then eat at Olive Garden, or we eat here at this Thai restaurant and then see your movie or we go to Olive Garden for a nice leisurely meal and forget the movie.  In that way, you show leadership and flexibility…and also, you’ve attached responsibility to her for the decision made.  You aren’t being demanding but you aren’t relinquishing your role either.  You’re a flexible voice of reason.”

 

“Boy, you’re right,” I sighed, “But does dating have to be as complicated as a chess match…always thinking 3 moves ahead?”

 

“With some girls, yes it’s a constant game of strategy.  You know you’ve found a special girl when the date goes awry and she doesn’t care.  Whatever movie and whatever restaurant is fine with her because whatever you do together is fun for both of you.  You know she’s special when she doesn’t say, I don’t feel like Thai food…  Instead, she says, I like Thai food…but I really feel like Italian.  Can we go to Olive Garden?  And she gives you an affectionate hug.  Which do you want…a girl who demands or a girl who asks with affection?  I’m off topic here.” Michael continued, “To get back on point, women become dissatisfied with a man when he’s not filling the male role.  Women respond to the man taking control of a situation.  They want traditional male/female roles fulfilled and are happiest when they feel strength and security coming from a man when he’s in charge of himself and his life.  Those affectionate women who ask instead of demand are most always women comfortable with those male/female rolls.”

 

“What about the other women?” I asked.

 

“The women who demand what they want?  Those are women who simply can’t be pleased no matter how hard you try.  They’re the ones who demand and want everything they want and they want it now.  They really don’t care if a man is weak or strong.  They only care about getting what they want.  Do we really need to talk about how to please them?”  Michael and I smiled.

 

Wow, what a great evening it had been.  When we got back to the apartment complex Michael asked me to wait while he got something from his apartment.  He came back with a DVD.  “Here, watch this.  You’ll love it.”  The movie was Three O’clock High.  I’d never heard of it.

 

*************************

 

Later that week we both got away from work for a long lunch.  We met at a restaurant where we were sure to see some nice looking girls.  I couldn’t wait to tell Michael how much I enjoyed the movie he loaned me.  We sat down and I handed him the DVD.  “This movie’s great.  It’s so funny and so true…and such a perfect complement to everything we talked about women being attracted to strong men.  I’d never heard of it.  It’s a classic.”

 

Michael laughed, “I know.  I love that movie.”

 

We talked for a while, ordered lunch and enjoyed watching some pretty girls walking by on the sidewalk.  As our sandwiches arrived I got back to my favorite topic.  “So what else do women want?  There must be more to it than a strong man.”

 

Michael pondered that for a while and then said, “Well, women want what they can’t have.”

 

I laughed, “So in that regard women are as illogical as men.”

 

“Yes.”  Now we were both laughing.  “But really, haven’t you ever noticed that?  It’s human behavior at its best…or worst…depending on how you look at it.  The most obvious example is the girl who’s only attracted to men with a girlfriend…or wife.”

 

“Oh my God!!!”  I was remembering exactly that example from high school.  “Yes, in high school we had a group of friends and there was a girl, Sandy.  I tried and some other guys tried to date her but she wasn’t interested.  She was always friendly but that was all.  Finally, I asked a friend of hers why she wasn’t interested in dating anyone.  Her friend told me she had a huge crush on this one guy…but he had a girlfriend and she knew it.  So what’s up with that?  Is there some deep twisted reason women go for the guy they can’t have?”

 

“Well, I don’t think it’s that twisted.”  Michael took another bite of his sandwich and pondered my question.  “Look at how pre-teen girls have crushes on the latest teen idols.  It’s a safe way for them to start expressing their early feelings of sexuality.  They have the feelings but they aren’t mature enough yet to act on them so they choose a safe crush on Justin Bieber, who they can’t have, instead of a crush on Billy, Biff or Scooter at school.”

 

Another example was coming to me.  “You know what?  Adults do the same thing only it’s called fear of commitment.  There’s a girl at work who fits that story.  I’ve overheard her make self-deprecating comments about her love life…or lack of love life.  A few men in the building have asked her out but she always has some polite excuse.  Here’s the tip off though.  She’s obviously in love with this one guy at work.  She’s always hanging out at his desk and makes sure she gets included in lunch groups with him….and yes, he’s married.  I mean if she was really serious about finding a boyfriend would she spend all her time with a married guy?”

 

Michael was nodding his head.  “Exactly, fear of commitment or being timid…whatever you want to call it.  Wanting what you can’t have is a safe choice but there’s more to it than that.  We see something hard to get as being of greater value than something which comes easily.  Consider this.  A girl has a guy chasing after her and asking her out every day.  Then she sees another guy at school with a girlfriend.  Which guy does she think is the catch?  Which guy has what women are looking for?  The girl doesn’t actually know because she hasn’t dated either guy but she’ll draw the conclusion a guy desperate for a date has less going for him than a guy other women are with.  So she concludes the guy she can’t have is the man with the qualities a woman wants.  And, there’s more to it.  Women love a challenge.  I should say both men and women are stimulated by a challenge.    The point is the man who’s the biggest challenge becomes the object of a woman’s attention.  How many times have we heard the advice, Play hard to get?  Another way to say that is, make yourself a challenge.”

 

By now it was time to get back to work.  Around the corner from the restaurant we walked by a Video store.  “Wait a minute.  Let’s go in here for a second.”  Our conversation of wanting what you can’t have reminded me of a good movie I’d seen.  “Here it is, Secret Admirer with C. Thomas Howell.  You’ll like this.  It’s funny and a good story of how men go for what they can’t have.”  I handed it to Michael and we headed back to work.

 

************************

 

The next Saturday Michael called and asked if I wanted to go to Applebee’s again to eat.  I was happy to get out of the house and do something.  Besides, I was intrigued by our conversation and wanted to pick Michael’s brain some more.  We sat down and I went straight to my favorite topic.  “Okay, so far we’ve established women are looking for strong men who seem a bit out of reach...a guy she can’t have.  What else are women looking for in a man?”

 

Michael pondered for a while.  I could see the wheels turning in his head.  Then he said, “Seduction.”

 

“Women like to be seduced?”  I asked.

 

“Actually, I was thinking of the other way around.  Women like to use their powers of seduction to win a man over.  This actually ties into other things we’ve talked about.  Obviously, it ties into wanting what she can’t have.  She must use her seductive powers to win over the hard to get guy.  But it also ties into our discussion of ancient history.  It is, after all, a woman’s seductive powers which allowed a woman to survive throughout history.”

 

“In ancient times, not only did a woman need to find and mate with the strong male but once she found him she needed to win his affection and keep him by her side to enjoy his protection over a continuing period of time.  We can look at it this way:  In ancient times, if a woman couldn’t seduce a man her chances of survival were practically non-existent.  So for a woman, honing her skills of seduction was basic to survival.  A woman’s power of seduction is one of her strongest instincts…and just as strong today.”

 

“Can I seduce you two guys into a drink?”  The waitress had come to our table and overheard Michael.

 

“Oh my God…We are totally busted.”  Michael threw himself at the mercy of the court.  “I’m really sorry if I offended you.”

 

Now the waitress started laughing.  “Don’t worry about it.  From what I heard, you’re right…I see it played out here every night.  Some women are very good at it.  I’m Stephanie.  I’ll be your waitress tonight.”

 

We ordered a couple of beers and dinner while enjoying the conversation with our waitress…She was pretty cute and had a great personality.  As she walked off our eyes followed her well-shaped ass until she disappeared into the kitchen.  Then I looked at Michael and said, “Wait a minute.  Did she just seduce us into a big tip?”

“Maybe she did…I don’t care.  It worked.”  We laughed and Michael continued.  “Seriously, a woman’s power of seduction is important to her.  It relates to her feelings about herself, her self-esteem and her self-image.  You could compare a woman’s need to know she has seductive powers to man’s basic need to know he can go out in the world and survive and prosper.  For a man, whether it’s hunting for his food in the back woods of Montana or making it in the jungles of big business, he needs to know he has what it takes.  A man needs to know he has the ability to rub two sticks together and build a fire…A woman needs to know she has the ability to attract a man.  Even if a woman isn’t out looking for a man (perhaps she’s married or has a career and is independent or has no time for a relationship) she still likes to walk down the street and know men find her attractive.  It makes her feel like a woman.”

 

“That’s interesting.”  I was absorbing what Michael had said.  “And it’s all common sense…I just never looked at it that way before.”

 

“Looked at what that way before?”  Our waitress and food had arrived.  Michael and I were looking at each other again but this time neither of us had a reasonable answer.  She put the plates down and then started laughing.  “That’s okay.  You guys are just too much fun.  Is there anything else I can get you?”

 

Michael and I were both reading double meanings into what she’d just said and consequently we were both stammering.  “Uhhhh…No, I think we’re fine,” I finally said, “Michael, is there anything else you’d like?”

 

“I think we’re fine.”  Michael said it in a humorous way as if he were at a formal dinner.  She walked away and Michael’s eyes followed her again.  “We’ve got to put a bell around her neck so she doesn’t sneak up on us again.”

 

The food was delicious and we settled in to enjoy our dinners.  Michael said, “You know, if our waitress is trying to seduce us…it’s working.  I like her.”  We chatted while we ate but we were both hungry and focused on the food.  As we finished our plates, Michael picked up the topic again.  “Different women may seduce a man for different reasons.  One reason would be food, shelter and protection.  There’re countries where this is a primary motive.  Of course, there’re other parts of the world where women are looking to a man for designer clothes and German cars.  Now that having been said, hopefully the most common reason is the traditional motive of love, affection and companionship.  What a woman is looking for when she seduces a man depends on her values…what’s important to her…what she’s looking for in life.  Is it love?  Is it money?  Is it both?”

 

“The topic of a woman’s values is really important.”  Michael continued, “Different women have different conceptions of what men are to provide for them.  There are…you could say…traditional women who expect nothing more than affection, respect, emotional support and a sharing relationship.  The other things in life she wants she expects to earn.  She was brought up to understand working for what you get brings self-satisfaction.  On the other hand, there’re women who never do anything for themselves.  Their whole existence revolves around getting things from others…usually men.  And they use their charms to do it.”

 

“Some men get totally blindsided by that type of woman.  A guy grows up with a mom who has traditional values and his sister is the same way.  The two female role models he grows up with are exactly what he’s hoping for in a wife.  He has no idea there’re women out there who’re looking for nothing more than a guy to pay the visa bill at the end of the month.  Raise kids?  Let the nanny do it.  I’m meeting the girls for a latte.  The poor guy gets married with all good expectations and ends up in hell.”

 

“Oh man, I have a friend in that situation,” I said, “It’s not quite that bad but seems to be heading in that direction.  Every day at work he’s talking about his wife driving him into the poor house.  Her spending is insatiable and she has no concept of a budget.  He’s totally stressed out.  He tries to explain it to her but it starts an argument at the slightest mention of financial restraint.”  I paused.  “He’s screwed.”  I put my hand over my mouth and looked around.  I was sure our waitress would hear that comment.  The coast was clear.

 

Michael was laughing at me or our ninja waitress.  I wasn’t sure.  Then he began again on topic.  “Women use seduction in all avenues of their lives.  Girls learn at an early age men can be manipulated with nothing more than a little attention.  Pre – school girls seduce their fathers to get an ice cream cone, a new doll or some loving reassurance after they received a scolding at the other end of the house from their mother.  A teenage girl knows it’s easier to get favors from her father who’s a pushover for a hug than from her mother because the mother knows all those tricks.  When women are shopping they can always try to get a better price by turning on the charm to the salesman.  The saleswoman can often close the sale simply by showing the male customer a little friendly flirtation.  Wearing a tight sweater won’t hurt a woman’s chances of getting special treatment at the auto repair shop.”

 

Michael continued, “It’s common…a little charm, a smile, eye contact.  Men are easy targets…Look at us with the waitress tonight.”  He collected his thoughts and began again.  “Look at everything a women does to attract men:  Her fashion, her hair, her make-up, her fitness class, her perfume, her walk…the list is endless.”

Now I chimed in.  “So what I’ve learned tonight is women like to know they can be seductive.  They like to feel seductive.  They love a man who’s a catch – someone who’s hard to get.  Someone they have to go after and seduce.  If a guy’s easy she’s going to be bored and wonder if he’s of much quality.  And, there’s no challenge for her.”

 

“Exactly,” Michael said, “Let her seduce you.  Let her affirm her feminine powers.  Human nature…anything you work for is of more value to you.”

 

Suddenly, the waitress appeared to clear our plates.  She had them all gathered in her hands and then asked, “So let me ask you guys something.  When a woman’s seducing a man what’s going to be her best asset?”

 

Now Michael and I looked at each other.  Our eyes telegraphed what we were both thinking….best asset?  How did she know we’d been staring at her ass all night?  Did a co-worker tip her off?  Does she have eyes in the back of her head?  Was it just a lucky guess?  Of course with us the odds on that guess were dramatically in her favor. 

 

Finally, Michael broke the silence.  He looked her in the eyes and with a serious, thoughtful tone said, “I would say personality is most important.”

 

She looked at Michael nodding and then with a charming, little smile said, “Good answer.”  She walked back to the kitchen…this time we were looking around the room to see if she had another waitress spying on us.  We didn’t see any.  Maybe it was a lucky guess.

 

A minute later she reappeared with a desert plate.  “This must be your lucky night.  You two just won a free desert to split.  Enjoy.”  She gave us a big smile and walked back to the kitchen.

 

“See how well seduction works…her tip just got even bigger.”  We devoured the desert and talked about our waitress’ great personality.  She brought by the bill and we chatted some more.  We left enough money in the leather folder to cover the bill and a generous tip.  As we were putting on our coats she came by to pick up the folder.  She opened it and asked if we need change.

 

“No, no, that’s for you.  The food was great and the desert was delicious.”

 

“Thank you.  I hope you come back again soon.”  She was looking at Michael when she said that.

 

“I’m sure we will.”  Michael said that with a simple sincerity…not at all like a cheesy pick up line.  I was taking mental notes.  Now Michael said to me so the waitress would hear, “I like Applebee’s.  We need to come here more often.”

 

As we walked out I said, “I think she’s interested in you.  You should’ve asked her for her number.”

 

“Actually, I think the reason she’s interested is because I wasn’t hitting on her and asking for her number.”  Michael raised his eyebrows and smiled. 

 

I slapped my forehead, “Dumb, dumb…My God, I’ve already forgotten everything I learned tonight.”  It was raining lightly.  We ran to the car laughing.

 

***************************

 

My phone rang.  It was Michael.  He told me he was going for a walk and wanted to know if I was up for some exercise.  I wanted to get out of my apartment so I said, “Yes.”

 

We got on to a nice bike path and settled into a comfortable pace.  Michael jumped right into our favorite topic.  “You know, I’ve thought of something else women are looking for in a man.  It’s another one of those obvious things no one ever mentions.”  He paused and made a hand gesture for emphasis.  “Women want…what other women find attractive.”

 

I repeated this over in my mind.  I was trying to follow the logic of it.  “Do you mean there’s a bit of herd mentality with women?”

 

“Yes, I suppose there is a herd mentality in women…and men…but it’s much more than that and it also ties into everything else we’ve talked about…seduction…what they can’t have…”  I could tell Michael had been thinking about this and he was enthusiastic to share his insights.  “I remembered something which happened in college and it perfectly explains this.  I went to school at a relatively small college where if you didn’t know someone personally then chances were you at least recognized them from a class or being on campus.”

 

“My roommate and I often went to the movies together when we didn’t have dates.  This was a small neighborhood theater and it was enjoyable to see friends and talk to people you hadn’t seen in a while.  I’d stand in line and observe people…particularly the girls.  I’d take notice of the girls who didn’t have a date as they might be available and I’d take notice of the girls who had a date to see what kind of a man they were with.  I’d also keep track of any female interest which might come my way.  I was watching for a look, a smile or maybe a second look…perhaps a “hi” from someone who recognized me from a class.  For the most part I’d stand unnoticed in line.”

 

“Then I began dating Kathy.  She’s a great girl…I have to tell you about her sometime.  She’s really quite attractive and vivacious…yeah…Kathy.  Anyway, she’d recently moved to town and didn’t attend college so she didn’t travel in the usual social circles.  We dated steadily and after a few months we had a great relationship…friends, lovers…We always had fun together.”

“Kathy enjoyed dressing up and looking good to go out and this particular night was no exception.  With her clothes, tight jeans, boots, make-up and blonde hair she was striking.  And now we stood in the same line at the same theater with many of the same people as before.  Only now the girls noticed me and these were the same girls who hadn’t taken notice of me many times before.  Mind you, these girls were among the social “A” list at school and previously I’d been invisible to them.  But now they kept turning around and looking at me.  Then they’d talk among themselves and another girl would turn to look as though they were discussing me and checking me out…And it wasn’t just one girl or one group.  It was several girls from different groups.  I started to wonder if it was just my imagination or maybe they were just looking at Kathy because she was dressed so nicely.  Then Kathy took my arm and pulled me closer.  I was wondering if she’d noticed the girls looking at me too.  Maybe it wasn’t my imagination.  I kept reminding myself these girls had never noticed me before.  I really was confused and finally decided they were just curious about Kathy.  She looked great that night and they were wondering who she was.  It was simply women checking out the competition.”

 

“So after the movie we were alone at her apartment.  We started to kiss and she fell back onto the couch with a look of despair and sighed, Every girl in this town is in love with you.  I’ll never be able to keep you.”

 

“I knew exactly where this was coming from.  It was because of standing in line at the theater.  My first thought was, Oh my God…they were looking at me.  Then I tried to reassure Kathy.  I told her she was crazy to think that.

 

“She said, I’m not crazy.  I saw them all looking at you at the movie.  They couldn’t take their eyes off you.”

“Again I tried to reassure her.  I told her they just wanted to see who was with her because she looked so stunning.  She wouldn’t accept that.  I didn’t tell her those girls had never taken any notice of me before.  She wanted to think she had the man all the girls in town wanted and I decided it was best to let her have her way.”

 

“The interesting thing is that night those girls were attracted to me.  They were looking at me and they wanted to know who I was.  Why?  Wasn’t I the same person as before?  Not really.  There was one important difference.  That night I was with a girl who thought I was special and they saw that.”

 

I was blown away by Michael’s story.  I actually stopped on the path, looked at him and said, “That’s an amazing story.  That really happened?”

 

“Yes, I’m not exaggerating one bit of it,” Michael gestured with his hands for emphasis.  “Honestly, I couldn’t believe it was happening either.  But think about it.  Look at what a woman thinks when she sees a man with a woman.  First, in general he must have things going for him or she wouldn’t be with him.  He must have money…no woman in her right mind would be going out with a guy who’s broke.  He must know how to treat a woman, take her out and show her a good time…”

 

Now I jumped in.  “He’s going to be hard to get…a catch.  After all, there’s at least one other girl vying for his time.  He’ll have to be seduced away from that girl.  It ties in to the other things we’ve talked about.”

 

“Also he’s someone a girl can flirt with,” Michael went on to explain, “If a man’s with another woman or has a steady girlfriend, it makes him safe.  He’s not out on the prowl.  Women love to flirt, to express their sensuality and to test their alluring abilities but, they want to be able to do this without being hit on.  So who better to flirt with and feel comfortable with than a man who’s safe.  Say a girl goes to a dance and wants to do some sensual dancing.  If she does that with a single guy she’ll have to fight him off for the rest of the evening.  She’s better off to steal some dances with a guy who’s there with a date.  She can be sexy and provocative yet safe from unwanted advances.”

 

“In a way, when a woman sees a man with a woman it’s like he now has a AAA credit rating…a seal of approval.  She concludes she won’t be taking any social risks by dating him.  He’s on the list of men acceptable for dating.”  Now, Michael collected his thoughts, “And finally, though most will deny it, women are very competitive.  They love to prove their seductive abilities by catching the man all the girls want.”

 

I pondered all of this and then concluded, “So the rich get richer when it comes to women too.”

“Yes,” Michael said, “Being seen with a girl is probably the single most important thing a man can do to help his image.  It says you have things going for you, you know how to treat a woman and you’re a catch…and it states all of this without you uttering a single word.”

 

“That really is a powerful statement and completely true.  I’m thinking of how many times in high school I went to the games, dances or other social events alone when there were plenty of girls I could’ve asked out,” Now I slapped my forehead and continued, “What was I thinking?  I was waiting to find that perfect girl…what an idiot I was.”

 

“Yeah, when that perfect girl came along, she saw you alone, without a date.  And besides, often those average girls you passed up turn out to be the real gems…just like in Secret Admirer.  I loved that movie by the way.”

 

Michael and I veered off the bike path to a coffee shop, sat down for a while and then walked back to where we lived.  All the while we told stories of missed opportunities…times we could’ve gone out with girls – as friends – and had nice times with nice people.  It would’ve been a boost to our image…and who knows that a friendship might evolve into something more.

 

***********************

 

The next time Michael and I got together was to watch a playoff game one Sunday evening.  It was men being men that evening.  I brought over some beer and chips and Michael ordered in a pizza….life is good.

 

As we settled in I asked Michael something I’d been thinking about.  “You know, I think everything we’ve been talking about as far as what women are really looking for in a man is right on the mark but where does romance fit in?  I mean, if you listen to daytime talk shows, read women’s magazines or look at survey results you’ll hear women saying the same thing over and over:  Romance is missing in my life.  I’m looking for romance and not finding it.  I want romance in my life.  It seems to be such a dominant topic for women.”

 

Michael pondered this for a few seconds and then asked, “Okay, when you say romance are we talking about romance novels and some mysterious stranger with a Latin accent coming into a woman’s life…or are we talking about candlelit dinners, love notes, unexpected presents, breakfast in bed, flowers and making love in front of a fire on a bear skin rug?”

 

“Interesting…I hadn’t thought about the romance novel kind of romance.  I was thinking about the candlelit dinners.”

 

“Yes, that’s what most people think about when they hear the word romance so let’s stick to that.  On the surface it sounds pretty simple:  1) Women want romance.  2) A man sends flowers and has candlelit dinners.  3) The man is successful.  There’s probably a book titled, How to Romance a Woman.  Problem is…it just isn’t that easy.  Romance is a feeling people have and sometimes those romantic gestures give that feeling and sometimes they don’t.”  Michael paused and then gestured with his hands as if he’d just had a revelation.  “I have the perfect story.  There’re two girls who work in my building.  I know Mary a little but she works with Vivian and I often have lunch with her.  She told me this story.”

 

“So Mary and Vivian come back to their desks after lunch and there’s a little vase with flowers on Mary’s desk.  They’re both intrigued and Vivian makes some comment about a secret admirer as Mary looks at the card.  Oh…they’re from Jeff.  He wants to take me out tonight.  Mary says this with no enthusiasm.  Vivian asks who Jeff is and Mary goes on, Oh, this guy who works on the seventh floor.  He keeps asking me out but I’m just not interested.  I wish he’d take the hint.  Vivian predicts he’ll probably be calling her soon and asks what she’s going to tell him.  I’ll dream up some excuse…Do you believe this guy?  Sending flowers…he’s such a geek.”

 

“Now the story so far is a pretty dramatic demonstration it takes more than romantic gestures to get a woman interested in you but it gets even better.  A few months later Mary and Vivian come back to their desks and Mary finds a note on her desk.  Oh my God…This is from Steve.  He and some friends are going out after work for dinner and he’s asking me to come along.  Vivian asks who Steve is and Mary tells her with much enthusiasm, He’s this really neat guy who works over in shipping.  Something about him…I’ve been putting out the vibes hoping he’d take the hint....and he did.  Isn’t that romantic?  So now Vivian reminds Mary she has other plans for the evening and she asks her what she’s going to tell Steve.  That’s right.  I told my mom I’d come over…No problem…Mom will understand.  I’m not going to turn Steve down and risk letting him get away.”

 

That story spelled it out for me.  “So women want romance but they only want romance from the man they’re attracted to…and they’re attracted to that man because of all the other things we’ve been talking about…He’s strong, he’s a catch…”

“You are correct sir.”  Michael gave me a nod of his head.  “Don’t get me wrong.  I’m not knocking flowers and dinners and love notes.  We’re just saying those things alone aren’t the answer.  I mean if flowers were a slam dunk to get any guy laid, I’d own a florist shop and be rich.”  Michael had gotten up to get another beer as he said that and I was still laughing when he got back to his seat.  “It takes three things to create romance – A romantic setting, a romantic happening…and the right person.  If you’re with the right person then the other two things are going to fall into place pretty much no matter where you are or what you’re doing.  But, you can be in the most romantic place on earth with a candlelit dinner and violins playing…if you’re with the wrong person it’s just not going to happen.”

 

Michael went on.  “Take a romantic setting like a deserted beach with a couple walking hand in hand.  What makes the difference between a woman describing her walk on the beach as real nice and describing it as a magical evening I’ll never forget?  The difference is the person she’s with.  The right person adds the chemistry which gives her the romantic feeling.”

 

“You know, there’s something else about romance and that’s how it plays in different phases of a relationship….For example, take that time when a woman knows someone but they’re not yet dating.  During this time a woman is forming an opinion of a man.  She’s deciding if she’s attracted to him and if she wants to date him.  She knows him from work or at school and has an opportunity to make judgments about him in a variety of areas.  Is he wealthy?  Is he intelligent?  Is he physically attractive?  Is he morally sound?  It all adds up to her deciding if she’s attracted to him…”

 

“…And during this time before dating, I’d say a man’s romantic ability isn’t that important as a criteria for a woman to decide if she’s attracted to him or not.  I’d say all the things we’ve talked about with strength and what she can’t have and seduction and what other women find attractive are far more important in a woman’s subconscious decision making process.”

 

“I’m guessing after a woman’s found a man attractive she’ll make the assumption…based on a deep down hope…that this is the man who’ll bring romance into her life.  She finds him attractive and wants to date him because she has romantic feelings towards him.  At this point, just asking her to a movie becomes a romantic gesture for her…”

 

“…Then come the dating and that first kiss…this is the phase where a relationship is filled with romance.  Everything you do together feels romantic.  The excitement of getting to know someone new, wondering how the other person feels about you, waiting by the phone to see if he’ll call again and the magic of the first kiss are all things which make dating so romantic.  Also, during dating the guy’s trying harder to impress the girl so he’s probably making little romantic gestures which add to what’s already a romantic atmosphere.”

 

“Unfortunately, the next phase becomes less romantic.  The excitement and romance of dating fades and dies as dating moves to a steady relationship.  For the excitement a couple found during their first dates to continue the man and the woman are going to have to work at romance.  When a couple has a commitment and is living together or married the excitement of something new has past.  The mysteries about each other are solved.  The insecurity of dating’s over as the security of a relationship sets in.  Now, the couple share daily activities which are usually far less exciting than going out on a special date with someone new.  Instead of best foot forward, they’re seeing each other in a truer light.  Will the magic continue?  Now a man’s romantic gestures become far more important to maintain the woman’s romantic feelings…”

 

“…After a woman settles into a relationship she still wants to have those romantic feelings.  She still wants to do romantic things in romantic places.  She still wants to be treated in that special way she was treated on the first date.  It’s the romance which keeps a woman happy in a relationship and keeps it strong.  Now is the time for candlelit dinners, love notes, flowers, breakfast in bed and dressing up to go out to a movie…”

“…Without both the man and the woman making those romantic gestures, the relationship will go stale…it’s what can lead to breakup due to ambivalence.”

 

The playoff game had started out a close match up but was becoming one sided.  We started talking about our jobs and then I brought the topic back to women.  “You know Michael everything we’ve been talking about is like a breath of fresh air.  All I’ve ever heard about women is the conventional wisdom you hear about being a nice guy or at the other extreme you hear the worst things about women from a very cynical point of view.  I see articles every day in magazines or on the internet about dating but I’ve never heard the things you’ve talked about and articulated in such a common sense way.  Ever since I started dating I’ve been totally confused.  The conventional wisdom just didn’t make sense with what I was seeing and experiencing.  I’d hear what women said they wanted but it didn’t jive with what they were doing.  Everything you’ve told me perfectly explains how women act and the things they do.  But, does all this apply to every woman?  Are there exceptions?”

 

Michael pondered this, “Are there exceptions…?”  He got up to clear some things off the coffee table as he thought about that one.  “Of course, every woman’s going to be different in the specifics of what she wants.  Some women are looking for wealth.  Some women are looking for a stable guy to be a good husband and father.  Some women are looking for a trophy-husband so all the girls will envy her.  But those specific priorities don’t conflict with anything we’ve talked about and whether a woman’s looking for a movie star husband or simply a good father she still wants a strong, vital man who she thinks is a catch.”

 

“Now of course, you’ll meet women who tell you they’re different.  They’ll tell you they aren’t motivated like other women.  Don’t believe it.  In fact, the women who claim to be different are the ones who follow the rules the most.  When you watch what they do it won’t follow with what they say.”

 

Michael started smiling and I knew he had a good story for me.  “I have a friend at work,” Michael began, “He was seeing a girl for three months totally on her terms.  She told him she wanted a sensitive man who didn’t try to control everything.  So he went along with what she wanted to do and always on her schedule.  He was at her beckon call and did everything for her to show her how much he cared for her…and sexually they never went beyond a friendly kiss goodnight.  She always told him how special he was and what a good friend he was and how she wouldn’t be attracted to a guy who pushed her too fast.  She wanted a guy to show her how he felt and didn’t play games, blah, blah, blah.  Then, all of a sudden she’s spending time with another guy…he’s just a friend, of course.  He’s the boyfriend of a friend of hers.  Her friends had broken up and she went right after the guy.  In like a week this new guy’s spending the night.  My friend was devastated.  He told me looking back he realizes he wasn’t anything more than a convenience to her.  His role was to fill time and take her out until she met the guy she really wanted.  Here’s the icing on the cake…He told me the new boyfriend didn’t fit the picture she’d drawn of her ideal man at all.  Apparently the guy’s ex-girlfriend was always jealous because he had other female friends who were after him and he’d pretty much dominated that relationship.”

 

I felt for the guy and could relate to the story.  “So what should he have done differently to win her?”  I was really curious.

 

“Well, there was probably nothing he could’ve done to win her.  I suspect he wasn’t her type.  But, if he’d understood what women really want in a man then at least he would’ve known being her doormat wasn’t going to make her fall in love with him.  As soon as she started talking about looking for a nice, sensitive guy to be friends…That’s code for I’m looking for a doormat…he could’ve moved on and saved himself the time and heartache.  Accepting the role of a faithful puppy dog pretty much sealed his fate.”

 

“You see.  Stories like that one are the kinds of things I saw happen and I just didn’t understand what was going on.  Now it’s so obvious.  The stories are great.  They’re like the practical application of the theories and laws of Michael.”  The game was long over and I was getting up to go home.

 

Michael got up and said, “Wait a minute.”  He started digging around in the cabinet under the TV and emerged with a DVD in his hand.  “Here’s some more practical application.  You’re going to love this movie.”

 

The Last American Virgin I read the title and looked briefly at the credits.  “Great, I’ll watch it tomorrow and get it back to you.”  I walked back to my apartment thinking about girls I’d dated and things I would’ve done differently.

 

***************************

 

Movies Referenced in this chapter:

 

Three O’clock High

 

Secret Admirer

 

The Last American Virgin

 

 

 





Chapter 2  Improving Your Image

“Man attracts not that which he wants, but that which he is.”


Two days later I dropped by Michael’s to return his movie, The Last American Virgin.  “Hi Michael, this movie is great.  It wasn’t what I thought it would be at all.  Watching it got me thinking about everything we’ve talked about and how to apply it.  I mean we’ve discussed the theories and principles…I’m ready for the practical application.  I’ve been thinking about how I present myself and what image that projects to women.  I want to know what I can do differently.  I guess I’m saying I’m starting to understand what women want…How can I become that?

 

“That’s interesting,” Michael’s head was nodding like the wheels inside his brain were turning.  “The practical application of the theories is really the next step, isn’t it?  I like that!  Let’s both think about examples and we’ll email back and forth…Yeah, how can a guy change his image to become attractive to women?”

 

***********************

 

The next day at work I emailed Michael a short list of topics.  I listed Grooming and Clothing, Money, Confidence and Be Seen with Women.  I added it was obvious to me how to improve grooming and clothing.  That was something I could do at home.  But, I needed examples of how to change my image with regard to the rest.  Was a guy to start bragging about how rich he was or how confident he was?  And, if a guy doesn’t have a girlfriend, how can he be seen with a woman?

 

A few hours later I got an email from Michael.  Here was the text:

 

Tom’s trying to get something going with a girl he met at a friend’s social gathering.  He’s very attracted to her and is calling to ask her out on what would be their first date.

 

Jane:  “Hello.”

 

Tom:  “Hello, Jane?  This is Tom.  We met at Bob’s barbeque.  How are you?”

 

Jane:  “Fine.  How are you doing?”

 

Tom:  “The reason I called is we spoke at the barbeque about how much you enjoy salsa dancing and I was wondering if we could go out to a club sometime so I can learn to dance salsa.

 

Jane:  “Well…I don’t know if I could teach someone…I’m just learning myself.”

 

Tom:  “Well, I know some basic Latin dance so I figured with your experience I could pick up salsa fairly easily.”

 

Jane:  “Well, I don’t know…uh…Oh, I guess we can try.

 

Tom:  “Great!  I’m available any night.  How about Friday or Saturday?”

 

Jane:  “No…..uh…Let’s make it Thursday.”

 

Tom:  “Sounds good.  I’ll see you at your place around 7 o’clock…if that’s a good time.”

 

Jane:  “Okay, see you then.  Bye.”

 

Tom hung up the phone congratulating himself for thinking of such a good idea to get together with Jane.  He was also fantasizing…maybe the date would go so well he’d get lucky Thursday after the club.  Would it be her place or his?

 

Wednesday evening, Tom’s phone rings.

 

Tom:  “Hello?”

 

Jane:  “Hi Tom.  This is Jane.  I’m sorry, but I won’t be able to make it Thursday night.  I’ve previously made plans to go away to a concert with some friends on Friday and since I’ll be leaving town for a while, I need Thursday to pack.”

 

Tom:  “Oh…Maybe we can get together when you get back?”

 

Jane:  “Well, I’m not sure.  I’ll let you know.”

 

Tom:  “Okay, Thanks for calling, Jane.  See you when you get back.”

 

Tom tried to get together with Jane two or three more times but she never seemed to be available.  Finally, he gave up.

 

Okay, print out this scenario and the next time we get together we can analyze what Tom did right and what he did wrong.

 

I printed it out and read it over a few different times.  I jotted down a few notes but overall, the story seemed kind of innocuous.  The guy asked her out and she was busy.  Maybe I was missing something.

 

Michael and I got together after work for a beer a few days later and I brought my printout.  Michael was anxious to hear my impressions of his little scenario.  “So, give me your critique of Tom and his dating efforts.”

 

“Well, I think I’m going to fail this test.  The whole thing seemed pretty normal.  He calls her up, she’s busy and they never get together.”  I paused and thought for a while.  “I give the guy credit for coming up with something different from the average date of going out to eat or a movie.”

 

I could tell Michael was getting ready to enlighten me.  “What you just said is really the point.  What Tom did seemed normal.  His mistakes aren’t blatantly obvious.  In fact, what he did seemed so normal to him he wasn’t even aware he’d done anything wrong.  He thought he’d done a lot of things right.  He complimented her dancing ability.  He focused the date on something she enjoyed.  He showed he wasn’t a chauvinist or insecure by asking her to teach him to dance.  He made himself available so she’d know he wasn’t dating other women.  He thought he’d presented himself well to Jane.  So why wasn’t she interested?”

 

I started to smile because now I saw some of what was going on with Tom and his efforts to date Jane…but, I let Michael continue.  “This story is, in reality, a perfect example of someone doing everything possible wrong.  I have seven major mistakes written down he made while dealing with Jane.  Any single one of them would make a girl back off but all of them together spells disaster.  I’ll start off and then I think you’ll be able to add some.”

 

Michael began.  “Okay, Number 1.  He made himself available every night including Friday and Saturday.  By doing this Tom has suggested he doesn’t have any other dates, activities or girlfriends and is sitting around waiting for her to teach him how to dance.  Women will judge someone who can’t get a date as desperate or a loser.  Jane’s thinking, If no one else wants him, why should I?  He’s certainly no challenge to me and probably the type who clings once he does get a date.”

 

“Ouch, that hurts because I’ve done similar things…making myself too available.”  I was starting to get the gist of it now.  “Okay, Number 2.  He put her in charge by asking if she’d teach him to dance.  Instead of demonstrating leadership, he’s showing dependency which makes him look weak.”

 

Michael gave a nod of approval.  “Very good, Grasshopper is learning.  Okay, Number 3.  He hasn’t made himself of any value to Jane.  He should be doing something for her like inviting her to a party, out to a movie, something to eat….anything.  Instead, she’s doing him a favor by teaching him how to dance.  In effect, he needs her more than she needs him.”

Now, I’m really shaking my head at how obvious that was.  “Oh my god, that seems like a nuance on the surface but so dramatic in its effect on her.  Give me Number 4.”

 

“Number 4.  He asked her to get involved in something she’s uncomfortable doing.  Remember she was unsure if she could teach someone to dance.”

 

Michael continued, “Number 5.  When she broke the date and didn’t offer to schedule another time to get together, Tom should’ve simply said in a pleasant voice, ‘Oh, okay well have a good trip. Bye.’  Instead, he’s almost begging as he suggests maybe they can get together when she gets back.  He makes it sound like he’ll be sitting around all week waiting for her call.  And he ends the conversation with, ‘…Thanks for calling…’  He’s basically saying, ‘Thanks for dumping on me.  Usually, I get stood up with no prior notice.’”

 

Now I’m laughing out loud at how Michael has dramatized how a simple poor choice of words can make us men look so pathetic.  “This is killing me,” I admitted, “Because, I’ve done so many similar things.”

 

Michael continued, “Number 6.  Tom’s idea of how to get together with Jane isn’t honest or straight forward.  It’s actually quite contrived.  It’s obvious Tom’s interested and wants to date Jane.  If that’s what he’s trying to do, why not be confident and straight forward.  He should simply invite her to join him and his friends for a drink after work or come to a party.  Both would be casual, friendly gatherings where no one would be pressured.  Most importantly, for a first date Tom should be doing something he’s completely comfortable with…like a drink with friends or a restaurant he knows.  He should only go dancing on a first date if he’s an expert dancer.”

 

Michael gave some fanfare.  “And finally, Number 7.  Tom continues to ask her out when it’s obvious she isn’t interested in dating him.  If she were interested, she’d have kept the date in spite of being pressed for time.  At the least, she would’ve made a point of arranging another time for the date.  He should’ve read her disinterest and quit asking her out.”

 

“Oh man…Amen to that one.  I’ve been guilty too many times of chasing after a girl when it’s obvious she isn’t interested.”  I was slapping my forehead to dramatize how dumb I’d been.

 

Michael put away the notes he’d brought and said, “And that’s a practical application of the theories.  It dramatizes how everything we say and do effects the image we portray to others.  Tom didn’t portray strength.  He certainly wasn’t hard to get.  He didn’t appear to be someone who had women interested in him.  To Jane he must have looked like a looser with no life…And he did all of that simply with his choice of words and his contrived idea for a first date.”

 

Michael and I were running poor Tom into the ground some more when we were interrupted by Sonia, a woman Michael worked with.  The three of us had eaten lunch together a few times and I enjoyed her personality.  “Hi guys.  Mind if I join you?”

 

Michael and I both sprang to our feet to rearrange the chairs and the things on the table.  “Not at all, let’s make some room for you.”

 

“So what are you two talking about?  Can I join in?”

 

“Yes, of course.  We were just talking about a friend of ours and his misguided efforts in the dating world.”  I liked Michael was honest about our conversation.

 

“There’re a lot of misguided men out there.  Let…me…tell…you.”  Sonia spaced her words out for emphasis.

 

Michael and I looked at each other, looked at Sonia and said in unison, “Please do.”

 

“Okay, I will…but this isn’t just me talking.  I hear this from all of my friends too.  I don’t want you to think I have some sort of pathology for bad men or seek these guys out.  They’re everywhere and all my friends find them too.”  Sonia finished taking off her coat and getting settled at the table.  “You know,” she said, “most men are far less than they first appear.  You meet them and they seem to be perfect.  They’re successful, secure and full of romance…a real catch.  They dress the part…They talk the part…And, most girls, including me, fall for it.  Then, after a few dates, the façade starts to fall.  First, it’s the money problems.  They aren’t quite as financially secure as they made out to be.  They start going for cheap dates and expecting me to pay.  Okay, it’s not like I’m after a millionaire and I don’t even mind sharing the movie tickets or the restaurant.  It’s just when a few weeks before they were acting like Mr. High Roller with all these deals going and just got back from Vegas and now they can’t afford a pizza…Well, it’s pretty obvious it was all talk.”

I was shocked by her stories and asked if she were exaggerating.  She continued, “No, I’m not exaggerating at all.  You meet these guys and they look like a rock of confidence…A month or two goes by…And then the insecurities start showing up.  Jealousies, defensive, won’t admit when he’s wrong, hates his job, constantly putting other people down…even his good friends.  The guy who looked so great three weeks before now looks like just another looser.”

 

I was fascinated listening to Sonia and jumped in with a question.  “So what made him look so good when you first met him?”

 

 “He looked the part.”  She answered, “He had all the right things to say.  He was dressed great.  He portrayed a perfect image of what I was looking for…actually, what all women are looking for.”

 

Now Michael probed Sonia.  “So does that mean women go for an image?”

 

Sonia pondered the question.  No one wants to admit to being shallow and I enjoyed she didn’t get defensive with her answer.  Her answer was a very important point.  “Hey, when you first meet someone, what else do you have to go on?  It’s not like I can dial up some dating credit bureau for his rating.”   Now she gave us a grin and added, “Besides you guys are worse than us…well, not worse, but just as bad.  Men will pass up the quality ladies for a total bitch if she’s a blonde with tight clothes.”
