Michael and I looked at each other and then grinned at Sonia.  “Yes, you’ve got us there for sure.”  Michael paused for a moment and followed up.  “Men are really good at going into denial about a woman’s obvious lack of substance when she’s beautiful.”

“Oh, I have to get going.”  Sonia said as she started picking up her things.  Michael and I stood and thanked her for coming over.  We made her promise to join us again and she enjoyed the fuss we made over her.

 

We watched her make her way through the tables to the door and then sat down to order an appetizer and another beer.  “Did you see that?”  I said.  “We were being seen with a nice looking woman…Good for the image.” 

 

Michael said, “I don’t know if you’re kidding or not but you’re right.  Making friends with girls from work and inviting them to join you for lunch or for a drink after work can really pay dividends.  The key is for it to be on a friendship level so the women feel secure and comfortable with you.  They know you’ll be a gentleman.  They know you won’t hit on them.  They feel safe but they also appreciate your attention and friendship…And other women will see that.”

 

“Michael, I can’t stop thinking about something Sonia said.  It’s so obvious but I’ve never heard it before.  She said when you first meet someone, what else do you have to go on but their image.  I mean, it may not be who they are…but it’s the image they present.”

 

Michael nodded his head and said, “The dirty little secret is, unfortunately, a large percentage of people in this country never get past image.  They never look into a person’s substance.  And of course, this means our image…or the image we portray…influences every part of our life.  It determines the friends we have, the jobs we get, our movement up the organizational ladder, our ability to negotiate and on and on.”

 

“Boy, tell me about it.  Corporate America is full of executives who are incompetent.  In reality, they couldn’t organize a walk around the block, yet they move up the ladder of success with nothing more going for them than the ability to dress, act and talk the part.  So tell me Michael, does all image and no substance get a man anywhere with women?”

 

Michael weighed his answer.  “Well, there are plenty of shallow women out there and as long as they’re getting what they want from the guy, they don’t care he’s of little substance.  A woman has a good looking guy and he maintains her life style…what does she care if he’s a phony.  On the other hand, there are many, many women out there who won’t go for that.  Some guy wraps himself up very nicely and a woman finds the package attractive.  He’s good looking and has money…You’ll never hear me criticize a woman for being attracted to a good looking man who’s also financially secure.  That’s what every woman should be looking for.  Anyway, say some guy wraps himself up very nicely but the woman soon finds out the box has nothing in it.  There’re a lot of women who’ll be gone in short order.”

 

Now I wanted to steer the conversation back to practical application.  “So, sadly, we often see image counts for more than substance.  I’d like to think I have substance.  I want to talk about people of substance who neglect their image and, consequently, don’t receive the credit they’re due in their professional or social life.  What are the things I can do to change people’s impression of me for the better?”

 

“Ah…Good question,” Michael nodded and continued.  “What all this boils down to are the clues we give off as we move through our day at work or through our social life.  We need to figure out how to give off the right clues.”  Michael paused for a thought.  “Hell, I guess we also need to figure out how we see ourselves and how others see us…And, is there a difference.”

 

I gave a chuckle at that and confessed, “I think I can safely say others see me differently than I’d like them to.  So I want to know how I can improve my image.”

 

It was getting late and time to head home so we settled up with the waiter and walked out.  But, before we left we decided to email each other and develop a list of practical ways a guy can positively impact his image.

 

********************

 

Over the next week we emailed back and forth constantly.  Michael would tell me an idea which would remind me of something.  Sometimes watching other people would give us an idea.  Pretty soon we had so many random tips and strategies I decided to organize them into topics…and then the list of topics grew to 15 or so.  It got to be quite a project but also a lot of fun.  Luckily, I’d started saving them to a file on my computer.

 

Grooming and Clothing

 

Michael emailed me to ask if I wanted to meet after work at a favorite hangout.  I arrived just as Michael arrived.  Luckily we beat the rush and got a table right away.  I started telling him about a guy I’d known a few years ago.  “I worked with this guy and was around him for a year.  He wasn’t particularly smart.  He didn’t have good reasoning skills.  He always had some complaint or resentment towards management he would express and he enjoyed belittling other employees…But, all of this was done behind people’s back.  He was quite two-faced.  All in all I had very little regard for him as a person.  One thing about him though was he always seemed to get the breaks at work and otherwise for that matter…”

 

“…Once he made a remark to me about being dressed for success.  Now, I realize how he was getting through life.  Whereas, I saw his ugly side, he never showed that to the people who he deemed important.  Whether it was the boss or someone wealthier than him or whoever, he was a different person to them.  In addition, he was careful to always be dressed and groomed just right…”

 

“…I saw him as ugly in his thinking, in his work and how he regarded other people, but I came to realize most people saw another person.  They saw a dignified gentleman on the success track.  He used that to his best advantage.  It really makes me realize how important dressing and grooming are.”

 

“You know,” Michael hesitated to collect his thoughts.  “To tell someone to shower and shave and use deodorant and brush your teeth makes you sound like…well…a mother talking to her nine year old son, but there’re a lot of men who need that advice.  And it actually goes beyond the basics.  Guys need to optimize what they have.  I’ve seen guys with hair styles that just aren’t working for them.  They’d be well advised to try a new stylist and find the style that works.”

 

“Clothes make the man…as they say.”  I paused before my confession.  “Honestly, I don’t care enough about what I wear…I know I should and so I work at it.  I try to be observant and keep track of fashions.  When I see someone dressed nicely I make a note and try to buy something like it.  One thing for me though is I have to feel comfortable in what I’m wearing.  For example, I’ve never felt right in a suit and tie.  For some formal event I put it on and suffer through it but thank goodness I don’t have to wear a tie to work.  I know guys who love wearing a suit…and they look like a million bucks…It’s them.  For me, I like nice slacks with polo shirts or sweaters…nice golf attire.  It’s what I look and feel best in…nice but casual.  Hey, it’s what Tony Soprano always wore.”

 

“At the other end of the scale, there are guys who look great in jeans and a work shirt.  They’ve got the build and the manner and they look right.  Go to Montana and you’ll see cowboys dressed like cowboys and the women love it because it’s authentic.  The men fit the clothes and the clothes fit the men.  Again, it looks right.  Back to what you just said…You have to find your style and dress well within that style.  I have a friend who loves sports and outdoors.  He only looks and feels comfortable when he’s dressed for whatever sport he’s playing.  He looks great when he’s dressed for golf or for the mountains or the beach or tennis.  The key is your clothes are authentic to who you are and appropriate for where you are.”

“You know what I did?  I finally went out and got a full length mirror for my bedroom so I could see the whole picture before I went out.  I was surprised a few times.  I put on pants or a shirt I liked but when I saw myself in the mirror…it just wasn’t working.  Sometimes the color or the pattern was wrong.  Sometimes the pants didn’t fit as well as I’d thought.  It was interesting and I developed the habit to look in the mirror before I left the house.  I try never to leave the house unless I’m dressed and groomed and prepared to be seen by the girl of my dreams.”

 

“You know something else,” Michael loved to give hand gestures for emphasis.  “Your grooming extends beyond your person.  Your home, your bedroom, your bathroom, your car are all things people can look at for clues about you.  You can look at those things and tell if a guy’s life is organized and on track or disheveled and a mess.  Dirty dishes in the sink, dirty clothes on the floor, mold growing in the bathroom, the car needs washing…These are all clues a good detective will use.  And believe me, all women are good detectives.  Where you live and what car you drive speak volumes about someone…These aren’t things which can be changed overnight like buying a new shirt but men need to set long term goals to upgrade their life.  I have a friend at work who did that.  He worked a second job on weekends for a year.  His master plan was to pay off his credit card so he could afford to move into a nicer apartment.  He was also able to pay off his car loan.  Now he has a nicer apartment and money left over at the end of the month because he isn’t living on credit.  He said living without financial stress has given him a sense of freedom…and he loves it.  His whole attitude has changed.  He’s always smiling and relaxed now.”

 

“Getting back to clues and what they say about you.  Say a woman sees a man well dressed and he drives a nice car.  She sees a man who’s successful and financially secure.  He looks strong and in charge of his life…”

 

“…Now consider what a woman thinks when she sees a man who’s disheveled and in dirty, grubby clothes.  Is he in charge of his life?  Is he successful?  Financially sound?  Is he a man who influences others?”

 

“The best thing a guy can do is to start looking at the men he sees every day.  Look at the guys who have it all together.  What’s your impression of them?  Then look at guys who don’t look successful.  How do they look different from the successful guys?  Then take a hard look at yourself to see where you can improve.”

 

Be Busy

 

“So other than physical appearance, how can a guy make himself more attractive to women.”

 

Michael gave me a big smile and said, “Actually, I’d been thinking about one thing you can do which is very easy.  Have you ever heard the saying, Lead, follow or get out of the way?”

 

“Isn’t that some well-known business leader’s favorite saying?”  I ventured a guess.

 

“Yeah, I think it is.  The saying is basically a challenge for people to get their ass in gear.  It refers to those people in this world who do nothing but stand around and get in the way of others who are going places, doing things and being productive.  Now off the top of your head, which type of person is a woman going to find attractive?  On the one hand, you have people who are boring, going nowhere and having no fun.  They’re usually emotional parasites who try to attach themselves to others hoping to have their lives enriched for them without putting out any effort.  This type of person is not found attractive by women…”

 

“…Women are looking for the opposite of this person.  Women want a vital, energetic man who’ll fill their life with excitement.  A friend of mine from college, Lane, was exactly this type of energetic person.  Lane was never just hanging out.  If he was at a bar, he wasn’t sitting there having a drink.  He was out on the floor dancing.  When he was at work, he worked hard and enjoyed whatever he was doing.  When he played, he put everything into it and enjoyed himself 100%.  He wasn’t rich but his life was full of travel and adventure.  Let me tell you, women were drawn to Lane.  They saw him like a train moving on down the track and if they didn’t jump on now then it would be gone and they’d miss their chance at excitement and adventure.”

 

I’d known guys like Michael’s friend and I’d always admired their energy.  “That’s an interesting contrast.  So how do I become a vital, energetic person who’s doing things?”

 

“You know,” Michael said, “Often it’s nothing more than your state of mind and choice of words.  Say you’re at work and ready to take a break to get something to eat at the little sandwich shop in your building.  On your way out of your office you stop by a girl’s desk and tell her, I’m on my way down to get a sandwich.  Can I bring you something?  Notice the choice of words.  You’re a man of action and you’re on the move.  You aren’t asking for someone to go with you.  You aren’t waiting for a group to form.  If she wants to go with you, then she’d better grab her purse and go.”

 

“Here’s another one:  When someone asks you what you did yesterday, never say, Oh, nothing.  Better to say, Oh, I went to gym in the morning, had a great lunch out with a friend and then relaxed at home.”

 

“Say someone calls and asks what you’re doing…Never say, Oh, nothing.  I’m bored.  There’s nothing good on TV.  Better to reply, Oh, I just walked in the door… or …I’m just on my way out to go for a run…”

 

“I worked with an attractive girl.  She was full of energy and quite active.  One time she started telling me about a guy she’d dated once or twice.  She started losing interest in him because he’d call her every night.  She told me the guy would call and keep her on the phone forever.  She started to assume he had nothing to do.  I remember her saying after work she’d go for a run or play tennis and then with shopping, housekeeping and dinner her evenings were really busy…So how did this guy have time to call her and go on forever about anything and everything.  She described her frustration trying to politely hang up so she could get on with what she was doing.  The best part was her describing his inspiring messages he’d leave on voice mail.  Hi, I was bored so I thought I’d call.  Give me a call.  She was so funny making fun of this guy.  She stopped answering or returning his calls.  He finally got the message and left her alone.”

 

“So she’d made a judgment of this guy based on his phone conversations.  She saw him as a boring guy who didn’t have anything to do.  She saw him as doing nothing for her but wasting her time.  So don’t hang on the phone for hours talking to a woman.  Make sure your phone call has a purpose.  Keep the conversation friendly and pleasant but short.  Get the business of the call over and excuse yourself.  Even if the conversation has become mutually interesting, don’t let the call go over 5 or 10 minutes.  Interrupt with, Gosh, I really have to run.  We’ll finish this conversation next time.  That’s like the oldest rule of entertainment:  Leave them wanting more.  The woman hangs up thinking how she enjoyed talking with you and hoping you’ll call again.  She’s also thinking you must be a busy, vital person to have to run off like that.”

“This brings up the question of how often you should call a woman.  Entire articles are written about playing the game and how often you should call.  That’s more of an art than a science.  You need to call often enough to be respectful but not so often you start to look needy.  Don’t forget when you don’t call every night she assumes you have other important things in your life and she’ll have to compete for your time.”

 

“It’s the same thing if you run into someone at the store or at the gym.  Be pleasant and friendly.  Don’t talk about yourself.  Focus the conversation about their day or what they’ve been doing.  Show an interest in them and then excuse yourself because you have things to do.”

 

“I’d also say to never hang out at a bar alone.  You always want to be in a group which is socializing.  If you go to the beach or to the park to girl watch, don’t make it obvious.  Go with a book to read or be walking plugged into your mp3 player.  You always want it to be obvious you have a purpose and you’re engaged in it.  By letting people know you’re a person doing things and active you suggest a lot to a woman.  She’s thinking you must be someone who knows where you’re going…hence leadership.  You play on her sense of wanting what she can’t have because she’ll have to compete for your time with all of your other activities.  She’ll suspect there are other women in your life or you wouldn’t be so busy.  And, she’s going to have to use her charms to seduce you away from all those activities…Women love that challenge.”

 

Strength and Leadership

 

“You know, luckily we aren’t carrying on this conversation with a bunch of feminists at a Women’s Studies forum.  The topic of strength and leadership in men most always causes an argument…When, in fact, there really isn’t an argument.  The problem is one of semantics or definition of terms.  Most often women react negatively to the concept of a strong male because they associate a man who’s domineering and controlling with strength.  In reality, men who are domineering, controlling and manipulating are weak and insecure.  They may be physically strong and intimidating on the outside but they’re weak on the inside so they have to control everyone around them.  A truly strong male has the inner security and self-assuredness to allow other people to be themselves.  A strong male will motivate, encourage and support another person in their pursuits.  An insecure male will try to control and bully others to do what he wants them to do.  An insecure male has a low self-image and has to prove himself at all times.  These men smother women and make them miserable.”

 

“Many, many women are drawn to a man who’s strong on the outside…Only to find later this man is insecure and weak on the inside.  These women find themselves smothered and controlled.”

 

“So when we say strength and leadership we’re talking about a man with inner strength.  This is a man who’s secure in himself.  He doesn’t control people.  He inspires people.  He motivates people.  He leads by example.  In the true sense of the word, strength and leadership is a completely positive quality and women are drawn to it.  They find comfort and support from these men.”

 

“Okay, so short of wearing a Superman costume under my suit at work, how do I project an image of being a strong and secure man?”

 

“The general statement is to make it a part of your personality or your attitude.  Start to believe in yourself.  Become a self-satisfied person who doesn’t need to prove himself.  Of course, entire books are written on that subject and it’s easier said than done.  So let’s talk about real life situations and how to act so people will get a positive impression of you.”

 

“Being decisive is a sign of strength and confidence.  Women want the man to take the lead and make decisions…and they’re judging men on how well they do it.  Of course, being decisive is an art more than a science.  You don’t want to appear arbitrary with decisions.  Your decisions should be thoughtful, practical and pragmatic.  Sometimes that can mean not to make a decision is the best decision.  There’s a big difference between, I don’t know, I can’t decide and I’m not going to decide now because I need more information.  Choosing not to make a decision demonstrates strength and common sense…especially when others are pressuring you to make a decision now.”

 

“But often when dating and socializing you’re faced with simple decisions to make about restaurant choices, which movie to see or what bar to go to.  Instead of standing there hemming and hawing, you should be ready with options and ready to pick one.  It’s good to have a mental list of restaurants and bars near where you live and work.  This list should include low, medium and high priced restaurants as well as restaurants with different menus and different atmospheres.  Then for any situation you’re ready.”

 

“Sometimes it’s better to simply help others make the decision.  For example, say you’re in a group and everyone wants to go out for a bite to eat.  Often, there’s a bit of reluctance for anyone to make a decision.  You can focus on one or two people and ask, Well, what do you feel like eating?  They may answer with Mexican food or pizza and because you’ve done your homework you can make a suggestion, There’s a nice Mexican restaurant a few blocks from here.  How does that sound to everyone?”

 

“Often plans fall through and you should be ready to adapt.  Say you’re out on a date and the movie you planned to see is sold out.  Don’t stand there saying, Gee, now what?  Make a decision quickly on what to do.  Hopefully, you had a backup plan ready for just this sort of thing.  It’s always good to show a minor set-back doesn’t interrupt your enjoyment of a night out.  Hey, no big deal.  Let’s go see this other movie instead.”

 

“Sometimes the simplest thing like ordering at a restaurant or bar can make someone look weak.  Know what you want and choose your order quickly.”

 

“Of course, the question comes up…Do you ask the girl what she wants to do to include her in the decision making or will this make you look like you can’t make up your mind without someone else’s help?  Say you’re on a date and after a movie you’re deciding where to go for a drink.  It looks weak to be standing there saying, Gee, I don’t know.  Where do you want to go?”  You may intend to be open to her suggestions but it can look like you can’t make a decision.  In that situation it’s better to say, I’d like to go to O’Malley’s Bar.  How does that sound to you?  In this way, you’ve made a decision, you’ve made a suggestion and you’ve asked for her input.  If she’s the type of girl who’s content to follow someone’s lead, then she’ll be happy to go along.  If she’s the type of girl who has her own mind, then you’ve opened up for suggestions.  Often, just your choice of words can make a big difference in a person’s impression of you.”

 

“Being in charge is probably the most important key to success…with women or with any aspect of life.  But, I don’t mean to be in charge of other people.  I mean you must be in charge of yourself.  I mean your life must be in order.  This means you’re gainfully employed.  You’re upwardly mobile.  Your car is in working order.  You have money in the bank.  You’re not in debt.  Your home is clean and tidy.  Your lawn is mowed.  You’re in good health physically, mentally and emotionally.  You keep up with your various obligations.  Your correspondence is caught up.  Your bed is made.  The dogs are fed.  And, you remembered to floss your teeth.  That’s being in charge of yourself and only when you’re in charge of yourself can you then take charge of every situation you’re in.  When your life is in order people will notice and they’ll see a strong and sensible person.”

 

“And once your life is in order you’re ready to take charge of all the situations you may find yourself in.”

 

“Here’s something to do…not on a first date but after you’ve dated a girl a few times.  Instead of asking the girl what she’d like to do, have every detail of the date planned out.  Just tell her when to be ready and let the rest of the evening be a pleasant surprise.”

 

“It sounds like one of those old fashioned things your mother told you to do but in terms of taking the lead, opening the door for a girl and taking her arm and helping her in and out of the car makes a lot of sense.  A woman notices a man who’s comfortable helping others.”

 

“When you’re at a restaurant, try to establish a rapport with the waiter so he or she will direct questions to you throughout the meal.  In other words, let the waiter know you’re in charge…your date will see you know how to deal well with people.  A cocktail waitress will pay special attention to you if you tip her on the first round.”

“Rule Number One has to be to drive your own car if at all possible.  As soon as you climb into someone else’s car you’ve lost control.  Going out with another couple, your date picking you up…it’s hard to imagine any situation where someone else driving works in your favor.”

 

“Here’s another example of a simple choice of words changing someone’s impression of you:  If a girl calls, answer the phone with Hi, what can I do for you?  By saying this, you become someone who’s used to being the leader and being of service to others.”

 

“Here’s another Rule Number One:  Always have enough cash or credit cards on you to cover any and all contingencies.  Do this not only when you’re out with a girl but also at work or play.  You never know when a social opportunity may arise to go have a drink or grab a bite with the girl of your dreams.  Are you going to miss it because you have no cash on you?”

 

“We’ll call this Rule #1A:  Never get drunk.  A few social drinks, yes…But, as soon as you start raising your voice or rambling on or being a bore, then you’re no longer in control.”

 

“Speaking of being in control…or actually not being in control, you have to be careful not to fall into no-win situations.  This is a situation where you’re not in control and there’s nothing you can do to get control.  I have a perfect example of this at our office party last Christmas.  There was a young secretary, Tanya.  She was a temp who’d been working for about a month in the office.  She was really attractive.  I was interested in her and had become friendly but I hadn’t asked her out yet.  Anyway, at the party she was surrounded by three or four men.  She was flirting with all of them and enjoying her ability to gather men around her.  I saw this and asked myself if I should go over to talk to her…and join the group of her fans.  But, she was in complete control of that situation.  Those men hanging around her were stupid enough to think she was going to single one of them out that night.  But that wasn’t going to happen.  If she’d been interested in a romantic, one on one with someone at that party then she would’ve already singled him out.  The fact she was keeping all those men around her meant she had exactly what she wanted…lots of male attention.  She probably went home that night and bragged to her roommate about how she had as many as four guys at a time wrapped around her little finger.”

 

“If I’d joined Tanya’s group, I would’ve looked desperate and weak.  I’d be allowing her to manipulate me and I’d be willing to stand there and wait my turn to have her favors doled out.  And the more desperately those guys surrounding her competed for nothing more than her eye contact, the more they put her in control.  Those guys put themselves into a no-win situation.”

 

“No, I didn’t go over and join Tanya’s group…but I did get her attention that night.  Here’s what I did.  I went over to the other group of secretaries who were enjoying themselves talking to each other.  They weren’t as young, most of them were married and they weren’t as attractive as Tanya…But they appreciated me coming over and giving them attention.  I joined their conversation and enjoyed the evening with them.  I took on the role of their waiter to refill their drinks and treated them like they were special.  The best part was they treated me like I was special and I had a great time with them.”

 

“At one point, I was getting more drinks for my group of secretaries and walked by Tanya’s group.  Tanya noticed me coming by and saw an opportunity to lure yet another man into her court.  She threw out her arms and said, Michael, where’ve you been hiding?  She was hoping the offer of a hug would be like a fisherman setting the hook and she could reel me in.  So I gave her a little hug, stood back as if to look at her, told her how nice she looked and then went back to my group of friends.”

 

“That night I didn’t hand control over to Tanya.  Instead, I had a great time with all those girls and they had a great time too.  The girls I was with, others in the office and Tanya all noticed I was comfortable and fun to be around.  For Tanya, I was the only challenge in the room…and for the other secretaries I was their hero because I was giving them attention instead of drooling over the hot, young temp…who they all probably hated.”

 

“Okay, I have a question…and this is leading to a situation I was in once…Should you always be in charge of women you’re around?  Should you demand control?  Should you force the issue?”

 

“And that question shows how men get into trouble.  Let’s go back to what we talked about before.  If you’re in charge of your life and in charge of yourself…if you have your life in order…then others, and especially women, will recognize your strength and common sense.  They’ll accept your leadership and give control to you.  Why?  Because you earned it and not because you forced it upon them.  If you’re not in charge of yourself and the situations you’re in, then people around you aren’t going to give control to you.  Why would they do that?  What’s the logic there?  Gee, this guy can’t even get his own life in order…I think I’ll put him in charge of my life?????”

 

“There’s something very important to remember and that’s the best thing you can do is to take charge of your life.  But all of us have our strengths and our limitations.  Not every woman or every date or every relationship is going to be a perfect match with you.  Just because you have your life together doesn’t mean every woman in the world is going to fall in love with you and every date is going to turn out perfectly.  But…very important…if you have your life in order, then you’re someone others will respect and you’re the type of man a woman will fall for.  And…very important…if your life is in disarray, others won’t respect you and no woman in her right mind will be interested in you.”

 

“Let’s paint a picture of a guy who doesn’t have his life in order.  He hates his job.  He’s in debt and sinking further because he spends more than he makes.  His home/apartment situation isn’t good either because of roommates or his house is a mess.  His car is in need of repair.  He’s often late for work or calling in sick.  He dresses like he doesn’t care how he looks.  He’s overweight and out of shape…and worse, he’s constantly complaining about how he needs to lose weight yet never does anything about it.  He’s often angry, negative and unpleasant to be around.  In general, his life is full of problems and it never seems to get better.  Again, why would a woman want to be around this guy?”

 

“And on the other hand, what are women attracted to?  There are always articles on the internet about dating or relationships and I saw one recently which related to this topic.  It was something about the traits of men which women find attractive.  Most of these traits were things like:  He radiates calm; He takes care of himself; He has style; and He laughs out loud often.  When a guy has his life in order, he does radiate a calm, easygoing and fun personality.  It’s not an act.  It’s authentic and people are drawn to it.”

“Okay, that partially answers my question but what do you do when you’re on a date and things spin out of control?  The woman isn’t following your lead.  You’re saying that trying to force control is not an option…What do you do?  I’m remembering a date from hell I had once in college.  Let me set the scene and then you can tell me what I could’ve done to save the evening…if anything.  This was a small college town so most of the students knew each other.  I’d been attracted to Heather for a while and now she’d broken up with her long distance boyfriend.  I was in a class with her and she was really putting out the flirtatious signals.  So, I draw the conclusion she’s hot for me and ask her out.  I really wanted to impress her so I took her to an expensive restaurant.  The evening was going well.  She was having a great time and was holding on to my arm most of the evening.  So when dinner was over, I asked if she’d like to go over to the local hang out.  There was a local band playing and I figured some dancing before we went home would improve the chances for romance later.  She was enthusiastic at the suggestion and was holding my arm while we walked over to the student hang out.”

 

“We get to the front door and the music is playing…People are having a good time.  There’s a guy at the door collecting a buck per person to pay for the band so he lets Heather walk in while I’m digging out my wallet to pay for us.  I’m still putting away my wallet when I hear Heather’s name being called.  I look up in time to see four or five arms reach out, grab Heather and pull her out on the dance floor.  She’s not resisting or waiting for me.  In fact, she’s dancing up a storm and loving every minute of it.  That song ended and Heather grabbed another guy for the next dance.  Now the floor is so crowded I can’t even see her.”

 

“I started to realize I wasn’t the only guy Heather had been flirting with.  I wasn’t special to her.  She’d just broken up with her boyfriend and was ready to circulate.  She’d been putting out the same signals I’d gotten from her to every guy she knew and probably a few she didn’t know.  Most of them were there that night waiting for her to show up.”

 

“So there I am feeling like a chump.  I don’t feel like standing in line to dance with the girl I just bought dinner for.  I can see it’s a lost cause and I can see her concept of the evening was for her to be out and about dancing and flirting with as many guys as possible.  I was simply one of those guys.”

 

“Luckily, I found a couple of friends and joined them for a beer.  We were all having a good laugh over my date and actually we had a pretty good time.  I danced once or twice with a girl I knew from classes.  The band took a break around 10:30 and Heather had mentioned she needed to get home early so I wasn’t surprised when she found me.  She made a fuss about not being able to find me and that we hadn’t danced yet.  I wanted to make a sarcastic remark but I just smiled and said I couldn’t find her either.  Then she asked if it’d be okay if I took her home because she had to be up early for classes.  Again, I wanted to tell her to find another ride but instead I said no problem, said so long to my friends and we left.”

 

“On the way home I wanted to tell her off but instead I talked enthusiastically about the band and listened to her talk enthusiastically about equally unimportant things.  We got to her house and she gushed about what a great time she had and we have to go out again, blah, blah, blah.  I smiled and promised we’d get together again soon and then drove off.  As soon as the car turned the corner, I started shaking my head and asking out loud how I’d gotten myself into that waste of time and money.”

 

“So what could I have done to save that evening?  How would Cary Grant have pulled victory from the jaws of defeat?”

 

“Michael looked at me and smiled, “Actually, you did about all you could’ve done.  In fact, you did stay in charge of yourself and that’s the best you could expect in that situation.  That night Heather was a selfish bitch.  She did whatever pleased her and totally blew off the etiquette of staying with the guy who brought her.  You made the best of a bad situation.  You had fun with your friends and stayed in good cheer.  I doubt there was any magic you could’ve performed to make the evening go the way you wanted so instead of fighting it you wrote her off and went with the flow.”

 

“There are a lot of things you could’ve done wrong…things that would’ve worked against you…badly.  Imagine if you’d tried to control Heather.  She was doing exactly what she wanted to be doing so any efforts to change her behavior would’ve failed.  If you’d gone out on the floor and demanded she dance with you instead of the other guys she was flirting with, you would’ve looked like an up-tight, possessive, jealous, insecure jerk.”

 

“Or imagine if you’d let your emotions take over and you’d gotten angry at Heather or brooded to your friends over her behavior.  You’d have lost control of yourself and again look up-tight and insecure to everyone.  You’d look like someone who’s no fun to be around which is the wrong image to project.”

 

“And some might say you should’ve told her off on the way home in the car but that would work against you too.  You would’ve looked like the kind of guy who’s possessive and clings to a girl.  Heather would tell her friends to stay away from you…Girls don’t like insecure and possessive men.  Also, telling her off would create a negative relationship with her and other people would notice that and wonder if they should avoid you.  Telling her off would’ve been a waste of energy.  It would’ve worked against you and it would be a missed opportunity…A missed opportunity?  How would that work, you ask?  Well, you’ve written her off as someone to date but by remaining on good terms with her she’ll continue to flirt with you and others will notice the positive and affectionate relationship you have.  With some luck you’ll run into Heather when you’re with another girl.  Heather will flirt with you and the other girl will notice how other women are attracted to you.  Also, Heather will talk to other girls about you in positive terms.  She’ll say you’re fun to be with if someone asks about you.  The date didn’t go as you’d hoped but by staying in control of yourself and taking the high road you, at least, will earn some dividends down the road and avoid a lot of negativity which would come back to bite you.”

 

“Being secure in yourself is another sign of strength…a quiet strength.  Guys who brag are not secure in themselves.  Some guys are obviously bragging to the point of practically beating on their chest.  Then there are the subtle ones who always manage to steer the conversation around to mentioning their car or their athleticism or their knowledge of wine.  It’s all bragging and really makes a guy look small.”

 

“Another sure sign of insecurity is when you put other people down.  That says more about you than the person you’re criticizing.  People who take the high road and avoid negative comments about others give off the appearance of a strong person who is above pettiness.”

 

“A secure person isn’t a slave to following the crowd.  You can enjoy being with the crowd but you always have to have a mind of your own.  Don’t be afraid to go home early because you have work to do.  Don’t be afraid to avoid the crowd when you have something better to do.  Being your own man shows strength…again a quiet strength.”

 

“Carrying a discussion to the point of arguing is always a losing strategy.  You must be ready to simply agree to disagree, smile and let it go.  This demonstrates you have the strength of your convictions and you don’t need to argue about it.  Again, it shows you’re secure in yourself.”

 

The Cardinal Rule of Life

 

“Never stay where you are not appreciated.  This has to be the Cardinal Rule of life and it doesn’t just apply to women.  It’s a rule for success in all avenues of life…your job, your career, the friends you keep, your social activities and the women you pursue.”

“Say your talents aren’t being recognized at work or you’re not being paid what you’re worth or your work conditions are unpleasant because of co-workers who are assholes.  Then it’s time to start exploring the job market in earnest and change your job.  If you’re a good worker and a good employee, then there’ll be better job situations out there for you.”

 

“Some people will try to improve the situation in their present job but this seldom happens.  When an employer or supervisor has a low opinion of you or takes you for granted this usually won’t change.  Most work places have a chemistry or culture and if you don’t fit in that culture it’s better to change jobs because that culture isn’t going to change.”

 

“I have a friend, Steve, who’s Mormon.  Steve is the nicest guy on earth and to me a perfect employee.  He’s honest, has a strong work ethic, is reliable and is always in a good mood.  He was always on time to work.  He often gave rides to co-workers.  When the boss needed work done on Saturday, he always asked Steve because he knew Steve wouldn’t complain…But…Steve’s co-workers didn’t like him.  There was no logical reason for this.  They just didn’t like he was “different.”  Steve didn’t drink and that bothered them.  There was one guy who was kind of like a second in command and he didn’t like Steve at all.  Steve was a threat to him…not because of anything Steve did…it was because Steve was a very good employee and the boss liked Steve.  That made Steve a threat to the co-workers and that one guy in particular.  Small minded people need someone to dislike or pick on.  They’ll single out someone who’s different and Steve was that guy.  The boss was unaware or ignored the way Steve was treated.  The bad treatment came in the form of passive/aggressive and subtle behavior along with the verbal tones and negative attitudes.  Steve is a very tolerant person.  He didn’t get angry but he changed jobs and moved on to a much better situation.”

 

“Changing jobs isn’t easy.  Looking for another job is a full time activity in itself.  Then there’s the natural resistance we all have to change.  Then there’s the guilt we feel for leaving, for quitting.  I remember something my father told me.  I was unhappy in my first job and wanted to change jobs but I felt guilty leaving the job I had.  I felt like I owed it to my boss to stay.  My father told me no one would ever blame me for finding a better job.  That made so much sense and it’s good advice to remember if you’re ever in that situation.  My father gave me a lot of good advice about that job change.  After he told me no one would blame me for finding a better job he told me the important thing about a job change was to do it right.  He told me:  First, always have other employment lined up.  Second, give your required notice in a positive manner.  Keep it high minded with no grudge, no gripes and most importantly no speech.  No one is going to care you weren’t happy on the job and exposing your anger will only work against you.  Your attitude should be of a person excited about your opportunity to move upward and onward in your new job.”

 

“I have a friend who worked for a small construction company.  The boss never gave him responsibility and often belittled him on the job.  My friend finally looked for and found another job.  His new boss was respectful and appreciative of his efforts to do quality work.  Within six months my friend was supervising projects.  His pay went from $12 per hour to $18 per hour.  The old boss, for whatever reason, didn’t want to give my friend a chance to reach his potential.  It happens all the time.”

 

“Not being appreciated in a job is common.  Not being appreciated in a career isn’t as common but it happens.  I have a close friend, Ron, who graduated from college in education and looked forward to a career as a teacher.  Ron had many important attributes to be a great teacher.  He was patient, enthusiastic, loved people…He enjoyed helping people.  His first teaching job out of college was in an inner city school.  There were lots of “at risk” kids at the school and those kids grew up in a survival of the toughest world.  Ron isn’t a physically imposing person.  The kids in that school, more than most kids, tested authority daily.  Pretty much only an NFL linebacker could command respect from those kids.  Ron got eaten alive…daily.  Finally, after about six months, Ron admitted defeat and left the job.  It was emotionally devastating for him.  He’d cared about his job and those very kids who ripped him to pieces.  Ron took a month off, did some soul searching and explored different careers.  He found a career as a medical technician.  With his existing BA degree he was able to complete the course study in about a year.  He immediately found a great job at a hospital and he loves it.  He makes much more than a teacher does.  He uses all of his people skills everyday working with patients.  He’s respected and appreciated by his co-workers and adored by the patients…Never stay where you are not appreciated.”

 

“Choosing your friends is one of the most important things you’ll ever do in your life.  Good friends who are positive and supportive of you will help you succeed in every other aspect of your life.”

 

“On the other hand, if you have friends who take you for granted, make belittling remarks towards you, constantly put you down or are negative towards anything you say or do then you need to put those friends out of your life completely…And find new friends.”

 

“Why would a friend treat another friend with such disrespect?  After all, that doesn’t make sense.  Well, there are lots of people in this world who need someone around them they can belittle to make themselves feel good.  They find insecure people to be their doormats and walk all over them to elevate their own sense of self-esteem.  They’ll never stop insulting their doormats until the doormat stops letting them by not being around them anymore.”

 

“Why would someone allow themselves to be walked on?  After all, that doesn’t make sense.  Well, insecure people are looking for approval.  They’re drawn to an offer of friendship as that gives them the acceptance and approval which is so important to them.  When the insults and put-downs begin the approval is taken away but the victim clings to the friendship as it still provides them with acceptance, which is so important.  The victim stays in the friendship always striving for the approval they want so much.  But, no matter how hard they try it’ll never come.  The victim stays in the friendship waiting for approval and the aggressor friend has his perfect doormat…someone who takes the abuse and comes back for more.”

 

“I have a friend, Bill, from school who told me a story of when he was in a friendship and found himself being a doormat.  Bill had a friend, Blake, who never said a positive thing to Bill and seemed to enjoy delivering the put-downs.  Bill tried to be as positive towards Blake as he could.  He thought Blake would return the same treatment.  But the nicer Bill was towards Blake, the worse Blake was.  Blake was getting positive reinforcement for his negative behavior.  Blake would make one recurring comment which especially bothered Bill.  So Bill asked Blake to stop saying it…Blake started saying it more.  Bill finally realized a friend like Blake is not a friend at all.  He severed his friendship with Blake and started spending time with friends who were positive towards him.  It was the best thing he could do for his self-esteem.  A footnote to this story is Bill smartly chose not to give Blake a speech or tell him off.  He simply stopped returning his calls and stopped being available.  He just moved on.”

 

“Don’t ever allow yourself to be around these people who are determined to bring you down.  Choose your friends carefully.  Only be around people who are positive towards you.  They’ll help you move forward in life and you’ll help them too.  That’s what a friendship is supposed to be.”

 

“Jobs and friends aren’t the only place to apply the Cardinal Rule of Life.  Let’s look at social situations.  For example:  If you’re at a party and not fitting in or no one is showing any particular interest in you, then leave…and leave now!!  Don’t hang around for 45 minutes to verify what you already know.  When you leave, you show you have better things to do.  If you stay you build an image of a boring chump who’s willing to sit alone.”

 

“Honestly, I don’t think there’s anything more pathetic than a guy chasing after a girl who’s not interested in him.  If a girl isn’t interested in you, then stop showing an interest in her.  It’s that simple and there’s no even if...”

“When a girl isn’t interested in a guy it’s obvious to everyone…except the guy.  So why are guys so stupid and keep chasing after a girl who’s obviously not interested in them?  I think guys are really good at losing their 20/20 vision when it comes to girls…And especially when a guy finds a girl that looks like the girl of his dreams.  But if she’s not interested in you then nothing you do is going to change that.  No matter what you say or how long you pursue her, it’ll lead nowhere so move on.  You’re wasting your time and worse you build an image to her and everyone else that you’re someone who’s found unattractive by women and you’re used to it.  Logically, if you had other women who were attracted to you why would you be wasting your time on this girl?”

 

“I remember I met a girl at a big party before a college football game.  She was beautiful and charming and I was really interested in her.  I asked her out for a casual date.  The day came and I showed up at her dorm but she wasn’t there.  Her roommate didn’t seem to know anything about the date and thought she’d gone home to visit her parents.  I got home and my roommate looked at me confused.  He asked what happened to the date.  I told him and he asked if I was mad.  I told him actually I wasn’t.  At least I’d found out how important I was to her before I spent any money.”

 

“There’s a sitcom about four physics nerds.  They have a beautiful neighbor who’s become friends with them and enjoys hanging out with them.  One time someone commented on the show and said it was unrealistic for the beautiful neighbor to be hanging out with those four losers.  But those four nerds aren’t losers.  They’d be losers if they tried to hang out and fit in with the good-looking, popular crowd.  That would be a disaster for them.  But they’re in a group of friends who accept each other.  They appreciate each other.  They support each other.  So the beautiful neighbor doesn’t see losers.  She sees four guys who are intelligent and successful and appreciated by their peers.  To me it’s realistic she’d be drawn to them.  Why wouldn’t she want to have friends like them?”

 

“I know it sounds harsh to leave a job or a friendship, but let’s face it…everyone will not fit in everywhere.  Every job situation will not be right for you.  There are friends out there you’re better off without.  And, most importantly, every girl you meet will not find you attractive.  This is a cruel reality of life but one you’d better accept.  If you find yourself in a situation where you’re not being appreciated then recognize it, accept it and get out of it gracefully.”

 

“Look at how this philosophy relates to being in charge of every situation you’re in.  Look at how staying in a situation where you aren’t appreciated affects your self-esteem.  A battered self-esteem from one avenue of life, say work, will affect your confidence when dealing with women.  The reverse is also true.  Staying with a woman who doesn’t appreciate you will affect your ability to be successful on the job.”

 

“Finally, look at how this philosophy relates to the image you project to others.  If you stay where you’re not appreciated women will look at you and think you’re a loser.  On the other hand, if you only allow yourself to be around people who do appreciate you then everyone will see you as a winner.”

 

Money

 

“Men often complain that all women are interested in is money.  Most men see this as a huge negative.  Men need to see the positive aspect that money is something they can have an affect over.  Consider the guy who’s not born tall, dark and handsome…Which is most of us.  We can’t change our looks but we can change our bank account balance.”

 

 “Let’s have a little discussion about money.  When you’re in high school good looks and grades and popularity pretty much determine your success.  After you’re out of school, life’s report card is how much money you make, how well you manage it and how well you invest it.  There’s a saying about money we’ve heard all our lives that Money cannot buy happiness.  This is a huge lie.  Money can buy happiness and it does just that every day for millions of people.  Now it’s true there are rich people who are unhappy but money didn’t cause their unhappiness.  The true statement is money can buy happiness.  And here’s another true statement:  Being broke will absolutely cause misery…every time.”

 

“Ask anyone who has been poor and has been rich.  They’ll tell you rich is better.  When I was just out of college I had a friend who was 30 years old.  He was a school teacher.  He had a second job driving the school bus in the morning and after school.  He had a third job working at a restaurant in the evenings.  I asked him why he was working three jobs.  He said, Don’t wait ‘till you’re 30 to find out money can buy happiness.  He’d set a goal to own a house and his car debt free and to have $50,000 invested…all before he turned 32.  He was almost to that goal.  He said working long hours when he was young would be nothing compared to the stress and frustration of going into debt, like most do, and working his whole life paying off mortgages, car payments and credit cards.  I learned a lot from him about the difference between being a slave to money and making money work for you.”

 

“But let’s get back to money and women.  I saw a one panel cartoon.  A guy and girl are sitting at a table at a restaurant, probably on a date.  There’s a bubble above each of their heads showing what each is thinking.  The guy’s bubble says, Sex.  The girl’s bubble has pictures of cars, houses, jewelry and money.  And of course, to be funny good humor must have some truth to it...and there’s a lot of truth to that cartoon.”

 

“So should we hate and despise women because they want a guy with money?  Women will argue all men want are attractive, blonde women with killer bodies.  Men like attractive women…it’s how they’re wired.  So, why should it be wrong for a woman to seek financial security and a more comfortable life?  In truth, a woman would be crazy to marry a man who’s broke.”

 

“Now let’s make a distinction right now.  There is a difference between a woman looking for financial security and a gold digger.  In one case we have a woman looking for a good relationship with a guy who’s financially stable.  A gold digger is a ruthless woman looking to extract money from any guy she can find.  She’ll do this by manipulating him to give her presents or go for the big score with marriage and a hefty divorce settlement.  In this conversation, I’m talking about normal women looking for a stable relationship.”

 

“Okay, so it’s normal and just good sense for a woman to judge a man on how much money he makes, how well he manages money and how well he invests his money.  So how can a guy present an image of a financially successful man?  Well, one way is to live beyond your means.  Lease a German car.  Max out your credit cards.  Present the illusion of wealth…And many men do it…And it works…Those guys do get women.  But, it’s not going to work forever and it’s not my idea of financial stability.  And frankly, most quality women see through these guys…eventually.”

 

“The first thing men need to do is set out a long term plan to get their financial world in order.  This means pay off your debt, live within your means, start an investment plan and put your job and career on the success track.  The reality is trying to date when you’re broke is a waste of time.  Get your financial house in order first.  Then, start dating and looking for a relationship.  Put first things first.  Then and only then are you ready to present an image of a man who is financially secure.”

 

“Okay, so how can a man present a good financial image?  Where you live and the car you drive paint a pretty big picture of you.  These aren’t things which can be changed overnight.  Your long term financial plan should include upgrading where you live and your furnishings.  It’s not important to drive a new car…It’s important to drive a nice car which is kept up and clean.  Again, these are things which should be in your long term plan.”

 

“So let’s say you’re not a millionaire and you want to be successful with women.  What are some things you can do to let women know money is not a problem with you?  You need to project yourself as a man who knows how to make money, how to use it and doesn’t worry about it.”

 

“The first thing is don’t hang yourself with your own words.  Avoid moaning about how broke you are.  When you buy something on sale try to frame it as being shrewd with your money instead of being cheap.”

“There’s a positive attitude about dating and socializing you must project to those around you.  If you’re a person who enjoys going out to movies or getting together with friends for dinner or drinks then people will see you as someone who puts a value on having a good time and aren’t afraid to spend your money on those things.  People will enjoy doing those things with you because they want to have fun just like you.  When you ask a girl out give the impression you enjoy an evening out and felt she’d enjoy coming along for a fun time.  The date should be the two of you enriching each other’s evening by doing fun things together.  Now, contrast that positive attitude of a person who values having a nice time socializing to someone who’s trying to get laid and begrudgingly spends some money on a dinner and a movie in order to do it.  It’s a nuance but women can feel the difference.”

 

“Some guys go on a date and base every decision on the cost…from the parking to the popcorn at the theater to the restaurant to what he orders to eat.  Don’t be one of those guys.  If you can’t afford $3.00 for parking, you shouldn’t be out on a date.”

 

“Be a generous tipper.  Women notice this and will notice how other people react towards you.  When you tip well, waiters will make a fuss over you on your way out of the restaurant.  Tip in increments of dollars.  Leaving some change won’t impress anyone.”

 

“Say you’re out at a bar with a date.  Tip the cocktail waitress when she brings the first round.  By doing this the cocktail waitress will service your table better and keep checking to see if you need anything.  Your date will notice you tip well but, more importantly, she’ll notice the waitress is paying particular attention to you and conclude women find you charming and attractive…And all this for giving the waitress what you’ll give her at the end of the evening anyway.”

 

“Spending money wildly when you don’t have that kind of money to spend isn’t the point here.  If you have to spend more money than you have to impress a girl then she’s the wrong girl for you.  Let the guy with the leased BMW and four maxed out credit cards chase after her.”

 

“The point is spending $15 additional dollars on better parking, some popcorn at the theater and tipping the cocktail waitress can make a big change in a girl’s impression of you.”

 

Romance

 

“How does a guy enhance his romantic image?  You have to be careful with this question.  First of all meeting a girl or getting to know her doesn’t involve romance.  First dates are generally a friendly, casual atmosphere.  Showing up at the door with flowers for a first date tells a girl this guy is trying too hard.  She’s expecting a friendly date to get to know someone and now she may be uncomfortable as she wonders what this guy is expecting.  And you could say the same thing about sending cards or jumping right into candle lit dinners.  All of those romantic touches are too much and too soon when you’re getting to know someone.”

 

“In this first, getting to know each other, phase of a relationship romance may be in the air and romance may happen but you shouldn’t be trying to make it happen…let it happen, yes.  For example, sending flowers to her the day after your first date is over the top.  But, say you’re on a first date and having a drink after the movie.  The evening is going well and a street vendor comes up selling single roses.  Buying her a rose is a nice, spontaneous gesture.  That would probably buy you points while not being over the top.”

 

“So where does romance come into the relationship?  As the getting to know each other phase of a relationship moves into the we like each other and want to continue dating phase, romance starts filling the air.  Now the little romantic touches like cards, flowers and candlelit dinners are appropriate and will enhance your image.  And don’t forget that simply a well-planned date is romantic in that you’ve thought ahead and thought about doing something nice with her…She’ll notice.  What’s a well-planned date?  A nice dinner and then a concert takes more planning than going to a movie and stopping at a fast food restaurant on the way home.”

 

“And then phase three of a relationship is where a girl separates the romantic men from the pretenders.  Phase three is when you both know you’re dating exclusively.  It may have moved to engagement or even marriage.  The key word here is exclusive.  And when men settle into an exclusive relationship, they’re notorious for becoming lazy.  Going out on a date is replaced by ordering in pizza to watch a football game.”

 

“Now, going out to dinner and movies and concerts every night of the week isn’t realistic and shouldn’t be expected but, once or twice a week doing something special is what keeps a relationship fresh and healthy…and romantic.”

 

“Okay, what are we talking about here?  Are we talking about spending big money?  Are we talking about weekend getaways to a bed and breakfast in the country?  Actually, a weekend, overnight getaway every so often is a darn good idea.  But here are some simple, day to day things you can do to make her feel like she’s with a romantic guy:  Meet her Friday after work for a nice dinner.  During the week bring home some take out and a rental movie.  Give her a $25 gift certificate for a restaurant near where she works and tell her to take her friends out to lunch.  She’ll love it and her friends will love you.  When you do go out put in a little time and find different restaurants.  The same routine is not romantic.”

 

“There’s something else a man can do to enhance his romantic image…and that is to listen.  Women talk and they’ll tell you what their interests are.  If she loves horses then rent the movie, The Black Stallion.  If she loves arts and crafts then take her to a weekend crafts fair.  If she jogs every morning then get her a pedometer or a heart rate monitor.  It’s not hard.  You listen and then you use your imagination.  Usually, romance is simply using some imagination.”

 

Mystery

 

“One time a friend told me he always tries to maintain a certain amount of mystery about himself.  I asked him why.  He said as a rule the more a girl knows about you, the more boring you become…There’s an attraction to the unknown.”

 

“I have another friend who needs to learn this lesson.  Tony is a very nice guy.  He’s hard working.  He’s sincere.  He’s honest.  He’s a person who’ll always be faithful in a relationship…And he fails miserably with women.  He’ll often vent and say his failures are because women don’t go for the nice guys.  He complains women always dump him and go for the guy who treats women poorly.  I’ve known Tony for a few years and I’ve listened to his romantic travails many, many times.  And while I don’t discount his Nice guys finish last theory, I think it’s more than that in his case.  I think women loose interest in him for a few different reasons but probably the biggest reason is he has no secrets about himself.”

“You’ve heard of people who wear their heart on their sleeve?  Well, Tony wears his heart, his soul, his life, his personal finances, his weaknesses, his loves, his hates, his bathroom routine, his past, his present and his future on his sleeve.  This is a guy who talks…for…ever.  And it’s always about him…24/7.  Now usually you’d associate this with a narcissistic person.  That’s not the case with Tony.  He is more of a George Castanza from Seinfeld type person.  What keeps him so focused on himself is because he’s so insecure and neurotic.”

 

“Tony will go out on a first date with a girl and tell her everything there is to know about him.  He’s killing his chances with her because he puts his entire life out there for her to see.  There’s no intrigue for the girl.  There’s no mystery.  He’s laid out his entire life for her and bored her to death doing it.  Even worse, much of his narrative is laced with how he’s the bad luck kid.  And of course, there’s something we all associate with people like Tony.  It’s something which is a sure-fire turn off for women.  As Tony tells a woman everything about himself, she begins to see him as needy.  And women run like hell for a guy who is needy.  Actually, there’s one type of woman who looks for needy guys…And that’s a woman looking to take advantage of someone.  But that’s a story for later.”

 

“Okay, how do we be a man of mystery…and do it without going over the edge and looking like we’re hiding something?  It’s very simple…Focus the conversation on her.  Ask questions about her.  Focus on the things which interest her.  By the way, that advice is like Rule #1 in every book on how to win friends.”

 

“But what if the girl read that same book on how to win friends and keeps asking you questions and focusing on your interests?  Let me give you an example:  You’re on a first date, the topic of investing comes up and she asks if you invest in the stock market.  You say, Yes, I have a retirement plan at work.  Period!!!!  And you say it in a casual way.  Now let me tell you how Tony would answer that question.  He’d tell the girl the exact dollar amount, the portfolio allocation between stocks and bonds, how much he lost in the last market downturn, how he lost half of it in his divorce, how he wishes he’d invested more in foreign stocks and how he’s going to try to open a Roth IRA before the end of the calendar year.  At that point, his date excuses herself to go to the bathroom, calls a friend on her cell phone and tells her to call back in 5 minutes so she can make up an excuse to go home early.”

 

“Crocodile Dundee said women are the nosiest creatures on earth…and he’s right.  Women want excitement and a challenge to their curiosity.  There’s a reason every romance novel has a mysterious stranger.  Deep down women love mystery.”

 

Confidence

 

“One time I was at a casual, community tennis tournament.  It was a round robin type competition so everyone played the same number of matches and your partner was randomly selected for each match.  Points and prizes were totaled at the end.  I was sitting with a female friend watching a match and on the court was a guy who captured our attention.  My friend said, He has an air of confidence about him…  At that point she hesitated.  I was thinking the guy was arrogant and cocky and expected her to complete the thought with a negative comment about him.  To my surprise she completed her thought with, I’m attracted to that.”

 

“I have to admit I was taken aback by what she said but, it hit home how women are drawn to strong men and when a man is confident it spells strength.  So what’s confidence and how to you get it?  How do you show it?  Some people are confident and it shows itself in arrogant, rude, cocky, bragging behavior.  Egocentric people who constantly talk about themselves are seen as confident.  And the truth is it does work for some people.  At a point, false confidence catches up to the braggart when he isn’t able to walk the walk, as they say.  But I see arrogant and rude people who seem to bully their way through life and they seem to get the things they want.  They’ll constantly bust the chops of those around them and somehow it works for them”

 

“To me, the highest form of confidence is the person who lets his accomplishments in life speak for him.  This is a person who’s so confident in himself he’s able to genuinely compliment others on their successes.  The strong, quiet, confident type of man has always enjoyed popularity in our culture…and longevity of popularity.  John Wayne epitomizes this popularity.  He became an American icon built on the strong and confident characters he portrayed in his movies.  He was never arrogant, never rude and never a bully.  He let his actions speak for him.”

“And John Wayne isn’t the only actor who reached legendary status because of his strong, silent characters.  Bruce Lee, Jackie Chan, Arnold Schwarzenegger, Jimmy Stewart, Charles Bronson, Liam Neeson…Jason Bourne are all actors and characters who enjoy enormous, lasting popularity and I’d argue it’s their quiet confidence which draws people to them.”

“Those are characters in the movies.  What about in real life?  Let’s look at sports.  Who are the true superstars who enjoyed long careers and universal adulation?  Look at Roger Staubach, Joe Montana, John Elway, Nolan Ryan, Hank Aaron, Walter Payton, Jack Nicholas, Arthur Ashe, Roger Federer…These are superstars who were confident without bragging.  They were never rude or arrogant.  They were gracious in victory and gracious in defeat.  I remember watching Roger Federer accepting his 2nd place trophy in what would’ve been his 14th or 15th grand slam win and sincerely complimenting his opponent on how well he played.  That is class and that is confidence.”

 

“I’d argue all of those athletes focus on their game…on being the best they can be.  They don’t waste their energy with bragging or trash talking next week’s opponent.  Bragging is thinking you’re good.  Trash talking is trying to be better by tearing others down.  In the end, the true champions are the ones who are loved, adored and respected.”

 

“Okay, so how do you become confident?  Who’s confident and who isn’t?  Why are some confident and others not?  I’d say confidence comes from experiencing success.  When you’re young you do your homework, practice your multiplication tables and as a result get a good grade on the math test.  This builds self-confidence that if you study and prepare, you will succeed.  Growing up if you experience success in school work, sports and dating then you will gain confidence in yourself.  Later you experience success in your job, your career and make wise investment decisions.  These experiences build more confidence.”

 

“Let’s look at a micro example of losing confidence.  You’re out golfing and shank 5 shots in a row out of bounds.  By the time you step up to that 6th shot, you’ve lost confidence in your swing.  It’s the same for life in general.  If you did poorly in school, never did very well in sports and had a disappointing social life then you won’t be filled with confidence in yourself as you enter college or the work force.  As an adult if you’re in a job you don’t like, your career is sputtering, you’re behind on your bills and you have no investments or net worth to show for your labors then how can you feel confident in yourself?”

 

“So your life experiences add up to a level of confidence or lack of confidence.  Years ago I was watching an episode of L.A. Law.  One of the lawyers, Kuzak, was telling his client he felt lucky about their case.  The client looked at him and said, You feel lucky…I don’t.  That was an interesting exchange.  Kuzak was a handsome, successful lawyer and white.  His client was a lower middle class, black, single mom.  Kuzak’s life had been one long series of successes.  The black woman struggled her entire life and had never gotten a break.  It was hard for her to conceptualize luck.  She wasn’t filled with confidence.”

 

“Remember what we talked about before…Being in charge of your life and having your life in order?  These are the things which give you confidence.  You have to build on success with your job, your career, your personal finances, your grooming, your social life, your hobbies, your sports and all the things you do.  Many people have failed at things in life because they’re doing the wrong things.  One person can be a failure in sales yet a genius at computer programming…One career track spells failure and the other spells success.  One friend of mine grew up skiing with his family but never became a good skier.  He took up golf after college and was a natural.  He told me how his “failure” as a skier had always affected his self-esteem and confidence.  Taking up golf and experiencing that success changed his entire outlook on himself and life.  It’s very important to find the things you’re good at in order to put yourself on the success track.”

 

“So if you’re a person who’s lacking confidence don’t think this will change overnight.  Feeling confident isn’t something which happens by taking a weekend self-help seminar filled with positive thinking and telling yourself what a good person you are.  Building confidence is a process which takes years and a well thought out strategy.  It begins with baby steps so you can experience one success after another.  Don’t jump ahead.  Don’t sign up for the intermediate computer course at community college and find yourself unprepared for the course material.  Sign up for the introductory course, do the reading and the assignments, get an A and then build on that success.”

 

“If you’re buried in debt then make a long term plan.  Sell the expensive car which cripples your monthly budget with high payments and buy a clean used car you can afford.  Cancel your NFL package and for that matter cancel your cable subscription altogether.  Go to the grocery warehouse once a week and cut your food bills in half.  No more lattes on the way to work.  Last but not least get a second job nights or weekends for 6 months or a year and pay off those credit card balances.  A second job sounds brutal but the silver lining is then you won’t need cable TV.”

 

“The little successes of getting an A in your class or seeing your debts slowly disappear are the successes you need to start building confidence in yourself.  The major successes of paying off your credit cards completely and getting an associate degree in computer technology at the community college are the things which change your life.  It’s those uplifting successes you’ll carry into every aspect of your life…And yes, including your success with women.”

“Having confidence in yourself will lead to confidence with women but just like in golf or computers you’ll need to practice and learn.  If you’re a bit insecure with women then don’t invite failure by asking out the prom queen.  Start slowly by going out to lunch with that group of women at work.  Learn to feel comfortable.  It’s just like golf.  You practice, get better and your confidence builds.  Of course, nothing makes you feel confident with women like having a girlfriend.  And people will see the spring in your step from knowing you have a girl to go out with that night.  But wait!  But wait!  If having a girlfriend gives me confidence, then how to I get a girlfriend if I have no confidence?  It’s a Catch 22.  Ah ha!!!  And this is why I spoke so long about building confidence in every other aspect of your life before I spoke of confidence with women.  It’s the confidence you gain from your school work, your job, your career, your sports and your hobbies which carries over to confidence with women.  When a woman sees a man who’s confident and successful, she’ll be attracted to him.  And then, the more you socialize with women and date, the more you’ll build your confidence with women.”

 

Be a Positive Person

 

“These topics really do lead one right into the other.  Confidence leads to a positive outlook and women want to be around happy, positive and up-beat people.  They want to enjoy their life.  So, are you a positive person?  Let’s have a test.”

 

  1. Do you arrive at work greeting people with a smile…or griping about the traffic?

  2. Do you wake up in the morning smiling and positive about the day…or are you just no good until you’ve had your coffee?

  3. Do you compliment people when they do a good job…or find a way to diminish their accomplishment?

  4. Do you speak badly about other people…or do you speak positively of others?

  5. When you encounter a small irritation like long lines at the check-out do you dwell on it…or do you shrug it off with a laugh?

  6. Do you get angry about things you can’t change like the weather…or do you simply adjust and make the best of it?

  7. When you tell a story is it about the fun you had…or about all the things which went wrong?

  8. When someone bumps into you do you get angry…or do you smile and let them know you’re okay?

  9. Do you accommodate other drivers in traffic…or do you get angry when someone wants to change into your lane?

  10. Do you take responsibility for you mistakes…or is it always someone else’s fault?

 

“My mother gave me a lesson once I never forgot.  She said, Intelligent people talk about concepts.  Average people talk about events.  And stupid people talk about other people.  And this is so true.   When you speak ill of others it says more about you than the person you’re running down.  It shows you to be an insecure person.  It shows you to be a person who dwells on the negative.”

 

“I had a friend, Hobbs…I should say acquaintance…who was always negative about everything.  He lived to criticize and run down others.  One time we were watching late night TV and Neil Diamond came on as a guest.  Hobbs is sitting on the couch and says I hate Neil Diamond.  I didn’t say anything but I sat there wondering what Neil Diamond had ever done to Hobbs.  Had he borrowed money from Hobbs and never returned it?  Had Neil Diamond dated Hobbs sister and treated her badly?  Then I’m wondering what Neil Diamond must think of Hobbs.  Here’s this looser at home with nothing better to do than sit on a couch hating people he’s never met.  Here’s this looser hating someone who’s reached a pinnacle of success…a creative and musical genius.  Successful people are who we should be admiring and emulating.  Maybe his music isn’t your cup of tea but, hate?  Did Hobbs know how pathetic he sounded at that moment?”

 

“That night I was inspired to do my best to eliminate the word hate from conversations.  Some years later I went out on a first date with a girl who was very attractive and very sexy.  She spent the entire first date telling me about everything she hated.  She hated where she lived.  She hated the town she lived in.  She hated going to the beach.  She hated being in the sun.  It went on and on.  I realized why such a pretty girl didn’t have a boyfriend.  She just wasn’t fun to be with.  She was always complaining and angry about something.  I lost interest and stopped dating her.”

 

“A positive outlook doesn’t always come naturally.  Life can be a minefield of problems and they can drag you down.  Looking at the positive in life is a choice you have to make.  Sometimes it takes strength and energy to make that choice.  Sometimes you must consciously tell yourself to take the positive road.  Sometimes you’ll catch yourself getting angry so then ask yourself if that’s the road you want to take.  If you make the choice enough times to be positive then it’ll become your habit.  A positive outlook in a man is very attractive to a woman…That should be all the motivation you need to make the positive choice.”
