Play Hard To Get

 

“Here’s something we’ve heard all our life about women but it also applies to other aspects of life.  In business and employment playing hard to get can pay off with getting the best price for your products and with getting better pay and benefit packages.  When someone is trying to sell you something, you should always be playing hard to get.  At a garage sale I picked up a DVD to look at it.  The seller saw this and told me the price was five dollars.  I put it back down because I didn’t want to buy it anyway.  The guy lowered the price to three dollars.  I told him I wasn’t interested and he offered it to me for a dollar.  If I’d shown an interest in that DVD I would’ve overpaid.”

“If someone is asking to borrow money then you should really be playing hard to get.  In fact, you should be playing impossible to get.  There’s a reason banks and pawn shops demand collateral.  Here’s something you should remember whenever someone asks to borrow money.  When that person asks for your money, you have all the power.  He has no money and you do.  He wants your money and you have the power to say no.  The very instant you hand him your money you also hand him ALL the power.  You have no collateral…Did you ask to hold title to his car?  Do you have a signed and notarized note which will hold up in court?  Probably not, so this person now has your money and the power because he can decide to pay you back or not…it’s up to him.  You can call and beg and threaten all you want but it’s his decision.  If you’re a loan shark with an associate named Guido, then you do have power but most people don’t know a Guido.”

 

 “Now back to playing hard to get with women.  Of course, this concept is based on people…and woman…wanting what they can’t have but it goes beyond that.  Let’s look at a guy who’s playing hard to get and not appearing too anxious with a girl and see what he’s accomplishing.  First, he‘s set himself apart from most of the other guys she encounters.  Secondly, he suggests he’s content and happy in his life and not needy…and that could even mean there are other women in his life.  She doesn’t know for sure but why else would he be willing to take things slowly?  All of this appeals to her wanting to express her powers of seduction.  Look how her interest in this guy grows.”

 

“A third reason to play hard to get is equally important.  When you take things slowly, you don’t threaten her independence.  This is important to remember.  Most women are cautious about beginning a relationship.  Even women who’ll casually have one night stands will run away from a guy who starts falling in love or getting clingy.  Don’t underestimate how you can scare a girl off by pushing things too fast.  Most women are more comfortable with a guy playing hard to get than a guy declaring his love on the second date.”

 

“Okay, so how do you play hard to get?  One thing to do is to end the date before she does.  You’re having coffee after a nice dinner and in a timely manner you ask if she’s ready to go.  You can explain you have a report to write before work tomorrow so you need to get home…You had a nice date and you politely stayed in control of the evening instead of letting the date drag on until she finally has to say she wants to go home.”

 

“There are books written on this subject but I’ll repeat it here and that’s the issue of how long to wait to call her after your first date and how often to call her.  I’m not sure what the newest conventional wisdom is on this but calling the next day is too soon and waiting a week is too long.  The right answer must be in there around the 2nd or 3rd night after the first date.  As far as how often to call goes I’d say don’t call every night but waiting too long can be a turn off to her.  It’s possible to play TOO hard to get.”

 

“In the first stages of dating and getting to know someone you never tell her she’s the only girl in your life.  Doing this will make you sound desperate and needy and it’ll make her feel like she’s being pressured into a relationship.  Let her wonder what you’re doing on the nights you don’t call or who you’re going to lunch with.”

 

“Again, in the first stages of dating don’t discuss the future or commitments.  People are cautious and afraid of being trapped or tied down so it’s better to remain aloof on the subject of the “C” word…Commitment.”

 

“Don’t always be available to do something with her.  If you’re at her beckon call then you’re not playing hard to get.  Be ready to say, Gee, I’d love to but I can’t.  I’m getting together with friends.”

 

“Playing hard to get is an art and not a science.  Often it’s no more than your choice of words or being more aloof rather than giving her infinite details on your every movement.  Saying you’re getting together with friends is honest but vague.  Saying you’re getting together with your boss to go to his son’s soccer game is detailed and makes her feel secure.  The whole point of playing hard to get is to keep her a bit insecure in exactly where she stands with you.”

 

Be Seen With Women


“Women aren’t interested in the guy chasing all the women…They’re attracted to the man all the women are chasing.  Being seen with women is probably the single most important thing you can do to help your image.  Women are very willing to take another woman’s word for it that a guy is approved.  In other words, if you’re seen with a girl you must have something going for you.  You must know how to treat a woman well.  You must be financially sound.  You’re going to be hard to get.  You’re someone she can’t have and you appeal to her powers of seduction.  Being with a woman says all of this about you without you uttering a single word.  How good is that!!!  Being seen with women is like getting a superior credit rating.  You’ve been approved.”

 

“Have you ever seen the movie, Can’t Buy Me Love?  It’s a 1987 movie with Patrick Dempsey…I think it’s like his first movie.  He plays this high school nerd who hires a cheerleader to be his girlfriend so he can become popular.  It’s a great little high school romance movie and it’s hilarious.  The funniest thing about the movie is the nerd’s plan works better than anyone could’ve expected.  And it works because of what I just said…Once he’s seen with a cheerleader it’s like he just got a triple A credit rating.”

 

“Anyway, women love the chase.  If a woman sees two men at a party and one is with a date while the other is stag, she’ll be more intrigued by the man with the date.  He’s the one with his life together.  He’s the one who stimulates her powers of seduction.  He’s the one who gets her competitive nature in gear to prove her superiority over other women.”

 

“Most men don’t realize how competitive women are…after all women are civilized…the fairer sex.  I remember a friend telling me about a trip he took to the beach in California.  He said there were beautiful girls in bikinis everywhere and they enjoyed walking up and down the beach to show off…to see if they could attract attention.  I asked him if all the guys were sitting there girl watching.  His answer surprised me.  He said the guys were glancing up to watch but it was the girls who were staring and scrutinizing every girl walking by.  He said it was like they were all sizing up the competition.”

“A few months later I had an interesting experience which reinforced my friend’s observation.  I was in a grocery store with a girl I was dating, Laurie.  She wasn’t that attractive in the face but she had large breasts and that day her blouse showed them off.  As we were walking down an isle a girl walked towards us.  The girl looked at me, then looked Laurie up and down and checked out her breasts and then she looked back at me with a flirtatious look in her eye.  Basically, she’d checked out Laurie and decided she had what it took to get me to switch teams.”

 

“But besides the competitive nature of women, there are other important reasons to be seen with women.  When you’re seen with women it tends to bring women out of the woodwork.  A woman might see you and think, Well, if he’ll go out with her then certainly he’ll go out with me.  This is what was happening at that grocery store.  Also, women see you with a date and they’ll think, Oh, so he is in circulation.  I never flirted with him because I wasn’t sure if he dated.”

 

“And something else…Not only does being seen with women affect other people’s attitude about you, it affects your attitude about yourself.  It gives you more confidence which in turn gives you strength which in turn puts you in charge.  Women sense this and they like it.”

 

“Okay, there’s one little flaw in this because being seen with women sounds like that old saying, It takes money to make money.  What if you’re broke?  What if you don’t have a girlfriend?  How are you going to get a girlfriend?  What if you’re not a rock star or an NBA player?  Do you ask your sister to go on fake dates?”

 

“Actually, the best approach isn’t far off from fake dates with your sister.  The number one thing men should do is to start making female friends.  That’s not girlfriend.  That’s female friends.  This can be groups of women from work you go out to lunch with.  You can spend time with girls you know from school.  You can meet girls at the gym or in aerobics class.  You can meet women at church or in volunteer organizations.  There are hundreds of groups which form for every activity under the sun you can join and meet women.  There are a few important things to remember.  First, try to meet groups of women.  Next, make sure you aren’t seen as someone trying to hit on them because this will give you credibility as a nice, safe guy who enjoys socializing.  Your goal is for the women to feel comfortable with you so they’ll include you in their activities.”

 

“Making female friends accomplishes a lot for you.  First, you learn to be comfortable around women.  Second, you’ll be seen with women.  And, here’s an added bonus…When you have a date you may run into your female friends.  They’ll say hi and be friendly towards you.  In fact, women being women they’ll probably act a bit curious and intrigued which your date will interpret as jealousy.  Your date will notice all of this female attention you get and draw the conclusion that women love you.”

 

“Most women enjoy having male friends and so your female friends will value you.  Women being women they’ll want to introduce you to their friends.  One of the best ways to meet women is through other women.  Yet another advantage of having female friends.”

 

“Sometimes there may be parties or social events you wouldn’t want to attend alone.  A female friend would come in very handy for those times.  You tell her about the event and say it would be more fun if you were with someone and would she like to go with you.  She’ll enjoy socializing with you.  You’re both comfortable with each other and others will notice.”

 

“And, of course, there’s always the possibility one of these friends will be interested in something more than friends and want to start dating.  She’s learned to trust you and appreciate you…why wouldn’t she be interested.”

 

“There’s another way to be seen with women and that is to lower your standards.  I don’t know about you but I look back on high school and really regret all the missed opportunities I had to date girls I would’ve had a great time with going to dances and football games.  And why didn’t I ask out those average girls?  Because I had fantasies and delusions I was going to date the prom queen or rally girls so I’d go dateless for months waiting for them to notice me.  It never happened and I was just wasting time I could’ve spent having fun.”

 

“Sadly, men are afraid to be seen with less attractive women.  Men think it’ll be seen as a negative and that’s not true.  First off, who cares what other men will think.  Importantly, other women won’t notice you’re out with a woman who is say, overweight.  What they’ll notice is you’re out with a woman who enjoys being with you.  And, you should spend your evening enjoying being with your date because that’s what people will notice.  People will see you know how to treat a girl well and have a fun time.”

“Sometimes you’ll meet women who are looking for a man to be just friends.  This can be a good opportunity to spend time with a woman, go out on casual dates and have a friend for social functions.  Admittedly, it can be frustrating if you’re looking for romance and she’s only looking for friends.  This happened to me a few years ago.  I’d met this girl, Suzi, at school but didn’t know her very well.  She was drop dead gorgeous.  So I ran into her at the tennis courts one day.  We started talking and then hit some balls on the court.  To my surprise she was being forward about getting together to play tennis again.  The thought crossed my mind of why this beautiful girl was a) available and b) pursuing me but I was enjoying being with her.  For being as beautiful as she was, she was a down to earth and sweet girl.  She wanted my phone number so we could arrange to play again.  I gave it to her but honestly didn’t expect her to call.  I thought she was just being polite.”

 

“To my surprise, Suzi called near the end of the week and wanted to meet at the courts Saturday morning to play.  At this point, my mind is spinning out of control with fantasies of landing a girlfriend this beautiful…and not stuck up…and athletic.  That’s pretty much every guy’s dream.  We meet around 10am and she’s as friendly as can be.  We hit balls, played some games, helped each other practice our serves and had a great time.  We took a few breaks and laughed about TV shows and movies we’d seen.  We played until almost noon and I asked if she’d like to go get something to eat.  She did and so we went over to a Starbucks.  Again we sat and enjoyed each other’s company.  At Starbucks I enjoyed seeing both men and women noticing her and who she was with.”

 

“We continued seeing each other a couple times a week but always in casual and friendly settings.  We usually played tennis but we also got together for lunch and once or twice went out to a movie.  Suzi completely enjoyed my company and I enjoyed hers.  She was always fun and upbeat.  Our conversations would often turn to relationships.  She talked about her ex-boyfriend and enjoyed talking about the good and bad of relationships.  She talked about being timid and cautious with men.  I found that curious because she seemed very comfortable with me.”

 

“Eventually I came to understand Suzi’s interest in me.  Suzi was completely commitment and relationship phobic and even more phobic of sex or of a sexual relationship.  But, Suzi was a person who did enjoy male attention and male friendships.  In a way, I suppose this reassured her femininity.  So, going out on casual dates and doing activities with me was perfect for her as long as I wasn’t trying to move to the next level.  She was able to socialize and feel desired as a woman without pressure.  However, this friendship was sorely lacking for me because I wanted a romantic relationship with her.  As it became more and more clear our “dating” was never going further than where it was, I lost interest…probably like many men had before.”

 

“My experience with Suzi taught me some valuable lessons about how different people can have different intentions and views for where a relationship is going.  I also learned there are lots of women like Suzi out there and becoming one of their activity buddies can be very useful to you as long as you don’t have romantic expectations like I’d had with Suzi.  The key is to recognize women like Suzi and then enjoy the friendship while you enhance your image.  In truth I should’ve continued contact with her as a female friend to be out with once a month.”

 

“Don’t underestimate the effect being seen with women will have on how women perceive you.  And most importantly, the reverse is true.  You may think it’s noble to be sitting at home alone waiting for the girl of your dreams to come along, but that’s not how she’s going to see it.  Women are going to see a single guy as being boring.  Women will think if a man isn’t involved with other women then he must have something wrong with him.”

 

Career

 

“Women are very conscious of a man’s profession and career.  That’s no secret and no one can blame a woman for wanting to be with a man who’s successful and fulfilled in such an important aspect of his life.  Being with a successful and upwardly mobile man affects her life dramatically for the better.”

 

“So if a woman sees a man in medical school or moving up with a large company, it gets her attention.  But every guy isn’t going to be a professional.  There are hundreds of careers and jobs of every kind.  The real question is what you do with that job and how you approach your work.  The real question comes down to work ethic and goals.”

“Let’s say there are two 19 year old guys flipping burgers.  One of those guys has no plan for the future and will probably still be working there 2 years from now.  The other guy is studying accounting at a nearby college.  In 6 months he expects to be able to get an entry level accounting job where he’ll continue to work until he graduates in 2 years.  Now if there’s a girl working at that restaurant with those guys, which guy do you think she’s more impressed with?”

 

“It’s true what a guy does is important to women.  But what’s also important is whether you have goals and a plan with your job.  Men should be mapping out their future with set goals and strategies.  Women will notice a man who’s on a path which is upwardly mobile and they’ll notice when a man is treading water…going nowhere.  It’s that simple.”

 

Sensuality

 

“I wouldn’t even try to tell someone how to be sensual.  It’s probably something you’re born with.  But, I will say one thing:  Learn to dance.”

“I was a terrible dancer and I couldn’t figure out why.  I was always well coordinated and had good timing and footwork in sports like playing tennis or hitting a baseball but I couldn’t dance to save my life.  I felt awkward.  I was inhibited and self-conscious.  I wanted to be able to dance well but it was a mystery to me.  Whenever I danced I’d try to memorize the steps and put my feet where they were supposed to be but it never felt natural and I wasn’t with the rhythm.”

 

“Then one summer I took a trip to Latin America where I met a girl, Ruth.  It was funny her name wasn’t Maria or Theresa or Ysenia.  Her name was Ruth.  She was really nice.  She worked at a touristy place selling crafts and paintings.  I was always trying to practice my Spanish and she was learning English for her job selling to foreigners so we started talking and pretty soon I asked if she’d like to go out to a restaurant that evening.  That was a treat for her because she didn’t have money to go out very often.”

 

“So, we met that evening and went to a little restaurant and had a great time talking and practicing our second languages.  After the meal she asked if I’d like to go to a little bar and go dancing.  I told her I’d love to but I was a terrible dancer.  She laughed and said she could teach me.  So we go to the bar and I buy us each a beer.  Luckily, the bar was pretty empty and we were off in a corner so I wouldn’t be too embarrassed when it was time to dance.  She told me she wanted to teach me to dance Salsa and had me stand up when she heard the right song.  She said to watch her and do the same so I watched her feet and then tried to imitate her steps.  I was stepping back and forth as she did but I was moving like a robot.”

 

“She smiled and said, No, you don’t dance with your feet.  You dance with your hips.  I gave her a confused look and she told me to watch her hips.  She started dancing again and now I was watching only her hips move back and forth with the rhythm of the music and she looked so graceful.  She told me to just shift my weight back and forth from one foot to the other without moving my feet and let my hips swing with the music.  Then she told me to continue to focus on moving my hips with the music but now I could let my feet walk in place.  Move my hips to the rhythm and just let my feet follow.  Then she took my hand and we were moving to the rhythm together.  I was amazed.  I actually felt like I knew what I was doing.  I could feel the rhythm and move with it.  I wasn’t a robot anymore.  She’d taught me in like 10 minutes.”

 

“After about an hour of practice I was actually dancing Salsa.  Of course Ruth and I spent many more evenings practicing Salsa and I continued to improve and feel more and more comfortable and confident dancing.  And once I’d learned to keep rhythm with my hips, I found I could dance to most any type of music.”

 

“I’m so grateful I finally learned to dance.  I dreaded that feeling of being inhibited and embarrassed to go out on the dance floor.  Women love to dance and are always impressed when a man dances well.  I’m sure they think a man who can dance is sensual.”

 

Charm

 

“One of women’s favorite words is charming.  As in, Oh, he’s so charming.  That means he’s polite and attentive and positive and fun.  Women enjoy being with men who are charming.  So where can a guy buy some of this charm?”

 

“When you’re with a woman, make eye contact with her so she knows you’re enjoying what you’re doing and who you’re with.  Direct your conversation towards her and the things she’s interested in.  Talk about her job, her education, her hobbies and her interests.  She’ll feel comfortable talking about the things she knows and will be flattered by your interest in her.”

 

“Compliment a woman when you notice her new hairdo, her clothes or her new glasses.  Make a note of the books she’s carrying and show an interest in the things she does.  Be happy, content and positive about yourself.  Be enthusiastic about the moment you’re in.  All of that will rub off on the women you’re with.”

 

“Treat everyone with charm and a positive outlook and you’ll have many friends who enjoy spending time with you.  Women will notice people enjoy your company.”

 

The Friends You Keep

 

“Entire books should be written about the importance of the friends you keep.  Who your friends are can change your life in dramatic ways.  Unfortunately, the wrong friends can be detrimental as they hold you back with their negativity.  So choosing positive, upbeat friends who bring the best out in you is one of the most important things you can do in your life.”

 

“In school I had a roommate, Ron, who was a real gentleman.  He was always upbeat and positive.  He always complimented and encouraged me with whatever I did.  He was polite and patient with people and had a wonderful sense of humor.  One of the nicest things about Ron was how easy it was to be around him whether we were going to the park to play tennis or going out for a casual dinner.  We never had trouble deciding on what movie to see or restaurant to go to.”

“The day I really came to appreciate Ron was one time when I met two girls at a Friday at four party at one of the college dorms.  The girls and I were getting to know each other as the party started to wind down and I asked if they’d like to go to get something to eat.  They were enthusiastic so I suggested I call my roommate to see if he’d like to join us.  That made them even more enthusiastic.  I called Ron and told him I’d just met two girls and we’d all like to go out to dinner.  Without hesitation he said that sounded great.  He didn’t ask me what the girl looked like.  He didn’t ask who was going to pay.  He didn’t demand a right of refusal if the girl wasn’t up to his standards.  You see, to Ron this was simply a nice evening out with friends.”

 

“So Ron met up with us on our way walking across campus to a nice Mexican restaurant and proceeded to be the perfect gentleman.  I introduced Ron to the girls and he immediately made them feel at ease and comfortable.  We sat down at the restaurant and Ron did something really amazing.  One of the girls was…uh…less attractive.  That was the girl he sat down next to and started talking to.  Later I asked him why he’d done that.  He told me all we were doing was having dinner so it wasn’t really important who was with whom but he also said he’d chosen the less attractive girl on purpose.  He guessed she was often in a similar situation with her friend and had watched as all the men would try to be with the more attractive of the two.  He said he wanted that girl to feel like he chose her.  He wanted her to feel special.”

 

“Back to the restaurant, the four of us had a great time.  The food and the conversation were both satisfying for everyone.  As the plates cleared it was obvious to Ron and I that all four of us didn’t want a Friday night to end that early so I asked Ron if he still wanted to go to the movie like we’d planned.  He picked up on my improvisation and said he would and then asked the girls if they’d like to join us for the movie.  They did and the entire evening was a lot of fun from start to finish.”

 

“During the entire evening I remember feeling so grateful to have a friend like Ron.  If those girls were judging me on the friends I keep then I’d received their highest rating.  The next day Ron and I were talking about the night we’d had and I reflected on a friend I’d had in high school.  Dave was forever asking his friends to fix him up with a date.  One time I’d met a girl, Sarah, from another school and we’d started dating.  We were going out one Saturday but when I called she told me she had a friend with her who’d love to go out with us and do I have a friend so we could double date.  So, I called Dave and he was all for it.”

 

“But when we showed up at Sarah’s house to pick up the girls to go out, the friend wasn’t good enough for Dave.  The story is too long and painful to explain but the short version is after about an hour Dave figured a way to weasel his way out of the date and take off.  I was mortified.  Sarah was embarrassed and confused.  Dave had made an excuse which was somewhat believable but I’m sure the girl knew what had happened and felt terrible.”

 

“So that’s the story of two different friends.  Which friend do you want your girlfriend to meet?  And which friend can you rely on if you need some support?”

 

Here are important questions to ask.  Are your friends inspiring and motivating you in other areas of your life or are they constantly putting you down?  Are your friends able to give you good advice in everything from career to investing to romance or are they leading you down the wrong roads?  When you’re with your friends do you spend the time upbeat and laughing or are you listening to whining and complaining about everything?  When a friend comes over to watch some sports and you hand him a beer, does he say, Hey, thanks!!  Or does he say, Is that all you got?  Don’t you have a Corona?”

 

“Women will judge you by the friends you keep and even more importantly you’ll judge yourself by the friends you keep.  Your conscious and subconscious feelings about yourself are dramatically influenced by how your friends treat you.  Find positive friends who are accomplishing things in their life.  They’ll positively affect you.  Find friends who you fit in with…friends who you feel comfortable with and who are comfortable with you.  Do this and your odds of succeeding in everything are greatly increased.”

 

****************************

 

Man attracts not that which he wants, but that which he is.

 

“If a woman has quality to give then she’ll expect it back from a man she spends time with.  She won’t settle for less so if a man wants a partner with quality, he must first be a man of quality.”

 

This chapter was about changing your image but it’s really about changing yourself.  It’s about being a better person in every aspect of your life.  You have the control and you can make the choices to change and improve yourself.  Some of the changes can be made with a moment’s decision in front of your closet.  Many changes will take long term goals and determination.  It’s up to you.

 

***************************

 

Movies Referenced in this chapter:

 

Can’t Buy Me Love





Chapter 3  Choosing the Right Women

Michael and I were out to lunch again with his friend, Sonia, from work.  The conversation had somehow gotten around to men looking for trophy wives.  She said, “Let me tell you a story on that subject.”  Sonia leaned forward to give her story more emphasis.  “This successful guy was dating three different girls.  He had a great life and pretty much everything he wanted but now he was thinking it was time to settle down and get married.  He can’t decide which of the three girls to ask to marry him so he sets up a little test.  He gives each one of them $10,000…just to see what they do with the money.  The first one blows it all in about two weeks.  She buys clothes and things for her apartment.  She goes to a spa, has her hair done…She spends it all.  The second one spends most of the money on the guy.  She’s really imaginative and buys him things he’d never get for himself.  She sends a masseuse to his office.  She takes him out to a nice restaurant for romantic dinners.  She takes him on a weekend getaway to a little bed and breakfast up the coast.  She buys him some nice clothes because he hates to shop.  She spoiled him and enjoyed it.  The third girl works in finance.  She invests all the money and makes a nice profit in a month.  She puts the profit from the investment into her retirement account and gives the original $10,000 back to him.  Okay, so which one does he marry?”

 

Michael thinks about it and finally says the generous girl.  That was going to be my choice so I think about it and say, “I’ll say number 3 but that’s probably wrong because it’s too logical.”  Michael and I lean forward with curiosity.  “So which one does he marry?”

 

Sonia gives us one of those looks like it’s so obvious and says, “He marries the one with the big breasts.”

 

We all had a good laugh.  Michael and I admitted the reason the story’s funny is because there’s a lot of truth to it.  Later that evening Michael and I got together and he brought up Sonia’s joke.  He said, “You know, too often men do choose women using the wrong criteria or no criteria at all other than primal instincts.  Choosing the right women is far more important and takes far more thought than men realize.”

 

I could tell Michael had some wisdom to share.  I asked him, “I’ve always chosen women I’m attracted to.  Am I missing something?  How do I know which woman is the right woman to choose?”

 

“I’ve got a great story,” Michael began, “I had a friend, Jason, in college and during summer break he had a full time job working at a restaurant…with a lot of girls.  Every day after work he’d talk about two of them in particular.  He’d become friends with both girls at work.  One girl, Janet, was attractive and nice.  He said the other girl, Angie, was beautiful.  He was attracted to both of them but he wondered if he should make his play for Angie, the beauty, or play it safe with Janet.”

 

“I’m guessing Jason was afraid the beauty was out of his league.  Am I right?”

 

“Yes,” Michael went on, “He pondered the question for a few weeks and then came to a strategy.  He reasoned if he made his first play for the beauty, he’d loose in both cases.”

 

“Oh wait,” I interrupted, “The beauty obviously gets many offers so by coming on to her he becomes like every other guy hitting on her.  So he’d get nowhere with Angie and then if he went for the less attractive one, she’d be saying, You come around now after striking out with her?  No thanks.”

 

“Yes, that’s right,” Michael gave me a high five, “He figured he’d do a casual get together with Janet first and if that worked out…great, but there was something even more devious to his plan.  He was hoping when Angie, the beauty found herself being passed over for the less attractive girl it would shock and confuse her so much she’d start pursuing him.  His plan was a perfect application of a girl wanting what she can’t have and appealing to her powers of seduction.”

 

Jason was becoming my hero.  “Diabolical!!!  This guy’s come up with the win/win plan.  If part two of the plan doesn’t develop, he’s still got Janet.”

 

“Exactly…and you aren’t going to believe this but three weeks after implementing Operation Shock and Confuse he was dating Angie, the beauty.  He told me Angie admitted what attracted her was that everyone else comes-on to her but he hadn’t.  And she admitted she was very affected by the other girl being so attracted to him.  She’d gotten jealous and went out to get him.  It’s an interesting story.”

 

“I love that story.  Going for Angie right off was a no-win plan.  Going for Janet first was a no-loose plan.  So is this what you mean by choosing the right woman?”

 

“Well, yes and no.  Most men don’t have the problem of which of two women to pick.  In addition, the wisdom of Jason’s pick looked good for a few months but Angie, the beauty, was one of those girls who felt entitled by her looks.  She eventually decided Jason wasn’t good enough for her and they stopped dating.  Janet, though not as attractive on the outside, had more quality on the inside.  If Jason’s intention was a summer fling with a beautiful girl then he chose right.  If his intention was a long term relationship he missed an opportunity with Janet.”  Michael paused to collect his thoughts and then said, “Surprisingly, I think most men do have the problem they don’t choose the right woman to pursue and I think their choices are a cause of their failures.  Men are looking in the wrong places, picking the wrong women and this is the biggest cause of their failures.”

 

“Okay, what did you say first…looking in the wrong places?  So where do you find the right women to choose…Not the single’s bars?  Please don’t tell me that’s the answer.”

 

Michael laughed and then said, “Well, that’s a good illustration.  Have you ever been successful meeting women at single’s bars?  I haven’t.  I think most of the women at single’s bars are there looking to hook-up with a macho-hunk type man and I don’t fit that description.  The important point is to look for women where you can find them.  If you aren’t the type of man who girls in single’s bars are looking for then look someplace else.”

 

“Yeah, I’ve never felt comfortable in single’s bars but there’re always a lot of girls at that sports bar we go to and also at Applebee’s when we eat there.  And, the girls at those places are more my type.  I don’t think they’re there to hook-up but I think they’re open to meeting people and socializing.”

 

“Yes, that’s exactly what I’m talking about.  Go where you can find women.  I’d say the number one best place to meet women is at school.  High School and college were heaven on earth for me.  I didn’t want to leave.  There’re women everywhere, they’re all single and they’re all interested in meeting people.  I had a friend in college who’d even take it one step further.  He was in engineering and there weren’t many girls in his classes so every semester he’d take one easy course in sociology, psychology, art or languages just to meet girls.  He said there were usually two to one girls to men in those classes.  You had to admire that guy’s sense of academics.”

 

“After you leave school your opportunities to meet girls decrease…unless you’re creative.  I have a friend who went to work for a government agency and he said 75% of the employees are women where he works.  He said it’s like a continuation of college.  Everyday lunch was a social event and all day Friday memos circulated about where everyone was going for happy hour.”

 

“I have a friend who works for an accounting firm.  Most of the accountants at the firm are married with children so they don’t like to travel.  My friend’s single so he always volunteers for the out of town audits where he goes into companies and works with the accountants and bookkeepers who are usually women.  He eats in restaurants while out of town so it’s easy to play the poor lonely guy in a strange city just looking for some company for dinner.”

 

 “Many professions and work places are infested with women…others not as much.  Not many people are going to change jobs or careers to find women but if you’re looking for a new job or thinking about it then adding the availability of women to meet as a criteria is a smart thing to do.”

“Outside of work there’re hundreds of clubs and groups to join.  Take up tennis or jogging or biking.  Join a health club or a hiking club.  Exercise classes are usually 75% women.  I have one word for you…Yoga.  Get healthy and meet women.”

 

“Every time you go to the grocery store or the book store or the mall or the library you’re going to meet women working there.  Pretty much every time you leave your house you have an opportunity to meet women.”

 

“Yeah, but how do you actually meet them?  I mean say I go to the grocery store and there’s a girl working there I’d like to meet…what do I do?”

 

“Ah, good question.  There’re books written about pick-up lines but every time I hear those lines I shake my head in disbelief someone would think repeating something which sounds stupid and rehearsed would actually work.  No, forget pick-up lines.  The trick is to give women the opportunity to make their interest known to you without appearing to be on the make.  It’s easy.  It’s honest and no memorization is necessary.”

 

“Step one is to be open and ready for anything.  You need to always be at your best and ready for an opportunity to meet someone.  You need to be prepared to be spontaneous.  How’s that for a contradiction?  Prepare to be spontaneous.  Actually, what it means is to be mentally prepared to be friendly with people you don’t know because step two is simply to be friendly.”

 

“Step two is to be friendly but the key is you must be genuinely friendly and not just another hustler.  You must develop an open and positive manner which isn’t the big come-on.  The most basic approach is a warm smile and a friendly, Hi.  If she’s interested or intrigued in you, her response will show it.  Here’s an example:  You go to your super market 2 or 3 times a week and see the same women working in the check-out line.  It’s a perfect opportunity to practice being friendly…and not just with the young and pretty girls.  Be friendly with all of the women…especially the older women and less attractive ones.  You arrive to check out with a warm smile and a nice greeting.  Hi, how are you?  How was your weekend?  Their job can be boring so they’re usually happy to have a nice conversation to break up the monotony.  In fact, they’re trained to be friendly so they’ll respond and ask how your day’s going or ask how you like the weather or ask how your weekend was.  Always be positive.  Give them a compliment.  Thank them and say, Bye, bye.  Show an interest in their life.  The next time you come through their line their face will light up when they see you.  You were friendly to them and they’ll remember.  All of the women there will notice you’re a happy, upbeat and positive person…and you must be successful or why else would you be going through life spreading friendliness.  Now you’ve established your friendly personality and so when you also have a friendly conversation with the young pretty checkout girl she won’t assume you’re just the next guy in line trying to get her phone number.  Of course, you are but it’s not as obvious.”

 

“Okay, but what if you run into a girl that you’re not going to see 2 or 3 times a week?  It seems like the pressure is on to get her phone number then because you may never see her again.”

 

“Exactly true and those situations come up often.  I gave that first example because the art of a warm smile and friendly conversation is the basis for everything.  The super market is the perfect place to practice it, perfect it and most importantly make it spontaneous and natural.  And you should practice it everywhere you go…and not just with the girls you want to meet.  Get in the habit of having friendly conversation with every check out girl, sales girl and waitress you encounter.  That way it becomes second nature and you won’t be nervous when you’re talking to someone you really are interested in.”

 

“Okay, another example:  You go to a clothing store at the mall and the salesgirl catches your eye.  What do you do?  You do exactly the same thing you’ve been practicing at the grocery store and everywhere else.  You give her a warm smile and friendly conversation without coming-on to her.  When you’re finished shopping and about to leave the store you say, I really enjoyed talking to you.  Would you like to go out to lunch sometime?  And you say that in the same friendly tone.  It’s that simple.  If she says no, then you don’t say anything negative or try to talk her into it.  You simply say, Oh, too bad for me and say goodbye.”

 

“Another option with that sales girl would be to mention you’re starving and heading over to the mall food court.  Then almost like an after-thought you can ask, Why don’t you join me?  Can you take a break? This sounds spontaneous and friendly instead of hustling her.  The nice thing about this strategy is even if she turns you down you can tell if she’s interested in you.  If she tenses up at the invitation then you know she’s not interested.  If she smiles and brightens up at the suggestion then you know she’s flattered and you can ask if she’d like to go out some other time.”

 

“The important thing is to give girls the opportunity to respond to you.  That’s the only way you’ll know if she has an interest in you.  And even if you aren’t particularly interested in a girl you should have friendly conversation.  It’s good practice for you and good for her to have some friendly energy coming her way.  And one other reason to be friendly to girls you may not be particularly interested in is so you can develop your ability to read women’s signals.  You need to learn when a girl’s interested in you and when a girl isn’t interested.  That’s the most important skill you can have.”

 

“Okay we started talking about choosing the right women and so far all we’ve talked about is being friendly to all women.  When does the choosing come into play and how do I know which ones are the right women?”

 

“That’s what I like about you,” Michael said, “You always get to the point.  You’re right we’ve decided it’s good to be friendly to all women…Which women do we separate from the herd to pursue for dating and more?  Please take notes because there will be a quiz later.  The women you choose are the women who are interested in you.  It’s that simple.”

“There’s a truth most men just don’t get which is if a woman isn’t interested in you, then nothing is going to change that.  Proving your love for her or calling her every night to ask her out or being like a puppy dog telling her how much you love her and how pretty she is won’t change that.  In fact, nothing is going to change her feelings for you.  The movie, My Best Friend’s Girl, is a must see to illustrate a guy in love with a girl who only sees him as a friend.  The guy becomes pathetic and his life goes into ruin because he won’t let go of his obsession for this girl.  You have to watch this and make sure you never become this guy.  And, the first 5 minutes of that movie could be the funniest first 5 minutes of any movie I’ve ever seen.”

 

The truth of this was obvious to me but also a bit painful.  “Why do men do that?  I’ve been guilty of it many times.  Is it the challenge?  For me, I think I was living in this fantasy world of wishful thinking?  I completely ignored the obvious signs.”

 

“It’s a good question.  People do love a challenge but climbing Mt Everest is a challenge a lot easier to accomplish than trying to convince someone to be in love with you when they’re not.  Honestly, I think it’s no more complicated than men can live in a fantasy world.  We men see a girl who looks like the girl of our dreams and we become blinded by her beauty.  We’re so sure she’s perfect in every way for us we assume we’re going to be the perfect man for her.  We ignore all of the subtle and not so subtle hints she gives that she’s not interested and just keep trying.  We talk to her, flirt with her, call her and ask her out but she just doesn’t seem to understand we were made for each other…so we try some more.”

 

“And worse than it being a monumental no-win waste of our time, this kind of behavior isn’t what a woman is looking for in a man.  Remember what women find attractive.  By chasing after a woman who isn’t interested in you, you aren’t portraying an image of power.  You aren’t someone who’s hard to get.  Other women must not find you attractive or you wouldn’t be wasting your time with someone who treats you with disinterest.  She doesn’t need to use her powers of seduction on you.  About the only thing you are to her is a pest.”

 

“Remember the Cardinal Rule of Life.  Never stay where you are not appreciated.  If a girl isn’t interested, then that is that.  Don’t waste your time with people who aren’t interested in you.  Even if she looks like the girl of your dreams, it’ll lead nowhere if she’s not interested so move on to someone who is interested.  By hanging around, you portray yourself to her and everyone else as a person who’s found unattractive by women and worse…you’re desperate.”

 

I interrupted Michael and said, “I don’t think I’ve ever thought about this concept before but there’s such a simple element of rational thinking here.  Pursuing women who show an interest in you is the only course of action which will lead to success.  I’ve been guilty of chasing after girls who weren’t interested in me but I’m remembering a friend of mine, Bill, who really took the cake.  Bill had become fixated on a beautiful, blonde girl, Betsy, who was kind of a campus queen.  I doubt Betsy even knew he existed but he talked about her constantly.  He’d tell me how he passed her on the way to class.  This was a big deal for him.  He’d been talking about her for at least 6 months and had never even spoken to her.  One time a good friend of mine, Gretchen, had her best friend visiting her.  She’d met my friend, Bill, and asked if the four of us could go out while her friend, Mary, was in town.  So we go out to a casual night spot on campus and everything’s going well.  We have a table, are having drinks and dancing.  Then Bill starts acting strange.  He’s kind of acting aloof and even leans up against a post near the table for a while instead of sitting at the table.  He’s leaning there with a drink and watching the dance floor.  Gretchen looks at me and I’m totally embarrassed.  I had no idea what he was doing.  The evening ended okay for me and Gretchen but Bill was acting strangely and Mary gave up and just stayed close to Gretchen and me.”

 

“The next day Bill calls me and is all excited about the night before.  I was confused and told him he didn’t handle the date the way I would’ve and he’d been acting kind of strangely the night before.  He said, No, I handled it perfectly.  Betsy was there last night and I think she saw me.  I was in disbelief and I asked him if he was kidding.  He was serious.  I bluntly told him I was embarrassed with the way he’d treated Mary the night before.  He was oblivious to his actions.”

 

“Wow, he is a case.  That guy needs an intervention.  He’s failing and he has no idea why.  There’s a test for guys like Bill.

  1. Has it been a year since you slept with a girl?

  2. Do you fall in love with women who don’t know you exist?

  3. Have you been asking the same girl out for 3 months and you’ll keep asking until she has a night free?

  4. Is there an excuse you haven’t heard?

  5. Do the women you’re interested in change the subject when you talk about going out?

 

“If you answered yes to any of those questions, then you’re choosing the wrong women and you need to stop wasting your time.”

“Remembering that story about Bill really puts the accent mark on the wisdom of this advice.  I think it’s easier to see other men making that mistake than when I’m doing it.  We get caught up in our rationalizations, denials and fantasies.  But now I’m going to pick your brain on the next obvious question.  How do I recognize when a woman is interested in me?”

 

“Yes, how do we know when a girl’s interested?  Honestly, I think it’s a skill which is developed with practice and experience.  I think when a man has the hots for a woman his powers of objectivity become so clouded he’ll interpret anything a woman does as her flirting with him.  We had a conversation…I think she’s interested in me.  She said hi in the elevator…I think she’s interested in me.  These are examples of a man making a conclusion that ain’t necessarily so.  Women will be polite to spare a man’s feelings.  Most people hate to say no and appear rude.  Men often confuse politeness for interest.  And having a nice conversation is something people do all day long.  It simply means they’re friendly.  A man needs more than that before he can start chilling the wine.”

 

“The first thing to learn is if a woman is interested she’ll let you know.  The man just needs to recognize the clues.  For example, there’re friendly conversations and then there are friendly conversations with clues.  Let me tell you about Denise.  I met Denise in college.  She’s a beautiful and sensual girl with that added, wonderful quality of being shy.  We met at a formal dinner.  She was with her group and I was with mine but the dinner seating had randomly put us across from each other.  Through the entire dinner I couldn’t take my eyes off her and she kept looking at me too with her shy smile.  As the dinner went on we started exchanging smiles.  After the dinner there was very little time before we had to go off with our groups but we had a short conversation and I did learn she lived in a freshman dorm.  That was in October.  I went by her dorm once to see her but she wasn’t there.  I thought I’d run into her on campus but never did.  I got busy with classes and work…”

 

“…So now fast forward to April.  It’s the first week of classes in the spring semester.  I’d sprained my ankle so badly playing soccer on Sunday I could barely walk.  There was an old pair of crutches in storage at my fraternity so I was using them to make my way to classes that Monday morning.  I’m crutching my way to class and see Denise coming towards me looking as beautiful as ever.  She sees me and comes right over.  Hi Michael, what happened?  I told her about the soccer game and started asking about how she’d been and how we hadn’t seen each other since last fall.  I was noticing how engaged she was in the conversation.  I kept expecting her to have to run off to a class but the conversation just kept flowing along.  Then one of my favorite professors came along and saw me with crutches so he stopped to ask what had happened.  Now I’m assuring the professor it’s just a bad sprain and I’m dying inside because I’m sure Denise is going to use the interruption to excuse herself so she can get to class.  Finally, the professor says he’ll see me in class and walks off.  To my surprise and delight Denise is still standing there patiently waiting to continue our conversation.  At one point she put her hand on my arm as she wanted reassurance I’d be okay getting to class on the crutches.  Eventually, we acknowledged it was getting late and we both had to get to class.  We said goodbye and went off in our separate directions.”

 

“Now if I’d had my head screwed on straight, I would’ve said something to Denise like, We have to get together sometime.  These are perfect openings to find out if a girl’s interested in you or neutral.  If Denise had enthusiastically said, Yes, I’d love too, then I know she’s interested.  Even better would be if she also asked when and offered an evening she was free.  So an ultimate response from Denise would be, Yes, I’d love too.  I’m always free Wednesday evenings…maybe we could go see a movie.  Compare that kind of enthusiastic response to a girl saying with a neutral tone, Yeah…Well, I have to get to class.”  The difference is dramatic and obvious.  Men need to learn and listen.”

 

“Wait a minute!  So what happened with Denise?  Did you ever get together with her?”

 

“Oh yes, Denise.  I spent the week trying to get up the nerve to call her.  Some friends were having a party Friday evening and I wanted to ask her to that.  Finally, I called her on Friday.  I didn’t have her number but I called her dorm and luckily the girl who answered went and got her.  I said, Hi Denise.  This is Michael.  I was wondering if you’d like to go out tonight.  Some friends are hav…..  She interrupted me and said, I’d love to.  She made it very obvious.  That evening things got romantic and we shared our first kiss.  We finished a long, slow kiss and she pulled back to look me in the eyes and said, I thought you’d never call.”

 

“So what are the clues you’ll get from a girl during a friendly conversation?  Denise gave many of them.  First, she didn’t want the conversation to end that day walking to class.  Second, she touched my arm during the conversation.  A touch is huge.  Women don’t touch if they aren’t interested.  Third, she initiated that conversation.  She came over and stopped me to talk.  Fourth, the tone in her voice was interested and enthusiastic.  She was upbeat and positive towards me and showed empathy for my sprained ankle.  All of this was an obvious contrast to a friendly conversation with a neutral tone.”

 

“And what are other clues a girl can give to show her interest in you?  One is eye contact.  Remember the night Denise and I met she was looking at me and smiling.  Another clue is when women put themselves in your way.  They sit next to you in a class.  They come up to say hi on the street or at work.  They’ll even make an excuse to call you.”

 

“Probably the biggest clue will be her response when you ask her out.  Denise responded with, I’d love to.  That’s about as good as it gets.  Compare Denise’s response to, I’m not sure.  What kind of a party is it?  Sometimes a girl will turn down an invitation.  Does that mean she’s not interested?  It depends on the clues she gives when she says, no.  What if a girl says, Oh no, I can’t make it.  I have a class on Wednesday evening…but I’m free on Thursday?  In that case you know she’s interested.  She wants to go out with you and she wants to make sure you know she’s available to go out.  She’s trying to get a time and date set to see you.  Compare that to asking a girl out and she says, No, I’m busy that night, and doesn’t offer any other time she’s available.  If a woman isn’t interested in you then she’ll usually be vague about her schedule and free time.”

 

“Another clue is the easiest and best to recognize.  That’s when a girl asks you out on a date.  Usually they won’t pick up the phone to do it, but often in conversation they’ll say, We should get together sometime.  That’s always music to my ears.”

 

“So if those are the clues a woman gives if she’s interested in you…are there also clues a woman will give off if she’s not interested?”

“Yes, there are.  Sometimes they’re obvious.  Sometimes they’re subtle.  I remember one time I had a class with my good friend, Ron.  There was a girl in that class I couldn’t take my eyes off of.  I’d run into her on the way to class and walk with her but the conversation never went anywhere.  This went on for a few weeks and Ron kept encouraging me to ask her out.  One time leaving class Ron and I timed it so we were walking with her on the way out the door.  I made small talk with her and then offered that Ron and I were heading over to Starbucks to have some coffee and she was welcome to join us.  She declined…politely…but she declined and there was no suggestion of next time or regret she couldn’t make it.  At that I lost interest.  Ron encouraged me to keep trying but I told him she wasn’t interested.  He protested and said I shouldn’t give up so easily…after all maybe she was just busy that day and didn’t have time to go for coffee.  I explained it was more than that.  She’d never shown a hint of interest in all the times I’d spoken to her.  She’d never put herself in my way as I’d done putting myself in her way.  Finally, Ron played his last card and suggested she was testing me and playing hard to get.  Okay Ron, I said, That is the song of the truly desperate.  I gave it a shot and she’s not interested.  I’m going to move on before I embarrass myself.”

 

“I’ve had encounters like that.  The girl is polite but does nothing to encourage you.  What are some other clues girls give when they aren’t interested?”

 

“The main clue is they just don’t make themselves available.  When you ask her out she’s always busy and they may sound like valid excuses but if she were interested she’d make time to see you or she’d offer to see you on a different day.  Basically, if arranging a date to get together becomes a struggle and you’re the only one working on the problem then it’s time to move on.”

 

“Have you ever been in a conversation which is awkward and forced?  That’s usually because you are the only one trying to keep it going.  This is a sure sign she has no interest in the conversation…or you.  The tone of the conversation is a clue too.  If her tone is neutral and absent of an up-beat, positive mood then you can see she has no interest.”

 

“I suppose most girls don’t want to hurt your feelings or create drama so they expect you to interpret their neutral tone and absence of an obvious come-on as a polite way of saying they aren’t interested.  I’m sure when the girls get together they vent their frustration over the guy who won’t take the hint.  I can hear them saying, What do I have to do…Tell him to get lost?  There’re books and articles for women about how to let the guy know you’re interested.  I wonder if there’re books on how to let the guy know you aren’t interested?”

 

“If there was…I’d like to read that book.”

 

“There’re times when you just aren’t sure.  She acts interested but every time you ask her out she can’t go.  They sound like valid excuses so what do you do?  The easy solution is to put the ball in her court.  Tell her you’d really enjoy getting together with her but you understand how busy she is.  Make sure she has your phone number and tell her when she has time to go out for a bite to eat to give you a call.  If she really is interested then she’ll call.”

 

“Sometimes a woman is interested initially and enthusiastic to go out with a guy and then her interest wanes.  This is a tough one for men to accept…especially after they’ve had sex with the girl.  Men don’t want to accept the rejection but they need to evaluate how the girl is acting now and not her interest when they first met.  If, after a few dates, a girl isn’t making herself available to get together or she lacks enthusiasm or she isn’t giving any energy towards you then you should bow out gracefully.  Don’t make a speech or a scene or an issue.  She has your number and can call you if she wants to.  In the meantime you can pursue girls who are interested in you.  You’ll be doing yourself a big favor.”

 

“I completely understand and agree with the wisdom of only pursuing the women who are interested in me but so far we’ve avoided one little glitch in that advice.  What if I’m not interested in the girls who’re interested in me?  Do I have to go after all those girls who aren’t doing it for me?”

 

“That’s a really good question.  I don’t know about you but I think back to high school and kick myself for all the opportunities I passed up because girls weren’t attractive enough or popular enough.  I really was a complete idiot.  I…and I think a lot of us…set up a standard of beauty which is totally unrealistic.  We’re all shooting for the rally girls and homecoming queens.  I passed up opportunities because of fear of peer pressure.  I imagined I’d somehow be embarrassed to go out with a girl who wasn’t socially connected.  What in the world was I thinking?  First of all, any friend who doesn’t approve of a girl I’m going out with based on popularity or outward beauty is no friend but secondly, I doubt anyone would’ve noticed or cared who I went out with.”

 

“There’s a great movie which really hits this topic home.  Some Kind of Wonderful is about a high school kid who’s fixated on the popular girl in school.  It’s a great movie.  We should rent that and watch it some night.”

“I had a friend, Rick Wilson, in college who told me a great story about his dad.  When Rick was in junior high and just starting to date his father had a talk with him and told him about the evils of popularity and cliques.  His father told him when he was in high school he was friendly to all the girls…even the less attractive and less popular ones.  He talked to everyone.  He walked to school with everyone.  He said hi and talked to all the girls outside of school.  He told Rick he made a point of treating the less attractive girls like they were special.  The result of that was during high school he went out with lots of girls.  Always at dances many girls were coming up to ask him to dance.  Sometimes he’d go to a movie with a date and two of her friends and they’d all have a great time.  All the girls liked him and the popular girls respected him because they knew he was genuine and not superficial.  But the real payoff came after high school.  His father told him most of those girls who’re “ugly ducklings” when they’re 15 or 16 turn into beautiful swans when they turn 21 or 22.  He told Rick how all those beautiful swans in their early 20s remembered who’d always been nice to them.”

 

“So in answer to your question:  Yes, you should go out with girls who aren’t doing it for you, as you said.  I’m not saying you should marry the girl.  I’m not saying you should start a committed relationship with the girl.  What I’m saying is it’ll benefit you to start a friendly relationship with her and go on casual dates or casual get-togethers with your friends or hers.  Going out with 10 different girls on 10 different first dates over a 4 month period is better than staying home alone hoping that dream girl finally notices you.  And who knows, on one of those first dates you might meet a girl you thoroughly enjoy being with.  And, those loose fitting clothes were hiding an awesome body.  It’s time to open our minds and realize…It’s only a casual date. ”

 

“Many men live in a dream world waiting for the perfect, beautiful 10 to come along.  It’s not wrong to shoot for your dream girl but you have to ask yourself, Is it better to stay home alone or to go out and enjoy yourself?  What do people think of a guy with no date?  He must not know how to treat a woman.  He must not be sexy or interesting.  Worst of all, he must be desperate.”

 

“Now what do people think when they see a man out with a girl?  Remember, women find men attractive who’re found attractive by other women.  When you’re seen with a woman people see a man who has something going for him.  They see a man who must be sexy and interesting.  Best of all, he must be hard to get.  A girl will have to use her powers of seduction to get him.”

 

“It sounds harsh to suggest you’re dating one woman as a stepping stone to finding another woman but sometimes that’s what happens.  After all every first date is simply a way to know someone better and decide if that person is someone you want to continue dating.  That’s true for men and women.  The reality is it’s always better to involve yourself with women, even if your interest is low, than to be involved with no one at all.  Being involved with women is probably the single most important thing you can do to make yourself attractive to other women…The rich get richer.”

Let’s list the benefits of dating:

  1. As soon as you start dating one girl you meet her friends…other women.

  2. You’re in a position of strength and not desperate.

  3. If the girl of your dreams does come along she’ll see a man found attractive by women.

  4. Dating is good practice for learning what women want and understanding women…if that’s possible.

 

*************************

 

Movies referenced in this chapter:

 

My Best Friend’s Girl

 

Some Kind of Wonderful

 

 

 

 

 

 






Chapter 4  How to Meet Those Women

A few days later on a slow moving Wednesday, I got an email from Michael at work.  He asked if I was up for dinner at Applebee’s.  I emailed back and said I’d meet him there at 5:30.  I arrived a few minutes early and waited at the entrance where I could enjoy seeing groups of women arriving.  I supposed most of them, like Michael and me, were meeting after work for an evening out with friends.  “Hi, am I late,” Michael said coming in the door.

 

“No, I just got here.”  We were led to a table and settled in.  “I’ve been watching all the groups of women coming in.  I’ve seen a few I’d like to meet.”

 

“And on that topic, I printed out something I found on the internet.”  Michael handed me the printouts.  “This is from an article for women.  It’s a list of places women can go to meet men.  How great is that?  It’s like listening in on their strategy sessions.”

 

I looked at the list:

 

The best places to find a man:

Sports Bars – Trying to get the attention of a man watching sports can be frustrating but if you know the score and the teams, that’s a great conversation starter.  Men love showing all they know about sports so ask them a question.

Happy Hour/After Work Restaurants and Bars – Men love to unwind after work and if he’s there, he must not have someone at home.

Supermarket – Men have to eat and you can learn a lot about him just from what’s in his cart.

Movie Theatres – Stand in line with a friend and ask the men behind you what movie they’re going to see.  Conversations about favorite movies are a short leap from there.

Class – Take a fun class in something you enjoy.  You’ll meet men with similar interests.

Driving Ranges – Men go there after work and on weekends to practice.  Ask for some help with your swing.  Men love to coach.

Parks and Dog Parks – Dog owners love to talk about their dogs so you have the perfect opening.

Trade Shows and Industry Events – The men are paid to be there and talk so take advantage.  Show up in the afternoon.  There’re always parties and dinners afterwards.

 

“This is great.  Here we are at kind of a sports bar/steak house and it’s happy hour.  We’re in the sweet spot.  When do women start throwing their phone numbers at us?”

 

“Hey, I remember you guys.  Good to see you again.”  Our waitress had arrived and it was the same one we’d talked with before.

 

“Hi Stephanie, it’s good to see you again.  How have you been?”  Michael had remembered her name.  I was impressed…and so was she.

 

Stephanie was flattered by this and it showed in her smile.  “You remember my name.  I’m flattered.”

 

“Yes, of course…by the way, this is Rob and I’m Michael.”  Michael extended his hand.

Stephanie shook his hand and said, “It’s very nice to meet both of you.”

 

“It’s our pleasure, Stephanie.”

 

I watched how positive and natural that entire meeting was.  There was no hustle going on…just three people being friendly.  We asked Stephanie to bring us a couple beers while we decided on our order and off she went.  “I like this girl,” Michael said, “I want to ask her out.  I think she’s interested.”  Now there was a pause as Michael pondered his next step and then he switched back to our discussion of the list he’d given me, “Yes, that list is great and the comments give some insights into how women think about where to go to meet men.”

 

“You know, 90% of the battle for meeting women is a matter of putting yourself in the right position.  If you’re not where women are then you sure aren’t going to meet them.  If you’re still in school, then you’re surrounded by women.  If there’re women where you work, then you have opportunities to meet them.  But that doesn’t apply to most men so we really need to be proactive and get out there to the classes or the dog parks…or Applebee’s.  So do you have a smooth line ready for Stephanie?  How are you going to ask her out?”

 

“Do you want to know the most common generality women make about men? …Hang on…Stephanie’s coming to take our order.”  Michael gave her a big smile as she approached.

 

“Okay guys, are we ready to order?”  We made our choices and gave her our full attention as she went through the options of types of potatoes, how the meat was to be cooked and salad dressing.  “Okay, are you good with your drinks for now?”  We said we were fine and off she went to put in the order.

 

Michael watched Stephanie’s cute ass disappear into the kitchen area and then turned back to me.  “Where were we?”  He laughed at how he’d been distracted.  “Oh, yes.  Do you want to know the most common generality women make about men?  Men are all the same.  Either they’re handing you a line to get laid or they want to get serious.  And I think their complaint is valid.  Women want to meet men but they don’t want to feel pressured.  They want things to start out friendly and easy going.  So now question number two:  Do you know the easiest way to get a girls attention?  Make sure you don’t appear to be trying to get laid or getting serious when you meet them.”

 

I loved Michael’s way of making things simple.  “Women must hate that pressure.  When a guy comes up and acts natural and friendly it must be such a breath of fresh air for her.  Stephanie is a perfect example.  She’s attractive so she must get hit on here at work but look at how you approached her.  The last time we were in here we had nice conversations with her and joked around but you didn’t hit on her or ask for her phone number.  So you got to know her in a friendly way.  She doesn’t feel pressured.  She’s comfortable with us.”

 

“Yeah, it’s a simple formula.  Be friendly and show an interest in her but don’t ask her out right away.  When she doesn’t get the come on then she’s a little confused and it makes her unsure of your intentions…Which is exactly what you want.  Women are the most curious animals on earth and they’re all amateur psychologists so they’ll be trying to figure you out.  Well, maybe that last part is a stretch.”

 

“Actually, that’s not much of a stretch.  Women are curious.”  Just then Stephanie arrived with our salads.

 

She set the plates down and then said, “I’m curious, (pause for effect) would either of you like ground pepper on your salad.”  Michael and I looked at each other and then looked at Stephanie.  She had the biggest grin on her face.  The three of us started laughing and then she said, “You guys are just too much fun…I can’t help it.”  At that she went back to the kitchen and left us there still laughing at our ninja waitress.

 

“Okay, now I’m curious.  We’ve established you want to meet women in friendly and natural ways but how do you ask them out?  For example, how are you going to ask Stephanie out?  Are there clever ways to leave her a note with the tip or ask her which dog park she goes to and then hang out there?  Do you say things like those secret pick-up lines which never fail?  Do I need to buy the book on how to ask a girl out in ways that never miss?”

 

Michael began to laugh, “That’s good.  You’re getting it.  You’re getting how men can over think and over complicate something which should be so easy.  And you’re right.  Cleaver, contrived strategies and ploys to meet a girl are childish and embarrassing.  You ask a girl out the same way you meet her.  You should be honest, straightforward, friendly and natural.  Hi, my name is Michael.  Let’s get together for lunch sometime.”

 

“Is that how you’re going to ask Stephanie?  Is there a chance she’ll turn you down?”

 

“That’s pretty much the script…I will say asking a girl out is simple but not a no-brainer.  Your choice of words is important so you give her the right impression.”  Now Michael thought for a second and continued, “As far as the chance she may turn me down goes, there’s always a chance of that.  I don’t see a ring but she may have a boyfriend.  She may be sick and tired of dating.  Maybe she’s lesbian.”  Michael gave me a look like Who knows?  “But the last time she went back to the kitchen she turned around to see if I was watching her.  That’s about the most important signal you can get a girl’s interested in you.  When you get the look back you know she’s interested.  That goes two ways.  Women are watching to see if the guy looks back too.  Anyway, I got the look back so confidence is high.  Oh, and luckily when she turned around I was looking at her hair and not her butt…That would put my innocent, friendly guy image in jeopardy.”

 

“There’s something else about being honest, straightforward, friendly and natural when you meet a girl.  This is a time for men to be real and genuine.  It’s a time to establish their values and honestly tell other people who they are.  Meeting a girl this way will stop the chameleon complex where a man (or woman) will attempt to appear to be just about anything they think the other person wants to find.  Being friendly and honest is much better and also more effective if you let your straightforwardness say to the other person I’ll tell you who I am if you tell me who you are.  Women will like this and be more likely to trust this man because he’s been honest with them.”

 

“Okay, are you ready?  These plates are hot.”  We picked up our salad plates to make room for the main course and Stephanie thanked us, “Thank you.  Are you finished with your salads?  Is there anything else I can get you…another beer?”

 

“I think we’re fine.  This looks great and we’re hungry.  Thank you Stephanie.”  She walked back to the kitchen area and I watched Michael’s eyes follow her.  Then he broke out a little smile.

 

“Did she give you another look back?”  Michael nodded as he started cutting off a piece of steak.  “Okay, what were we talking about?  Oh yeah, what a concept…Be honest and friendly when you meet a girl.  You know there’re books written about gaming women and being a pick up artist.  They have strategies to use and teach phases of the pick-up.  It becomes a real science…and art, actually.”

 

Michael was thoughtful while he finished a bite.  “Yeah, and I really don’t disagree with that approach but for the most part it’s effective when one night stands and scoring chicks is the goal…and I’m not judging that goal.  Every guy should know what he’s looking for.  In addition, I really don’t think there’s anything we’ve talked about which would be at odds with those books or philosophies. If your goal is to be a pick up artist then adopting that style may work for you.  If your goal is to meet a girl for ongoing dating and a possible relationship then an honest approach is the way to go…my opinion and also it’s what works for me.”

 

“I’m going to check out those books on Amazon when I get home.  The reader reviews usually tell you most of the book.”  I took another bite and then asked, “I have a question though.  Meeting Stephanie, for example, was easy.  She was our waitress and we started talking.  It’s not always that easy.  Are there some…like…What to do and what not to do type rules?  For example, what if I wanted to meet that girl sitting with her friends over there?  What do I do?”

“Good questions,” Michael thought for a second, “Every situation is different but always default to being honest and straightforward.  Your attitude should be you’re a quality guy and you find her interesting and would like to meet her.  Your intentions are friendly and respectful.  Okay, let’s see how you could meet that girl you pointed to.  First, you try for eye contact and a smile.  If you get it then you’re half way home.  If you don’t get that is it because she noticed you looking and chose not to make eye contact?  Be honest answering that question.  But, let’s say you made eye contact.  I think the best way would be to simply ask your waiter to go to the table and tell the girls you (or we) would like to buy them a drink and would that be okay.  If they’re interested they’ll accept and, best case scenario, invite us over to join them.  Or, they smiled and sent their thanks with the waiter so you wait for them to get up to leave and walk over to introduce yourself and ask if she’d like to get together with you sometime.”

 

“The real key is what you said with your attitude…You’re a quality guy.  It’s confidence which carries you.  Be confident, friendly and straightforward.”

 

“Okay, that example is for a restaurant or bar.  What are some of those other places on that list…golf course, theater line, class, Dog Park?  Those are all the same.  You smile.  You say, hi and introduce yourself.  You have a friendly conversation about her golf clubs, what movie she’s going to, practicing your Spanish or what a beautiful dog she has.  The key is for it to be spontaneous and friendly.  If she’s uncomfortable then smile and go on about your business.  If she’s engaged in the conversation, then tell her you’re going to Starbucks for coffee and would she like to join you.  Sometimes you have to be spontaneous and ready for anything no matter where you are.  But, all you really need to be ready for is to simply be friendly with someone.  Think of it that way and it becomes easy to practice.”

 

“You asked about what not to do.”  Michael thought for a while and then said, “Don’t make it complicated.  Don’t hesitate longer than you need to.  Don’t watch her for so long she may think you’re stalking her.  Don’t make up some contrived excuse to talk to her because she’ll see through it.  It’s better to walk up to a girl and say, Hi, my name is Michael and I’d like to get to know you.  Would you like to go have some coffee?  That may seem bold, and I suppose it is, but it’s also honest and filled with confidence.  I used to be nervous doing something like that but I learned not to take it seriously.”

 

“I seriously need to clear these plates,” Stephanie said in a low, serious tone.  She’d snuck up on us again.  Michael and I laughed with her and Michael told her he was going to put a bell around her neck so she couldn’t sneak up on us.  She liked that we appreciated her humor and played along with her.  “Can I bring you anything else…another beer, coffee or desert?”

 

“Thanks, I think we’re ready for the bill.  The food was delicious.  Thank the kitchen for us.”  Stephanie carried the plates off and we continued talking until she came back with the bill in its little leather folder.  We put enough cash in the folder to cover the bill and a generous tip.  I started to get up but Michael asked me to wait until she came back.  After a while Stephanie came back to the table.  We stood up and Michael handed her the folder.  She asked if we need change and Michael said, “No, no, that’s for you.”  Stephanie said thanks and Michael said, “Stephanie, you’re really fun.  I’d love to get together with you.  Why don’t we meet for lunch sometime?”

 

She gave him a big smile and said, “I’d love to.  Here’s my number.  Call me and we’ll set something up.  Lunch sounds great.”
