Michael didn’t try to act like a big important guy to the lowly waitress.  He looked at her with a smile and said, “I look forward to it.  Is it okay to call during the day?”  She said yes and then said bye to us as we put on our coats and headed for the door.

 

Outside I asked, “What would you have done if she’d turned you down?”

 

“I probably would’ve broken down and cried…Well, cried inside.  Actually, the key is to project a mild disappointment it didn’t work out but my life is full and I have lots of friends so no big deal.  In practice, you smile and say, Oh, too bad for me.  I would’ve enjoyed it.  Well, have a nice evening.  It’s very important you handle rejection with good nature and a smile.  It’s like sports.  You should be gracious in victory and in defeat.”

 

“See you soon Michael.  Email me as soon as you call her.  I want to hear all about it.”

 

 

 

 

 





Chapter 5  Dating

Michael called me Friday and asked if I wanted to meet after work for a beer and then maybe get something to eat.  We met at a popular happy hour type bar and settled in to unwind.

 

“Oh Michael, by the way, I watched My Best Friend’s Girl last night.  You were right.  The Jason Biggs character was so pathetic.  His entire life goes into ruin trying to make that girl love him.  I hope watching that movie cured me of ever making the same mistake.”

 

“And how’d you like the first 5 minutes of the movie?  That’s got to be the funniest thing I’ve ever seen about dating.”

 

 “I know.  When the movie was over I re-watched the first part and it was funnier the second time.  It’s like the ultimate what not to do on a date instructional video.  Hey, speaking of dates, have you called Stephanie yet?”

 

“Yeah, I called her the next day.  We’re getting together for lunch on Sunday.  We cross referenced our work schedules and Sunday popped up as the best time for us to meet.  Actually, I like lunch on Sunday for the first date.  It’ll be casual…no pressure…We’ll have fun.  We’re going to meet at the Westside Café around noon.”

 

 “Amen to keeping a first date casual.  Going out to lunch completely eliminates the stress of going out in the evening, picking her up, dinner and a movie, is it getting late, should I go for the goodnight kiss?  That’s too much stress.”

 

“You know, Dating 101 should be taught in school.  I’m serious.  There should be a textbook.  They can watch instructional movies like My Best Friend’s Girl.  They can go on field trips to Olive Garden and learn how to order a meal.  At least it’d be a course the kids would be interested in.  What’s the current drop-out rate in public schools…like 50%?  The kids are bored out of their minds.  All they’re teaching is political correctness and political agendas.  Take some kid in the 8th grade.  He’s 14 years old.  His hormones are on fire.  They outlawed Dodge Ball, the one activity where he could work off some of his sexual frustration and now he’s looking at the list of elective courses for the spring term.  Here’re his choices:  Gay and Lesbian Issues, Women’s Studies, Multi-Culturalism, Global Warming and Conflict Resolution.  Is it any wonder teenage boys are bored with school?  Now tell me a class titled Principles of Dating wouldn’t have 90% of the 8th grade boys signing up.  Hell, that class would go a long ways to solving the illiteracy rate of graduating seniors.”

“Michael, that’s so funny…And what makes it funny is you’re exactly right.  Kids are bored with school because they aren’t learning what’s relevant to them.  So tell me the course outline.  How would you teach the course?”

 

Michael took a long sip from his beer and pondered Dating 101.  “I suppose you have to begin with dating theory.  Let’s see…Well, the first thing you have to understand is men and women have different goals and therefore different approaches to dating.  You see for women a long courtship suits her purpose.  In a sense, dating is like a job application system designed by females for her to choose which male is best for her.  She’s looking for the strongest male who’ll provide the best security and best genes for her offspring.  And, women are content to take their time in that selection process.”

 

“On the other hand, the male has a different strategy.  The male can produce sperm all day long with little effort so he wants to mate as often as he can with multiple partners.  He wants to spread his seed far and wide.  So the male’s approach to dating is to go all the way on the first date and move on to the next partner.  All of this means that usually men and women go on a date with different goals.”

 

“Okay, another difference between men and women is women are much better at the game of seduction than men are.  Women are born with the instincts, or at least learn them at an early age, of how to play hard to get, how to tempt men and all the subtleties of male/female pursuit.  Most men are ignorant about seduction.”

 

“The end result of all this is first, men don’t understand what women want from dating and secondly, men don’t understand the principles of pursuing females.  In a nutshell, men are jumping into the dating game without a clue and they’re up against the masters of seduction and manipulation…the female animal.”

 

“Okay, so what did we learn?  Women want a long courtship.  They see dating as a way to carefully select a man.  Also, women know men want a short courtship so they’re cautious and playing hard to get.  So step one for a man is to turn the tables on the women.  A man should approach dating as casual socializing to have a good time and get to know someone.  This is different from most men who go on a date in order to get laid.  When this happens to a woman, it gets her attention because this guy is different.  She’s happy because you both have the same expectations for the date but, it also throws her off her game.”

 

“How does a man play hard to get?  Don’t call her every night.  Don’t make big sexual moves on her at first…You should appear interested in her yet still holding back a bit like you aren’t quite sure if you want the relationship to go further.  Make her feel as though she has to work harder to convince you that you want the relationship to develop.  Make her use her power of seduction.  Let her use her sensuality and sexuality to catch you.”

 

“What you’re doing is convincing her she wants you more than you want her.  Here’re some other ways to do that.  End the date before she does.  Say you’ve gone to a movie and are now having a bite to eat at a restaurant.  Don’t let the date drag on to that awkward time when she finally says she has to go home.  You need to maintain control of the evening.  While the conversation is still good, while the date is still going well, bring up it’s getting late and it’s probably time to head home.  Tell her you enjoyed the evening and you’re looking forward to getting together again.  This is going to surprise her since every other guy thinks by some miracle if he hangs on long enough maybe he’ll convince her to have sex with him.  You’ve just set yourself apart, demonstrated control of the evening and shown her you’re not easy to get.”

 

“Another way is not to rush things sexually.  You should appear as if you’re still deciding whether or not you want to be involved with her sexually.  Ending the date before she does is one way to accomplish this.  Also, don’t get stressed out over the first kiss.  If it happens fine but relax and let it happen naturally.  When she wants the first kiss to happen she’ll probably give out the vibes.”

 

“Don’t always be available to do something with her.  Say you call a girl and ask her to a movie on Tuesday and she declines.  Don’t say, How about Wednesday? or What day can you make it?  What you should say is, Oh, sorry you can’t go.  Well, maybe we can make it another time.  You want to avoid making it sound as if you’re always free and available every night and you’re just waiting for her.”

 

“Be better than her…Now here is a tough one to explain.  It really goes back to all we talked about with your image but it’s mainly about your self-confidence.  If you have your life together with your finances, your career, where you live and how you dress then your self-confidence will speak for itself.  Whatever you do don’t brag about yourself to try to make yourself a better person.  Women have heard bull before and they won’t fall for it.  Again, let your image and self-confidence speak for you.”

 

“Another way to convince a girl she’ll be lucky to be with you is to play on her insecurities.  This is another tough one to explain because it has to be subtle so you don’t come off as being a negative a**hole.  For example, if she mentions she doesn’t like her job, then say, It’s hard to work in those conditions.  You should get out of a situation where you’re unhappy.  You’re simply putting an accent on the fact her life isn’t perfect.”

“There’s one more thing you can do to convince a girl she wants you more than you want her and that’s if she suspects you’re seeing other women as well as her.  That’ll make you more attractive to her.  She knows she’ll have to work hard to get you.  How do you accomplish this?  Of course, you don’t talk about other women.  It’s all accomplished simply by doing the things we’ve been talking about.  Playing hard to get, not rushing into sex, not calling every night and not always being available will suggest to her there’re other women in your life.  You can let her imagination and curiosity do the work for you.”

 

“There’s a caveat to her thinking other women are in your life.  This is subtle but you want the girl to feel as though she’s special.  In other words…Yes, there’re other women interested in you.  No, you don’t have any trouble getting a date.  Yes, you socialize with friends who’re women.  But, you find this girl to be more interesting, more intriguing and more fun to be with than the others.  Treat her as though she is a special person to you and she won’t feel as though she’s just one of many.  She’ll be flattered by the special treatment because you’ve deemed her to be above the rest and she’ll work harder to maintain her position as number one.”

 

How to Ask a Girl Out

 

“Chapter II of Dating 101 would be How to Ask a Girl Out.  You know what they say about first impressions being so important?  Well, asking a girl out is equally important and most guys get it wrong.  They don’t know there’s a right way and a wrong way to ask a girl out.”

 

“There’re some important principles to follow.  First, ask her in a way which limits rejection.  Second, make it sound like going out with friends and socializing is what you do often.  Third, make it sound like what you’re inviting her to is something you’re doing anyway.  She has an opportunity to come along.  Fourth, make sure not to imply your entire social life hinges on whether she says yes or no…If she says no, it’s no big deal.  You’ll go as planned and she won’t be going.”

 

“Okay, so how do you accomplish this in practice?”  I was ready to take notes.

 

“There’re good ways and bad ways to ask a girl out.  The bad way is, Would you like to go out with me this Friday night?  By saying this you set yourself up for rejection.  You asked a definite question and limited it by asking her out on Friday night.  That night is usually set aside for dating so odds of her being busy are greater.  The wording implies you need a date for a formal event…Like she’ll be doing you a favor by going out with you.  If she says, No, you aren’t sure what that means.  Does she mean, No, I don’t want to go out with you?  Or does she mean, No, I can’t make it that night?  To sum it up, when you ask a girl out like this you sound like an insecure Jr. High kid asking a girl out to his first formal date.”

 

“The right way to ask a girl out is, We should get together and do something sometime.  Memorize these words.  When you say this you give an impression of a casual meeting…no big deal…Friends getting to know each other…Not a formal date…No stress…No come-on.  The beauty of asking a girl out in this way is her response will let you know if she’s interested in you or not and then you go from there.”

 

“If she’s interested she’ll respond in the positive but also her tone will be positive.  Her choice of words will be positive like, Yes, I’d enjoy that or I’d love to.  The expression on her face will be positive.  She may continue the conversation to make a specific day and time to get together.  If you do get a positive response, you can pursue the conversation and arrange to meet on a casual date.  You can also leave it up in the air (assuming you see her often).  If you leave it up in the air, you know she wants to get together and she’ll be waiting now that you’ve teased her with talk of a date.  You’re being elusive and playing hard to get.  Her anticipation works in your favor.”

 

“You may get a negative response but it’ll seldom be something like, No, I don’t want to get together with you.  If she’s not interested in you she’ll say something like, Gee, I don’t know.  I’m awfully busy these days.  She may even say, Yes, to spare your feelings but her tone and her facial expression and her desire to drop the subject will let you know she’s not interested.  She won’t let a time or place be established.  She’ll want to leave it vague.  In this case, she’s not interested in you so don’t pursue it further.”

 

“But whether you get a positive or negative response, there’re many advantages to asking a girl out by saying, We should get together and do something sometime.  First, you aren’t set up for rejection because you haven’t asked her a question.  You simply made a suggestion.  Second, you’re making a friendly offer to her.  It’s like you’re doing her a favor.  Third, you’ve implied a casual get-together to get to know each other.  It’s not a formal date.  Fourth, you’ve learned whether she wants to date you or not without actually asking her out on a date.  Fifth, you’ve made a statement about yourself.  You’re a confident person who likes to do things with friends.  You’ve put no pressure on her.  You’re a friendly, fun-loving person who enjoys going out to have a nice time…Girls want to be around this type of guy.”

 

“Another good option for a first date is to offer an invitation to spontaneous, informal gatherings rather than a date arranged days in advance.  An example would be something like; By the way, some friends are coming over for dinner tonight.  It’s very informal.  Come and join us.  If she does come over, you’re in a position to present your best side to her.  If she can’t make it then say, Oh well, maybe next time.  Too bad, we’d enjoy having you join us.  Again, you haven’t set yourself up for rejection.  You simply made a friendly offer and to her you’re a person who enjoys socializing with friends.”

“In the same vein would be an offer to join you and friends for a drink after work at a nice bar.  Hey, a group of us from work are going over to Friday’s today after work.  We’d love for you to join us.  Once again you’ve simply given her a friendly invite.  If she comes to the gathering, then you have an opportunity to see if she’s interested in you in particular or socializing in general.  If she circulates to meet and talk to everyone that would be different than if she tends to stay by your side and prefers to talk with you and get to know you better.”

 

“Say a girl turns you down.  How many times should you ask a girl out for a date before you give up?  I’d say if you get one rejection, then you’ve learned she’s not interested and you shouldn’t pursue her further.  Move on to someone who is interested in you.  Now, you have to keep in mind there’s rejection and then there’s turning you down but asking for a rain check.  If a girl can’t go out and must say no, but is interested then she’ll probably say, Oh, I’d love to but I have a class that night.  Can we make it another time?  That’s not a rejection…In fact that’s proof she is interested in you so keep asking.”

 

“And that brings up the subject of rejection.  Knowing how to handle a girl saying no is important.  If you don’t know how to handle it then you’ll be afraid to ask a girl out…After all, she may say no and you won’t know what to do.  Rejection is a tough pill to swallow.”

 

“Okay, so what do you do when a girl turns you down for a date?  You have to be gracious and smile.  You have to condition yourself to understand this is a non-event.  You’ll ask someone else to go have coffee with you.  It’s no big deal.  So you smile and say, Oh, that’s too bad…Maybe some other time.  Smile and move on.  Maintaining this attitude says a lot about you.  It shows you have a stable ego and you have a friendly temperament.  If she talks to other girls…and she does…she’ll tell them you seem like a friendly guy.”

 

“One last caveat for when a girl turns you down for a date.  Don’t EVER try to talk her into going out with you.  Don’t try to pressure her or continue to press the issue by saying things like, You can’t make it on Friday…What about Saturday?  Some men will try to manipulate a girl with things like, Oh you don’t need to study.  You can study in the morning.  Or how about, You can get together with your friends anytime.  You should go out with me tonight.  Insecure and controlling men who can’t handle rejection are the ones who do this and you don’t want to be like that or appear like that.”

 

The First Date

 

 “So now the Dating 101 students know how to ask a girl out.  It’s time for them to learn how to make the first date a success.  It’s not hard to do.  The important thing is what type of date to plan.  My date with Stephanie is a perfect example.  We’re meeting for lunch.  It’s casual.  We’re going to a nice but casual restaurant I go to often so there’ll be no surprises.  There’s no pressure.  Two people are getting together to eat and chat.  That date is a pretty good template to go by but let’s break it down to some details and specifics.”

 

“First, let’s talk about the When for a first date.  Daytime dates are good.  Get together for lunch.  Take the dogs to the park.  Go for a bike ride.  Play tennis.  Go to the mall.  Go to the flea market or farmer’s market.  Go to a museum.  Many parks have live music on weekends.  Remember though, weekends are precious for people with full time jobs so make sure she knows you aren’t going to take up her entire day.  You can say something like; We can get together for a couple hours.  How about we go to the park and then get a bite to eat.  Again, that lets her know the date is casual and no big deal.  You’ve proposed a nice break to get out of the house for a couple hours.”

“Because of work schedules it’s sometimes impossible to get together for lunch.  An early dinner is also a nice casual date.  You can propose to meet after work at 5:30 for a drink and dinner.  It’s a nice break from cooking at home.  She’s probably already dressed nicely for work.  It’s very little effort for her and with a nice relaxing dinner the evening is a net positive.  Students would do the same type of thing after school.  Tell a girl you’re going to Burger King or Starbucks after school and she’s welcome to join you.”

 

“If possible, avoid asking a girl out for the first time for a Friday or Saturday night.  Here’re possible pitfalls for a Friday or Saturday night date.”

  1. The odds of her being busy are high so she may have to turn you down, which creates bad vibes right off.

  2. It tells her you don’t have a date for the weekend with someone else so you may have trouble getting dates.

  3. The weekend implies formal dating so the pressure is on.

  4. If you do get the date, she may have to turn down offers from her friends because of her commitment to you and go into the evening regretting having to turn down others.  After all, she has no way of knowing whether she’s going to have a good time with you or not.

 

“On the other hand, if you ask her out for a mid-week evening date or a lunch date or a daytime date on the weekend…”

  1. You imply you’re busy on the weekend nights.  In other words, you’re involved with other girls.

  2. The odds of her being available are greater.

  3. It implies a casual, no-pressure atmosphere to get to know her.

  4. You’ve turned a dull weeknight or lunchtime into a good time for her.  You have good vibes right off the bat.

 

What Type of Date for the First Date

 

“Make the first dates with a girl casual and of shorter duration.  Doing something during the day like going to lunch accomplishes this.  An early evening dinner after work accomplishes this.  Going to a movie is a casual get-together.  Save the formal dates for later on when you’re more comfortable with her.  Most men do the opposite.  They put out the effort with formal dates at first when they’re trying to impress the girl and then as the relationship develops, the dates become more and more casual.  This can imply to the girl she’s being taken for granted…And that’s usually the case.”

 

“Don’t try to be romantic on the first date.  There’s no need for romantic places, candle lit dinners, roses and violins, holding her hand and telling her how beautiful she is.  She’ll wonder what this guy is up to, think she’s being fed a line and probably run like hell.  If you simply go to a nice place and have a friendly attitude then the excitement of the first date and getting to know someone is excitement enough.  There’s no need to try to make it more than it is.”

 

“Do you remember the movie, Fast Times at Ridgemont High?  There’s a classic scene in that movie when Mark Ratner takes Stacey out on a first date.  It’s all too formal and the poor guy’s a nervous wreck.  They both work at the mall.  He should’ve just asked her to get a bite to eat or go to a movie after work.  Something simple would’ve saved him a lot of stress.”

 

“Even though the first date is casual, you should have every detail planned.  You should go somewhere familiar to you so there’re no surprises.  Know where you’ll park so you aren’t circling the block for a half an hour.  Even know what you’ll order to eat.  Of course, in spite of every detailed being planned the date should seem spontaneous to her.”

 

Do’s and Don’ts for the First Date

 

“First of all be a gentleman.  That means to be polite and positive towards her.  It means to pay for the entire date.  It means to be gracious even if you decide you don’t want to continue dating the girl.  It means to end the date on the same positive note you began it with.”

 

“During the date, do keep the conversation on a positive level, but don’t patronize her.  Don’t try to please her by saying what you think she wants to hear.  Don’t always be agreeing – hoping if you agree to everything, she’ll like you.  Your efforts to bend over backwards to please someone will most likely make you appear to be a wishy-washy, spineless wimp who’s desperate to be loved.  You certainly won’t appear to be your own man and confident in your convictions.”

 

“Do talk about her and don’t talk about yourself.  You want to learn as much as you can about her so ask questions and focus the conversation on her.  She’ll be comfortable talking about herself or her interests so accommodate her.  By not talking about yourself you let your image speak for itself and the less women know about you, the more curious they become.  Be a man of mystery…but not to the point it looks like you’re hiding something.  Just be content to focus on her but don’t make her feel like she’s being interrogated.”

 

“Don’t talk about your ex-girlfriends or old relationships.  Do talk about her relationships if they come up.  Most likely she wants to talk about the relationships…because the ex-boyfriends wouldn’t.  Also, hearing about her old relationships is a good way to learn more about her.”

 

“Don’t say anything negative about anything in your life including your job, your ex-girlfriend, your friends, your health, your parents or whatever.

 

“Do make your body language and eye contact inviting to her during the date.  Don’t watch other girls.  Don’t cross your arms.  Don’t crowd her or make her uncomfortable.”

 

“Remember this…People go on dates to have a good time and not to be serious.  Keep this in mind when you plan the activities and keep this in mind during the date. Do be relaxed, upbeat and positive.”

“What if the date goes badly?  It happens.  Sometimes it’s just bad chemistry.  Sometimes she’s a bitch.  Whatever the reason, you’re now sitting at a restaurant wishing you could radio Scotty to beam you up and get you out of there but you can’t so there you are.  If this ever happens to you then you’ll understand why it’s a good idea to keep the first date casual.  It’s also a good idea to put a time limit on the date…Aren’t you glad you’re only spending a casual one hour lunch with this date from hell?  It’s also a good idea to meet the girl at the movie instead of driving her there.  Yes, all of those suggestions for keeping a first date casual have dual purposes.”

 

“When women (and probably men) go on a blind date they often have a friend call 45 minutes into the date.  This cell phone call is designed as an excuse to get out of the date if things aren’t going well.  It’s not a bad idea.  I’ve never done it myself.  If I’m on a bad date I simply smile and treat the girl with respect.  I try to make the best of it.  If you’re ever on a first date and the girl wants to end it, you should accommodate her.  Smile and act like she’s doing you a favor…because in fact she is doing you a favor.  You have better things to do with your time and money than spend it with her.  Whatever you do, don’t get mad or act defensive.  Your demeanor should be that of relief.  She’s done you a favor.”

 

“Sometimes the date goes badly simply because the two of you have nothing in common and the conversation is…well…there is no conversation.  When there’s no conversation you start asking questions.  What does your father do?  Do you have any brothers?  Where were you born?  Do you have a pet?  Keep asking questions and eat fast…the date will end even though it doesn’t seem like it.  Smile and be a gentleman.”

 

“Sometimes a girl will call and break the date.  Sometimes a girl won’t show up where you were going to meet…She’ll stand you up.  Sometimes you’ll come to pick her up and the lights are off and no one is home.  Okay, so why would a woman do this?  Maybe she didn’t want to go out with you in the first place but it was easier to say, Yes and then not show up.   Maybe she got a better offer.  Maybe she likes screwing with men.  It could be a variety of reasons but don’t get mad if it happens.  She just did you a favor.  You found out for sure she’s not interested in you and you didn’t have to waste your time, energy and money on her.  Don’t get mad at her.  Don’t call her to ask what happened.  If you see her don’t bring it up…Just smile and act as if nothing happened.  If she brings it up just shrug your shoulder like it’s no big thing to you and act as if you had better things to do anyway.”

 

“There’s another possibility which can happen on a first date and it never ends well.  That’s when the guy’s totally infatuated with the girl and falls head over heels in love with her on the date…but she’s not interested in him for anything beyond friends.  In reality, the guy’s better off if the girl would be a complete bitch on the date or even better the guy gets stood up.  Most girls don’t want to be rude or hurt a guy’s feelings so they’re friendly and polite.  The guy is so smitten with her he interprets her friendly manner as a go signal.  You have a persistent guy who thinks he’s in love, a girl who’s not interested and it doesn’t end well for the guy.”

 

“I have a friend Paul who just went through that.  He went out of town to a friend’s wedding.  The wedding and reception became a kind of reunion for the groom and his friends.  Many of the men and women knew each other from college.  At the wedding Paul met Marcia.  Marcia was a friend of the bride and she knew Paul was a close friend of the groom.  Marcia was at the wedding with some girlfriends…so unescorted.  Paul fell for Marcia the first moment he saw her and so spent the entire reception with her dancing and chatting and introducing her to the groom’s friends.  Marcia thought Paul was nice and friendly but she had no interest in him beyond friends.  Still, it was nice for her to have someone to dance with and talk to so she had a nice evening and didn’t think anything of it when Paul asked if she’d meet him for lunch the next day, Sunday, before he flew home.”

 

“The next day they meet for lunch and Paul starts telling Marcia he couldn’t sleep because he was thinking about her.  He even tells her he thinks he’s in love with her.  Marcia isn’t the least bit interested in Paul and has no intention of letting anything develop.  But, she knows Paul is friends with her friends so she doesn’t want to say, Listen, I’m not attracted to you so back off.  Instead she politely points out they hardly know each other and she’s suspicious of sudden infatuations.  She hopes this will give Paul the hint.”

 

“But Paul doesn’t get the hint and reacts to wanting what he can’t have.  He only becomes more determined and his confessions of strong feelings for Marcia become more adamant.  Paul thinks if he can just convince Marcia that his love for her is real, then she’ll suddenly fall for him.  In other words, he misreads Marcia’s hint.  Thankfully for Marcia the lunch ends but walking to the cars Paul promises he’ll call her and they can email.  He asks her to come to visit him and tells her he’ll be thinking about her.  Paul continued talking to Marcia until she finally told him she was late to visit her parents.”

 

“That evening, and every evening for the next week Paul calls Marcia.  He hangs on the phone telling her how much he loves her and how much he’s thinking about her.  Paul’s determination does not falter.  He thinks all Marcia really wants is sincere love and by calling every night it’ll prove his love is sincere and not just an infatuation.  Of course, Marcia does want sincere love…But not from Paul.  Marcia finally comes out and tells Paul she has no interest in him and tells him to stop calling her.”

 

“Here’s some advice to never forget.  As you’re pursuing a girl, if you ever find yourself telling her how much you love her, trying to convince her how much you love her or telling her how special she is in order to coax her into a relationship, then you’re in big trouble.  You probably need an intervention.”

 

“So what could Paul have done to be successful with Marcia?  NOTHING.  Marcia wasn’t interested and never would be interested in Paul.  Marcia had been polite and Paul interpreted politeness for interest.  If he’d recognized her disinterest from the beginning he would’ve saved himself a lot of time, money and sleepless nights.  More importantly he wouldn’t have made a complete fool of himself…Yes, the word about Paul and Marcia got out to all of Paul’s friends.  He’s now trying to live it down.”

 

“So what’ve we learned here?”

  1. If you’re telling a woman you love her like Paul was, you’re doing so because you feel insecure.  You’re hoping if you say it to her, she’ll say it back.  But there’s a good reason why you feel insecure and that’s because in truth she doesn’t love you and doesn’t want your love.  You’ve sensed this deep down and are reacting in desperation.

  2. If a woman’s telling you that you don’t love her and refuses to accept your admissions of love then it’s because she doesn’t want you to love her because she doesn’t love you and she’s trying to tell you that.  Don’t take what she’s saying literally and take it as a challenge to prove you really do love her.  When Marcia said it was only an infatuation and we barely know each other, she was really politely saying, I’m not interested so cool it.  Paul missed the hint, took what she said literally, thought Marcia was only waiting to make sure his love was real before she fell for him and proceeded to spend the next week being a complete fool.

  3. Don’t be so naïve as to believe things you were told when you were young.  Your love for her is not going to make her love you.  It’s not like she’s only waiting for your love to close the deal.  Either she’s attracted to you or she’s not.  A woman doesn’t need a guy’s love in order to find that guy attractive.  Women fall for men who don’t love them every day.

  4. Trying to convince a girl you love her breaks all the basic rules of pursuit:  a) You show you want her more than she wants you.  b) You scare her by being too serious and trying to push a commitment on her and c) You suggest you must be a loser if you’re that desperate for someone to love you.

 

“The final word about first dates isn’t going to help you be relaxed and comfortable during the date but it’s something you must never forget…You’re being judged by this girl.  She’s putting you under a microscope and she’s taking mental notes which will be shared by her team of friends, who’re all dating forensic scientists.  They’ll scrutinize everything you did from how you dealt with the waiter to the amount of the tip to the make and year of your car to what you talked about and especially your job.  That isn’t very comforting but at least now you know why having your life in order is a good idea.”

 

During the Date

 

“Here’s some more advice for dating.  The easiest way to impress a girl isn’t to brag about yourself, but rather to give her a simple compliment like, Wow, you look beautiful.  I love your jacket.  It’s important you give these compliments with simple sincerity and not sounding like you’re giving her a line.  You don’t want her to think you’re fishing for a return compliment or hoping to get her in bed.”

 

“When you take a girl on a date treat her like she’s special and you are her escort.  This means you aren’t scamming on other women or looking at other women at the restaurant or wherever.  This also means treat her well but don’t smother her.  Men can dote on a woman to the point it ceases to be flattery.  It can start to look like the guy hasn’t had a date in years and he’s desperate.  This is a turn-off to women.  I remember going on a spring break road trip in college.  We were in a bar filled with college kids and one of my friends had made a connection with a nice girl.  As the evening went on he started smothering her.  He had his arm around her and wouldn’t let go.  The affection he was showing was obviously one-sided and I could see the girl start to get disenchanted.  I finally pulled him aside and told him he was looking desperate.  He started focusing on joining in the fun of the party instead of corralling that girl and the girl became more comfortable with him after that.”

 

“Don’t forget to be a good listener.  This alone will set you apart from most men.  Listen to her and be interested in her as a person…not just as a potential sex partner.  Get her to talk about who she is and what she’s interested in…And if she isn’t interested in anything then you’re learning what a long term relationship with her would be like.”

“Women go on dates to get to know the man.  They want to get a sense of who he really is and what he stands for.  Men should learn what values they hold as important and how to articulate that.  One of the problems most men have is they don’t stand for anything, or at least they haven’t given it any thought.  It’s true some women go for a smooth presentation but many women want substance and won’t stay around if they find nothing behind the presentation.”

 

“Dating can be a minefield of unexpected events.  There’s no way to be prepared for every contingency but here’re some common ones.  You’re at a restaurant with a girl looking at the menu and having a hard time finding something you want to order.  The waitress comes and you’re still unable to decide.  Now you’re asking the waitress questions but still can’t decide.  The waitress takes your date’s order and comes back to you…  Okay, here’s the advice:  DON’T DO THIS!!!  When you’re ordering your meal, decide quickly and put down your menu.  This will make you look like a decisive man who has many important things to do and decide everyday so picking a meal is something you can do in your sleep.”

 

“You’re out on a date having a good time and suddenly the girl asks if you’re seeing other women…What do you say?  Here’s what you say, I go out and do things with lots of friends and some of those friends are women?  This should be said with an innocent tone and an attitude that this is normal behavior…That is to socialize with friends.  Now, some women will try to trip you up and respond with a conclusion like, So you ARE seeing other women.  To this you reply, Wait, wait, wait…You think I’m a player.  I socialize with friends.  That’s not a player.  Above all don’t get defensive.  Have a bit of a chuckle over the suggestion…almost like you find it amusing she thinks you’re a player.”

 

“Did you see the movie, Old School?  That movie is hilarious.  Luke Wilson spends most of the movie trying to convince this girl he’s not a player but every time he’s with her they run into some girl he has a history with.  Old School is a must see if you like comedy.”

 

“Sometimes a girl will start criticizing you with no apparent purpose or logic.  She’s testing you.  She’s seeing what you’re made of.  If you react to the criticism you’re dead.  If you take the criticism literally you’re dead.  If you try to please her you’re dead.  There’s only one way to respond to her.  You smile, give a little laugh and say, Oh, am I being tested now?  This lets her know you’re on to her, know exactly what she’s doing and are too secure in yourself to fall for it.  If she keeps it up you can then smile, give another laugh and say, Gee, I guess the secret’s out.  I’m not perfect.  Once again you’ve let her know you aren’t buying into her game.”

 

“If you respond to her test in the proper way, one of two things will happen.  A)  You passed her test and she backs off…but be careful of this girl.  You won this round but she’s not through.  Or B)  She gets pissed off you didn’t fall for her little game.  In this case you want to end the date as quickly as you can because you don’t want to date this girl ever again.”

 

“Actually, there is a third possible option.  Say you’ve already decided you don’t want to date this girl anymore but you’re being a gentleman on the date and then she starts criticizing you and testing you.  This is the last straw for you so to put the final nail in the coffin you say, Gee, did you learn that in your Women’s Studies class?  This will pretty much put this kind of girl over the edge.  If she’s the type that criticizes a guy who’s paying for her dinner then she’s the type that has a complex about women being superior to men and you’ve just made fun of the sisterhood.  Don’t turn your back on her.  End the date as quickly as you can.”

 

“Here’s another important piece of advice to remember.  There’s an old saying in film making, Kill the scene before it dies.  What this refers to is not letting a good thing drag on until it becomes boring and loses its energy.  If a movie dwells on every scene too long, the audience becomes restless and bored.  The humor is lost.  The excitement is lost.  The same thing can happen on a date so you always want to end the date before she does.  Consider the example of a guy on a date and the evening is winding down.  The possibility of romance with this girl in the future is good but probably not this evening.  The guy makes the mistake thinking if he hangs on ‘til the bitter end then the million to one shot will come through and he’ll get lucky.  Wrong!!!  It’s not going to happen.  What will happen is the girl will finally get tired and maybe bored and it’ll get so late she’ll have to announce she wants to go home.  This puts a negative tone on the date and is a transfer of control to the woman who’s now making the decisions…which she has to do as the guy can’t figure out it’s time to go.”

 

“So end the date before it dies.  You need to alertly perceive that point in the evening when it’s time to wrap it up so the mood doesn’t begin to fade.  You politely explain it’s late, assure her you had a great time and tell her you’d like to get together again.  She’ll appreciate you making that decision so she doesn’t have to herself.  You’ve maintained control of the evening and you’ve shown her you aren’t desperate.  Amazing how much you can accomplish simply by ending the date at the right time.”

 

Steady Dating

 

The next time Michael and I got together was Sunday evening to watch a basketball game.  I immediately asked him how his date with Stephanie had gone.  “Oh, we had a great time from start to finish.  We met around noon at the Westside Café and you know how popular that place is for breakfast and lunch on Sunday so there was about a 20 minute wait to get a table.  She didn’t care we had to wait…a good sign.  We talked and laughed the entire time we were together.  She’s a little chatterbox…in a good way.  She has an enthusiastic, bubbly personality and she’s really funny.  There were times we were laughing so hard people were looking at us.  After we ate it was obvious we both wanted to spend some more time together so we walked around in the area window shopping and getting to know each other.  Around 2:00pm I looked at my cell phone and we looked at each other and she said, You’re right.  It’s time for this girl to get home.  I walked her back to her car and she said, I had a great time.  I’d love to see you again.  Bless her little heart.  When a girl likes you, she doesn’t keep it a secret.  So I said, That sounds great.  Don’t you have Thursday evening off?  I knew her work schedule from when we set up our first date and I knew she had Thursdays off.  Anyway, she was enthusiastic to go out on Thursday and I told her I’d think of something for us to do and call her on Tuesday to set up an exact time.”

 

So then I asked how she looked and Michael broke into a big smile.  “Oh, my God she looked amazing.  The first thing I said to her when she walked in the café was how nice she looked.  It was obvious she’d spent some time picking out nice clothes and getting ready for our date…another good sign.”  Then I asked what he was going to do for the second date.  “I don’t know.  I have to think about it and consider some options.”

 

I wanted to learn more about dating so I asked, “Michael, didn’t you say the next topic in your Dating 101 course was going to be Steady Dating?”

 

“Oh yeah, steady dating is the goal isn’t it.  I mean finding a girl and meeting her and going through the first date can really be a lot of work.  Once you settle into steady dating you can relax and enjoy the rewards of all that work.”  Now Michael gave a dramatic gesture and said, “Not so fast there, hot shot.  You’re not home free by any means.  Steady dating has a whole new list of things to learn and consider.”

 

“Meeting a person is one thing…Staying with her is quite another.  A man can work very hard getting those first few dates.  Now it’s time to follow through and capitalize on the opportunity he’s created.  The strength of a relationship or maybe what we’re talking about here is the likelihood a relationship will develop is really based on how two people treat each other.  Up until now the man was attracted to her physical appearance and the woman was drawn to his image.  Now they’re going to get to know each other and see how one treats the other.  In a sentence, substance will start to replace image in its importance.”

“Okay, what is it a man gives to a girl when dating…And, what is it a man should expect in return?  They should show Respect for each other and not take the other for granted.  If they give Romance and Affection they have a right to expect it in return.  Both the man and the woman must appreciate how precious each other’s Time is.  When one offers their Communication, they can expect it in return.  Consistency means you’re the same person from day to day and you treat others the same.  People around you have the right to expect that.  Humor and Humility are wonderful traits to have and wonderful traits to find in others.  A good person will Challenge their friends to be better with their Inspiration, Motivation and Support…and it’s reasonable to expect that in return.”

 

“Those general concepts are all very important to keep in mind as you date a girl and continue to know her better.  You should always be asking yourself if you’re living up to those concepts…And, you should always be asking yourself if this girl is doing the same.”

 

“With those concepts in mind, let’s move to more practical suggestions for steady dating.  First, some general guides.  It’s always best to be a little unpredictable.  This is easiest to explain by describing what you want to avoid…and that’s becoming routine to the point of boring.  Every Friday you go to the same restaurant and then a movie.  Now, that’s a nice date but what if it’s the same routine every week.  Things become stale and boring.  So it’s good to be a little unpredictable.  It keeps things fresh and keeps the girl on her toes.  It’s good to do different things and do them on different nights.  It’s good to occasionally be unavailable.  It’s good to call her when she doesn’t expect you to call.  By doing this you become a bit elusive.  Your call and suggestion to do something different becomes a pleasant surprise instead of the same old routine.  It has her wondering…And women love a bit of the unknown.”

 

“Occasionally add some imagination to your dates.  It’s always easy to go to a movie so see if there’s a concert you could go to for a change.  You should have a feel for her hobbies and interests so surprise her with an afternoon at the museum or the zoo or the equestrian competition.  Find different restaurants to go to with different cuisine.  Go for a drive in the country and visit a winery.  Open up the Arts and Entertainment section of the paper for ideas.  This doesn’t have to happen every week but these are the dates she’ll remember.”

 

“I work with this guy, Steve, and he did everything right meeting Lisa.  She worked at a bookstore he used to go into once or twice a week.  Lisa and Steve had often chatted and even been a little flirtatious on occasion.  Finally one day, a few weeks after Steve first noticed Lisa he was leaving the store and said, We should get together and go to a movie sometime.  Lisa’s face brightened with enthusiasm and she said, Great!  There’s a new movie playing I want to see.  When can we go?  So they went out on their first date and it went well.  They enjoyed the movie, the conversation flowed and Lisa pulled close to Steve when they walked.  By all indications romance was in the air.  The next time they got together Steve suggested they meet after work to go to dinner.  Once again the evening went well and Lisa seemed to be charmed by Steve.”

 

“Up until that point Steve had done very well but then he started to make mistakes.  He found Lisa very attractive and the prospect of a sexual relationship with her was very enticing.  He was anxious for that relationship to develop and so he started trying to spend more time with her.  He was coming into the bookstore like once a day and everyday he was asking her out or trying to get together in one way or another.  With her work schedule she didn’t have many nights off and Steve was trying to fill them all.  If he asked her out for one night and she couldn’t make it then he’d say, Well, what night can you make it?  I’m available any time.”

 

“Then Lisa was leaving town for a week and was very busy getting ready for the trip.  In spite of her hints she wouldn’t be able to do anything with Steve until she returned, he continued to pressure her to go out.  Finally, she consented to go out to dinner the night before she left.  This date didn’t go well.  She was nervous about her trip and the last minute packing which needed to be done.  She didn’t want to go out in the first place and was starting to resent Steve for pressuring her.  Steve could sense the affection he used to enjoy from Lisa was absent.  He became insecure and started trying harder to establish a romantic mood.  The more she resisted, the harder he tried.  He was getting desperate now and started talking about the two of them in romantic terms.  In spite of her efforts to divert the conversation, he persisted.  He started telling her how interested he was in her and how much he liked her.  He was hoping if he said it to her then she’d say it back.  He was fishing for compliments in desperation.”

 

“Needless to say the date ended on a low note.  When Lisa got back from her trip, Steve went in to the bookstore.  She was behind the counter and kept her eyes on her work pretending not to see him when he approached.  Standing at the counter he finally had to say, Hi Lisa.  She was cold and distracted and turned down Steve’s offer for dinner making up an excuse.  She turned down all of Steve’s offers after that and he finally gave up…wondering where he’d gone wrong.”

 

“Steve became over anxious and lost patience to let the relationship develop at its own natural pace.  He stopped playing hard to get.  He scared her off by appearing too serious.  He made himself so available she sensed there were no other women in his life and he was desperate for a relationship.  Only losers are that desperate.  Coming into the store everyday was one thing but pressuring her to go out the night before her trip was the last straw for Lisa.  She felt smothered by Steve.  And what did he expect to accomplish from that date?  Did he think he was going to get laid?  The relationship hadn’t progressed to that point yet.”

 

“He tried to push the relationship to the next level before Lisa was ready.  He became insecure about her feelings for him and she sensed that.  Steve was no longer the secure guy she’d met and Lisa lost her interest in him.  Lisa was cautious of jumping into something too quickly.  Steve should’ve dated Lisa once a week.  He should’ve continued to visit her store once a week.  He should’ve sensed Lisa was too busy to see him before her trip and let it go until she got back.”

 

“Don’t become over anxious like Steve did.  It’s easy to do.  She seems interested.  He starts calling her too often, wanting to get together too often and coming on too strong.  She senses he’s too serious too soon.  Boom, she’s turned off and starts pulling back.”

 

“So, how often do you call and how often do you go out?  There’s no way to answer that.  One real date a week is expected but after that it depends.  You have to respect her demands from work.  If she’s a single mom then her free time is limited and precious.  You may call her with a spontaneous offer of meeting after work for a drink or coming over with take out and a movie, but if she sounds hesitant then don’t press her.  She probably just doesn’t have time and you don’t want to be the cause of her getting stressed out from too little time to take care of her responsibilities.  On the contrary, a good friend helps alleviate a friend’s burden.  Consider a date where you run errands together at the mall, get the shopping done and grab a bite at the food court.  Not only will you be helping each other take care of things which need to be done but you’ll find out how well you work together on everyday tasks. ”

 

“If a girl calls you to get together, that’s great but don’t feel guilty about occasionally turning her down.  Simply say, Oh, I’d love to but I’m busy Wednesday night.  Is there another night we can get together?  Make sure she knows you’re happy she called and you’d like to go out but you’re tied up that night.  Don’t offer details beyond the fact you’re busy.  This will tell her you aren’t easy or desperate for a date.   And, she’ll think there’re other women in your life, which isn’t such a bad thing…Women love to be curious…and they hate to be bored.”

 

“A woman I worked with was dating a guy who called her two or three times a day and at work.  He’d hang on the phone for hours as if he had nothing better to do.  She had to cut his calls short at work or he’d never stop talking.  She complained about him all the time.  One time she told me he’d call and say, Hi, I’m bored.  What’re you doing?  She’d laugh at how the guy must not have a life.”

 

“Never hang on the phone for hours as if you have nothing better to do.  Call, be pleasant, get the purpose of the call over and tell her you have to run.  If she’s going on and on talking you should do the same.  Tell her you have to run.  Let her know you’re busy and the two of you can talk some other time.  Remember, you’re a vital, important person and too busy for small talk.”

 

“Here’s some more phone etiquette.  Say she said she was going to call you after work around 5pm.  Now it’s 8pm and she finally calls.  Don’t answer the phone with an attitude and say, Well, it’s about time you called.  I’ve been waiting here by the phone for hours.  This would send a terrible message.  You’d sound like a whining little puppy dog scratching at the door waiting to be let in.  It’d suggest your entire life revolves around her phone call.  What you want her to believe is you’re busy doing things and you didn’t even notice her call was late.  So what you should do is answer the phone with the usual upbeat tone in your voice and say, Oh, hi.  How’s it going?  Don’t mention her call is late and if she brings it up say, No problem, I’ve been busy taking care of some business on the computer.”

 

“What do you do if a girl calls while you’re with another woman?  Don’t try to bluff your way through the conversation.  Both of them will sense you’re uncomfortable and suspect you’re being deceitful to one or both.  The best thing to do is to speak in a positive, comfortable voice and say, Hi, How’re you doing?  Listen, I can’t talk right now.  Can I call you back later?  This can be tough to do but remember if you’re not in a committed relationship then you’re not doing anything wrong so don’t act like you’ve been caught at something.  You must have that conviction in your voice.  Moreover, by doing this you’re being completely up-front and honest.  It’s also important you’re not rude or insulting to the girl you’re with by talking on the phone while she waits.  If the girl who calls asks what you’re doing, simply say you’re with a friend.  Of course, both women will suspect or assume other women are interested in you, which is what you want them to think so let their imagination do your work for you.  In fact, I have a friend who occasionally tells a girl he’s busy and will have to call her back even when he’s home alone watching TV.  He does this to establish a mystery woman and keep her off balance and guessing.”

 

“Romantic touches like flowers, small gifts, candlelit dinners, romantic notes and romantic cards should be postponed until you’re in a committed relationship.  While you’re dating gifts should be practical and thoughtful.  Say on your first date you’re walking by a Jamba Juice and she mentions how she loves fruit smoothies.  Two months later the two of you’re still dating and you want to give her a present.  Give a gift certificate to Jamba Juice.  It’s not romantic but it is thoughtful and she’ll be surprised you listen to her and remember.  She’ll feel special.  It’d be the same thing if she mentioned on the phone she lost her computer memory stick.  On the way to pick her up you stop by Office Depot and pick one up for $10.  You’re a thoughtful person and she’ll notice.”

 

“Sometimes a woman changes her mind about a man after the first couple dates.  She loses interest in a man who initially she thought she wanted to know better.  This may happen for many different reasons.  It could be as simple as finding him attractive at first but once she got to know him realizing she felt no chemistry.  It could be she met someone else who she’s more interested in.  It could be she’s devious and started dating you in hopes the guy she really wants gets jealous and comes after her.  She wanted to force the issue, so to speak.  Whatever the reason, the end result is the same and if it happens you need to recognize it, accept it and move on.”

“Along the same lines is when a guy hears the five words no man wants to hear and that’s when a girl blocks advancing the relationship by saying, No, let’s just be friends.  Stop dating that girl because continuing this kind of relationship is a castrating experience…with one possible exception and that is you’re using her as a female friend to enhance your image and attract other women.  In that case, she’s perfectly suited for that purpose.”

 

“But let’s say you want the relationship with a girl to move to the next level and she tells you she just wants to be friends.  You don’t want to continue seeing her.  Being in this kind of relationship you portray yourself as someone who has nothing better to do than hang around with a girl who’s not that interested in you.  The relationship will never get to where you want it to go…sex.  And even if by some miracle the relationship did advance to the bedroom, she’d be the one controlling sex.  She’d dictate the where, the when and how much.  If she wants to cut you off at any time, she can and you have to accept it because that was the implied agreement from the start.  She’s in complete control and she will, therefore, never have respect for you.”

 

“On the other hand, if you walk away from this relationship you’ve established you’re the type used to being in charge and you have plenty of other girls willing to take you on your terms.  Even if she comes running after you because she misses your company, keep walking.  She’s not the girl for you.”

 

“Here’s something which happens more than you’d think.  That’s when the girl you’re dating starts suggesting you’re seeing another woman.  She’s doing this because either a) she’s insecure and worried you might be seeing another woman and she’s trying to get reassurance you’re not; or b) she’s seeing other men and it’d make her feel better if you were seeing other women.  She creates the other woman to relieve her guilt and/or to give you the hint you should be seeing other women too.”

 

“So what should you do?  In either case you’re better off to let her think you’re seeing other women.  In the first case where she’s looking for reassurance, it’s best to keep her insecure.  In the second case where she’s seeing other men you’d be a fool not to be seeing other women.  Nowhere is it written if you only date her she’ll only date you.  But, if she’s seeing other men then you need to re-think whether you want to continue to date her.  There is a truism which is very important to learn and remember.  If a woman is dating two men then it’s because neither one is the man of her dreams.  If she can’t decide between two men then it’s because neither man fills her needs.  It’s not a good idea to be one of those two men.”

 

“But let’s get back to what you say if a girl starts asking if you’re seeing another woman.  You laugh a little and say, Yes, I’m seeing two other women…they’re twins.  Make light of it and then give her a simple denial.  Above all, never go into a lengthy denial in order to reassure her.  This will just make you sound guilty and also trying to reassure her loses for you the golden opportunity to keep her slightly insecure and guessing.”

 

“Okay, this brings us to the dreaded question.  What if the girl you’re dating is dating other men?  What do you do?

  1. You can make yourself more attractive than the other guy using every trick you’ve learned.

  2. You don’t want to confront her on it.  This will only create hassles and suggest your insecurity.  Better to show her you’re not the type to be bothered if she has a social life beyond you.

  3. You can let her think you’re seeing other women.  That’ll turn the tables on her.

  4. You don’t ever want to criticize him.  You’d look insecure and petty.

 

“But before you spend a lot of time trying to win this competition you need to consider how men competing with each other work to the woman’s advantage.  In the animal world males compete to sort out the toughest, most ambitious and most resilient genes from the weaker and less capable genes.  Is this nature’s plan or women’s plan because it sure makes her job of selection easier for her?  In many species of animals the woman encourages the struggle so she can pick the best.  She can inspect the men as they compete.  It brings to mind the culture inside a single’s barred and begs the question of who’s in charge…the men or the women.”

 

“So back to the dreaded question and what do you do?  You can accept and enjoy it or drop her.  Those are really your only choices.  You don’t want it to get to you.  Living with jealousy is hell on earth.  She may be using you to get the other guys attention.  You may be the odd man out.  Maybe her plan is to get the other guy jealous so she can manipulate him into a commitment and she’s using you to do it.  The possibilities are endless but it all comes back to something you must never forget.  If a woman can’t decide between two guys then she isn’t really interested in either one…so continuing with this girl isn’t going anywhere but games and more games.

 

“And by the way, that truism if a woman can’t decide between two guys…goes the other way.  If you’re seeing two women and you can’t decide which one to become serious with, then neither one is the girl of your dreams.  When you meet the one, other girls don’t exist.”

 

“That’s interesting because that’s what happens on the TV show, The Bachelor.  It comes down to the end and he can’t decide between the two or three left.  It means those girls are all nice and pretty but none of them are…The One.”

 

***********************

 

“Up until now you and I’ve been very tough and demanding of men.  Men should get their life in order.  Men should be the gentleman and treat the woman like a princess.  Well, now that you’re dating a girl it’s time to start judging her.  It’s time to look deep inside this girl and decide if this is someone you want to move into a relationship with.  You really need to use every bit of insight you can gather to know this girl.  You have to get past how good looking she is in tight jeans because a relationship with a selfish or dysfunctional or negative girl gets old fast.  You need to scrutinize her and always be asking yourself questions like…”

 

  1. Does she really know what she wants or does she only say she knows what she wants?

  2. Is she someone who wants it all and expects someone else to give it to her?

  3. Is she interested in the event or the person taking her to the event?

  4. Is she moody or is she the same person rain or shine?

  5. Ultimately, is everything all about her or is she a giving person?

  6. How does she react when you say, “No, I can’t go out tonight?”  Is she understanding or a spoiled girl who demands immediate gratification?

  7. How does she react when things don’t go her way?  Does she take it in stride or get grumpy?

  8. When you’re walking in the park and it starts to rain, does she laugh or cry?

  9. Is the relationship advancing the way you want it to?

  10. Does she always get everything she wants…not so for you?

  11. Are you getting what you want or is what you want not her problem?

  12. Does she show her appreciation for you?  Remember The Cardinal Rule #1.  Never stay where you are not appreciated.

 

“Those questions may sound harsh and it may sound like you’re nit-picking on someone.    So what if she got a little grumpy.  We had to wait 45 minutes for a table and she was hungry…It’s understandable.  So what if she got angry when I had to work late and couldn’t go to the movie…It’s understandable.  So what if you always go to her choice of restaurant…It’s understandable…”

“…Well, I’ll tell you the so what.  You’re investing your time and energy and money into being with this girl.  You really need to learn if this is a quality person you’re spending time with.  If she is a quality person then you’ll want to let the dating become something more serious.  If she’s a person who’s selfish or entitled or moody or treats you poorly, then do you really want to continue seeing her?  Is she really that good looking?”

 

**************************

 

Movies referenced in this chapter:

 

Fast Times at Ridgemont High

 

Old School

 

 

 





Chapter 6  It Takes All Types

Tuesday Michael and I got together for lunch and I asked if he’d decided where to take Stephanie on their date Thursday.  He smiled and said, “Yeah, I thought about it.  One option would be to go to a movie.  It’s a safe, no-brainer date but a little conventional.  Another option would be to go out to eat but if we did that, I’d want it to be a more interesting restaurant.  So, I came up with going to Typhoon, that Thai restaurant.  It’s a fun place to eat, the food is great, it’s more upscale and there’re a lot of little bars and shops in the area so after we eat we have options to walk around and stop in for drinks or desert.  I called Stephanie this morning and when I mentioned Typhoon she went crazy.  She’s heard about it and is dying to go so we’re going to meet there around 6pm.  You know something I like about Stephanie is the enthusiasm she has.  Some girls would respond in a monotone and say, Yeah, fine…What time?  When I told Stephanie I wanted to take her to Typhoon, the enthusiasm in her voice was off the chart.  Really!!!!!  I’ve wanted to go there!!!!  How did you know!!!!  I can’t wait…What time do you want to meet?  You’ve got to love that kind of enthusiasm and appreciation for just a simple invitation.”

 

“And why shouldn’t a girl express enthusiasm?  You went to the effort to plan the evening, you call her and invite her out to a nice restaurant and you’re going to buy her dinner.  Any normal person should be enthusiastic and appreciative…”

 

 Michael continued my thought, “…Exactly, when someone treats you to a nice evening, it’s normal to express your gratitude.  Yet, there’re so many women who get taken out to dinner and a show and barely acknowledge it.  I guess in their world they’re entitled to the royal treatment.  Anyway, I like Stephanie’s enthusiasm.”

 

“Hey Michael, I thought of something I wanted to ask you about.  In the spring of my senior year of high school my speech class went to a tournament which was attended by all the high schools in the district.  I met a girl, Vicky Wooding, at the tournament.  I fell for her looks and personality instantly.  She lived about 45 minutes away from me, which was tough being in high school, but that summer I started dating her and we were going steady.  I did a lot of driving but we dated all summer and I was totally in love.  She was young and only a freshman when we met so when September came she started her sophomore year in high school and I went off to college about 1 ½ hours away from where I lived.  Within days of her being back in school I was getting the signs of impending doom.  She was distant on the phone and she was taking a day or two to answer an email.  She cancelled our date for that first Friday after her classes began and I knew what had happened.  She let me down easy with a polite Dear John email.  I was broken hearted.  What a way to begin college.”

 

“Anyway, I wrote a tearful email telling her how much I cared for her, yadda, yadda, yadda and that was that.  So now it’s like 5 or 6 weeks later and out of the blue I get an email from Vicky.  Hi, how’ve you been?  I’ve been thinking about you and I miss you.  Please call me.  So I immediately call her…like the puppy dog I am.  She’s sweet and seductive on the phone repeating how much she misses me and wants me to come to see her on Friday so we can go out.  Of course, I say yes and run back to tell my buddies the great news that the love of my life wants me back.”

“My plan was to leave college in time to arrive at her house at 6pm for our date and then after our date I’d drive home to spend the weekend with my parents.  I arrive to her house, knock on the door and no answer…huh?  I knock some more and still no answer.  So I’m standing on the porch wondering what to do and I see a car down the block which looks out of place.  The lights go on and it drives off.  I don’t think anything of it at the time.  I know her family very well and they all like me so now I try the door and it’s unlocked so I open it and call out to see if anyone is home.  Then her mother comes into the front room and is surprised to see me.  I come in and explain I have a date with Vicky at 6pm.  She says she doesn’t know anything about the date and doesn’t know where Vicky is.  Then suddenly Vicky walks in the door and the three of us are standing there.  I’m greeting Vicky and start to ask what she wants to do that evening.  She abruptly asks me to come outside because she wants to talk to me.  I assume she just wanted some privacy from her mom to tell me what we were going to do that night so I was completely unprepared for what happened next.  Vicky got right to the point and bluntly said, Listen, I don’t want to go out with you.  I was speechless and could barely get the words out, Why not?  She was completely cold and without emotion.  She said, Because I don’t like you.  That hit me like a knife through the heart.  I was barely able to say, Oh, as I turned to walk to my car.  I was too shocked and embarrassed to ask any questions so I just drove off.”

 

“To this day I have no idea what in the world that was all about.  I’d always doted on her and she’d broken up with me so it couldn’t have been revenge because I never did her wrong.  The way she acted that day was completely out of character.  She was always very nice to people.  We’re in love during the summer, then she breaks up with me, then she misses me and then she doesn’t like me.  Why in the world did Vicky Wooding do that?”

 

“You don’t know what she was up to?”  Michael studied my eyes for the answer.  “Vicky was using you to make some other guy jealous.  You were perfectly cast in the role.  I’m guessing she was one of the many young, pretty sophomores to arrive at the high school that fall.  She fell for one of the older, dreamy, popular senior boys who flirted with her.  They dated for a few weeks and then he probably started flirting with one of the other pretty, young sophomore girls and was throwing Vicky aside for a new conquest but she took action.  Like I said, you were perfect.  She told him…no…she let one of her friends tell her boyfriend that Vicky’s college boyfriend was coming to take her out this Friday.  The boyfriend reacted exactly as men do when faced with losing a girl to another man.  He got jealous and they made up.  Vicky got what she wanted and her plan worked perfectly…Now all she had to do was deal with you when you showed up.  By the way, using a guy from out of town was particularly genius on her part.”

 

“Oh…my…God!!!  That’s exactly what happened.  That explains every detail.  As I drove home that night I guessed Vicky was in the car that had been parked up the street.  She had her house staked out.  She was hoping I’d give up and leave when no one was home.  So when I didn’t leave she had to deal with me but she took care of that quickly.  Wow, little Vicky Wooding was quite the calculating girl.”  Michael patted me on the shoulder and congratulated me on being the perfect pawn.  We had a good laugh as we ate but then I suddenly put down my sandwich and said, “Sue Horton.”  Michael looked at me.  “Sue Horton did the same thing to me in the 9th grade.  Sue was a year younger than me and we’d always been friends.  She was very pretty but not in the popular group.  She was kind of a free spirit, saw through the popularity thing and had a reputation for being a little wild.  We’d always talk and joke at school but then suddenly one week she was being extra friendly and suggesting we should get together.  This continued all week, she made a point of finding me in the halls and she became more and more flirtatious.  Then she suggested we should meet at the movies Friday night.  Every Friday all the Jr. High kids would go to the local theatre.  Boys would meet up with their girlfriends, sit together and make out.  So now Sue was making a date with me to meet at the theater on Friday to sit together.  I was on cloud nine.  I had a date with Sue to make out at the movies.  Friday came at school and Sue made a point of confirming we’d meet up that night.”

 

“I don’t remember why but somehow I was late getting to the theater and the movie had already begun.  It was dark.  There was no chance to find Sue but I saw a friend near the back and sat with him.  I figured I’d wait until the intermission and find Sue.  This was a funky neighborhood theater which catered to the kids so they’d have an intermission to sell more snacks.  Intermission comes and a bunch of kids come walking up the isle saying, Oh, wow.  Did you see Mike Tawney.  He was totally making out with Sue Horton.  I couldn’t believe my ears.  I saw a big group further down where those kids had come from.  I went down to see for myself and there was Mike Tawney sitting in the middle of the group being the king stud with his arm around Sue.  She was talking and laughing with the others and enjoying her sudden social promotion.  Mike Tawney was one of the most popular kids in the 8th grade.  Sue is talking and enjoying being with Mike and then she saw me.  It was like in a movie where all the other people and their voices become a blur so it’s only Sue and I.  She looked at me, her smile dropped and with all sincerity said, Sorry Robert.  I nodded and went back to where I was sitting.”

 

“To this day I thought if I’d just gotten to the theater on time that Friday it would’ve been me instead of Mike Tawney.  Now I know she’d been trying to get Mike Tawney all along.  Sue was not…how to say this…socially acceptable for Mike to date.  He was in the A group and she wasn’t.  He probably had the hots for her and was flirting with her but wouldn’t pull the trigger and date her.  So Sue decided to force the issue and let him know he had until Friday to jump on board the Sue Horton Express because she had a date lined up and wasn’t going to wait.  At least Sue had the guts to look me in the eyes and apologize.”

 

Michael shook his head and said, “That’s amazing.  You should hire yourself out to girls who need to make their boyfriends jealous.  You must be a natural at it.  Your stories emphasize, though, how important it is to learn to understand people and the things they do.  It takes experience and also you need to hone your skills at reading people to understand their true motivations.  If you take what people say literally, you can miss their true intentions.”  Michael took another bite of his sandwich and then continued, “Let me tell you a story.  During college they were shooting a movie on location near campus.  You know how much I love movies so I thought it’d be fun to try out as an extra.  It was a sports movie and they needed background people running on the track.  Somehow I got interviewed by the director.  I sat down across from him and he asked me a few questions about sports I played.  The way I answered was revealing about my insecurities and he made a very blunt suggestion to me.  I was stunned that in 30 seconds this guy gave me some advice which was right on the mark.  He knew me better in 30 seconds than I knew myself.”

 

“I was in awe of that director and his ability to psychoanalyze people.  I realized how important and useful that skill would be for everything from dating to business to family relations.  So ever since then I’ve studied psychology 101 but also things like body language, eye contact and eye movements, facial expressions and even astrology.  It’s fun to pick up the clues and learn more about people than what they tell you.  The key, though, is to understand you’re doing this to understand people and not to judge them.  As soon as you become judgmental you lose your objectivity.  We want to understand people so our relationships are better…So we can better choose who we want relationships with…friends and lovers.”

“My mom taught me something about understanding people.”  Now I put down my sandwich and remembered her exact words.  “She said, Intelligent people talk about concepts.  Average people talk about events.  And, stupid people talk about other people.  I’ve found it to always be true.  Of course, here we are talking about people.  Well…I’m selling us short.  We’re talking about concepts and using people as examples.”

 

“That’s a great saying.  One person will talk about how he hates the quarterback because he’s so arrogant.  Another person will talk about the amazing pass he threw for a touchdown.  Another person will talk about how well the quarterback read the defense and used the play action fake to set up the touchdown pass.  The topics are people, events and concepts.  Maybe that’s where the saying came from, Small minded people talk about other people.”

 

“I want to improve my skills at reading people…Especially girls.  There’re all types of girls and all kinds of games.  It takes a lot of experience to recognize who she is and what game she’s playing.  Look at me.  It took 10 years for me to figure out a simple Jr. High jealousy game.”

 

We laughed and then Michael said, “Actually, remember when we talked about the importance of being a good listener when you start dating a woman?  Now you know the real reason.  By being a good listener you learn the clues to who she is.  Just like your mom said.  Is she talking about concepts, events or other people?”

 

Beautiful Women

 

“I love beautiful women…Who doesn’t?  And dating a beautiful woman is great.  She’s on your arm and you feel like a million bucks.  Touching her, kissing her, dancing with her and making love to her…It’s all great.  But marriage is when all the bad qualities come to light…All of those bad qualities which were evident before but were ignored because the package was so beautifully wrapped.  Marriage is when it becomes hard to ignore a woman who’s selfish, stupid, nagging and unappreciative.  Some men’s dream in life is to have a trophy wife.  They get it…And they pay for it.”

 

“That’s not to say all beautiful women are horrible, selfish people who make the men around them miserable.  But beauty is a real test for a woman because they get so many things without having to put out any effort.  When you don’t have to work for anything, you don’t develop as a person and this happens to beautiful women and handsome men.  They grow up having everything handed to them.  They don’t learn the values and ethics necessary to develop as a person.  They have nothing inside to give.  I suppose when a girl grows up beautiful the entire world is spoiling her and that’s unavoidable.  The real question is if her parents are holding her to higher standards with her studies, her behavior and how she treats other people.  I guess if you’re dating a beautiful woman and want to know what’s inside her, you should spend time with her family…actually, that’s true of anyone.”
