“Whenever I see a beautiful woman who’s divorced, I ask myself, Why is she divorced?  Look, if a beautiful girl is 18 to 25 and single, never married…there’s nothing odd about that.  But when I see a really attractive and fit 35 year old woman who’s divorced, then I’m curious.  Why would a man divorce this woman?  Doesn’t she like sex?  Is she a nagging bitch?  Is she impossible to live with?  Maybe the ex-husband was the problem, but you’d better find out before jumping into a relationship with her.”

 

Why Are We Interested In a Woman?

 

“Physical attraction has got to be the number one reason why a man is attracted to a woman.  It’s certainly the first impression.  It’s the reason we want to know a girl.  For some men beauty is the only criteria for choosing a girlfriend.  For other men it’s beauty and personality.  Beauty is in the eye of the beholder though so different men define beauty differently.  Some men like tall, for some it’s thin.  Some men fixate on legs and others on breasts.  Some men like an athletic build, some like the exotic look.  Some men love brunettes…and more than a few men love blondes.”

 

“You know the old saying blondes have more fun…That’s not a myth.  Let me tell you a great story.  I work with a girl and this subject came up.  She told me about when she was in college and dyed her hair blonde.  She said she’d never have predicted the difference it made.  She’s kind of an average looking girl and she described walking through campus and going to class with her natural brunette hair color.  She said occasionally men would smile or say hi but she never got a lot of attention.  Then over the summer break she dyed her hair blonde.  She said she did it just for fun and for a change.  She went back to school for the new school year and in her wildest dreams she wouldn’t expect the change.  She said she’d be walking down the street and guys were hanging out the window of a passing car whistling and calling out to her.  She said at first she was looking around to see who they were whistling at.  It was the same thing walking to class as men would be smiling and hitting on her.  And it wasn’t her imagination.  She said her friends were also amazed at all the attention she was getting.  The most dramatic example she told me was one night out at a campus pub a guy came over to talk to her.  She knew the guy because they were in a class together before when she was a brunette, so she’s talking to him and brings up the class and he looks lost.  Finally, she realized the guy didn’t remember her at all and had just come over to hit on her because he thought she was such a hot chick.  But before, when she was a brunette, she’d been invisible to the guy.”

 

“It’s true some men have such a specific set of criteria for what they deem to be beautiful that any girl outside of their template is of zero interest to them.  It sounds like a bad trait but, at the same time, it’s human nature and no one should apologize for wanting what they want.”

 

“So, it’s safe to say physical beauty is a big part of why we choose a girl but there’re other reasons.  I know I find a girl’s energetic spirit to be very attractive.  I know lots of girls who get prettier the more you get to know them.  It sounds corny but when a girl is beautiful on the inside, she starts to wear her personality on her face.  You begin to see the charm in her smile and the good heart in her eyes.”

 

“You know, I can think of another reason why a man might choose a woman.  It has to do with peer pressure or group think.  Often men look for a woman who fits the mold.  If she fits in with his friends, it’s a plus.  Choosing the same ethnicity is the easy choice.  Certainly in high school and middle school men are affected by peer pressure and want to date a girl who’s socially acceptable.  A boy’s friends may critique a girl he’s dating because she isn’t pretty enough or isn’t thin enough…Yet the boy finds her attractive and likes her.  Years later, that boy will look back and have regrets if he gave in to the pressure from his friends.”

 

“I have a good story along those lines.  I have a friend, Gary, who’s older than us.  He got married out of college, was married for 7 years and then divorced.  He talks about his ex-wife in glowing terms that she’s a great person in every way.  They were great living together and got along well…But, they weren’t meant to be married.  They had different goals in life and they ended up separating and divorcing on friendly terms.  He said as he looked back to when they met and dated in college he came to some conclusions about why he wanted to be with her.  Carol was very athletic and popular among his friends and the groups they were in.  Many men had wanted to date her but she never showed an interest to have a boyfriend or a serious relationship.  He said that for whatever reason she started showing an interest in him and he was totally seduced by the thought of having the girl all the guys wanted.”

“But, winning Carol was a long process.  She was still very elusive about having a serious relationship.  In fact, at one point Gary gave up on her and started dating another girl.  The second girl, Angie, was attractive, sensual and had a body like a Playboy model.  She was totally in love with him, treated him like he was a king and they had a sex life which was off the charts.  But, Angie was an outsider to his social world.  In addition, Angie wasn’t a challenge.  She fell in love with him when they met and she never played games.  He dated Angie for about 5 months in the spring and over the summer.  During that time Carol was aware he’d moved on to dating someone else.  Now Gary was becoming a challenge for Carol and that next fall back at college she started making herself available.  Gary gave in.  Looking back, he said his ego was really affected by how Carol was considered the catch.   Being her boyfriend was like he’d just won the lotto.  I remember him saying to be careful what you wish for.”

 

“Gary told me the worst part was though Carol was a very nice person and quite pretty he didn’t have a strong sexual attraction for her…other than the emotion of wanting what he couldn’t have.  He said on the other hand his sensual and sexual attraction for Angie was off the charts.  They had a sexual chemistry which was explosive.  She aroused him just walking into the room…not so with Carol.  Amazing how susceptible men are to the challenge of winning what they can’t have and winning the trophy all the men want.”

 

“It brings up the point to have a long talk with yourself when you find yourself falling in love.  You have to ask yourself, Why do I love this girl?  Do I love her looks?  Do I love her personality?  Do I love her values and how she lives her life?  Or, do I love her image?  Do I want her so badly because she’s a challenge?  Do I want her because winning her will give my self-esteem a needed boost?  Am I ignoring obvious red flags about her but they don’t matter because I just HAVE to have her.  These are all questions that if you won’t ask yourself, you’d better hope you have a true friend or your father or your brother to ask them of you.”

 

“Wow!  That’s an interesting story.  I wish there was a book which compiled stories like that so you could learn from other’s experiences.  Of course, there’re a lot of movies which tell those stories.  Have you ever seen The Wedding Singer?  That movie is funny and cleaver.  It’s about choosing mates who perhaps aren’t the perfect match.  It’s a good movie to watch on this subject.”

 

What Phase Is She In?

 

“Sometimes two people meet and everything is right except they’re out of phase with each other.  One of them is ready to settle down in a committed relationship and the other one isn’t ready.  One is ready for the necessary compromises which come with serious relationships and the other cannot or will not make those compromises.”

 

“People evolve from being more selfish to being less selfish as they grow up and mature.  Children and teens focus on themselves.  As they mature into adults it’s natural for them to think more about the needs of others until ultimately they have their own children and then being selfish is no longer an option.  They’re devoted to their children.  This evolution from selfish to selfless can define moving from one phase of your life to another.  For some it can occur by the time they’re 18 and for others it takes much longer.”

 

“Also, priorities can define phases of one’s life.  Say a 24 year old man’s number 1 priority at this point in his life is career and accumulating wealth.  His number 2 priority is recreation and sports as he enjoys climbing and hiking with his friends.  Way down at number 3 is to have a girlfriend.  Now if he meets a girl who’s an investment advisor and an avid rock climber, then there won’t be a conflict for him.  His priorities won’t be challenged by this girl because she’ll relate to and contribute her knowledge to his number one priority.  They’ll spend their free time together doing the recreation they both enjoy so his number two priority won’t be challenged.  And so, having a girlfriend will be in harmony with the other important things in his life.”

 

“Now let’s suppose this same guy meets a girl who’s beautiful and sexy.  He loves her personality and their sexual chemistry is as good as it gets.  They love each other and seem perfect for each other.  But her number 1 priority in life is to settle down and have kids.  When they first meet and start dating, everything is fine.  Their relationship flourishes.  But after a year it becomes evident they’re in different phases of their lives.  The man’s priorities are being fulfilled perfectly.  His job and career are going well.  His life style is modest so he’s able to set aside a lot of his pay to invest.  He spends almost every weekend with his friends in the outdoors and he has a loving girlfriend who’s there for him.  He has it all.  Sometimes he thinks about getting married and having kids but that’s at least 5 years off in the future.”

“On the other hand the woman’s priorities aren’t being fulfilled.  Her number one priority is to get married and have kids.  Everything else is secondary to that.  She has a good job but expects to give it up to be home and raise her children.  She likes sports and being outdoors but that’s secondary to her dream of having a house and home.  She was very happy with her boyfriend as they moved from dating to committed relationship but now she’s ready to move to the next level and she’s coming to realize that she and what she wants are a distant 3rd on her boyfriend’s list of priorities.  These two are in different phases of their lives and conflict is inevitable.  He’s exactly where he wants to be but now he’s getting pressure to give up his top priorities in favor of what she wants.  Getting married and then supporting a wife at home with kids will put a lot of strain on his top priorities of saving/investing and spending weekends with his friends out climbing.”

 

“What about her?  She doesn’t have what she wants and so what’s she to do?  She can patiently wait for 5 years or more for her boyfriend to come around but is that wise?  Maybe he’s a Peter Pan and will never change.  She can pressure her boyfriend to give her what she wants but how will that affect him and will the relationship survive this pressure and conflict?”

 

“The important thing to understand is both the man and woman in this example are good people with reasonable aspirations and priorities in life.  There’s no crime in pursuing what you want in life and that’s what both are doing.  The problem is they’re in different phases of their lives and this is causing problems.  So learning what a person wants in life is very important.  What people are focusing on in their life changes and evolves as they move to the next phase in their life and affects all of their relationships.”

 

“It’s very important to recognize what phase of life a person is in if you’re entering a relationship with them.  It’s key to understanding what their goals are and if those goals are in harmony with your goals.”

 

“Sometimes people go through mini phases.  People experience a bad breakup and aren’t interested in a serious relationship for a period of time.  People have financial setbacks and need to focus all their energies to working and saving for a period of time.  People go through serious health issues which take up most of their time to heal and recover.  Taking time out to heal emotionally, financially or physically is normal and healthy.  If you meet a person in one of these mini phases it’s important you recognize having a serious relationship may not be as important to them as it is to you.”

 

Selfish Girls

 

Michael and I got together Friday after work for a beer and something to eat.  I wanted to hear about his date the night before with Stephanie.

 

“Oh yeah, we had a great time.  She gave me a hug when we met at the restaurant and that set the tone for the whole evening.  She loved her meal and insisted on sharing back and forth so we could try different foods.  She was so funny with exaggerated facial expressions of pleasure every time she put something new in her mouth…It was a turn on for me, actually.  Then after we ate we walked around in the area.  We browsed in some of the shops.  We went into one shop which had perfumes and lotions and I didn’t notice but she bought a little scented soap and had them wrap it.  She gave it to me as we walked out.  She was so happy to give me that little gift.  I thanked her as I opened it and smelled it, then she wanted to smell it and I gave her a hug and a little kiss.  She melted.  She’s such a happy spirit.  We walked around some more and ended up at a desert shop.  We shared a warm brownie with ice cream and sat there talking until it was like 10pm.  I looked at the clock and she commented that I had to be up early for work and said it was time for me to get home.  On the way back to our cars I asked if she’d like to go out again for lunch on Sunday so we’re meeting at a nice little restaurant which is close to where she lives.  She gave me a big hug at her car and thanked me for such a wonderful evening.”

 

“You know, listening to you tell about your date with Stephanie…I can’t help but reflect on this girl I dated who was the complete opposite of her.  This girl was never happy and nothing was ever good enough for her.  If I took her to the movie, then the movie was silly or stupid.  When I suggested a restaurant she’d roll her eyes like who’d want to go there.  She’d keep picking away at my choice until I’d finally give in and we’d go to her restaurant…And then she’d be happy with the food.  As I look back, only the movies or restaurant or activity which she chose was good.  Anything I chose or any idea I had was bad…and she’d make sure it was bad with her attitude.”

 

“What really kills me is how stupid I was to put up with it.  I think it goes back to how I was raised.  I grew up being taught to be a gentleman and please a woman…Which is a good value to hold.  But stubbornly adhering to chivalry no matter what can cloud one’s vision to the point you don’t see you’re dealing with a woman you shouldn’t be wasting your time on.  And that’s what I was doing.  I was so totally focused on pleasing the girl that when she wasn’t satisfied, I looked at myself.  I felt I’d failed to be good enough for her.  I dated her for over 3 months and I shouldn’t have gone past the first date.  All the signs of what was to come were evident from the get-go.  So how do you categorize that girl?  Is she selfish or just negative or enjoys manipulating people with her negativity…?”

 

“It’s manipulation as she plays the poor girl who needs so much to make her happy, but ultimately she’s a selfish girl.  Whatever her method, she wants more things and more attention and wants others to give them to her.  She’s so selfish that in addition to wanting what she gets, she doesn’t want you to get anything…including to experience any feeling of success like choosing a good movie.  She’s selfish to the point of being sick.  Let me guess, when you broke it off she didn’t take it well and tried to use her sexual hold on you to pull you back.”

 

“That’s right.  It was really funny.  I met another girl, Vicky, who was a very average looking girl but really sweet and happy to be taken out.  In a way, I picked a girl like her on purpose so I could just relax on a date.  Anyway, I didn’t call selfish girl for about a week so she called to get taken out and I said I was busy.  One thing led to another on the phone and I said it was best if we no longer go out.  An hour later she shows up at my door.  First she tried to seduce me but I held strong.  Then she told me if we were going to break up it should be face to face like adults.  So I agreed and repeated we should break up.  So then she started telling me all of my shortcomings and all of my problems.  Belittling had been how she manipulated me before so I’m guessing she thought it’d work again.  At that point I was so numb to all of her games I shocked her by agreeing.  I told her she was right, I had a lot of problems and she should feel lucky to be rid of me.  At that I told her I had to get ready to go shopping and got her out the door.”

 

“That’s a funny story.  It was like she showed up at your door with a rolodex of manipulative ploys.  When one didn’t work, she flipped to the next one.  So what happened with Vicky, the average looking girl?”

 

“Vicky was a sweetheart and we dated for a while but it never developed into a strong relationship.  This is tough to admit but the selfish girl had an incredible sexual hold on me.  She had my number and knew how to push my erotic buttons.  She used that power to manipulate me.  Anyway, it took me a while to get the sexual desire I had for her out of my system.  Unfortunately, my feelings for Vicky were affected by that.  I really liked her but I wasn’t feeling that hot desire for her I should have.”

 

Michael thought about my story for a moment and then said, “Selfish girls really use up people.  They drain people of everything.  It’s not just things they want.  They drain people of their time, their money, their energy, their emotions and their soul if you let them.  Look at your selfish girl.  She was happiest when she could drain you of any feeling of accomplishment.”

 

“Sometimes it’s blatantly obvious a girl is selfish.  You can tell from the look on their face to the tone in their voice to their every action.  In a way, they’re honest.  They’re selfish and proud of it.  I suppose we can respect their honesty.”

 

“It’s the selfish girls who hide it well who’re dangerous.  Your selfish girl is one type.  They want to be entertained.  They want someone to make their life wonderful.  They want to be taken to a movie and if the movie isn’t good enough then they’ll declare they’re bored and want to be taken to dinner.  Their method is to blame the guy for everything lacking in their life and make that guy responsible for everything…Just like your selfish girl did.”

 

“One way a selfish girl can be difficult for us to identify as a selfish girl is when money and things aren’t the most important things she’s after.  She wants your time and energy and emotions.  She wants to take those things and give nothing in return.  These women have to be emotionally carried through life.  They’re constantly draining everyone around them and especially a boyfriend or husband.  No man is a rock of never ending energy.  Even the best of us has a down day or needs an emotional boost occasionally but when a man comes to an emotionally selfish girl for a lift it isn’t there.  In fact, more often than not she’ll be angry at the man.  How dare you burden me with these problems?  Don’t ever bring me down like that again.”  With an emotionally selfish girl there’s always a net energy drain.  If a girl can’t or won’t give you a compliment or a lift when you need it then run for your life.”

 

“Another red flag to identify selfish girls are the ones who’re more interested in the event than who’s taking them out.  This girl is always weighing options and taking the best offer.  The opposite of this is when a girl is happy with anything you do together…just as long as she’s with you.”

 

“One time I was on vacation in Sun Valley.  I was going up the quad chairlift with three women.  I could tell they all lived there in life styles of the rich and famous.  Their conversation was about the career moves they’d all made in order to be living in the mountains at a posh resort and have it all.  Basically, the plan they outlined was to find and marry an upwardly mobile man who promised to attain wealth.  Then to have children by this man which was very important because that would secure their legal position.  Then deprive the husband of sex and in general make his life a living hell.  At that point, the husband would wander into the arms of his secretary or some other young and beautiful woman.  Now that the marriage was a sham the woman would either, sue for divorce based on the husband’s infidelity and secure a huge settlement, or there was a Plan B.  The Plan B was to get the husband on a vacation to Sun Valley.  Once there, the wife would suddenly be warm, sexy and affectionate.  She’d convince the husband it was the mountains and clean air.  Then she’d reason wouldn’t it be wonderful to build a house here in the mountains and then the kids and I can live here and you can visit on the weekends.  This appeals to the husband because he can now be alone in L.A. with his secretary and visit the wife and kids once a month in the mountains.  He’ll have to build a multi-million dollar mountain house…which appeals to his ego…and put his wife on a $25,000 a month budget but all that’s worth it to get her out of his hair and have some peace in his life.  She, of course, now has the life style she wants, has maids and nannies and can be the rich bitch she always wanted to be.”

“The conversation went on as I sat there in disbelief these women would be saying this in front of me.  I sat quietly taking it all in.  They went on to talk about this friend and that friend and how much they admired the ones who’d gotten the best settlement or the best living allowance.  Honestly, I think they enjoyed saying these things in front of a man.  It was like they were thumbing their nose at all men saying, That’s right.  We’re the smart ones.  You men are so stupid and we can manipulate you and use you any way we want.”

 

“Now those are selfish women…and ruthless.  Do you think their husbands have any idea how calculating they were from the beginning?”  Michael was thinking about his answer when I saw a nice table open up outside on the sidewalk.  “Oh, Michael let’s grab that table outside and get something to eat.”  We made a dash to secure a nice place on the sidewalk and ordered some chips and salsa.

 

“I don’t know.  I suppose some of them do and some of them don’t.  I’m imagining these highly successful lawyers and businessmen getting taken to the cleaners by their college sweethearts.  That must be a tough pill to swallow.”

 

At that point, we sat back to enjoy a beer and the chips.  We both started hearing the conversation next to us and gave each other a look.  We started listening while acting as if we weren’t.  The conversation went on for about 10 minutes.  There were 4 girls all in their early 20s.  From the conversation we could tell they were friends from college.  One girl, the most vocal, was going on about her fiancé.  The others were mostly listening with occasional comments.  One or two of them seemed uncomfortable or disapproving of the vocal one, but they remained polite nodding and agreeing with her.

 

Michael and I occasionally looked at each other in horror as we listened.  The entirety of the conversation was filled with subtleties and nuance and code words to soften what she was saying but the Cliff Notes version of the conversation was this.  This one girl was getting married in a month to her college boyfriend.  At some point after graduation they’d gone through a rough patch and she caught him cheating on her.  My take on this was the guy wanted out of the relationship and was making a trial run at freedom.  She used that indiscretion as leverage.  She used guilt, guilt and more guilt.  She made it sound like she had this poor guy by the balls to the point he had to ask permission to see his friends.  She bragged to her friends how she’d used his guilt to get him to propose to her.  From the entire conversation it was obvious this woman intended to use the guilt of that affair against him for the rest of his life.  And it wasn’t just Michael and I who heard it that way.  We could tell some of her friends had a hard time listening to her.  It was like they were thinking; This is not what you base a marriage on.  But this girl was very proud of how she had her fiancé by the balls.

 

That girl was selfish to the point of self-destructive.  I don’t see how she could ever be happy unless seeing this man pay for his crime was her definition of marital bliss.  I’m not sure how much more selfish a person can be than this girl who wanted to control and own this poor guy’s life.  Michael and I speculated what we’d do if the fiancé showed up to the outdoor restaurant while we were there.  Would we pull him aside?  Would we slip him our phone number?  What’s the moral duty in that situation?

 

The girls finished their meals and eventually left.  Michael and I were left there talking in low tones.  We were both horrified by what we’d just heard.  We’d ordered some Mexican food and were enjoying our meals when Michael asked, “Have you seen Spanglish?  I didn’t see it in the theater when it came out because I thought it was just another comedy.  Spanglish is one of the best movies I’ve ever seen.  It’s about a nice, successful guy who’s married to the perfect Southern Cal spoiled, selfish, entitled bitch.  They bring a Mexican maid/nanny into the house and she serves as a brilliant contrast of cultures and values.  After you see Spanglish, you’ll want to marry a Latina.”

 

“Sometimes…actually, most of the time selfish women try to mask what they really are by being fashionably liberal and concerned about the poor and less fortunate.  I should explain that being concerned about those less fortunate is admirable and we should all aspire to it.  I truly admire those who give their time and money to people in need.  I have some friends who live modestly and are totally involved in charities.  They do community work and they travel to foreign countries to volunteer.  I’m not talking about those people…”

 

“…The women I’m talking about are the latte liberals.  These are the liberal women driving their BMWs, milking their husbands so they can live high while nannies and maids take care of the kids and the house.  They’re selfish bitches who treat the baristas at Starbucks like shit…but are liberal because it’s socially correct.  That assuages their guilt and lets them claim to be a good person.  They prove their concern every election by voting for the socialist candidate…Well, except one election.  When they vote for husband, they vote capitalist.”

 

“A man should look for a woman to marry who’s truly compassionate and charitable…Not someone who just says they are.  A woman who genuinely cares about others will be her husband’s partner and will take care of her family.”

 

The Games Girls Play

 

“You know, as long as we’re talking about types of girls.  I have a story to tell.  This is the story of Diane.  She’s pretty in a cute way with her wire rim glasses, beautiful hair and very nice figure.  She’s smart and funny with a charming laugh and has a nice personality…but it can turn confrontational with no warning.  I met her at a yard sale.  She was skeptical to give me her number but she finally did and we went out to dinner once or twice and we also went for hikes in the mountains.  We’d been seeing each other for about 2 weeks and she came over to my apartment.  We were talking and then she simply asked if I wanted to go to bed.  We went to bed and had amazing sex.  She was aroused and screaming and had this incredible orgasm.  Afterwards she went on about how amazing it was for her and how well we moved together…which I agreed with completely.  Okay, so you’re wondering, where’s the problem?”

 

“So far I’ve told you the good side to the story.  All along the way, little things started coming up and this seemed to increase as we became closer and more intimate.  I noticed she was quick to find fault with me and it seemed like she was trying to find flaws.  When we first met she was reluctant to get involved with me and it was like she didn’t want to believe I was simply a nice guy who found her attractive.  One time when we were driving to the mountains to go for a hike, she gave me one of those tests to reveal your true personality.  She told me to imagine I was a tree and what kind of tree would I be.  When I answered she was silent.  I assumed I’d failed the test and ask her if I’d failed.  She said, No, you passed…Actually your answer is about the best it could be.  But she was a little angry when she said it.  It was like she was hoping my answer would prove I was a selfish bastard and she was pissed when it didn’t.  There were some odd things too about when we slept together.  At one point, I asked her if she wanted to be on top.  She said, Oh come on, you don’t care if that’s what I want.  That’s what you want.  Also, at one point she made a comment I can only interpret as she didn’t like the idea I was enjoying having sex with her…That all of this should be for her benefit and I was only there to give her what she needed.  It was like she didn’t like the idea of a man having pleasure from her body.”

 

“But the thing which got worse and worse was during our conversations she’d snap at me about nothing.  She’d be talking about something and I’d be enthusiastically listening and then say for example, Oh yes, the same thing happened to me when I was in college when I was…  She’d get angry and snap at me telling me I was so selfish and it was always about me and college.  I’d tell her I was sorry but I’d just spent 4 years in college so a lot of my experiences came from there.  So then I’d be afraid to speak and nod my head and she’d find fault I wasn’t talking.  Finally, one evening we were walking in the park having a nice time and she snapped at me.  I stopped walking, took a deep breath and waited for her to realize I was no longer walking beside her.  When she turned around I said, You know what Diane, I’m just not that bad of a person.  I’m going home.  I was calm.  I wasn’t angry.  I turned around and walked to my car and drove to a good friend’s house so she couldn’t find me at home.”

 

“I was just finishing telling my friend the whole story when she drove up.  She knew where my friend lived.  She came to the door crying and begging me to take her back.  She told me she knew she had this problem and I was so good for her and she needed me and needed my help.  I told her no, I didn’t want to be with someone who found me so irritating.  A few days later she came to my apartment to talk but we never dated again.  A year later I ran into her at a copy center.  I sincerely said hi and asked how she was.  She could barely talk to me like she was devastated seeing me and not over me.  I think about Diane a lot.  I adored her good side.  I remember one thing about her.  She talked constantly about her father.  Her parents had divorced so her father had, in effect, abandoned her.  She’d talk about how much she loved her father and he was the best father in the world and then 10 minutes later she was talking about what a bastard he was because he didn’t care about her and left her.  I think Diane is a girl who has a love/hate relationship with men.  She loved intimacy and loved dating and having a boyfriend but at the same time she wanted to find fault with me and hate me.  She loved sex but didn’t want me to enjoy it…She didn’t want a man using her for his pleasure.  Diane has these inner conflicts and I don’t know if she’ll ever resolve them.  She’s a woman who really, really, really wants a man in her life but for some reason there’s another part of her which needs to hate all men because they’ll all abandon her like her father did.  I suppose I did…but not without cause.”

 

Michael enjoyed that story and then brought up a game some girls play.  “Have you ever been with a girl who’s constantly testing you?  These girls will challenge who you are.  They’ll enjoy putting you into situations to see how you handle it.  For example, a girl may comment on your car being dirty and ask; Can’t you afford to have it washed?  A girl will say she’d like some wine with dinner and then hand the wine list to you and ask you to choose one for her.  After you choose she may ask if you know anything about wine or if you chose by the price.  She’ll have a challenging tone in her voice when she asks these pointed questions.  If you hesitate choosing off the menu or deciding on which movie to see, she’ll comment, Having trouble deciding?”

 

“Now if you ask her why she’s constantly testing you, she’ll claim complete innocence and it’s only friendly kidding…and then accuse you of being insecure and having thin skin.  However, there’s light hearted kidding with a friend and then there’s challenging a person with the intention of testing them and doing it with an agenda.  You can tell the difference in their intention if you pay attention.  One clue is when they can dish it out but don’t take it very well.  They don’t like it when the friendly kidding is directed at them.”

 

“But the real question is why are they doing this?  Is this a first date and a girl is honestly testing a guy to see if he’s for real or is a phony?  Some girls do this.  Another possibility is when a girl is over you and she’s trying to end the relationship.  She’s consciously or subconsciously trying to create disharmony.  Sometimes a girl becomes disenchanted with a guy and these little indicators are like an early warning system for the man…as long as he’s listening.”

“Here’s one to watch out for.  A couple years ago I lived in a nice neighborhood and there was a divorced mom who lived across the street.  She was quite pretty with perfect hair and her body was perfectly thin.  She had all the appearances of an ex-trophy wife.  She had a 10 year old son who she drove 5 blocks to school every day in her perfect Range Rover.  All the other kids in the neighborhood walked to school as the area was upscale and safe.  Her house was perfect and looked as if it were a picture out of a magazine at all times.  It always looked as if it were freshly painted.  The landscaping was always perfect.  Every blade of grass was perfectly groomed and even in the fall there was never a fallen leaf to be found.  Her cement driveway was power washed yearly, as was the roof.  All of this perfection was done by a steady stream of workmen, handymen and landscapers who were there almost daily maintaining something.”

 

“As I observed this over the two years I lived across from her, I kept thinking some guy pays for all of that perfection.  I assumed the guy who paid for it all was the father of her son…her ex-husband.  That guy pays a lot of money each month to maintain the perfect life style to which she is accustomed.  I’m sure when he met her he thought she was a real catch and would make a perfect trophy wife.”

 

“Anyone who follows astrology would guess this neighbor of mine was a Virgo.  Virgos need everything to be clean and organized.  Everything must be in line and everything in its place.  There’s nothing wrong with being organized and clean but if it becomes obsessive then it can be difficult to live with.  If a man meets a girl who needs everything to always be perfect he should ask himself who’s going to pay for that perfection and is perfection that easy to live with?  Now that neighbor of mine may be a very nice person and she certainly is beautiful and she appeared to be a good mother but I wouldn’t want to be married to her.  I wouldn’t want to be trying to keep everything perfect for her.  I wouldn’t want to be paying for that perfection.  And I certainly wouldn’t want to be an ex-husband paying for a trophy wife but getting none of the privileges.”

 

“Here’s a story for you.  Most men have an image in their mind of their ideal woman.  They’ll have certain body types or looks they like.  Some men will favor outdoors, athletic girls while other men will favor fashion queens.  Well, here’s a news flash for the men out there…Women are no different and have an image of their ideal man.  Some women may dream of a professional, Wall Street type while others dream of a rough and strong cowboy.  Some women want their men to be soft and polite while others are attracted to aggressive, strong men.  It’s important when you’re one kind of guy not to chase after a woman who’s looking for another kind of guy.  She may look like the girl of your dreams but you aren’t the man of her dreams and you’ll be beating your head against the wall trying to get her attention.”

“I have this friend, Thor.  He’s one of the nicest people I’ve ever known.  He’s intelligent, thoughtful, funny and the kind of guy who makes a caring and devoted boyfriend.  He doesn’t drink or smoke.  He’s athletic and always a positive person to be around.  To most girls Thor would be a catch.  During college he worked nights in a restaurant.  I remember him telling me about a girl he worked with, Kathy, and described her as his dream girl.  And, I understand why.  She was quite attractive, was athletic and healthy, and a very nice person.  One glitch, however, was she had a boyfriend.  Thor often commented if she ever broke up with her boyfriend he was going to go after her.  In the meantime, Thor had established a good friendship with Kathy and hoped that would put him in a good position if she ever became available.”

 

“There was another glitch in Thor’s plan.  Kathy’s boyfriend was 6” 2”, 220 lbs., strong and fit and had rugged Hollywood good looks.  Now there’s nothing unusual about a girl finding this guy attractive.  But there was more about Kathy’s boyfriend.  He had a well-known reputation and was actually notorious for beating up girlfriends.  This was so well known he’d bring it up in a pre-emptive way when he started dating a girl.  He’d say in the past he’d had a problem but he’d sought help, yadda, yadda, yadda and the girl would melt at this big, strong guy’s sensitivity and vulnerability.  But, that’s not all.  There was more about this boyfriend.  One might ask how a guy known to beat up women could get a girlfriend…And he always had a beautiful girl on his arm…Well, Kathy’s boyfriend was also notorious, or at least it was rumored, he was…well endowed.  He was hung like a horse.  So, we have a big, strong and dangerous porno star with a never ending supply of beautiful women who’re willing to take a risk to be with him.”

 

“I told Thor based on what we knew about Kathy’s choice of a boyfriend I didn’t think he fit the profile of what she was looking for.  I told him when a girl goes for a big, strong, dangerous porno star with a never ending supply of women it says something about her deep desires.  She seems to want to live on the wild and dangerous side sexually and otherwise.  I told Thor I doubted he’d fulfill her fantasies.  Some girls go for bad boys and some girls go for nice guys.  He should pursue the girls who like nice guys.”

 

“Thor was certainly aware of all of this, but his position was it didn’t hurt anything to continue to foster his friendship with her.  If they did break up then he’d be a trusted friend she could find comfort with.  And, I agreed with him.  Being her friend certainly didn’t hurt him and actually helped him in the big picture.”

 

“And it came to pass…or should I say inevitably…Kathy’s boyfriend abused her and then moved on to the next girl who was certain this time it would be different because she’d be the one to change him.  And Thor was there to console Kathy and be her friend…Again, I agreed with his desire to be a good friend.  That was the type of person he was and one of the reasons I thought so highly of him.  But, his hope to be Kathy’s new boyfriend never happened.  When he subtly tried to suggest they move beyond friendship, she put him off with talk of needing to stay single for a while after the bad experience, etc.   She loved Thor as a friend and appreciated him, but when she finally did start dating again it wasn’t with Thor.  It was with another bad boy.”

 

“Kathy’s story plays out over and over.  With Kathy it was bad boys.  With other women it might be cowboys or business executives.  Different women find different types of guys attractive.  It’s best to know what kind of guy turns a girl on before you go after her.  It goes back to only pursuing women who find you attractive.”

 

“You know, that story brings to mind a common trait in woman.  They think they can change a guy.  Dr. Laura, the radio therapist, gets calls every day which go something like this:  A woman calls complaining bitterly about her husband of 7 years.  She goes on about how he won’t stop smoking and he goes out drinking with his friends and he doesn’t help out enough around the house and he doesn’t like to do the things she likes to do.  Then she says she doesn’t love him anymore and wants to divorce him but they have 3 kids and stays with him for the kids.  At this point Dr. Laura will ask, Did your husband smoke and go out drinking with his friends when you met him?  Was his house a pig sty when you met him?  Did he enjoy activities you don’t enjoy?  To this the woman would sheepishly say yes.  Then Dr. Laura would ask, Then why did you marry this man who wasn’t what you wanted in a husband?  To this question the woman would answer, I thought I could change him.”

 

“The amazing thing is everyday Dr. Laura gets a similar call.  The details are different but the last question is always the same and the caller’s answer is always the same, I thought I could change him.  In fact, this trait of women to be determined to change all men is so common I hesitate to refer to this as a type of woman.  It seems to be a universal part of a woman’s makeup:  They will try to change their man.  The only question is how obsessive are they about it.  Some women are like drill sergeants in their determination to bring their man in line and others are more accepting and philosophical.  They realize men will be men.  Anyway, unless you want to live your life in the marine corps look out for women obsessed with changing you into their image of how you should be.”

 

“Okay, I’ll change the subject a little to talk about another type of woman, The Man Collector.  They’re not dangerous…They just want your attention and your time but not much else.  This girl flirts a lot.  She wants you interested in her.  She wants you to find her attractive and to chase her.  She wants you all hot and bothered over her.  But, when you ask her out, she’s always busy and that’s because she’s a serial flirter.  She wants all the guys after her but she’s not really interested in any of them.  Dating her is a waste of your time and money because it’ll never lead anywhere.  All you really are to her is another notch on her belt so she can brag to her friends about all the guys who are in love with her.  The girls who’re Man Collectors are usually attractive but not the total knock outs.  They’re usually 7s but they want to think they’re 10s so they want to prove how gorgeous and desirable they are by garnering male attention.  The man collector behavior helps them believe the dream.”

 

“I knew a girl similar to a man collector in her pathology of attracting men but different in that she’d only go after one at a time.  Roslyn was one of those beautiful women whose only real talent was for attracting men.  She was discriminating in who she went after but then once she found a guy she was interested in she’d seduce him, begin dating and start a sexual relationship.  But, then she’d loose interest in a few months and cut it off.  Roslyn was very attractive but I always felt her ego was so weak she needed to prove herself over and over again.  She needed to seduce a man…even when she didn’t want him.  I was never quite sure of her real inner motivation.  Was it that once she had a guy she lost interest because he wasn’t a challenge anymore?  Was it that she needed to prove herself over and over?  Maybe it was that deep down she didn’t like herself enough to move to the next level of a committed long term relationship.  Maybe if she found self-satisfaction and fulfillment in her job or career, she’d find herself content with herself and a relationship.”

 

Two nice looking girls had sat at a table near us.  I’d been noticing them and then Michael said, “You know Robert, one of those girls has been looking at you.”  I told him I’d noticed them also and was about to say something.  I asked Michael if we should act on this opportunity.  He agreed and said he’d be the wingman on the mission.

So we reviewed the plan and then I approached the two girls.  “Hi, my name is Robert.  We’re sitting right over there and are about to order dinner.  We’d love to have you join us.”  The girls were flattered and said they’d love to and picked up their drinks to move to our table.  We introduced everyone and began to get to know one another.  The girl who’d been looking my way was Kim and her friend was Sonia.  They worked together for a telecom company near the restaurant and were unwinding Friday after work just like we were.  We had an enjoyable dinner and conversation.  Both of the girls were fun and interesting.  Kim focused more on me.  She was sending out the message she was interested and I was receiving it.  I did my best to be positive towards her without coming on too strong.  Sonia, as it turned out, was also playing the role of wingman…wing woman.  Early on she found a way to mention her boyfriend so we’d know she was there to enjoy a nice evening with her friend but for us not to expect more.  When Sonia mentioned her boyfriend, Kim used the opportunity to mention she didn’t have a boyfriend.  Michael and I were taking all of this in.

 

Later we discussed how Sonia had mentioned her boyfriend.  We liked that she’d been politely up front with us.  It made it easier for us because we knew the evening would most likely end with dinner.  We weren’t wondering if we should ask them to go out to a bar after dinner.  So the dinner went well and everyone was content to have another drink and talk more before leaving.  Michael and I picked up the check while fighting off the girl’s protests.  We smiled and told them it was our pleasure and after all we’d invited them to join us.  The girls were gracious and thanked us.  We could tell they enjoyed meeting two men who were simply gentlemen.  They didn’t have to suffer smooth lines or lame compliments.  Then as we stood up I told Kim I enjoyed meeting her and suggested we get together again sometime.  She smiled and said she’d enjoy that.  She asked if I had my phone so we could enter her number into memory.  I asked if I could take a picture and she enjoyed posing like a model.

 

We all said our goodbyes and then Michael and I were left to decide what we were going to do.  I asked him if he’d like to stop by Applebee’s.  We could have a beer and watch a game and he could see Stephanie.  Michael was funny when he said, “I’d love to see Stephanie…I think about her all the time.  But, that’s the kind of thing which starts making you look needy and desperate.  Here it is 8pm on a Friday night and the guy who’s had two casual dates with her comes by to hang out.  In other words, he has no life and he’s getting clingy after 2 dates.  That’s what her co-workers are going to be telling her.  When you’re dating you have to be careful not to look clingy.  Now let’s make a comparison.  Let’s say Stephanie and I are in a committed relationship and you and I stop by for a beer and to say hi.  In that case, her co-workers are going to swoon because Stephanie’s boyfriend is so thoughtful and sweet to come by and say hi when he could be out doing who knows what with who knows who.  Hell, a couple of her co-workers would probably want to know you just based on the fact you’re friends with such a good, thoughtful boyfriend.  That’s a big difference.”

 

There was so much wisdom in what Michael had just said.  We laughed over it for a while and then decided we both had better things to do at home so we talked about getting together Sunday afternoon.  He had a lunch date with Stephanie but she went to work at 3pm.  We thought about getting a pizza and watching a game around 4.

 

**********************

 

Sunday Michael came over to my apartment with a large Canadian bacon and pineapple.  I had a salad made so we were set for action…or inaction as we settled in to enjoy the food.  I was anxious to hear about Michaels lunch date.  “How was your date with Stephanie?”

 

“It was great.  We always have fun and she looked better than ever.  I think Stephanie had a little plan for this lunch.  She suggested the restaurant and it’s only 4 blocks from where she lives.  So when we left the restaurant I asked where her car was and she said she’d walked.  So I suggested I could drive us to a park and we could go for a walk and then drop her off at her apartment when she had to get ready for work.  I think she was hoping for that.  Anyway, we had a nice time at the park and then when I drove her home she asked if I’d like to come in and see her apartment.  Of course, I said yes.  Her apartment is very nice.  It has that woman’s touch I’ll never master on my own.  She showed me around…including her bedroom…and then said she had to get ready for work.  We walked to the door and she moved around in front of me, put her hands around my neck, looked into my eyes, thanked me for a wonderful afternoon and gave me a soft, slow kiss on the lips.  We held the kiss for a moment and then pulled away.  We both took a few breaths and then I said I wanted to go out again next week.  She said she’d wait for my call.  Stephanie’s a smart girl.  She wanted to share a kiss and a bit of romance so she walked to the restaurant so I could give her a ride home and come in to see her apartment…but, she had to be to work so that held rein on how far it could go.  She also showed me where she lives.  I’ll bet when I call her for a date next week she asks if I can pick her up instead of meeting there.”

 

“But enough about the lovely and gracious Stephanie…Did you call Kim?”

 

“No, I decided to call on Monday.  We met on Friday so it seemed like calling Saturday or Sunday would look like I have nothing to do on weekends.  I figured Monday is a logical time to make plans for the week…”

 

“…So Friday, before we met Kim and Sonia, we were talking about the games girls play.  I think it was your turn.  Do you have another story?”

 

“I have a story which is kind of frightening…Actually, it’s really frightening.  I’ve always been close with my uncle and he’s been like a mentor most of my life.  He told me this story when I was 17 to teach me and put the fear of God in me.  It happened to him when he was in his early 30s.  He got married out of college and had very little experience with women or relationships.  His wife, my aunt, is a really nice lady but they just had different directions to go with their lives and they divorced after 7 years.  So at 30 my uncle was suddenly single and dating.  He enjoyed that and had no plans to remarry.  Looking back, he realized other than dating in high school and some in college; he didn’t really have any experience with women.  My aunt was such a nice and normal person he’d never experienced any of the games and manipulating which can go on.  In a word, he was naïve.”

 

“So life was going along well for my uncle as he dated and had a few short term relationships.  Then he met the devil woman, Stella.  Stella was English.  She was average looking but cute.  Her most pronounced…and useful…features were her cute, innocent face and equally cute, innocent, tiny British accented voice.  These features gave her a disguise.  No one who met her could imagine a sociopath lurked behind that angelic face and voice.  It was as if a pit bull was hiding inside the body of a golden retriever puppy.  And, Stella used that disguise very well to portray this poor little innocent girl from a foreign country who’s lost her way and needs a man to come and rescue her.  That’s the girl my uncle met and he fell into her trap.  When he first met her he wasn’t struck by her beauty.  He found her desirable.  If you watch BBC mysteries, you’ll see English women can be a bit plump in the butt and breasts.  Stella had those features.  My uncle liked that and in addition Stella’s shy, innocent manner with her cute English accent made her very sexy to him.  She exuded a shy sexuality and sensuality.  The kind that drives men crazy and her allure was tough to resist.  My uncle said it was like she put out a scent.”

 

“My uncle met Stella at the tennis club where he played 3 or 4 times a week.  He’d belonged to the club for a few years and was among a group of regulars.  Stella joined the club and quickly singled out my uncle.  She approached him and with her shy manner asked if he could help her with her game.  Of course, this is exactly the kind of approach which appeals to a male ego.  The shy girl asking the big strong guy for help…And my uncle took the bait.  Things were great at first.  They met often at the club and she stroked his ego.  Stella easily seduced my uncle who jumped headlong into a relationship with this English sex kitten.  He told me she had a way of transforming ordinary sex into an exciting, life altering experience.  She made it seem like their relationship was so intense that they must be breaking rules or taboos for it to be this good.  She made reference to my uncle being the best lover of her life.  Her ability to create sensual moods for sex was matched by her ability to make the physical sex equally intense.  She’d have screaming, crying orgasms and her eyes would roll back to where she seemed to have reached another spiritual plane.  Keep in mind my uncle had very little sexual experience before his marriage and my aunt was a woman who had no interest in sex.  So my uncle had never experienced anything like this and he thought he’d just landed on Sexual Fantasy Island.  The hook was set.”

“Stella was separated and in the process of divorcing.  This was another method she used to stroke the male ego as she needed my uncle to be the white knight and help her through the trauma of a bad marriage and divorce.  In the first weeks of their relationship she’d often talk about her ex-husband.  She portrayed him as a looser and a terrible lover.  This all helped boost my uncle’s ego.  She also enjoyed bragging how she was able to manipulate and control her dimwitted ex.  She often said how she could wrap him around her little finger and even told how easily she could convince him with her lies and she’d have him pulling his hair out with frustration as she’d change her story daily claiming she’d never said what she had said the day before.  Of course my uncle ignored these red flags because she’d established that her ex-husband was an incompetent, lazy fool who’d cheated on her and deserved everything he got.  He reasoned Stella was left no choice but to lie and manipulate in order to survive this horrible marriage.”

 

“After maybe three months my uncle started having problems with Stella.  Of course, the disharmony was created by her and it was exactly what she wanted but this reality only became clear to him much later as he looked back on the whole experience.  The signs were subtle a first.  Stella began with little criticisms.  But what was really cleaver was they were unfounded criticisms and she enjoyed watching him object and then get frustrated when she wouldn’t accept his defense.  For example, if someone says you’re 10 pounds overweight, then you agree because you know you’re overweight and that’s the end of the story.  But Stella would say things like, You walk funny.  My uncle would say he didn’t walk funny but she’d continue her claim.  She knew he was right but she enjoyed watching him get frustrated when she wouldn’t accept this obvious truth.  Her goal was to never allow him to be right.  Her goal was, over time, to get him to question his own reality.  She wanted him to start asking himself if maybe he did walk funny.  These games were phase one of her strategy.  They were designed to begin to deteriorate his self-esteem.  She’d never allow him to be right about anything.  She’d create no-win situations for him and then revel in his failure.  And, of course, when he’d complain that she was always criticizing him, she’d say it was he who was always negative towards her.  She’d say, just like now…accusing me of criticizing you.”

 

“Stella started using the tennis club as her torture chamber for my uncle.  When they first met she treated him as a personal coach she looked up to.  That began to change.  She started questioning his advice and would seek help with her game from others at the club and the tennis instructors who she was befriending and flirting with.  She was purposely belittling my uncle to diminish him in front of his friends and the tennis community.  Stella set upon to marginalize him from any of his friends.  She was working to alienate him from any of his friends who were telling him the truth about her.  Stella made up things the friends supposedly said about him.  She was isolating him so he had no support system left which could help him see what she was doing.”

 

“Along with this Stella began to take away the sex.  This was a well laid plan to first addict my uncle to this amazing sexual experience and then take it away.  But she wasn’t denying him sex.  Her method was to establish that my uncle was no longer worthy of sex with her.  She wasn’t the person ruining the party…It was him.  She blamed him as the reason she no longer desired sex with him.  Her spin was it was because he didn’t do enough for her to prove his worthiness.  In this way, she was able to put my uncle on call to be her personal assistant for whatever she needed.  If her car needed service, he’d leave work to take it in to be fixed.  If she needed shelves put up at her apartment then he’d be doing that while she was playing tennis.  If she needed a stretching routine then he’d be at the library finding a book on stretching…Of course, the one the tennis instructor gave her was better.” 

 

“Stella found fault with everything he did and his shortcomings were blamed for the relationship not sailing forward on calm waters.  At this point my uncle was getting fed up with Stella and her games.  He tried a few times to break it off but whenever she sensed an escape to freedom she’d pull him back into line.  This was easy to do since she could often find him at the club.  She’d use her shy sexy voice to push his sexual buttons.  My uncle was indeed addicted to Stella’s sexuality.  One of the times when he’d stayed away from her for a few weeks and hadn’t called her she finally came over to his house to talk.  She knew just how to slowly seduce him and get him so aroused that he had no resistance.  They went to bed and she was performing like a woman in ecstasy.  As they were making love he said to her, Tell me you love me.  In an instant her face went from a woman experiencing fantastic sexual pleasure to a cold, calculating look.  She had this knowing look on her face like she’d accomplished what she’d come there for.  She’d regained complete control over my uncle.  Suddenly she was ambivalent to the love making and soon left to go home.  That cold, calculating look had terrified my uncle.  He wouldn’t fully understand it until a year or two later.”

During this time the brainwashing continued.  She had a way of making herself out to be this incredibly special, amazing person and he was so lucky to be with her.  She was good though because she did this without actually bragging.  She also convinced him they had a love which was too special to give up on…this was designed to keep him trying to make this amazing relationship work.

 

 “During this time Stella pushed every button in the book on male insecurities.  She was a master at it.  Taking away approval and especially his sexual approval was her goal and she did it well.  When they first met, he was the only man in her life.  Now, more and more she had many male suitors and male friends and men showing an interest in her.  This was all encouraged by her to make him jealous.  She claimed all the other men were just friends but all of these men were pursuing her and she was encouraging it.  Actually, Stella was now using everyone.  She enjoyed the attention from the other men to see what she could get, and she was able to hold off their advances because of the relationship with my uncle.  At the same time she was able to make my uncle jealous with all of the other men chasing her.”

 

“The jealousy, the lies, the games and all of it took its toll on my uncle.  He said he’d listened to so many lies that he was starting to question his ability to see reality.  When she lied and twisted things around he brought up what she once said about how she lied to and manipulated her ex-husband.  In her most innocent voice she said she’d never said those things.  She’d blame him for doing something but it was what she’d asked him to do…though now she denied it.  It got to the point where my uncle felt he needed to tape record every conversation they had.”

 

“It’d now been a few months since they’d made love.  Stella was constantly torturing him with jealousy.  Her lies were never ending.  Her games were all played by her rules.  He was in a downward spiral.  It was affecting his work and his friendships.  His life was so out of his control he realized he had to make a break from her…which he did.  He stopped playing tennis so he wouldn’t be around her and focused on work while hoping to meet another girl.  He hadn’t seen Stella for several weeks and then she called him one evening.  She tried her usual games on him but this time he wasn’t biting.  He more or less said, Nice try, and hung up.

 

To his surprise she didn’t call back but a week later she came by his house.  It was a Saturday and my uncle was outside washing his car.  She was feeling cheery and asked if he’d go for a walk with him.  They walked to a nearby park and sat down to talk.  She was in a confident mood and began to talk about how good she felt and then began to tell him he needed to feel good too.  Then she suggested he should have an affair because that’s just what he needed.  This entire conversation was meant to let my uncle know she’d had a one night stand the night before with some lawyer she’d met out at a bar.  She was careful to choose a profession which sounded more successful than my uncle.  Her plan worked.  When her phone call had failed to restore her power over my uncle she went to more extreme measures and now the uncontrollable emotion of jealousy swept over her target.  For the past few months she’d claimed she had no interest in sex, yet now she was admitting to an affair.  Suddenly my uncle felt jealous and possessive.  The girl who’d once told him he was the best lover in her life was now with another man.  He crumbled and they went home to make love.  She re-established her control.”

 

“Stella played the good girlfriend for the next week probably because she was leaving on a trip back to Europe and would be away for two months.  She wanted to keep my uncle under her spell while she was away.  My uncle had a different intention though.  He also played the good boyfriend waiting for her to leave.  He was now determined to do what he had to do to rid himself of the devil woman…even if it meant having an exorcism.  During the two months she was away Stella called occasionally but for the most part they had little contact.  Meanwhile, back home the gods smiled on my uncle.  He met a very pretty and sweet girl, Kandi.  There’d been an immediate attraction and they began dating.  Kandi breathed life back into my uncle.  She loved life and brought out the best in people…especially my uncle.  Their relationship grew quickly.”

 

“My uncle wasn’t sure exactly the day Stella was to return so her call caught him by surprise around 7pm one evening.  He heard her familiar voice.  Hi, I’m back.  Did you miss me?  I want to see you.  I’m not sleepy because of the time change.  Take me to a movie.  This was Stella’s way.  She never asked my uncle to do something (Will you take me to a movie?).  She’d simply tell him to do it and say it with a tone like a normal command from a boss to a subordinate (Take me to a movie.).  The tone wasn’t demanding because there was never a doubt in her mind that my uncle, or any other man in her web, would do exactly what he was told.  That was her view of reality.  Everyone else was on earth to serve her.  Asking for something never occurred to her.  My uncle was polite and warm but explained it was late and he had a lot of things he needed to do before work the next day.  He was really sorry but he just couldn’t see her that night.  She tried again to get him to take her to a movie.  Again he politely explained he couldn’t.  Now she started trying all the manipulations which had worked before.  She tried guilt telling him how unreasonable he was…explaining she’d just finished a long trip and needed to unwind.  This time he came back and told her she was the one being unreasonable to call out of the blue at 7pm and expect him to drop everything at her whim.  Stella couldn’t believe her ears.  My uncle had calmly stood up to her.  Now her tone became threatening.  Her true colors came out.  She said, How dare you talk to me that way.  How dare you say no to me.  I’m not taking you back this time.  My uncle calmly said, Okay, have a good life.  He hung up marveling at his new found strength and immediately called Kandi to ask if he could come by to see her.  He really didn’t want to be around for more calls or a possible visit.”

“Stella tried a few more times to lure my uncle back into her web but soon gave up.  He supposed she’d done some detective work and found out he was happily dating Kandi.  Stella realized my uncle now knew her game and wasn’t falling for it anymore.  She also knew she couldn’t compete with Kandi.  Stella was cold and calculating.  She knew she could no longer win this game so she went looking for a game she could control and win.”

 

“Over the next year my uncle was nearly obsessed with understanding how all of that with Stella had happened to him.  He came to realize that after his divorce his self-esteem was fragile.  The emotional upheaval of the divorce and the resulting debt had left him feeling low.  His job and career were stalling and in total he wasn’t feeling very good about himself.  Said another way, deep down he didn’t approve of himself and was looking for the easy way out.  If he won someone else’s approval, then it would make him whole.  Of course, that doesn’t really make you whole but it does numb the pain.  So when he met Stella she gave him approval.  He desperately needed it and liked it.  Then she took it away in every way she could and that was like withholding drugs from an addict.  He was ashamed of how desperate he’d been to win approval from such an evil person.  In truth he’d allowed himself to be a victim of emotional abuse and just like women who’re with an abusive man, he’d stayed in the relationship.  His self-esteem had sunk so low he didn’t think he was worthy of anyone else and worse, he thought his only salvation was to be worthy in the eyes of Stella, the devil.”

 

“As he looked back on all that had happened, he began to understand things which had confused him at the time.  One thing he was trying to understand was the logic of the things she’d done.  There was only one possible explanation for her behavior and that was she enjoyed hurting people.  The concept of enjoying hurting someone was so foreign to my uncle he just couldn’t accept that explanation, but it was the only logical conclusion.  One time in desperation he’d told Stella how deeply it hurt him when she did a particular thing.  He begged her to stop doing it.  When he told her this, a glow of self-satisfaction came over her face.  He said it was terrifying.  It was like watching a scene from a horror movie.  Not only did she not stop doing that hurtful thing, she did it more.  He also realized Stella only had sex to hurt someone.  When she first met my uncle she had sex with him to hurt her ex-husband.  Remember they were separated and waiting for the divorce so having sex with another man was her way of twisting the knife in her ex.  Once she was through with her ex, she focused her evil on my uncle and withholding sex was now the way to hurt him.  Then when he tried to leave her she’d have sex with him once or twice to get him back so she could hurt him more.  Then one time she had sex with a one night stand because that was a way to hurt my uncle.  To Stella sex was a tool to control and ultimately hurt men.”

 

“My uncle wanted to talk with someone about what he’d gone through.  Kandi knew full well how evil Stella was but she really didn’t want to hear about it.  She loved my uncle and didn’t want to hear about his obsession with another woman.  Then one day he ran into a female friend who knew Stella.  She’d always seen through Stella and knew her for what she was.  Because of this Stella had disliked this girl and had manipulated my uncle to be unfriendly to her.  My uncle asked if they could talk for a while.  He apologized for being mean to her.  He told her he was ashamed to say Stella had made him do that.  They had a long talk and this girl verified everything my uncle had come to realize.  This lady said Stella was the most distasteful person she’d ever known in her life.  They also talked about how good Stella was at fooling people.  Everyone thought she was just the nicest, most innocent girl on earth.  That had been one of the most difficult things for my uncle because no one would believe his side of the story.”

 

“After a few months Stella took up with another guy.  He was hand-picked to fit her profile just like my uncle was.  She found a guy with some missing pieces in his life…someone with a fragile self-esteem…someone who needed approval.  My uncle knew the guy a little from the gym.  He was kind of shallow and had a big ego.  He was the type who strutted around being friendly to everyone and fashioned himself as being irresistible to women.  About a year later Kandi was at the gym at the juice bar talking to a friend who worked there.  Stella’s new boyfriend was sitting at the bar talking about Stella.  He was a mere shadow of the man he’d been the year before.  He appeared beaten down and defeated.  He was going on about how special Stella was and all the amazing things she was doing and how lucky he was to have her.  Kandi said it was frightening seeing what she’d done to the guy.”

 

“One day at work my uncle saw a book, The Stranger Beside Me by Ann Rule.  A friend was reading it.  It was the true crime story of a serial killer.  My uncle started reading it because he’d seen a movie made from the book.  About halfway through the book the author defined a psychopath or sociopath in order for the reader to understand how someone can do such things.  She defined the Deviant Sociopath as an anti-social personality without any concern for the rights of others; a brilliant mind with no conscience to guide it; a narcissist who sees herself as the center of everything who’ll get what she wants no matter what means she has to employ and no matter who or what stands in her way; she’s not able to have or understand love.  When my uncle read that the hair stood up on the back of his neck.  He realized it described Stella perfectly.  He remembered how he’d thought there was only one logical reason to explain her behavior and that was she enjoyed hurting people.  He had no doubt Stella’s intention was to destroy men.  She’d amuse herself destroying a man until a better victim came along.  She’d destroyed her ex-husband and the ex-husband before that and my uncle and then the hapless guy who came next.  She found her victims and then went to work.”

 

“My uncle seldom saw Stella after that.  However, he’d get a call from her about every 2 years for the next 10 years.  It was like she was throwing out a trial balloon just to see if maybe she could catch my uncle in a weak moment.  She probably hoped she could get him to give in to her sexual allure…just to let him know she could have him anytime she wanted.  He’d simply laugh and say he wasn’t interested and hang up.  He told me there was no way he could do justice to the story because you just had to be there to see how calmly she’d look you in the eye and lie.  He’d watch this glow of accomplishment and satisfaction come over her face as she twisted the knife.”

 

“That’s quite a story.  Men are so susceptible to the power of the pussy.  I remember a guy told me once.  He said, You know that area on a woman below her waist...it forms a triangle?  Well, that’s like the Bermuda Triangle.  Many men who go there never come back.”  We had a good laugh but then I was serious.  “We laugh but it really is true.  How many men have ruined their lives with bad decisions based on the momentary need to go into the Bermuda Triangle?  Men marry the wrong women.  Men cheat on their wives.  Men loose fortunes…All do to the power of the pussy.  Can you say Paul McCartney?  Can you say Tiger Woods?  Can you say that governor from South Carolina…or was it North?”

“How does a guy know if he’s being seduced or manipulated?  The problem is at the time he doesn’t care.  All thoughts of caution are tossed aside as the attractive woman starts whispering in his ear.  The real question to ask as a woman starts seducing a man is what is her intention?  Does she have sincere feelings for this guy or is she after something?  Does she want to make love to this guy or does she want to use this guy?  Unfortunately, whether you’re being seduced or being manipulated it all feels the same in the beginning.   There’s a must see movie on this, Body Heat.  This is a 1981 movie.  It’s old but it’s really good.  The first time I watched it I watched it again the next night.  The story is so intriguing you want to go back and see who was doing what and when.  No man should have a fling before watching that movie.”

 

Michael wrote down the title so he could rent it and then said, “Speaking of movies, I recently watched Prozac Nation with Christina Ricci.  First, there’s something very sexy about Christina Ricci no matter the movie.  But, in this movie she plays a college girl who suffers from depression or bi-polar disorder or something.  It’s based on a true story and is very interesting.  The character she portrays is an extreme case but I’ve known people who’re mild versions of her character.  They have mood swings and are unpredictable.  They’re suddenly yelling at you for no reason.  I dated a girl, Shirley, who was a nice girl but she’d become completely irrational over nothing.  She’d get defensive and it would make her so ugly.  I loved the girl but she terrified me.  One time she was over at my house and I was doing my laundry so I took the clothes out of the dryer and dumped them on the bed to fold them.  I always turn my t-shirts inside out to wash them.  She started folding a t-shirt and I casually told her I like to turn them right side out before I put them away.  She threw a fit.  She got defensive and threw it down and told me to do it myself.  I looked at her and said all I did was tell her how I like to fold my clothes.  She pumped up the volume and was screaming at me.  Another time she calls me in a panic because she had to finish a paper and her computer crashed.  She asked if she could borrow my laptop.  I told her my laptop was really old but I’d bring it over.  She starts it up and starts looking through the programs and the laptop didn’t have Word.  I‘d forgotten it didn’t have Word because I hadn’t used it in a while.  She starts screaming at me.  I told her it wasn’t my fault her computer crashed.  Normal people don’t yell at someone who’s simply trying to help.  It was the same thing.  She was so self-focused and everything was always about Shirley.”

 

“Most every time she did something like that she’d cool off after an hour or two and apologize.  She’d blame it on her hormones or she was really emotional because of whatever.  But when I watched Prozac Nation I realized Shirley has a medical issue or chemical imbalance or emotional imbalance or something.  I’ve heard stories of people who get married and then later find out their spouse has a major medical issue which wasn’t disclosed.  You really have to be careful when you start seeing irrational behavior.  It’s easy to excuse it.  It’s easy to dismiss it as temporary stress or hormones or just a bad day but it isn’t.  Normal people don’t do these things.  Take these little red flags very seriously…They only get worse after you’re married.”

 

“Okay, I have a story but it’s pretty benign compared to Stella and Body Heat and Prozac Nation.  Tina was recently divorced and had some health issues she was healing up from.  Basically, Tina had been through some challenging times so she was taking things one step at a time as she got her emotional and physical strength back.  Now along comes Bill, who’s known Tina for a few years including back when she was married.  He always had a thing for her so he starts asking her to get together and go out.  Tina’s always been friendly with Bill and is happy to hear from him but she tells him that after all she’s gone through she’s still on the mend and doesn’t yet feel like going out socializing.  She does suggest he’s welcome to come over sometime in the evening to watch TV.  Bill takes this as an indication Tina is ready for something more than friends.”

 

“So Bill goes over one evening to watch TV and is the perfect gentleman.  Bill sees Tina is nursing back to health and decides to be her knight in shining armor.  He starts going over 2 or 3 times a week and brings health food with him so she won’t have to cook for herself.  Tina is very appreciative and starts telling him what a good friend he is.  Nursing Tina back to health has become Bill’s central focus and he’s totally into it.  After a few weeks Bill wants to move the friendship to romance.  He begins leading the conversations to the subject of what makes a good relationship and what she’s looking for in a man.  While watching TV he puts his arm around Tina and kisses her but she’s unresponsive.  She pulls back and tells Bill she just isn’t ready for romance because first she needs to concentrate on her health.  She tells him it’s not him, it’s her.”
