“During their conversations over the weeks Tina listed all the attributes of what she was looking for so Bill followed the recipe to the letter.  He became her best friend.  He brought her dinners.  He brought flowers.  He came over and sat with her to watch their favorite shows.  He ran errands for her.  He gave her neck and back massages…no sensual touching allowed.  So Tina is getting everything she wants and Bill is getting nothing in return but nice conversation.  This went on for 2 months and then all of a sudden he calls on a Friday night and she’s not home.  Later he finds out she’d gone out on a date with some guy.  She makes excuses and explains it away blaming it on some girlfriends but within a week she’s sleeping with this new guy and Bill’s standing there like a fool.  Bill went over and had it out with Tina.  He accused her of leading him on and what about all those things you said about a man had to prove his friendship and devotion first, blah, blah, blah.  She told him how special he was to her and he was such a nice guy and she had no idea this was going to happen and on and on.”

“So what happened here?  Tina’s minding her own business and Bill throws himself at her.  Tina knew exactly that Bill wanted a girlfriend but she’ll never admit that…even to herself.  She liked Bill as a friend.  He’s a nice guy and she saw a chance to use him.  She was bored sitting at home alone so Bill can serve her dinner and amuse her while she waits for Mr. Right to come along.  Why did she say all those things about friendship and being attentive and letting a friendship grow into something more over time?  Well, all women will say those things because they want to believe that.  Tina especially wanted to believe those things were true because she was stringing Bill along.  Saying those things and believing them to be true helped her relieve the guilt of knowing she was using Bill.”

 

“It’s difficult to be too hard on Tina though.  Yes, she knew Bill wanted something she wasn’t going to give him but at the same time she wasn’t manipulating Bill to do all those things for her.  It was always his initiative.  The lesson here is to be more selfish.  If a relationship isn’t advancing to where you want it to go then you have to start asking if you want to be in that relationship.”

 

“Actually, that story of Tina isn’t that dramatic but it sure is common.  Women know what they’re looking for and when they find it they don’t waste any time and they’re not ambiguous about letting the guy know they’re interested.  If a girl doesn’t make it obvious, then she probably isn’t interested…”

 

“…Okay, it’s my turn and I have another more benign story.  This is the story of Carol and Mick.  They met in college, had a lot in common with sports and started dating.  About a year after college they got married and began married life.  Carol was a good person.  She’s honest, has good values and never created drama or turmoil.  She never played a game in her life and wouldn’t know how if she wanted to.  Mick was also a good person and honest.  He was always well intentioned but had the flaw of being too immature for marriage.  Carol and Mick got along very well as roommates and spouses.  They did sports and outdoor activities together and shared the work on a house they bought.  They were good friends, good partners and good spouses.  The one area where they weren’t compatible was sex.  Mick had a healthy appetite for sex.  Carol was ambivalent at best.  Eventually their different sexual needs and other things caused them to separate and divorce.  They had no children so the divorce was fairly painless and tame.”

 

“As I mentioned before Mick wasn’t ready for marriage at age 23.  He was immature and naïve about many things.  Seven years of marriage and going through a divorce had however taught him a lot about himself and his failings.  He’d also learned something about Carol.  Carol was a lesbian.  Actually, this was only now becoming evident to her.  In fact, it would be years after the divorce before Carol fully accepted this.  One of the reasons Carol had trouble recognizing it was because she didn’t really have sexual desires for men or women.  Carol was more or less asexual.  She did, however, prefer the company of women.”

 

“For anyone, it’s difficult to deal with a failed marriage and it was no different for Mick.  But, he was very accepting of Carol and was happy she now had such a nice group of female friends as a support group.  Looking back, Mick realized how naïve and inexperienced he’d been when they were dating because all the signs of Carol’s feelings were obvious in college and throughout their marriage.  In college, nothing was dearer to Carol than her closest friends in her sorority.  For her, going out drinking with those friends was her favorite thing to do and that fact was often painfully apparent to Mick.  Given a choice of spending time with Mick or being with her friends, Carol would choose her friends.  A few times this had caused some friction when Mick wanted to know why her friends were more important to her than he was.”

 

“Besides her group of friends from the sorority, there was always one girl in particular Carol would become best friends with.  When she was a sophomore, it was her big sister in the sorority.  The next year it was another girl who became best friends with Carol.  When Carol was a senior she had a little sister in the sorority and they became best friends.  It’s not unusual for people to have best friends but with Carol it took on an importance which was far more intense.  She’d talk about that girl and their friendship constantly.  It was obvious those friendships were very important to Carol.  On the surface this all looks innocent.  Of course girls will form friendships and all of Carol’s friends were wonderfully nice people.  The clue was Carol’s special friend meant more to her than anything.”

 

“Mick didn’t have any sexual experience when he started dating Carol so he had no point of reference to compare Carol’s sexuality.  Because of this, Mick missed this important clue.  Carol had no interest in sex and no apparent sex drive.  She was mildly curious like most college age kids but beyond that she had little interest in sharing romance.”

“After they were married Carol no longer had her sorority sisters and she settled in to married life.  However, throughout the marriage Carol would periodically develop a friendship with a girl she met at work or through sports.  Again, these friendships became very important to Carol.  Again, looking back Mick supposes the longer Carol was married the more she wondered if being married was what she wanted.  Carol was a gifted athlete and loved sports.  One spring she joined a women’s softball team.  She quickly became one of the star players and in a way she had her sorority back.  She developed a close group of good friends and it was probably those friendships which gave her the strength to decide she wanted to separate and divorce.  Of course, this was difficult for Mick who adored Carol for all of her qualities but it was the best for both of them.”

 

“So what’s the lesson here?  One lesson is Carol got married not because of a burning desire to be with a man and not because she wanted to build a nest and have children.  She got married because society told her that was what she should do.  Mick didn’t recognize this because of his inexperience with women.  One has to wonder what would’ve happened if one of Carol’s best friends in college had been a lesbian.  Carol would’ve had an inner conflict between her desire to be with her friend and society’s plan for her to marry a man.”

 

“But, the real lesson to learn is it can be difficult for a man to truly know a girl’s sexuality.  Especially because it’s often the case women don’t truly know their own sexuality.  Learning what a woman’s sexuality is…her true sexuality…is the most important thing a man must do as he enters into a relationship.  Here’s the difficult part…and please listen to this well…You do NOT learn this by asking her.”

 

“Okay, I have one.  This is about women I’ve encountered who’re not lesbians but along the same vein.  They’re women who like men and want male attention and even seek it out with how they dress and make themselves up.  They’ll attract men and socialize with men and date men…that’s all good…but when the moment of romance arrives they back out and won’t let it happen.  These women are usually quite social.  They like social settings, parties, groups, gatherings and going out to events.  Being socially correct is important to them and so they want to be normal and have a date at the social events.  But, whereas they’re infinitely comfortable in their social gatherings with lots of people, they’re not very comfortable when it comes down to just her and a man.  One on one makes her nervous because she’s afraid of romance.  So even when the romantic setting is there and the right man is there, she won’t let it happen.  She isn’t ready for romance at this point in her life…and she may never be ready.  She’ll never admit this to anyone.  She’ll say she wants romance and wants a good man and even go on how frustrated she is because she can’t find the right man.  In fact, that’s one of the clues to recognizing this type of woman.  When there’s always something wrong with every guy you have to wonder if finding something…anything wrong with each guy is her method of never allowing a relationship to go beyond dating.  The worst part of these women is when romance doesn’t happen she blames the man.  This is her guilt relief.  This is her way of being in denial.  She won’t admit the real problem is her.  She’s too scared or just simply doesn’t have it in her to have a real one on one, romantic, sexual relationship but she won’t accept the responsibility so she must blame it on the man she’s currently dating.  Prince Charming himself could show up and she’d find something wrong with him.”

 

“Oh yeah, I dated a girl like that.  She was quite pretty.  Like you said, she was very outgoing and comfortable in groups.  She took care of herself and always looked great.  I was even asking myself what was wrong with this picture.  Why was this girl who seemed to have it all unattached?  And just as you said, she was completely relaxed and at ease in a group but as soon as I was alone with her you could see her go into defense mode…There was no way she was going to let things go further and she was looking for escape routes.” 

 

“You know, these women can be very useful to a man.  Remember we talked about being seen with women.  Develop a friendship with one of these women…say from work…and then you always have a nice looking and charming girl to go to events with when you don’t want to be seen alone.  Say there’s a salsa band playing at a bar and you know there’ll be a lot of single girls there and it’ll be easy to approach them asking them to dance.  Of course your friend will enjoy going to this kind of event and she won’t mind you dancing with other girls because that maintains her position as just friends.  Your image is enhanced because you’re with a nice looking girl.  Your date enjoys the opportunity to safely dance with other men.  She can fend off advances by saying she’s with someone.  You’re free to dance with other girls and get phone numbers.  It’s a win/win.”

 

“Have you ever known women who avoid relationships and dating by only being attracted to unattainable men?  These women will talk to their friends about how lonely they are, how unfulfilled they are and how they’re looking for a man to come into their life, but they’ll have crushes on men who they can’t have.  They can be in a room full of available bachelors and the only man they find attractive is the one who’s married or completely out of their league.  These are the women who’ll talk endlessly about some movie star or athlete and how dreamy he is but they always find something wrong with the good looking, available guy working in her office.  I think they’re terrified of a relationship but they’ll never admit this and I’m not even sure they can admit it to themselves.  All of this may be on a subconscious level from a deep down fear of rejection or fear of becoming emotionally vulnerable or a fear of losing their freedom or a fear of sex or even a fear of men in general.  Whatever the reason, these women will protect themselves by being attracted to the unattainable man and complain to their friends about how all the good men are taken.  You see it most often with school age girls.”

 

“In a way, this is like a pre-teen girl.  She feels an attraction to the opposite sex and a need or fascination for romance but she’s not yet mature enough to deal with the reality of a boyfriend.  So her room is wallpapered with pictures of rock stars and her romantic needs are filled safely by fantasy dates with her teen idols.  Most girls grow out of this and the fantasy dates are replaced by real dates with Billy, Biff or Scooter from high school.”

 

“These women are harmless because a guy will probably not even get a first date with them but I’ve seen men wasting a lot of time and energy pursuing these women.  The guys may know them at school or at work or through groups and frustrate themselves to no end trying to get their attention.  I remember one friend was totally in love with this girl at school.  He spent a month trying to get her attention.  He joined a hiking group she was in to be able to spend time with her and get to know her.  She was completely indifferent to his friendly advances.  I kept telling him she was a waste of time but he was undeterred.  Finally, he was making subtle inquiries about her and the word came back from a friend of hers.  She had a crush on…guess who?  She had a crush on a guy with a girlfriend.  I tried to explain it to the guy but he wouldn’t listen.”

 

“Okay, enough abnormal psych for one day.  We don’t want to become jaded…just aware and cautious.  Thanks for the salad and good luck with Kim tomorrow.  Let me know how it comes out.”

 

************************

 

The next Tuesday Michael and I got together after work to have dinner and he asked if I called Kim.  “Yes, I called her Monday and she seemed happy to hear from me.  I suggested we could meet for lunch sometime this week and she was enthusiastic.  We arranged to meet Wednesday at the same place we saw them last Friday.  It’s close to where she works and convenient for her.  I followed the template of keeping it casual and friendly.  That really makes it easy.  I wasn’t nervous or stressed out worrying about a rejection.  It was like calling a friend and seeing if they want to get together…Which is what it is.  So what’s the latest with Stephanie?”

 

“Things are great with Stephanie.  I called her today to say hi and ask how work was Sunday evening after our lunch…And to talk about going out later in the week.  So I told her I had some ideas but wanted to know if there was anything she was thinking of doing.  And bless her little heart, she didn’t disappoint me.  She said she was glad I asked because there’s a movie opening on Thursday and she really wants to see it with me.  Did you get the key words there?  She didn’t say she wanted to see it.  She said she wanted to see it with me.  Some would ask, what’s the difference?  But I’d say it’s a huge difference because it shows what she really enjoys is sharing experiences with me.  She’s more interested in the person than the event.  These are little clues a girl’s not selfish.  She thinks in terms of sharing.”

 

“It gets better though.  I half expected the movie to be a chic flick so I was surprised.  She wants to see From Paris with Love, the new John Travolta action adventure movie.  How great is that?  Stephanie likes action films.  Oh, and more, remember I thought she wanted me to know where she lived so we could go out together instead of meeting there?  She asked if I could pick her up so we could go together.”

 

“You really like Stephanie don’t you?  I don’t blame you.  She seems so down to earth and genuine.  I’m curious, are you calling her at home…You know, just calling her to talk?”

 

“That’s a good question.  I haven’t done that yet.  I only call to arrange our dates.  Calling more in the early dating phase can be dangerous because you start to look needy and whipped.  More than that, when you don’t call then women start wanting you to and they start thinking about you more and feeling a bit insecure.  Human nature is working in your favor.”

 

“I really enjoy Stephanie and think about her all the time.  I want to call her everyday just to say hi and see how she’s doing…It takes a lot of restraint not to call her.  I just feel as though after only a couple dates it’s too soon to start acting like we’re exclusive and committed.  Oh, and something else, she’s thinking the same thing.  She said something about that when we talked yesterday.  She said she was watching something on TV after work and wanted to call me because it was so funny and reminded her of one of our conversations.  She said she didn’t call because it was kind of late and she knew I get up early for work.  So she’s going through the same thing and thinks it’s too early to be acting like we’re exclusive.  Of course, she’s hinting that she’d like for us to call…just to talk.”

 

It was fun watching Michael’s little love affair develop but I wanted to continue our discussions of female behavior so I changed the subject.  “I have a question.  This has happened to me a few times over the years in school and after.  I see a girl…now she isn’t gorgeous.  She isn’t a 10 or a 9.  She’s a 6 or a 7.  Maybe she’s a little chunky in a nice way or maybe she’s average looking in the face but has a nice body.  Anyway, I find her attractive so I approach her to be friendly and see if I get a positive response.  Well, not only is her response not positive but she gives me an attitude like, How dare you think you’re good enough for me.  Don’t even think about asking me out.  So what’s up with that?  I mean I know I’m not Brad Pitt but that girl was no Jennifer Aniston either.”

“That’s funny.  Yes, those girls exist and you’re not the only one to have those encounters.  My theory on them is they really have a complex about wanting to be beautiful.  Like you say, they aren’t beautiful…but they’re almost beautiful and they’re always trying to close the gap.  They want to be the prettiest girl in the room so badly they keep telling themselves they are.  Consequently, what kind of men they date is very important to them.  They expect…and want…only the hunks to ask them out because they see themselves in the elite group.  If a hunk asks them out, it elevates them and validates their fragile self-image as a beautiful girl.  If an average guy asks them out, they take it as an insult.  It angers them because it suggests they aren’t as beautiful as they hope they are.  After all, no average guy is going to try to ask out someone who’s out of his league.”

 

“It’s interesting.  The almost beautiful girls will insult you but the truly beautiful girls won’t…They’re secure with themselves so feel no need to lash out at people.  Have you ever noticed in all those high school movies there’s always this group of girls and one of them is the school beauty queen.  She’s always surrounded by a posse of friends who hang with her and elevate themselves by being her friend.  They’re almost the queen but not quite.  They’re the wannabes.  It’s those wannabes who’re always the real insulting bitches.  They love putting people down.”

 

“You know, I ran into a girl like this about a year ago.  It was interesting.  She was maybe 24 or 25 year old.  She had beautiful blonde hair and a very pretty face but she was maybe 40 pounds overweight.  She carried it well because she was tall but it hurt her looks.  Looking at her face and hair and figure I’m guessing before she gained weight she’d been considered very beautiful all of her life…Grade school, Middle school, high school and college.  She was used to being a 10.  But, now she’s gained weight over the last 4 years and is dealing with the trauma of no longer being treated like the campus queen.  She doesn’t like the demotion to the ranks of average and when average men talk to her it reminds her she’s no longer the 10 she once was.  The prettier they are, the harder they fall.”

 

“I’ve never told you about my friend Dan.  I met Dan his first day on the job.  He’d been assigned to me for a few days as part of his orientation so we were working on a project together.  He was quiet and subdued which I chalked up to being his first day at a new job.  So we start talking and I find out he’s recovering from a hellish divorce.  His quiet demeanor was actually because of being emotionally battered and traumatized for the last 6 years with a horrific marriage and a divorce which almost destroyed him.  Dan’s a marine and tough as nails.  He’s probably the smartest guy I’ve ever known and one of the most charming and likeable guys I’ve ever known.  And yet, a woman had beaten this marine to his knees.  So I told him a few horror stories of my own and we started opening up to each other.  Dan needed someone to talk to about all of this and the fates put us working with each other.”

 

“So I started hearing about his wife, Myrna.  This woman is a piece of work.  She’s just trouble on steroids and a queen bitch.  She only liked sex…I should say used sex…to trap a guy.  Then she withheld sex to control him.  They’d gotten married because she was pregnant.  At the time, Dan thought it was an accident.  Later a friend of hers told him Myrna had made sure she got pregnant to trap him into marriage.  Myrna was pretty much delusional in her total focus on herself.  It was like she thought she was the Queen of England and the entire world were her subjects, all placed on this earth to serve her.  Everything she did was a double standard.  She expected everyone to follow her set of rules but those rules didn’t apply to her.  She wanted what she wanted even when it was completely unreasonable.  She’d justify everything she wanted with completely irrational logic.  Dan’s life with Myrna had been one lie after another as she could say anything to justify her actions and then not be held to that lie the next day.”

 

“I was horrified listening to Dan.  It was interesting, though, because his story started sounding exactly like a story I’d heard before.  Do you remember Stella, the bitch from hell my uncle was involved with?  It was as if my uncle and Dan were talking about the same woman.  I thought about it and then made an educated guess.  I said to Dan Myrna must be a Pisces and asked if her birth date was the end of February or March.  He got very quiet and looked at me for a few seconds and then said yes, she’s a Pisces.  He wanted to know how I knew that.  I told him a girlfriend had taught me about astrology and Pisces women have all of those traits…maybe not always to that extent but those are Pisces traits.  Then I told him about my uncle and Stella and he was really intrigued.  So Dan and I went over to my uncles one evening and they really hit it off.  It was something to watch.  They were telling each other stories and finishing each other’s sentences because their experiences were exactly parallel.  The next day Dan said talking with me and then with my uncle had been the therapy he needed.  He was feeling so low about himself because, just like Stella had done to my uncle, Myrna had Dan doubting his own perception of reality.  It was a relief for him to meet someone who’d gone through the same thing.”

“One time Dan and I were talking about red flags.  We were talking about all those warning signals we ignore when the girl is beautiful.  Of course, in hindsight Dan realizes all the signs were there for anyone to see.  That made me curious.  I asked him if any of his friends had tried to warn him about Myrna before they got married.  He thought for a few seconds and said, No, but HER friends did.  I was speechless and thought he was kidding.  He wasn’t kidding and told me how two of her best friends got him alone once and tried to warn him that Myrna was a lying bitch.  He said at the time he thought they must be jealous of her or wanted to get something going with him so he ignored it.  Looking back he thinks they liked him and thought he was a nice guy who didn’t deserve what Myrna had in store for him.  Then he laughed a little and told me her father had also tried to warn him.  The father didn’t come out and say it in so many words but he tried to tell him how spoiled and difficult Myrna was.”

 

 “One day Dan brought a picture of Myrna.  He said she was pretty but I was blown away by the picture.  She was breathtaking.  She’s about 5’ 10” with blonde hair and just a gorgeous face.  She has a voluptuous, hour glass figure.  Super models are tall and thin and you see them on the runway.  Myrna is tall and built.  She’s the kind of girl you’d see holding up the round card at a Las Vegas heavyweight title bout.  As Dan told me more and more of the things she did to him, I realized being that beautiful let Myrna think she had a license to do whatever she wanted…And that was probably true most of her life.”

 

“I asked Dan how long after the wedding it was before the sex ended.  He said it ended the first night of the honeymoon.  The years he was married were hell for him so he immersed himself in his work.  They were living in Las Vegas during the boom years so he was making a lot of money.  He walked away with nothing.  After they separated, Myrna moved to St. George, Utah which is close to Las Vegas.  She moved to Utah because that state is more beneficial to the woman in a divorce.  She was calculating.  She stole his business equipment, drained his accounts and put his vehicle in her name.  Many of those things she did illegally.  One thing she did was forge his signature on a tax return check.  Myrna was so beautiful and had gotten away with anything she wanted to do her entire life so in a way she hasn’t had to be smart and isn’t.  The thought she’d ever be held accountable had never crossed her mind.  The whole thing ended up in a series of arbitration hearings and was going badly for Dan.  Then a marine JAG Corp officer showed up to defend Dan at the last hearing.  He had all the evidence of the things Myrna had done illegally.  Suddenly, the judge saw the entire picture and Myrna was nailed to the wall.  She almost went to jail and had to pay back a lot of money to Dan.  He said that may have been the greatest day of his life to see Myrna finally held accountable for her actions.  He said she was so mad he thought she was going to lose it right there in court.”

 

“Dan has two sons with Myrna.  He has to be in contact with her so he keeps up on the continuing drama of her life.  During the separation she found a boyfriend which was designed to hurt Dan.  That’s another case of Myrna having sex with one man only because she can hurt another.  Then very quickly after the divorce she found some other guy and got married.  She had another child by this second husband.  Dan had to deal with the second husband when he saw his 2 kids and the second husband was always contentious with Dan.  Myrna had painted Dan as the worst person on earth with countless lies and fabrications about all the terrible things Dan supposedly did.  The second husband believed everything she told him and was a complete asshole to Dan.  It was like a year after that second marriage when the second husband called Dan for help.  The guy was separated from Myrna and now he was seeing the light.  He apologized to Dan for how he’d treated him and begged him to help him understand what Myrna had done to him.  It was the same story only a different guy.  And, it was the same right down to the reason they had to get married.  Myrna was pregnant and made the guy marry her.  He asked Dan if Myrna had gotten pregnant on purpose.  Dan told him that was what she’d done to him.”

 

“Some of these stories are pretty wild and the women involved are real psycho girls but those are true stories and those psycho girls get a hold of unsuspecting men all the time.  Men don’t see it coming because that kind of psycho behavior is so illogical we don’t imagine people doing it.  There’re women who marry men for completely psycho reasons like a woman might marry one man simply to get back at some other man.  Women will marry because they’re on the rebound.  It has nothing to do with feelings for the poor guy they’re marrying.  Some women get married because they hate men and live to punish them.  It’s not logical.  It’s not rational but they’re so filled with hate and anger and self-loathing that they don’t care how destructive it is to them and everyone around them.  Look at the story of Dan and Myrna.  She married again and again and each time it was the same story…”

 

“…All I can say is take heed because you don’t want one…Well, maybe you’d want one night with one because those psycho girls can have explosive passion when they’re seducing a man.  But, I’d only recommend that if you were reporting to the witness protection program in the morning.  Hey, remember the movie, Fatal Attraction with Michael Douglas?  That’s a great movie.  It’s good to know those stories because, though they aren’t common, it is common to run into mild versions of those same pathologies and we should all beware.”

 

“When I hear stories like these I want to have a vasectomy.  If a Las Vegas showgirl wanted something from me, I’d be toast.  None of my brain cells would be functioning on any level.  It’s horrifying to hear how easily a woman can take over a man’s life.  These stories really hit home how important it is to recognize the red flags.  I need to school myself in all those skills people use to read a person and recognize games and lies…also, to recognize sincerity and embrace it.”

 

Normal Abnormalities

 

“I think over the years behavior which used to be considered abnormal is now accepted.  Look at the behavior of young celebrities which has become the norm for young girls to look up to.  It’s common for women to be proud of being a bitch.  Men should have a zero tolerance for this behavior in women just like women should have no tolerance for it in men…”

 

“…Here’re the types of behavior I’m talking about:  Mood swings and fits of anger.  Both of those may be from a woman suffering from depression.  God bless her and I hope she finds help but she shouldn’t be dating or in a relationship with those problems.  Then there’re selfish women who’re only thinking about themselves.  They live in an entitlement world where they only see their self-interests.  Pity the poor guy who falls for this kind of woman…and they’re everywhere.  Some women are just unhappy all the time.  Somewhere they’ve learned this attitude or behavior and they see life as one irritation after another.  How about women who can’t admit when they’re wrong?  That’s really dysfunctional behavior and it’s so difficult to have any kind of constructive relationship with a person like that.  One behavior which is a giant red flag is a woman who gets defensive all the time.  It’s so difficult to be around a woman like this.  You’re terrified to say or do anything around these women.  If she’s driving you dare not warn her of a hazard.  It’s better to risk a wreck than to suffer her getting defensive and yelling at you for “criticizing” her driving…”

 

“…I mentioned this before but now being a bitch is acceptable.  I don’t think this is right.  I don’t treat people like that and I won’t be around a woman who does.  Here’s a pet peeve of mine, eating disorders.  That may seem like nothing when a girl’s 21 years old but you don’t want to be around a woman who whines constantly about food.  And I’m not just talking about an extremely thin or fat woman.  Often it’s women who’re at normal weight but they can’t enjoy eating a meal.  They have to feel guilty or whine about how fat they are or constantly be on some diet.  They’re constantly in a state of discontent about themselves.  These women are just not fun to be around.  Of course, often it’s the constant dieting and fasting which messes up their metabolism and body chemistry to the point their weight problems become systemic.  And worse, their bad nutrition causes emotional problems.  I have no problem being around a woman who’s 10 pounds overweight…and happily so.  She enjoys food and enjoys her life…”

“…I have one more to mention.  It’s an application of that old saying you’ll catch more flies with honey than with vinegar.  I’m talking about women who only know one way to get their husband or boyfriend to do something and that is to nag and yell and scream and bitch and get angry at him.  Am I the only person who thinks this is unacceptable behavior for people who’re supposedly in love?  I simply won’t accept it.  I don’t treat people that way and I don’t want to be treated that way.  I want to live in a world where a woman asks me nicely to do her a favor.  I want to live in a world where a woman comes over, sits on my lap, puts her arms around me, gives me a big kiss, tells me how handsome I am and whispers in my ear if I take out the garbage for her she’ll fix me the best dinner I’ve ever eaten.  Is that so much to ask?  Am I out of line here thinking a man and a woman could treat each other like that?”

 

“Wow, I think you’ve just written your manifesto.  This is the point in the movie where you walk out of the meeting and spontaneous applause breaks out.  You’re not out of line.  I think you’re right our culture has come to accept all those selfish behaviors as being normal.  They shouldn’t be accepted.  I need to hone my skills so I’m able to recognize all of the abnormalities, normal or otherwise.  I want to see those red flags before I wake up one day and find myself in a committed relationship with a woman who has unacceptable attitudes and behavior.  I want to be like that movie director you told me about who had the skills to see inside a person in seconds.”

 

Body Language

 

“Yeah, my uncle turned me on to those skills.  It’s like the show The Mentalist.  He uses all his powers of observation to understand people.  One important skill is Body Language.  One time I was sitting at an outdoor restaurant.  At a nearby table was a group of 6 or 8 college kids.  They were having a great time.  One girl caught my attention because she was especially social and flirtatious with everyone there.  Then a guy came late to join the group.  The flirtatious girl was sitting with her chair away from the table.  She had her legs open and with open arms called out the late comer’s name.  Her open arm gesture and the way she called his name was an obvious signal to come and give her a hug.  He approached her with his arms spread to hug her but at the last split second the girl closed her legs and pulled her elbows in to cover her breasts.  With her legs closed the guy couldn’t get very close to her and had to lean over.  She put her hands on his shoulders to prevent him from getting too close and gave him a Hollywood air kiss.  It was an amazing display of body language.  She was a girl who loved to flirt but couldn’t let a guy get too close.  Her body language was unmistakable.”

 

“Yeah, I’ve seen some things on TV about body language.  People cross their arms when they feel threatened.  They’ll also cross their arms if they don’t like a person or are uncomfortable with them.  On that show they showed a man and a woman sitting across from each other.  The man was leaning forward and reaching across the table with his arms in a gesture hoping to hold hands with her.  The woman was leaning back in her chair with her arms folded.  She was as far away from him as she could get and still be at the same table.  Her feelings towards the guy were obvious.”

 

“I’ve been on dates with girls who weren’t interested in me and when we walked together she maintained a distance.  There was no touching.  I’ve also been on first dates with girls and they were brushing against me and bumping against my side as we walked.  Obviously, they were comfortable with me.”

 

“Honestly, I don’t know enough about body language.  I need to find a book on Amazon and learn more.  People will lie with their words but their body language always tells the truth.”

 

Facial Language

 

“Facial expressions also reveal a person’s true feelings.  I have two stories about that.  The first one was a teachable moment.  My friend Jeff’s cousins were coming to town for a few days and he thought he’d try to fix me up with one of them.  The cousins were identical twins and quite pretty.  They were tall and blonde with nice figures.  So Jeff organizes for the 4 of us to go out to a casual dinner.  He can visit with his cousins and I can meet them.  I drive over to Jeff’s house to pick him up and we go to pick up the cousins in my car.  We arrive where they’re staying and knock on the door.  When the door opened the cousin looked at me and there was an immediate reaction written all over her face.  It only lasted for a split second but it was unmistakable.  Her facial expression was a frown of disappointment or disgust.  Again, it only lasted for a moment but it was obvious I wasn’t the man of her dreams.  I wasn’t handsome enough or tall enough…whatever it was, she wasn’t interested in me.  After the cousin’s facial reaction which was, in essence, her first impression she smiled and was cordial as Jeff and I came in to make the introductions.  Now even though I’d seen that first impression and knew what it meant I then went into denial and tried to rationalize I could win her over with the power of my personality.  After all, we’re told everyday a man’s character and personality are the most important things to a woman.  And it was because I was ignoring her facial language that I then made a terrible mistake.”

 

“As we’re standing there in the room discussing where we’re going to eat, the topic of how to get there comes up.  The cousins asked if they should follow us in their car or should we all go in one car.  I, in a moment of complete stupidity, said we can all go in my car.  I was probably thinking I could impress them with taking charge.”

 

“So we arrive to the restaurant and take a booth.  The cousins are both sitting on one side of the table and Jeff and I are sitting on the other side.  The cousin sitting opposite Jeff is locked in conversation with him.  They’re family catching up on what’s new since they last saw each other...that’s to be expected.  The cousin sitting opposite me has no intention of even speaking to me.  She must be terrified that this man who she’s not interested in will start making a move on her or ask her out or tell her he’s in love with her.  Her plan was to face Jeff and her sister to involve herself in their conversation and completely ignore me to the point when I tried to enter into a conversation with a simple question she pretended she didn’t hear me or thought I must be talking to someone else and wouldn’t even make eye contact with me.”

 

“Well, of course I wasn’t happy with the predicament I was in.  This cousin is totally insulting but we encounter insulting people every day.  The big problem is I’m trapped.  I drove everyone and so I can’t leave without leaving them stranded.  I could care less if the cousins had to walk home but Jeff was a good friend and shouldn’t be punished for trying to set me up with a pretty girl.  So I’m sitting there cursing myself for not taking that obvious facial expression seriously.  If I had I would’ve foreseen the possibility things wouldn’t go well.  I would’ve suggested the cousins follow in their car.  In that case I wouldn’t be in that predicament.  I could’ve feigned not feeling well, excused myself and gone home.  The evening didn’t get better.  I sat there being ignored through the entire dinner.  Gratefully, the evening did eventually end but I vowed to never ignore facial expressions again.”

 

“Believe it or not the exact same thing happened again some years later.  I met a girl on the internet and we arranged to go out for a drink.  The plan was for me to come by where she worked at closing and then we could go from there.  When I walked in where she worked I saw her face drop.  For a split second her facial expression revealed her first impression.  Her disappointment was written all over her face.  Apparently, I wasn’t the hunk she’d hoped for.  The disappointed look on her face only lasted for an instant and then she became quite friendly and up-beat but I’d learned my lesson.  I knew she wasn’t interested in me for more than friendly conversation.  So this time I suggested a place to go for a drink and she should follow me in her car.  She agreed and off we went.”

 

“The evening turned out to be pleasant.  I think mainly because I was totally relaxed and not worried about trying to make an impression.  I knew she wasn’t interested so I wasn’t trying to smooth talk her or hit on her.  She probably felt more relaxed because she wasn’t fending off unwanted advances.  Anyway, we had a drink or two and talked.  The best part of the evening was I wasn’t trying to figure out if I should make a move on her or ask her out again or get her phone number or…you get the idea.  I just enjoyed a night out with no stress and so did she.”

 

“Do you know what’s interesting about those experiences?  When I told the stories to friends, their reaction was complete disbelief in my interpretation of those facial reactions.  They pooh-poohed me for believing such things.  They all tried to assure me in both cases that what had happened didn’t happen at all…that I didn’t know what I was talking about.  I was reading things into the situation and overreacting.  They, who were not even there, knew better what had happened those evenings.”

 

“Detecting obvious facial reactions like fear or anger or disapproval is easy.  There’s a science to it which goes to the next level where they read your blinking patterns and facial twitches.  It’s yet another topic I’d love to study more.  I guess I’ll be spending more time looking for books at Amazon.”

 

Birth Order

 

“Birth order is an interesting science which can help predict personality traits.  People use it to help determine compatibility for couples.  If you do a Google search on Birth Order Compatibility you’ll find a lot of interesting articles.  Basically, it divides people by whether they’re an oldest, middle, youngest or only child.  For example, birth order theory says the youngest child is creative, energetic, thrives on attention and can be competitive as a result of being compared to their older siblings.  It also says because they’re the youngest, they tend to be spoiled or expect to be spoiled and can mature less quickly than others.  And, that all makes perfect sense.”

 

“Then birth order can become more specific with details like the gender of the subject and the siblings.  For example, the youngest tend to be spoiled or expect to be spoiled so if the youngest is a girl with 2 older brothers then you have a girl who probably thinks she’s a princess who should be taken care of.  She grew up with 2 older brothers and a father and mother watching over her and doting on her.  That’s basically her view of the world.  And, that all makes perfect sense.”

 

“Things like birth order are very interesting and probably have validity to one extent or another.  If nothing else it’s a good way to pass the time on a first date.  You ask about her family and siblings.  You show an interest in her.  It’s nice conversation.  And, you’re gaining an insight into her personality traits.”

 

Astrology

 

After a pause Michael asked, “Do you know anything about Astrology?”  I must have reacted to this question because Michael laughed and then said, “Your facial expression just gave you away.  You rolled your eyes like you think it has no merit.  Don’t apologize.  I did the same thing when my uncle brought it up.  He’d just started telling me about Stella and in the description of her he referred to her saying she was a Pisces.  To him that was very descriptive but to me it meant nothing and I’d always thought Astrology was a myth so I rolled my eyes when he said it.  He smiled and told me I should learn about it if I ever wanted to understand women.  I put it in the back of my mind but never looked into it.”

 

“Then I met Vickie and the day we met she invited me over to dinner.  She was staying with her best friend’s mom.  I went over to her house and we had a nice dinner and then after dinner the mom said she wanted to do my Astrological Chart.  So she asked me my date and time of birth and then asked my mother’s birthday and my father’s birthday and she did all these calculations.  Now keep in mind the mom had only met me 2 hours ago and Vickie had only met me 8 hours before.  The mom began to tell me what my chart said.  She told me more about myself with dead on accuracy than I even knew.  I was amazed but then she proceeded to describe my parents to a tee.  She described perfectly my relationship with my mom and dad and their relationship.  Again, I was dumbstruck.  I couldn’t believe how accurate Astrology had been at telling my personality traits.”

“Most people only know their daily horoscope from the paper which tells them it’s a good day for trying something new or some such thing.  I’ve never paid any attention to that but I’ve found Astrology very useful to understand a person’s personality.  Vickie and I dated for a long time and she taught me the different signs and their personality traits.  Astrology helped me in a lot of ways.  It taught me how different people are.  I’d grown up thinking everyone must be alike with similar motives and desires.  When I found out people are actually different it made me more tolerant and accepting.  A Taurus can be stubborn.  A Virgo has to be right all the time.  A Leo has to be in charge.  An Aquarius is a free spirit.  When I learned these different traits were just part of a person’s make up, it made it easier to understand their actions and be more tolerant of their little quirks.  For example, if I had a Leo friend who wanted to be in charge of something I understood it was simply a Leo being a Leo.”

 

“The thing which really got me intrigued was when I found out different signs have different attitudes about sex.  The realization I could learn a girl’s sign and have an indication if she was passionate or cold was like someone had just given me the power of mind reading.  When Vickie started teaching me about sex and astrology I became an “A” student.  I started back testing what I’d learned.  I started remembering the birthdays of old girlfriends to see if what Astrology said about them was correct and it always was.  I even called a few old girlfriends out of the blue to ask them their birthdays.”

 

“I have two books which have been useful to me for reference.  The best for a quick reference is The Bedside Astrologer Companion by Francesca Stuart.  It’s out of print but you can still get it on Amazon.com.  It’s a book about women written for women.  For example it’ll describe the Libra woman and then tell her compatibility with men of all the different signs.  It’ll explain her positive traits, her negative traits, her strengths, her desires and her love style.  It’s a very good book.”

 

“You can get the second book on Amazon.com also.  It’s much more in depth but is mostly about the sexuality of the different signs.  That book is Sex Signs by Judith Bennett.

 

“I mean what man wouldn’t like to know more about a woman’s sexuality when he’s first dating her?  Astrology has given me yet another insight into a woman’s sexual needs.  Honestly, you should order that first book from Amazon.com.  It’s a fun read.”

 

“I’ve got a lot of reading to do.  Knowledge is power though.  Learning about people and trusting your instincts is really important.  It’s important for dating and everything we do.    Hey, Stephanie always works on Friday doesn’t she?  So are we on for Friday after work?”  Michael could see no reason why not.  Our Friday at 5pm for a beer and dinner had become one of our favorite pastimes.  We agreed to confirm it by email.

 

************************

Books recommended in this chapter:

The Stranger Beside Me by Ann Rule

The Bedside Astrologer Companion by Francesca Stuart

Sex Signs by Judith Bennett

Movies recommended in this chapter:

The Wedding Singer

Spanglish

Body Heat

Prozac Nation

Fatal Attraction





Chapter 7  Sex

Michael confirmed our usual Friday at Five.  It was fun to start off the weekend with a beer and a casual meal.  No wonder they named a restaurant Fridays.  We met in the entry and got a table.

 

“Michael, before we talk about anything else, I went on Amazon and found books on body language by Gregory Hartley.  The title I remember is, I Can Read You Like a Book but he’s written many books on the subject.  I read reviews on all of them.  In his books he lists signs a girl is into you.”

 

  1. She tilts her head.  If a woman feels comfortable with a man, she’ll tilt her head.  A tilt in any direction…right, left or down are all a good sign.

  2. She’ll mirror his actions.  If a woman is drawn to a guy, she’ll instinctively copy him.  This could be in the form of shifting her body, picking up a glass to drink or even blinking.  He suggested to lean forward and see if she leans closer too as a test.

  3. She twirls her hair.  If a girl begins twirling or playing with her hair, it’s a good sign she’s interested and is subconsciously trying to attract you.

  4. She gets a glow.  When a woman is attracted to someone, blood flows to her face, causing her cheeks to redden.  Her lips and eyelids may also become fuller.

  5. Her pupils dilate.  When a woman is attracted to a man her pupils will dilate.

 

“Now to me most of those are subtle.  Twirling her hair would be easy to spot or tilting her head but judging a glow or dilated pupils would take practice for me to identify.  In contrast, his signs a girl is NOT into you are pretty obvious…maybe even blatant so a guy has no excuse if he misses these signs.  Sadly, I’ve experienced every one of them from girls and the signs were completely accurate.”

 

  1. She crosses her arms.  This body language reads loud and clear.  She’s closing herself off from the man.

  2. She places her bag between you two.  She’s subconsciously placing a barrier between the two of you and this is a bad sign.

  3. She speaks at a rapid-fire pace.  Conversation between people who are attracted to each other typically slows to about three-quarter speed and softens in tone.  It may be that she’s nervous at the beginning of a date and speaking rapidly.  But, if she’s still talking fast by the end of the date this is a bad sign.

  4. She has a chin-up smile.  Not all smiles are a good sign.  A soft, chin-down smile means she’s attracted to you.  But, a stiff, raised-chin smile is a very bad sign.  This raised-chin smile means she’s not interested in you and may even dislike you.

 

Michael asked to see the print out of my notes I’d brought with me.  I anticipated his interest and told him that copy was for him.  “Those are really interesting and useful clues.  A poker player would call them ‘tells.’  I’ve seen those bad signs before too…and not just from girls.  I’ve seen them among co-workers.”  We exchanged a few body language stories and then our beer arrived.

 

I asked Michael how the movie was.  He said it was good and would tell me all about it but first wanted to hear about my lunch with Kim.  “Lunch was good.  I enjoyed talking with Kim.  We talked about all sorts of things.  She likes movies.  She plays tennis and golf.  She had a boyfriend in college but it ended…That got me curious but I didn’t press for more information.  If she brings it up again I’ll ask a probing question like, Was the break up tough for you?  That sounds like a question from a concerned friend when I’m actually digging for all the dirty details.  She’s a middle child and has a brother and a sister.  She’s an Aries so I need to find out what that means.  Her facial expressions were always charming and positive.  Her body language was also positive.  She leaned forward when we talked and when we walked out to the street she was close enough to brush against me and touched my arm when we said our goodbyes.”

 

“As we were saying our goodbyes I said, That was fun.  We should get together for dinner sometime.  She enthusiastically said she’d like that so I told her I’d call next week so we could set something up.  She said, Great, I look forward to it.  The lunch went very well in every way.  The best part was I felt confident.  I felt like I knew what I was doing at every step…Starting with choosing a casual lunch for the first date.  That removed all pressure and stress from the date.  We were just two friends getting together.  I enjoyed the conversation more too because it had a purpose.  I wasn’t trying to impress her.  I was trying to learn things about her I could use as clues about who she really is.  The best part was how relaxed I was asking her out for a second date.  I was relaxed because I really wasn’t asking.  I was simply suggesting we get together again.  There was little chance of rejection.  Even if she didn’t want to go out again, she wouldn’t say no.  She’d simply be vague and less than enthusiastic.  I’d get the message.  But, that didn’t happen because she was enthusiastic.  It also helped that I suggested we go out during next week instead of throwing the dice and trying to get a date for this weekend.  We were having lunch on Wednesday and asking her out for Friday or Saturday was high risk.  She could have a date.  She could have plans.  She could decide I was in a hurry and desperate.  She could conclude I have nothing else to do but wait for a date with her.  I don’t want to risk any of those things so I suggested next week and let her draw the conclusion I already have plans for the weekend…Sorry, I’m going on and on…I guess I just want you to know I’m learning.”

 

Michael gave me a broad smile and said, “Good job.  It really is different when you have a plan and understand the reason behind that plan.  You can be relaxed and calm when you know what you’re doing.  Well, let me tell you about the latest with Stephanie because it involves you.  Last night she suggested you and I come by Applebee’s tonight because there’s a girl she works with who’d like to meet you…What do you think of that?”

 

“You’re kidding…Interesting.  Did this girl see me when we were there or is she just going on Stephanie’s description?”  Michael said he didn’t know so I had some more questions for him.  “Do you think this is really just a ploy by Stephanie to find out what you’re doing on Friday night?”

 

Michael laughed, “That’s always a possibility and I’m sure Stephanie wants to see me but my instinct is there really is a girl who’s available and would like to meet you.  Do we dare double date?  They get off around 10pm so if we go over to meet her, we may have to be ready for them promoting going out for a drink.  We’d better have something in mind.”

 

I pondered my new found popularity for a while and then remembered I wanted to hear about Michael’s date so I asked him to tell me all about it.  “Yeah, we had a great time last night.  I picked her up at 6 and the movie started at 7:15 so we decided to eat something fast near the Cineplex.  Stephanie’s really fun to be with.  Just eating at the food court in the mall is entertaining.  I wanted a falafel and so did she but she also wanted stir fry.  So she made me promise to share mine…and was laughing the whole time at how silly she was being.”

 

“We both liked the movie a lot.  It was just good action/adventure with humor thrown in.  The best part was seeing Stephanie’s reactions to the movie.  She’s so animated.  She laughed out loud, she screamed, she slumped and hid her eyes with her hands and grabbed my arm when it was suspenseful.  She was totally immersed in the movie experience.  I love that.  Have you ever gone to a movie with someone who just sits there expressionless?  So after the movie I suggested we go to Cheesecake Factory to share a desert but Stephanie had another plan.”

 

“Stephanie said she had something special for me at her apartment.  It was still kind of early but she wanted to take me home and give me desert.  We got to her apartment, she turned on some nice music and then she told me to hang on because she wanted to change.  She disappeared into her bedroom and came back with a pair of sweat pants and a t-shirt on.  Then she spent about 10 minutes in the kitchen preparing my desert.  I tried to come into the kitchen to talk to her but she hustled me back to the couch saying it was a surprise for me.  Finally, she brought me a slice of Key Lime pie and a raspberry, herbal tea.  I made a big fuss over it and she deserved it because she’d remembered on our first date I mentioned I love Key Lime pie and I also mentioned I love raspberries.  You see it’s little things that make a girl special.  She could’ve served some ice cream which had been in the freezer for a month and I’d have been happy but she’s a girl who enjoys doing things for people.”

“The pie was delicious and I made animated expressions and sounds to show my pleasure.  She was thrilled I liked it.  So we’re on the sofa and we talked a little about the best parts of the movie and got to that awkward quiet moment where we both know we want to kiss.  She’d strategically sat very close to me so we only had to lean over a little and we shared an amazing, slow and soft kiss.  We necked on the couch for about 10 minutes and she asked me to wait there.  She went into the bedroom, came back with a comforter, spread it out on the carpet and said, Let’s be here.  We lay down together and continued kissing for a long time.  Then she had me lay on my stomach and straddled my legs to give me a back rub.  She just touched me softly all over.  It was very sensual.  Then she had me roll over and continued to straddle me.  I put my hands on her waist and she started rubbing my chest with her hands.  Then she looked at me and placed my hands on her butt and leaned over to kiss me.  I’m in heaven.  Stephanie feels great all over and I’m in sensory overload.  Then she sat up and slid forward just enough so she could tell how aroused I was.  Again, she looked at me as she was slowly moving her hips.  She took my hands and brushed them against her breasts…just for a few seconds and then she came down to start kissing me again.”

 

“We did this for about an hour.  We were fully clothed but it was fantastic.  Now it was about 11pm and she whispered, This is killing me too.  I know you get up early and have to go but call me when you get home so I know you’re safe.  She took me to the door and kissed me goodbye.  I called her when I got home and she thanked me and said good night.  It was a great date and Stephanie planned the whole thing.  She wanted to spend time alone with me and planned the desert at her apartment.  She’s imaginative.  I like that.”

 

Michael paused and then said, “Okay, it’s 6:30.  Are you up for Applebee’s and meet the friend?”  I said it sounded like a plan and off we went.  We met up again in the entrance and asked if we could be in Stephanie’s section.  She came over with our water and menus and politely told me she had a friend she thought I might like to meet and would it be okay if they came over when she had a moment.  I told her of course we’d enjoy meeting her friend.  Stephanie was as bubbly and energetic as ever.  Also, she looked happier than ever.  Good for her.

 

Michael and I ordered and chatted while we waited for our food.  Stephanie was fun to watch.  She never neglected her tables but she always managed to stop by ours.  It was her way of thanking Michael for coming by to see her.  Then the salads came and Stephanie had a girl with her.  Michael and I stood and told Kristin how nice it was to meet her.  Kristin was charming to both of us and seemed like a nice girl.  She was plain in a way but then pretty when you really looked at her.  She had a nice figure too.

 

Michael and I finished our salads just as the main course arrived.  Stephanie set down the plates and asked if we needed anything else and then she lingered.  Michael and I looked at each other and smiled.  We knew Stephanie wanted to ask what we thought of Kristin but didn’t want it to sound like we were in Jr. High.  I spoke up and told Stephanie that Kristin seemed like a very nice girl and was quite pretty.  Stephanie smiled but still lingered at our table.  She was trying to ask something but wasn’t sure how to ask.  This time Michael interrupted and said he had an idea.  He asked if maybe the four of us could go out later and have a drink.  Stephanie was very happy that Michael and I’d read her mind.  She was about to go back to the kitchen and I asked if when she clears the plates she could bring Kristin with her so we could invite her in person.  Stephanie loved that and off she went.  Michael liked that too and gave me a gold star.

 

We ate and discussed some of the places we could take the girls.  We decided on a nice place which had good music but it wasn’t loud and we could talk.  The girls came by to clear the plates and we told them we were thinking of going to Margarita’s later and asked if they’d like to come along.  They loved the idea and we agreed to meet back at Applebee’s at 10pm.

 

Michael and I both went home to shower and change.  I also stopped at an ATM to get some cash.  I had no idea where the evening could go and wanted to be prepared for anything.  We got back to Applebee’s and the girls were almost ready to go.  We waited and then everyone knew where Margarita’s was so off we went.

 

The evening went very well.  We all ordered margaritas and that pretty much set the mood.  The four of us got along quite well laughing and telling stories.  The girls were having a good time.  I think it was a treat for them to go out with us and unwind.  Kristin was comfortable with the group and she spent most of her time talking with me.  She seemed as interested in getting to know me as I was in getting to know her…a good sign.  After two margaritas the girls were ready to go.  On the way out I told Kristin I enjoyed meeting her and suggested we get together for lunch sometime.  She smiled, said she’d like that and put her number into my phone’s memory.  At her car she gave me a hug and thanked me again for such a nice time.

 

********************************

 

Sunday morning I got a call from Michael.  He wanted to get together that afternoon for an early dinner.  That was good for me because I needed to get my strategy straight with both Kim and Kristin.

That evening Michael came over with some take out.  I had a salad made and beer chilling in the refrigerator.  I told him Kristin was enthusiastic with my suggestion we go out to lunch sometime and wants me to call her so I intended to call her Monday.  I also reminded him I intended to call Kim Monday to suggest we get together for dinner sometime after work.  At that point I got down to specifics and said, “So, I’m on the verge of juggling two women and need to bounce some things off of you.  Tomorrow I’m going to call Kim and suggest we go out.  My plan is to suggest we get together for dinner sometime after work.  That way it’s still casual and simple.  What I know about Kim is she’s a middle child, she likes sports, she had a boyfriend in college but that ended after they graduated and she’s an Aries.  So, what does all this tell me?”

 

Michael paused to collect his thoughts and then said, “The middle child, in general is calm and rational because they grow up keeping the peace with the oldest and youngest.  They tend not to create the problems but instead are the people pleasers.  The Aries woman is strong, energetic, confident, positive, fun and passionate though they can be impatient.  They tend to be independent and love their freedom.  The Aries woman likes strong men and is turned off by weak, boring, petty and cheap men.  That’s your general profile for an Aries woman.  So the pressure’s on.  Don’t be boring…but then we knew that anyway.”

 

I pondered this information.  It would motivate me to be decisive and confident with Kim.  Then I asked about Kristin.  “Okay, Kristin is a Cancer and also a middle child.  She likes sports and fitness but not in an aggressive way.  I think she’d prefer to share a jog with a friend than compete in a foot race.  She likes her job but is still finishing school and she’s studying finance…we share that interest.  What say you about Kristin?”

 

“First, I’m curious.  You’ve spent an equal amount of time with each one.  Does one capture your interest more than the other?”

 

I thought about that and said, “Physically, I like them both.  They’re both pretty.  I think Kim has the prettier face and I like Kristin’s body better.  Kristin has a bit of shy vulnerability going for her I find attractive but Kim does have an enthusiastic energy about her which is quite seductive.  At this point, I’m probably more turned on, be it logical or not, by Kim.”

 

“Okay, the Cancer woman is sensitive, sentimental and her love relationship is very important to her.  Therefore she can be cautious.  She’s home loving and great at making a house into a home.  They’re very good with money so studying finance is perfect for her.  She loves romance, is warm and is the type who likes to touch and cuddle.  She’s turned off by arrogant, bad mannered, cold, detached men.  She needs to feel loved to be at her best.  If she feels neglected it can bring her down.”

 

“You’re telling me Kim and Kristin are different.  That’s interesting.  I hope after hearing this I can maintain my objectivity and still come to my own conclusions instead of fitting everything they do into those templates.  It’s interesting though to wrap my arms around the idea that women are different.  Logically, we know this but I think when we’re young we just see all women as being the same inside.  They just have different physical appearances so choose on that basis because everything else is going to be equal.  Unfortunately, I/we/men blind ourselves to those differences and only look for the right physical appearance.  All we see is the beauty on the outside.  Okay, I’ll call them both tomorrow and enjoy the ride.”  Then I remembered I wanted to see how things were going with Stephanie and I’d been talking all evening about my interests.  “So tell me about Stephanie.  Did you do anything with her Friday after Margarita’s?”

 

Michael took a deep breath and then said, “Yes, we did and it went great.  Sometimes I have to pinch myself to see if I’m dreaming because Stephanie’s my dream girl.  She’s always happy, treats me like I’m her prince and is always affectionate.  What more can a guy ask for?  And, all that actually brings me to comment on what you just said because Stephanie’s pretty and has a nice body but she’s not gorgeous.  She’s not a 10…or a 9.  But her personality and energy and her soul are so attractive to me that she’s become a 10 to me.  It’s like she wears her beautiful personality on her face and upgrades herself to a 10.  Maybe it’s also the connection we have.  Maybe we bring the best out in each other.  Maybe other guys don’t bring out her personality.  Anyway, as far as I’m concerned I’m dating a 10…for the right reasons.”

 

“Okay, back to your question, after Margarita’s she asked me to come over.  When we got to her apartment she took my coat and set me down on the couch.  She put on some water to make tea, turned on some nice music, turned down the lights and then went in to change.  She came back with her sweat pants and t-shirt on and had her comforter and a blanket with her.  She made the tea and brought it over to the comforter and we lay down next to each other on our stomachs to drink our tea and talk.  We talked and sipped our tea for 20 minutes and then the romantic setting she’d created took its natural course.”

“We put our tea cups aside and began kissing and making out.  We pulled the blanket over us and some clothes came off but not past the underwear.  Basically, we did everything you would do with all your clothes off but we didn’t have intercourse.  We just imitated it.  Honestly, it was better than most sex I’ve had.  The anticipation drove us both crazy.  It was like 2 hours of foreplay.  We touched each other everywhere and gave each other sensual massages with like full body rubs.  We imitated lots of positions and would both get so aroused and then we’d back off and start again.  It was amazing.”

 

“So the whole time I’m wondering if there’s going to be a move to the bed but after a while I realized this was where we were going to stay.  Stephanie isn’t ready for sex or me sleeping over…and she’s right about that.  We’ve only been dating for like 3 weeks and that next step is full on commitment for her so she needs to know I’m the right person and she needs to know the next step is full on commitment from me too.  So now it’s getting close to 2am and we relaxed for a time and looked at each other.  Then we both smiled because we knew it wasn’t time yet to sleep over and I had to go.  So I said, I don’t want to but I have to go.  I could see how relieved she was I wasn’t going to start begging her to let me stay so I could try to score.  She kissed me and whispered, Thank you for a perfect evening.  I told her it was her plan, I just listen well.”

 

“So we got dressed, she took me to the door and asked me to call her so she knew I was safely in bed.  And there you have it…another perfect date with a perfect girl.  And for my part, honestly, I have no complaints.  The romance and almost sex we’re having is fantastic.  It’s just so sensual.  The anticipation for both of us makes what’s to come that much better and we both know it.”

 

Now Michael paused, collected his thoughts and really got animated.  “Let me ask if this has happened to you because it’s happened to me.  I start dating a girl and everything is comfortable and friendly but not much romance.  She’s affectionate and walks close to me but not much more than that and not even kissing.  This goes on for 3 weeks and then one night we’re sitting on the couch, we kiss and she asks if I want to go to bed and make love.  What’s up with that?  I have to ask myself how important romance is to this girl.  I have to wonder just how special or important sex is to her.  She’s offering me sex like asking if I want another cup of coffee.  I start wondering if maybe she would’ve gone for it on the first date if I’d asked.  And in one sense that’s what every guy wants but then on the other hand that’s all it is…Sex with no connection.  It’s just that with girls like that I get the feeling sex is no big deal to them.  I mean if you dated a girl who was a total hot nymphomaniac and you had sex on the first date, at least with her she’d be rubbing your leg and purring like a cat all evening so at least there’d be 4 hours of foreplay and anticipation.  With this other girl it was like, Well, what do you want to do now?  For her, sex or watch TV is 6 of one and half a dozen of the other.  It’s like she let me have sex with her just to get it out of the way because it’s expected.  And after the novelty of first time sex is past, will she have any sexual desire?  For me, that girl quickly became a starfish…She looked pretty but she just lay there on the bottom.”

 

At this I had to laugh.  “That’s a great analysis of the different…uh…sexual appetites of women.  You really explained it well.  I think most men…I know I am…are so focused on the score that they only think in those terms.  If we scored, then that’s a good thing.  It’s not until we encounter a truly sexy, sensual, romantic girl we realize all we’ve been missing.”

 

“You know, you bring up something I’ve long thought about.  I don’t think I’ve ever shared my theory on this with anyone.  I agree with you that men look at a girl’s sexuality purely on a physical level.  They look at a girl and if she has tits and a butt and a pussy then she’s good to go.  Everything else about her sexuality is going to be the same or they can shape it to match what they want.  My theory is every one of us is born already hard wired with our sexual appetite…a perfect term, by the way.  So specifically with women I think women are born with their sexual desires already in place and those desires can vary from can’t live without it to have no interest in it.”

 

“I’ve come up with demographics to go with my theory.  I figure 25% of women are sexual animals who love it and can’t live without it.  Another 25% enjoy sex but it doesn’t control their life and they can go a few nights without it.  The third group of 25% of women don’t hate sex but can take it or leave it…and actually, would just as soon leave it.  The last 25% of women hate sex and beyond a possible curiosity when they were young want nothing to do with it.  Now all of these descriptions are based on women in long term committed relationships.  So the normal boredom and routine of a relationship can bring out their true interest or lack of interest in sex.  When a girl’s young and curious her interest in sex may be heightened.  When a girl’s in the excitement of a new romance her sexual desire may be heightened.  So I’m removing those influences from these descriptions.  For the man with girls from these 4 demographics, what we have is a range from a very happy guy, though maybe often exhausted, to a miserable and frustrated guy who takes a lot of long showers.”

 

I was fascinated by what Michael had just laid out to me.  I thought back through my experiences with different girlfriends and realized what he’d described was so true.  There are women who like sex and women who don’t.  But now I had a question.  “Michael, you said a woman is born with her sexual desires.  Aren’t there those who say it’s all because of their life experiences as they grow up…Their relationship with their father or potty training, yadda, yadda, yadda?”

 

“I have an amazing story to tell you about that.  This was actually the genesis of my theory on women’s sexuality.  When I was just out of college I had a wonderful girlfriend, Anita.  She was young.  She was barely 18 when we met.  She was very smart and mature.  She was one of those girls who wanted to be mature and grow up.  She was bored with the kids her age and so found older guys attractive.  We met at the gym and were immediately attracted to each other.  We started seeing each other on kind of like non-dates.  I was concerned what her parents were going to think of a guy out of college with their daughter.  It turned out they were relieved.  Anita had started to equate growing up with experimenting with drugs and worse, decided she’d outgrown school and was letting her grades slide.  She went to a very good private school so her teachers alerted her parents.  Anita and her mom were very close so when they talked Anita realized she was headed down a wrong path.  It was about that time we met.  My interest in fitness and health rubbed off on her.  Also, she seemed to be inspired with how I’d just graduated from college and was motivated by my job and career.  Her mom noticed a dramatic change in Anita’s attitude towards everything.  She was happy at home, motivated with her studies and became the perfect big sister to her young sibling.  Best of all, the great relationship she’d always had with her mom was back.  Anita started telling her mom about the great guy in her life and how he was healthy and responsible and inspired her to study so she could go to a good college.  She told her mom about the conversations we had and that I’d challenge her to be her best in everything.  Well, bottom line was her mom was so ecstatic with her daughter getting back on track at such an important time in her life that the fact I was 5 years older didn’t bother her.”

 

“Okay, so where is this going?  I needed to give you that background for the important part of the story.  Anita and I became full on boyfriend and girlfriend and started a sexual relationship.  Being with Anita was wonderful.  She was the most sensual and affectionate lover on earth.  She loved sex for all the right reasons.  She loved the emotional connection, the touching, the passion, the holding, the pillow talk, the conversations and the complete surrender.  She loved what we did before.  She loved what we did during and she loved what we did after.  After she climaxed she’d curl up in my arms with her head on my chest and close her eyes in complete ecstasy and satisfaction.  She was amazing.”

“Now for me, Anita was the best relationship I’d ever had and far and away the best sex partner in my life.  I was really amazed by how comfortable and natural she was with sex at such a young age.  She seemed to have no hang ups about it at all.  Everything for her was natural and loving.  She never spoke about her past sex life but she did tell me she’d lost her virginity when she was 13.  As I said, she was mature for her age and wanted to grow up.  Still, I was fascinated by her comfort with sex and finally I asked her how it was such a young girl was so comfortable with sex and had such a healthy attitude about it without the usual hang-ups or guilt or games.”

 

“So this is what Anita told me and this is almost word for word.  Anita said when she was 11 years old her parents got divorced.  Anita, her little sister and her mom moved into a one bedroom apartment in a small resort town where there was an active night life.  Her mom was young, pretty and sexually active so she’d often come home with men who would spend the night.  Anita and her sister slept in the living room on a fold out sofa but the walls were paper thin and she could hear her mom having sex in the bedroom.  So at an early age Anita learned sex was something to enjoy and not something to be ashamed of.”
