***********************

 

Saturday afternoon I gave Michael a call to see if he wanted to play tennis.  It was one of those perfect June days that needed to be enjoyed.  We played until 5pm and then went to a Mexican restaurant.  We sat outside under an umbrella.  Play tennis and then have a beer with a friend…Priceless.

Michael asked how the date with Kim had gone the night before.  I filled him in on the restaurant and then having drinks after dinner at a nearby pub.  Then I said, “We had a good time on the date.  We laughed and had great conversations…like we always do.  Then we went home to her apartment and things were as hot as ever in bed.  I didn’t sleep over…She was subtle about it but let me know she didn’t want me to stay.  As I was getting ready to go I wasn’t sure how to leave it.  Was I to say I’d call her?  Should I have made a date for a certain day?  Anyway, I asked her about today, Saturday.  I asked if she wanted to get together.  She told me she was getting together with friends and she’d call me Sunday…maybe we’ll play tennis.  That’s why I’m here with you.  I have the night off.”

 

Then I asked Michael how Stephanie was doing.  He smiled and said, “Stephanie is great.  You haven’t seen her in a few weeks.  She’s prettier than ever.  She’s happily in love and has a glow about her.  Also, she’s going to the gym with a morning workout class.  It’s nice to see a happy girl.  I like to think I’m part of the reason.  Actually, Stephanie grew up in a happy home.  She’s a country girl.  She grew up in a small town in farm country with 5 brothers and sisters.  Her father drills wells and everyone in the family works hard.  She graduated from high school and went a few semesters to community college but lost interest.  She’s been working ever since and then moved away from home a few years ago.  Stephanie is a smart girl.  As a high school graduate she’s smarter than any girl I knew in college.  She knows more about life than most anyone I’ve ever met.  She has the most important wisdom of all and that’s how to be happy in life...Of course she’s still hinting about our relationship…And I’m still being evasive.”

 

That reminded me of something and I said, “Speaking of relationships, I read an article which has relationship advice for men and for women.  One of the points for women was something you’ve said a few times.  The article told women that men are simple and all they want is to be a hero.  Treat him like he’s a hero and he’ll do anything for you.  Yet, women can’t seem to figure that out.  They’ll nag and criticize, but women won’t take 2 minutes out of their day to greet their man at the door, give him a big hug and tell him he’s a stud.  It chastised women for not doing the simplest thing to make their man happy.”  Michael enjoyed that and then asked about the article’s advice for men.  I recalled as many of those as I could…

 

“…One of them was to express your feelings through actions and not words.  It’s better to show a woman you love her, than to tell her you love her.  It said a man constantly telling a woman he loves her starts to look desperate and weak.  For example, you showed Stephanie how you felt about her when you took her to the concert.”

 

“I liked this next one too.  The article said when it’s time to pay attention, do it totally.  When you have other things to do, let her know and do them.”

 

“Another one was more of a warning to never make comparisons to other women or past relationships.  Compliment her looks, her cooking, her lovemaking and everything else but don’t ever make a comparison.  No matter how innocent it may begin, it won’t end well.”

 

“The last one was interesting because it was tough advice.  The article said, Don’t ever give anything away because you can’t give things away forever.  Sooner or later you’ll have to stop and when you stop no one will remember what you gave…They’ll only remember you stopped.  They gave some examples.  One was giving a no limits credit card to your wife.  Another one was giving so much of your time to your girlfriend that your performance at work suffers or you neglect doing the things you love.  The point was you have to put limits on everything before it gets out of control.  Don’t think you can be generous and go overboard in whatever thing this month and then next month we can all tighten our belts.  It won’t work.”

 

Michael smiled at that one and said, “That’s funny.  My uncle told me the same thing.  He told me his lawyer had given him that advice during his divorce.  He told me to never forget it because we all want to be too generous and it’s not good for the giver or the receiver…”

 

“…Email me the link to that article because those are the things I’ve been thinking about with regards to being in a relationship.  I adore Stephanie and want to please her.  I’d love to give her everything she wants…And that’s dangerous.  Too easily I could end up turning my entire life over to her.  Would she take advantage of that?  Could cute, sweet, innocent Stephanie take advantage of me?  Well, I doubt it, but Stephanie is a woman and women have a way of wanting to control every aspect of a man’s life…”

 

“…Now, I understand I’ll lose a certain amount of freedom in a relationship.  We go into the relationship willing to give up that freedom because of all we think we’ll gain being with the girl we love.  But, here’s what men need to do.  Before they enter a relationship, they should decide what they’re going to give up and what they’re not willing to give up…and stick to that.  You see most women are benevolent and want their man to be happy but it’s just the nature of women to control more and more of a man’s life until they have total control.  To a woman, a man is this great, endless resource.  He goes to work and helps pay the bills, which is normal and expected.  But then she figures out he can take out the garbage and clean the gutters.  Then she takes him to the mall and he’s really useful carrying the bags from Gap while she tries on clothes at Nordstrom’s.  Then she takes him to Home Depot and figures out he can paint the bedroom and remodel the bathroom.  Then one Saturday morning he plans to play golf with his friends and his wife reminds him they are going to Bed, Bath and Beyond to get the new towels and curtains for the master bath.  Not one of those things, looked at separately, is unreasonable. Yet, slowly this woman has taken total control of the man’s life…and that is unreasonable…”

 

“…And on that Saturday morning does the man play golf with his friends or go to Bed, Bath and Beyond?  What does he do?  He crumbles and picks out towels with his wife.  And, how did his wife win this battle where she’s up against a testosterone filled, athletic man who outweighs her by 50 pounds?  She uses the one weapon women have, guilt.”  Michael hesitated for a moment of thought.  “Correction…Women have 2 weapons, pussy and guilt.  And with these formidable weapons, used separately or in combination, women can defeat any man.  This is what we’re up against.”

 

At this I started laughing so hard other tables were looking at us.  Michael laughed too but explained he was serious.  When I finally stopped laughing I said, “I know you’re serious.  I’m laughing at how for centuries men have written and pondered on the mysteries of women and here you’ve encapsulated it all down to 2 words…pussy and guilt.  It’s a completely honest and accurate assessment of the battle of the sexes.  I admire your brevity.”

We laughed some more and then Michael picked up where he’d left off.  “So let’s go back to men deciding what they’re not going to give up in a relationship…You can see why men need to do this.  Women have these 2 powerful weapons and can start eroding away the control a man has over those things he vowed would be sacred in his life…like having a certain amount of free time each weekend to do what he wants to do…”

 

“…Are all women like that?  I’d say to one extent or another, yes.  There are some women who’re just total control freaks.  They have to completely control men.  It’s like a psycho-girl pathology with them.  Pity the poor guy who gets caught in her web.  But, that’s an extreme…Most women just control men because it’s in their nature and it’s done with good intentions.  They have those mothering instincts. They want to keep their man healthy and out of trouble.  They want help with their list of priorities in life…like decorating the master bath.  And after all, how does playing golf add value to the house or help with important priorities?  The man stumbles on that question and doesn’t know what to say…”

 

“…But, there’s a good answer.  He needs to realize the man plays golf because that’s who he is.  It’s what makes him feel whole.  It’s the balance he needs in his life to feel complete and happy…and a woman must understand that or she and her man will be miserable.  And a man must understand that or he’ll crumble under the weight of guilt heaped upon him.”

 

“Okay, so first the man must go into the relationship with a commitment…not to her but to himself…a commitment to maintain control over certain aspects of his life.  That may include things like spending time with his family or playing sports he enjoys or having his place in the house where he can be messy.  Maybe it’s watching the Indy 500 with his father every Memorial Day like he did when he was a kid.  It can be any number of things which are uniquely his.  These are the things which make him feel whole and if they’re reasonable a woman should understand that…If she doesn’t, then the guy needs to re-think being with her…”

 

“…And the man must stand firm.  He must maintain control over his life and not feel guilty for doing that.”

 

“Maintaining control over the important things in your life isn’t the only battle you may wage in a relationship.”  Michael thought for a second to collect his thoughts and then continued, “You know on the back of magazine centerfolds they have the girl’s biography.  I read one and under likes and dislikes the girl said, I’m not interested in men who let me walk all over them.  I read that and asked myself why does she try to walk all over men in the first place?  I don’t go through life trying to walk all over people.  I realized this is a very interesting behavior in women…Women will test men.  They’ll try to walk all over men to see how far they can push them.  I suppose they’re testing a guy’s manhood.  The more grief a man will tolerate from a woman, the worse he scores on the test.  It’s this sick system where the more understanding and patient you are, the weaker you’re seen and the lower you score on the test.”

“So what’s a man supposed to do when he’s being tested?  Well, you don’t want to react to it.  You don’t want to get mad.  The best approach is to look at her like she’s being childish and say, What’s your problem?  And women who do this are acting like little children on the playground.  Being dismissive of her games is a good, passive way to stand your ground…And the same would go for any kind of negativity a woman may direct towards you whether it’s moodiness or nagging or unfounded criticism.”

 

“Speaking of unfounded criticism, I’ve had a few girlfriends who qualify me as an expert on the subject.  Let me explain.  When I say unfounded criticism, I’m not talking about being criticized for coming home drunk or for losing your job and not looking for another one or for getting caught in bed with the neighbor’s 19 year old daughter.  What I’m talking about is being criticized for admirable behavior.  I’m talking about being criticized for being responsible.  Let me give you a short list of things I’ve been criticized for.”

 

“I’ve been criticized for working too much…by someone with no job.  I’ve been criticized for eating healthy food instead of French fries.  I’ve been criticized for refusing to buy something on credit and it was something I didn’t even need.  I’ve been criticized for flossing my teeth...in the bathroom.  I’ve been criticized for drinking water instead of a coke.  And the piece de resistance…I’ve been criticized for studying for a college exam which was being held the next day.”

 

“Oh my God, you remember Lori, the cold Virgo who hated sex.  You’ve just described my life with her.  In fact, it was one of those moments when I knew the relationship was over.  She’d just graduated and was taking a break for a month or two before looking for a job.  She’d moved in with me during that time.  Lori came down with the flu and was too sick to even get off the couch.  So I come home from work one day and here’s this pathetic scene of Lori lying on the couch with a blanket watching TV and blowing her nose.  I’m sympathetic, fix her some tea and start cleaning the kitchen to prepare dinner.  I’m just putting the food on her plate and from the couch she announces she hasn’t done anything all day and asks me if I want to go out later to a movie.  I tell her I was pretty tired and I needed to finish a report due the next day at work…but maybe she could find a movie on cable to watch.  As I carry the plate of food and hand it to her she looks at me and says, You don’t do anything fun do you?  I was in shock that someone unemployed, sick on the couch and being handed dinner would say that.  I didn’t even respond but that was the moment I knew it was time to break up and move on.”

 

Michael shook his head and laughed a little as he said, “It’s not just that you’re being criticized.  You’re being insulted.  What world do they live in?  These are supposedly adults…”

 

“…Now, I suppose someone could try to be sensitive and understanding and try to learn what’s really bothering them but then you reinforce the behavior.  Once again, the best approach is to look at her like she’s being childish and say, What’s your problem?  If they realize how illogical they’re being and laugh at themselves and apologize, then maybe there’s redemption.  But if this is ongoing behavior and they get defensive with you, then this is not someone you want to be with.”

 

“Michael, let me ask you this.  It sounds to me like Stephanie has never done anything like this.  What if suddenly, out of the blue, Stephanie said some crazy thing like we’re talking about?”

 

Michael nodded his head and said, “That’s a good scenario to discuss.  You’re right.  Stephanie is always positive and simply not the type to criticize people.  So if, like you say, out of the blue she came up with some illogical criticism of me, I’d have to take her seriously…not the words she spoke because that’s not what’s bothering her…I’d have to take her seriously because something under the surface is bothering her.  I think the right approach would be to stop what I was doing, sit down with her, tell her how much I care about her and ask her what the problem is.  I’d calmly tell her what she said made no sense whatsoever but I know there must be something bothering her…so what is it?”

 

“That story you just told me about Lori, the cold Virgo, reminded me of something my uncle told me.  This is one of those rules which should be etched in stone.  He said, Do not marry a girl who’s never lived on her own.  Until someone, man or woman, lives on their own they cannot appreciate the realities of life.  When a girl lives at home or goes off to college with everything paid by her parents, she learns nothing about life.  If she’s never paid rent or had utilities in her name or been responsible for food being in the cupboard, then she still lives in a fantasy world…And moving in with a boyfriend doesn’t count as living on her own.  I’m talking about living alone and paying her own expenses from money she earns at a job…”

 

“…Consider this scenario.  A guy is out of college, works and has been on his own for two years.  He lives in a two bedroom apartment.  Now his girlfriend graduates from college and is starting grad school.  She’s lived with her parents or has been supported by them her entire life.  Now she moves in with her boyfriend.    She has an internship through grad school which pays her a little and her parents pay for her tuition.  For her life is good…as it always has been…”

 

“…And for the boyfriend life is good.  He enjoys having his girlfriend with him and feels good taking care of her.  But after a few months he starts to notice some things.  His girlfriend doesn’t seem to be aware of all the work he does to keep the household in order.  She complains when he doesn’t have time for socializing as often as she’d like and reacts negatively when he suggests an extravagant  weekend getaway she planned was simply too expensive.  She expects her carefree college life to continue and that includes no financial constraints…”

 

“…The boyfriend comes to realize that, as far as his girlfriend is concerned, all of these things he does like working or paying the rent and utilities or grocery shopping or doing the laundry or putting new tires on her car or shipping that box off to her family for her are nothing…He’s done NOTHING.  These are all things which get miraculously done, through no effort, by the tooth fairy.  All her life she just goes to sleep and mommy and daddy do all these things at night.  She wakes up, her clothes are folded at the foot of her bed and breakfast is ready.  She doesn’t even know utility bills exist.  All she knows about groceries is you open a cupboard and there they are…See?  Here’s the food…”

 

“…The worst part of this situation is yet to come because a long-term resentment is developing between these two love birds.  The boyfriend resents the girlfriend because she doesn’t appreciate all he does for her and she judges him harshly because he doesn’t have the free time to spend with her that she’d like.  The girlfriend also resents the boyfriend because he’s the one telling her to do things she’s never had to do and doesn’t want to do.  He’s telling her to cook breakfast and go to the store.  It’s not the realities of life telling her to do those things…It’s him.  He’s telling her she’s not being responsible.  Mommy and daddy were never mean like he is.”

“Now, someone could argue eventually the girlfriend will learn about life and become responsible…Probably when she gets a job.  I don’t believe that.  Unless a girl lives on her own and has to deal with the day to day responsibilities without help from anyone else, she’ll never truly appreciate what the boyfriend does.  She went from zero responsibilities to sharing the responsibilities.  She never experienced having to do 100% of everything by herself.  Until she’s had to do everything by herself she can’t fully appreciate how much easier life is when the responsibilities are shared…”

 

“…No guy should be looked down on as being a boring, kill joy because he has to stay home, pay the bills, do the laundry and prepare for the important meeting the next day at work.  When a girl does that, it’s unsupportive and shouldn’t be tolerated.”

 

I pondered all of this and said, “So when you go into a relationship you have to be positive, supportive, respectful, understanding, energetic, responsible…And you have to have a firm grip on your limits.  You give your respect and must have it in return.  You can’t allow yourself to be a doormat.”

 

“Exactly, and if the disrespect goes unchecked, it’ll continue.  For the health of the relationship and your health, you need to calmly sit down with the girl and explain you won’t be talked to that way or criticized like that or taken for granted.  Don’t let it turn into a fight but hold your ground and be ready to walk away from that relationship.  This is not someone you want to marry.”

 

I asked Michael if he was meeting Stephanie later.  He said he was and should probably get home and clean up.  I wasn’t sure what would happen with Kim on Sunday so we headed home and left it open for the next time we would get together.  I spent the evening at home taking care of all those boring things around the house…All the while hoping Kim would call.

 

********************

 

Sunday came and still no call from Kim.  I called Michael in the afternoon to see if he was free.  He told me to come on over.  I arrived and Stephanie was there.  She was on her way to work.  I came in and said, “Hi Stephanie, you look great.  How have you been?”  Michael was right.  Stephanie did look prettier than ever.  She gave me a big smile and thanked me for the compliment.  Then she mentioned that Kristin had been asking about me.  So I asked how she was doing with school and exams and told Stephanie to say hi for me. 

 

Stephanie left for work and I started talking about Kim because it was on my mind that she hadn’t called yet.  “The truth is I’m falling for her.  She’s fun to be with, sexy and athletic.  It’s always been my fantasy to find a girl like her.  The downside to all that is here I am preoccupied because I haven’t heard from her in 2 days…How pathetic am I?”

 

Michael laughed and said, “See how effective playing hard to get is?  She’s got you and simply by being a few hours late with a phone call.  And, now what do you do?  When do you call her and what do you say?”  I told Michael that was my big dilemma and I was open to suggestions.  Michael replied, “Well, you can hold out and not call her…but that has some pitfalls.  Look at it this way.  If I’d said I was going to call you today and maybe we could play tennis and I didn’t call, what would you do?  You’d probably call me around 5 and simply say you called to see how I was doing.”  That was good advice and I asked Michael to remind me when it was 5pm.  Then I asked Michael what was new with him and Stephanie.

 

Michael said, “Oh, big news.  We’ve wanted to do a weekend get-away but she works on weekends.  She arranged to trade some work days so next weekend we’re going up the coast.  We were looking online at some motels and inns this afternoon.  That’ll be our first road trip.  It should be fun.”

 

We talked for a while about different places we’d been in that area and where the best place to go would be and then I changed the subject.  “Hey, we’ve been talking about relationships a lot lately and I have a friend at work who’s married.  He complains about how his wife never wants to have sex anymore.  He started opening up to me one day and he absolutely loves his wife in every way but he’s so sexually frustrated and doesn’t know what to do.  He said he can’t imagine leaving his wife but now he thinks about it.  So, how common is this.  Is that why the divorce rate is so high?  What’s my friend supposed to do at this point?  Is he totally screwed?  Should he learn to live without it?  Is he going to become a statistic for divorce or extra-marital affairs?  How important is sex for the health of a relationship?”

 

Michael winced as he said, “Your friend is screwed.  I don’t think they make a pill to suddenly make his wife a vixen.  The options you just listed are about it for him.  You know there are cultures in the world where girls are taught by their mothers that one of their responsibilities is to keep their man happy.  America is not one of those cultures. That’s for sure.  We could probably be thrown in jail by some politically correct feminist for even having this conversation…”

“…But to your last question of how important sex is for the health of a relationship, I’d say it’s very important.  I think when we’re in a relationship with someone the compatibility is on 2 levels.  There’s compatibility of personalities and values and that’s very important.  There’s also sexual compatibility and that’s equally important.  Some people argue sex is more important.  Which is most important depends on the person.  I think most of us want both…need both…to be happy in a relationship.”

 

“Look at your friend’s situation.  It sounds like he and his wife are compatible and happy in every way except one.  In other words, he’s happy 23 hours a day and yet he’s thought about giving all of that up to find sexual satisfaction.  That gives you an idea how important sex is in a relationship…”

 

“…And you know what?  Many times I’ve seen the opposite situation where nothing about a couple was compatible except the sex…and they stay together.  I’ve seen it many times…the most dramatic was a college friend, Steve, and his girlfriend, Laurie.  They had this sexual chemistry…this lust…and it was the only glue holding them together.  They were constantly fighting and broke up at least once a month.  Yet, they always came back to each other.  Believe it or not they got married after college.  I think later they got divorced but the point is a strong sexual chemistry will hold a couple together when nothing else in the relationship works…That’s how important sex can be.”

I started laughing and said, “Do you remember the episode of Seinfeld where Jerry is dating a girl who he doesn’t like.  He doesn’t like her personality but he’s addicted to the sex.  At one point in the episode his brain and his penis have an argument whether to keep seeing her or not.  Of course, what made it so funny is it’s the truth…and most guys have been there.”

 

Michael reminded me it was 5pm and I wanted to call Kim.  I pulled out my phone and called…Her phone was turned off…Hmmm…It went to voice mail and I left a message, “Hi Kim, just called to say hi.  You said you were going to call about playing tennis…So, I wasn’t sure if we were going to do anything later or not…Anyway, give me a call.”

 

Michael winced and I asked what for.  He said, “You sounded a bit insecure and like you’ve been sitting around all day waiting for her call…Which you have but you don’t want her to know that.”  I got a little defensive but then Michael repeated what I said and I realized he was right.  I asked him how I should’ve handled it.  He said, “You have to put yourself into the right frame of mind for those calls.  You can’t call feeling insecure or wondering what she’s doing or why she hasn’t called.  You have to put yourself into the frame of mind that life is good.  You and she are good.  You just had a great day and assume she did too…That positive frame of mind will come out in your voice.  Then your words should reflect that too.  You say something like, Hey Kim, just called to say hi.  Hope you had a good day.  That’s it.  Saying anything more gives you away.”

 

Now I’m cursing myself for leaving that message.  Michael was right.  Keep it short and positive.  My message left…uh…The wrong message.  Now I started laughing at myself and said, “My life is imitating Seinfeld episodes.  There’s an episode where George leaves a long, angry message on a girl’s machine and then realizes the damage it’ll do so he and Jerry go to her apartment and try to replace the little cassette tape before she listens to it…Oh my God.  I’ve become George Castanza.”

 

Michael jumped to my defense, “Hey, your message wasn’t that bad…I was nit-picking.  Don’t even think about it.  But speaking of phone calls, are you going to call Kristin?  Stephanie asked if you were going to call her before you arrived.  It sounds like Kristin has been putting out the message.”

 

“Oh yeah, Kristin,” I said, “I kind of let that drop…You know, with falling for Kim and having the best sex of my life and all.  My instinct tells me not to call unless I’m ready to start dating her and right now I want to concentrate on Kim.  I don’t want to start dating two girls.”

 

Michael agreed and said, “Yeah, you can’t call her and then not ask her out…better to just not call.  Put her phone number in a safe place for now.”  Michael paused and then said, “Speaking of phone calls, I have some advice for when Kim calls.  This goes to a pet peeve of mine.  This is when you haven’t spoken to someone in a long time and then when you call they start chastising you.  They have this judgmental tone and try to make you feel guilty for not calling more often.  They expect you to account for why you haven’t called, blah, blah, blah.  Honestly, when this happens I’m wondering why I called at all.  The best friends and the real positive people will always greet your call with enthusiasm…Even after a year’s absence.  Hey Michael, great to hear from you.  Anyway, when Kim calls you should simply be enthusiastic to hear from her and not even bring up her Sunday absence.  Think about it from her point of view.  Does she want to get together with someone who’s asking for explanations on her whereabouts or does she want to get together with someone who’s enthusiastic and glad to hear from her?  And if she does give you an explanation, let her know it’s no problem.”

 

I was getting tired so I headed home and tried to act like I wasn’t waiting for Kim to call. 

 

***********************

 

Kim finally called Monday.  We went out to dinner after work on Tuesday and then she came over to my house.  We had a good time but things had changed.  I was anxious to get together with Michael…I was exhausted from analysis overload and needed an objective opinion.  Michael and I met after work for dinner on Thursday and I started filling him in on the details.  “Okay, you remember I was waiting for Kim to call on Sunday.  She finally called Monday and was upbeat on the phone.  I asked her how her weekend was and she went on about spending time with friends.  Then she asked if I wanted to get together for dinner Tuesday after work.  So we met for dinner, had a good time like we always do and then she wanted to come over to my house.  She came over and I showed her around a bit.  We sat on the couch, had a drink and talked and then the clothes started coming off.  We ended up in bed and she left around 10pm.  Now, on paper, it all sounds like things are great but, something has changed…”

 

“…There were little things.  When she told me about her weekend, it seemed like she was trying too hard to convince me she was busy with her friends.  At dinner I told her about your weekend get-away with Stephanie and mentioned we should do something like that.  Instead of being enthusiastic about it, she dropped the subject.  Before this weekend we both had this carefree enthusiasm to know each other and just close our eyes and jump in together.  Tuesday Kim was friendly and affectionate but there was a difference.  Now she wants to put the brakes on and not move beyond where we are.  She’s not really looking for anything serious.”  Michael asked how I knew that for sure.  I told him, “Well, I left the best for last.  Lying in bed after we made love she said, I like doing things with you…But you know…I’m not really looking for anything serious.”  Michael was shocked and asked if she really said that.  Then he asked if I’d said something like I was getting serious or falling in love.  “No, I didn’t say anything obvious but I’ve fallen for her and I think it’s written all over my face.  Also, I said things which implied I was thinking in terms of us being together.  I didn’t say or do anything over the top but I think enough for her to make that pre-emptive statement.  So, I was a little surprised when she said that but I told her that was fine and I liked being with her too.”

 

I asked Michael what he thought.  He pondered the information for a while and then said, “Well, it’s interesting.  Looking back, you’d have to admit Kim was calling the shots from the beginning as far as when and where you went out.  It sounds like she wants a guy she can date a few times a week and be intimate with but without complications…And she has that.  How are you with that?”

Now I pondered the question and finally said, “You know, it’s funny.  This situation is every guy’s dream…To have a girl that’s fun to go out with, have great sex with but no commitment.  It’s a dream come true…But, now that it’s happened I want more.  Maybe it’s human nature, maybe it’s wanting what I can’t have, maybe it’s that desire to possess someone…or maybe it’s just me but when I felt her pulling back, I wanted her more.  I found myself wanting an exclusive relationship.  So have I fallen in love with this girl or am I just feeling those emotions because she’s pulling away?”

 

Michael nodded his head and said, “You’re asking the right questions.  Is it love you feel or is it the emotions from wanting what you can’t have you feel?”  Now Michael shrugged his shoulders and said, “Who knows?  But, I have another question for you…What do you do now?  You have some choices.  You can just go along for the ride.  Adjust your expectations and accept this good friendship with Kim.  She’s fun to be with and a passionate lover.  You’ll just have to know it may never go beyond casual dating and hot sex…”

 

“…Another choice would be to continue with Kim but all the while hoping the friendship blossoms into true love and the relationship you want.  That choice is usually a death wish.  You desperately want something more but she’s the one in control.  As time goes on your disappointment and frustration grows.  The tension grows and soon she’s uncomfortable being around you.  It always ends badly…”

 

“…Another choice would be to end it with Kim because you want more of a relationship than she’s willing to give.”  I asked Michael what he thought I should do.  He thought for a moment and then said, “I’d opt for the first choice to go along for the ride.  Be positive and have fun with her.  Above all you have to remember she wants no drama and no complications.  She wants to enjoy herself going out with positive, energetic people…So that’s what you must be for her.”  Michael hesitated and then said, “You know, last Sunday it didn’t make any sense to call Kristin unless you were going to date her…Now it makes sense.  If you invest all of your time and energy with Kim then it’s going to be difficult to keep your emotions in check.  It’s just in the cards that you’ll want more from her.  Dating other women will help you keep it casual with Kim.  You should call Kristin.”

 

This all made sense but I wasn’t sure I was ready for juggling 2 women.  Michael asked if I had a date with Kim for this weekend and I didn’t.  I hadn’t heard from her since I saw her on Tuesday.  Michael asked if there was a chance I’d wait all weekend for her to call and be disappointed.  He was right.  There was a good chance of that.  Michael picked up his phone, called Stephanie, talked for a minute, hung up, turned to me and said, “Okay, Kristin is working tonight.  Here’s my proposal.  We go over to have a desert and coffee an hour before closing and then you can ask if she’d like to go out for a drink after they get off work.  There’s no pressure and you have a chance to ask her out for this weekend.”

 

A big part of me wanted to believe Kim and I would progress into the loving relationship I wanted, but Michael was right and I knew that.  I also knew I’d probably spend the weekend waiting for Kim to call.  I decided to go see Kristin.

 

Michael and I arrived at Applebee’s about 45 minutes before they closed.  Kristin came to our table to take the order…hmmm…Was that planned?  I started chatting with her and asked if she was still busy with school and exams.  She said the exams were over and she was on break for another week.  So I said, “Oh great, we should get together and do something.”  She enthusiastically said she’d love to so I said, “Maybe we can get Michael and Stephanie to go out for a drink after work.”  Her face lit up and she said she’d make sure Stephanie wanted to come along.  Then she took our order and went off to another table.  I looked at Michael and said, “See, that wasn’t so hard.”

We had a good laugh over my blind luck of finding a nice girl to help me work through the Kim withdrawals and then enjoyed conversation while we waited.  When the girls were ready we all went over to Stephanie’s house.  She made tea for everyone and we had a good time telling stories.  Around 11pm I was ready to go and I walked to Kristin’s car with her.  I said, “That was fun.  We should get together and go to a movie or something.”  Kristin wasn’t going to leave it up in the air this time.  She said she worked Friday night but was free Saturday and would love to go to a movie so I said, “Perfect.”  I pulled out my phone, confirmed her number and told her I’d call tomorrow so we could choose a movie and a time.  She gave me a big smile and then leaned in to give me a kiss on the cheek.

 

So, a few hours before I was down in the dumps and looking forward to a weekend in hell wondering if Kim would call.  Now the weekend was full of big events.  Michael and Stephanie were off on their first weekend get-away.  I had my first date with Kristin and first dates are always exciting…in this case made even more exciting because I was living on the edge.  I mean what if Kim calls and wants to go out Saturday?

 

**********************

 





Chapter 9  Problems in the Relationship

“It’s not you…It’s me.”

Monday was a holiday.  Michael called mid-morning and asked if I wanted to get together later for movie night.  He was thinking to rent a movie and watch it while we ate.  He also wanted to hear about my weekend…And I wanted to hear about his.  I came over to his house around 5pm with some food and he wanted to hear about my date with Kristin.  “It was quite a weekend,” I began, “The last time I’d talked to Kim was on Tuesday.  I asked about getting together on the weekend but she was unsure about her plans so I told her to give me a call if she wanted to do anything.  Then on Thursday you and I went over to Applebee’s and that night Kristin said she could go out Saturday night.  I threw caution to the wind and said, yes.  I spent Friday with one eye on the phone wondering if Kim was going to call but she didn’t.  Saturday afternoon Kim calls and asks if I want to get together!  So there I am.  I have a date and now Kim calls and wants to go out.  I’m dying because I want to be with Kim.  It took every ounce of strength I had but I told Kim I couldn’t because I’d already made plans…And I said it that simply.  I didn’t give her any details and she didn’t ask.  I could tell she was a little surprised.  There was a hesitation but then she said she should’ve called sooner and said to have a nice weekend…”

 

“…I picked up Kristin and first we went out to eat.  We went to Olive Garden.  I wanted to go to a familiar place where I knew the food would be good…And it was.  We had good conversations.  Kristin has a nice calm personality.  We were both relaxed and having a good time.  Then we went to the movie, When in Rome.  It was a cute, romantic comedy.  We both enjoyed it.  It’s a perfect movie for a first date.  Kristin surprised me and took my arm in the movie.  She was letting me know she’s interested.  So we left the theater talking and laughing about the movie and went to an ice cream place to sit and have desert.  We had a good time start to finish.  So I took her home, she invited me in for some tea.  We sat on the sofa talking and I remembered your principle about hanging around too long until she has to tell you it’s time to leave.  So I told her it was getting late and I’d better go.  She came to the door and gave me a big hug.  Again, she surprised me.  I have to say Kristin has this shy allure which is getting to me.  She gave me a lot of physical contact and it was arousing me.”

 

Michael jumped in and said, “I think I know why she was giving you clear messages about her interest.  Stephanie asked about you a few times.  It was during that time you asked Kristin out but she was busy with exams and then the next week or so.  I assume when Kristin didn’t hear from you, she asked Stephanie about you.  I didn’t say anything to Stephanie about Kim but women have a way of knowing or suspecting other women are in your life.  It’s their sixth sense.  So here’s how it went.  Kristin had you interested, then she lost contact with you and now she has it back.  She doesn’t want to take any chances this time.  She suspects there’re other women competing for you so her competitive and seductive instincts have kicked into gear.  It wasn’t any kind of master plan but everything worked to your advantage.”

 

I nodded my head and said, “Yeah, at one point in the evening she said she was glad I called again after she couldn’t go out that first time.  It sounded like she was waiting.  And, you’re right.  She made sure I knew she wants to go out again…soon.”  Then I used my hands for emphasis and said, “The weekend didn’t end there.  Kim must also have that sixth sense because Sunday she called mid-morning and asked if I wanted to play tennis later.  Of course I did, we played tennis and then went over to her house for a couple of hours…And, she was as seductive and passionate as ever.  It’s just like you said.  When you’re dating a girl, other girls suddenly find you irresistible.  The rich get richer.”  Michael’s mouth was open expressing his surprise at the story about Kim.  He asked a few questions about Kristin and Kim.  He was curious which girl I liked more.  I told him Kristin is a nice girl and fun to be with and probably even a little better looking but Kim has me chasing after her.

 

Then I asked Michael about the road trip.  He sighed and said, “The short story is it was a great time start to finish.  I came by to pick up Stephanie and she was so excited.  Other than visiting her parents, she hasn’t been on a trip since she left home…Not that this was a long trip but for her it was special.  So she’s got her bag packed and packed some sandwiches and snacks for the road.  The drive was almost 3 hours.  We talked and laughed and listened to music the whole way.  We get to the hotel…well, it’s like a bed and breakfast.  So, get this.  They lost the reservation.  We look at each other and Stephanie starts laughing and asks if we can sleep on the beach now.  So I start laughing and the owner of the little hotel comes out to see if he can help.  He adored Stephanie and starts calling around to find us a room.  He finds a place and puts us on the phone with the other hotel and we confirm and we’re back in the car driving up the road.  Now we’re calling our trip, Stephanie and Michael’s Excellent Adventure and wondering what’s next…”

 

“…We get to the new hotel and it’s nicer than the first one.  It has a pool and hot tub, easy access to the beach, great view and a fireplace.  By now it’s around 2pm.  We’re all checked in but we’re looking at the beach and the weather has clouded over and it looks like rain.  In fact, it’s started to drizzle.  We look at each other and Stephanie declares we’re going to the beach.  She asks for the keys to the car and tells me to take the rest of the bags to the room.  10 minutes later she comes back with a grocery bag and puts the things she bought into the little refrigerator.  I tried to see what she bought but she told me it was a surprise for later.  Then we put on some workout clothes and headed to the beach.  It was a little cold but not that bad.  We walked way down to the end of the beach to the rocks.  Stephanie put her hands on my chest, looked at me and said, Thank you.  I asked, For what, a rainy day?  She said, Yes.  We climbed around for a while on the rocks and then headed back.  It started raining harder and we picked up the pace.  We ran the last bit and got to room out of breath.  Then I learned Stephanie’s surprise…”

“…Stephanie had bought some wine and cheese and bread.  We put on our swim suits and went to the hot tub.  Climbing in the hot tub after running in the rain with a girl sitting on my lap feeding me wine and cheese was about the best thing that’s ever happened to me.  It was perfect.  We stayed in there for over an hour and then went to the room and went to bed.  The whole weekend was amazing.  We went out to dinner, made love in front of the fireplace, went to the beach in the morning and then out to breakfast.  We checked out and then walked around the little town.  Stephanie bought a little carving for a souvenir…”

 

“…On the drive back Stephanie was sleeping so I was thinking about our little trip.  Some things occurred to me.  Going on a trip is really a good way to get to know someone.  It’s like a little test flight for you to see how easy that person is to be with.  You can see how well the two of you work together, especially on this trip where everything didn’t go according to plan.  The reservations got mixed up and then it was raining the first day.  Little challenges were put in front of Stephanie and nothing fazed her.  She really didn’t care what happened.  As long as we were together, she was happy.  That’s really a good test.”

 

Michael and I talked some more about our weekends and then we watched the movie Michael had rented, 500 Days of Summer.  The movie is a funny and intelligent look at relationships and break ups.  We laughed through it and then started talking about why relationships work…or don’t work.

 

Michael said, “Being in a relationship should be good for everyone.  Sharing responsibilities and sharing good times together is the best way to go through life and this is what we all hope for.  But even the best relationship takes work.  There’ll be bad days that need understanding and support.  Living with someone requires compromise and sacrifice.  The dirty little secret is relationships need energy to survive and both parties have to want it to be strong and have to be willing to put in the effort necessary to keep it strong…”

 

“…Think of my weekend with Stephanie as a relationship.  Both of us put out a lot of energy to make it a great time…but it didn’t seem like we put out much effort because we each got so much help and positive energy from the other.  I paid for the room and drove my car.  Stephanie made sandwiches for the trip.  We were both pro-active and positive about finding a new hotel.  Stephanie had the idea and went to the store to buy wine and cheese.  We both braved the weather to walk on the beach.  I was an enthusiastic participant in everything she wanted to do like browse in the shops and she was an enthusiastic participant in the things I wanted to do like practicing with my sand wedge in the dunes.  We both put energy into the weekend.  But what we got out was much greater than what we put in because of our shared effort…”

 

“…Okay, lets imagine that same weekend but I’m with a selfish, spoiled girl who only cares about a relationship in as much as how it’ll benefit her.  If the relationship benefits her, then fine she’ll stay in it, but she’ll never make a sacrifice or be inconvenienced in any way for the benefit of the relationship or her boyfriend.  Her life is a one-way street.  So, how would that weekend go?  I’d pick her up and she wouldn’t be ready to go and would want to be taken to breakfast before we get on the road.  She wouldn’t have fixed sandwiches for the trip and would expect to stop along the way for lunch.  The restaurant I stop at wouldn’t be up to her standards and I’d have to stop 2 more times to find something suitable.  When I arrive and discover the reservation problem, she’d call me a moron and then start bitching out the person at the desk.  This reservation problem and change to another hotel would be her excuse to decide she’s in a bad mood.  When we arrive at the new hotel she sits in the car instead of coming in to the desk.  She wouldn’t help bring any of the bags to the room.  Once in the room she complains about the number of towels, the size of the shower and the firmness of the bed.  The rainy weather is her excuse to sit on the sofa, watch TV and complain to me because her weekend is ruined.  A suggestion to bundle up and go for a walk on the beach is sneered at.  In desperation to make things right for the spoiled princess, I go to the store and buy wine and cheese.  I ask her if she wants to go to the hot tub.  She asks me to go check the temperature before she’ll put on her swimsuit.  Once in the tub she complains because the bubbles aren’t working and asks why I didn’t get a better type of cheese.  That night we don’t make love…You can pick any excuse…”

 

“…You see basically what you have there is one person riding in the cart and the other person doing all the pulling.  I’d be the only person putting out the energy to keep the relationship alive.  Unfortunately, there’re many relationships like that where one person is the giver and the other person is the taker.  That relationship can survive for a while but it’ll never be strong and probably couldn’t survive the slightest bump in the road.  Relationships aren’t always going to be a bed of roses.  There’ll be problems.  There’ll be times when you’ll have to bend and give for the continued well-being of the relationship.  If both parties really want the relationship to survive then they must be willing to put out the energy necessary to keep it strong…”

 

“…To continue the comparison of my weekend with Stephanie to a relationship, we had a few bumps in the road but we both enthusiastically worked together to make the weekend a success.  Looking back, I don’t even think about the effort we put in.  All I remember are the wonderful experiences we had.  And the reason none of it seemed like work was because both Stephanie and I let the other person know how much their efforts were appreciated.  When we were in the car and Stephanie pulled out the lunch she’d packed I thanked her, leaned over and gave her a kiss, told her how thoughtful she was and went on about how delicious the sandwich was.  That kind of appreciation makes her time in the kitchen seem like nothing.”

 

I loved Michael’s illustration of what makes a relationship strong.  It really captured the importance of being with a person who’s willing to make the effort to be happy.  I went with his theme of how it takes 2 people to make a relationship a success and said, “You know, also in a relationship it takes 2 people to have peace and harmony.  It only takes one person to cause disharmony and a fight.  If one person wants to cause trouble or wants to have a fight, then there’s nothing the other person can do.  Harmony will not exist.”

 

Michael liked this and added, “That’s right.  Now, some people would ask, But why would someone want disharmony or a fight…That seems illogical.  Well, why does anyone go to war?  A person will cause a fight or start a war because they want something.  Maybe they want more control over the decisions.  Maybe a wife wants a new car and the husband won’t buy it so she creates trouble until the husband relinquishes.  He wants peace in his life and is willing to pay for it.  The wife knows this and this is her method to get what she wants.  Sometimes a person wants out of a relationship and instead of just saying so, they create disharmony and turmoil in order to precipitate a break up…”

 

“…There’re people who love making trouble.  They’re comfortable with it and it’s how they get what they want.  If you’re with this kind of person, you’ll constantly be in battles over everything.  We only have 2 things in life…time and energy.  Don’t waste both with someone who only wants to cause trouble.  Life is too short.”

 

“Yeah, when you’re with someone who’s actively creating problems then you’re in real trouble.  Problems are going to arise even when both parties have the best of intentions.  You mentioned one cause of problems which is almost inevitable.  That’s when the excitement of a new relationship wears off.  When you start being with each other more often and you see each other in a truer light, the magic can wane.  That excitement of a new relationship created energy for both of you…Now it takes effort to make the energy.  So, just the natural progression of a relationship can invite problems.”

“Speaking of things which can inevitably lead to problems, I’ve been thinking about the age old topic of the differences between men and women.  Women are complex.  Most of the things they want in life are mutually exclusive.  Let me explain.  Women want a career but they want a family and children.  Women want their freedom and to make it on their own but they want the security of being taken care of by a man.  Women want the tough, macho, man’s man but they want him to be sensitive and understanding.  Those are all mutually exclusive so women become confused and unhappy.  No matter what they have, they don’t have something else.  And then men become confused.  A woman says what she wants and so a man gives it to her…but she’s not happy.  The woman is unhappy and the man is confused and frustrated…”

 

“…Now, on the other hand, men are simple.  Men only want 2 things.  They want to be rich and successful and they want to get laid.  And, these aren’t mutually exclusive.  In fact, one leads to the other.  For the most part, this simplicity allows men to be more content.  They know what they’re all about.  However, this simplicity is also why women can manipulate men so easily.  Women know exactly what men want and they use it against them.  All a woman has to do is suggest her boyfriend isn’t the most successful guy in the room and he becomes insecure.  She can do that simply by flirting with another man or even just complimenting another man’s hair or height or how he dresses.  By withholding sex a woman has her man right where she wants him…begging, desperate and willing to promise anything…”

 

“…And how can a man manipulate a woman?  To do that he must know what she wants, but she doesn’t even know what she wants so good luck to the man figuring it out…”

 

“…So women have the advantage in game playing because men are so predictable and this can lead to trouble in a relationship.  If a woman doesn’t feel she’s getting what she wants, she can resort to these games and that spells problems because women will not relent until they get what they want…”

 

“…So the woman’s complexity can/will inevitably lead to problems.  A woman’s confusion over what she wants and her mutual exclusive desires leads to unhappiness.  If a woman is unhappy then the man around her will be miserable.  And worse, if a woman is unhappy and not getting what she wants, whose fault is it?  It’s the man’s fault.”

 

“So, sometimes problems are just inevitable in a relationship and through no fault of the man.  The excitement of a new relationship wears off and the woman becomes disenchanted.  The woman’s biological clock starts ticking and she’s suddenly unhappy with her life.  In both those cases the guy was just cruising along happily and suddenly the problem landed in his lap.  But, make no mistake.  Men can cause their share of the problems…”

 

“…Sometimes what men do is bore a woman to death.  Once again I use Stephanie as an example.  A few weeks ago I took her to a concert and then we had our weekend get-away.  These are things Stephanie loves to do and they make her feel special.  What if I was lazy and never wanted to do those kinds of things with her?  A year from now Stephanie would be bored and wondering why she puts out so much energy and never gets to do anything fun.  Think of all the girls out there who look at their boyfriend and are thinking all he wants to do is hang out with his friends watching football and drink beer.  It’s like Carrie looking at Doug on The King of Queens…”

 

“…Do you remember I told you about my friend, Marcial, the womanizer?  He has quite a history.  When he was in his late 20s he worked at Chippendales, the male strip club.  He wasn’t one of the performers.  He was a cocktail waiter.  They wore tight black pants and bare from the waist up with one of those black bow ties.  Every night the club was filled with groups of women on their girl’s night out.  Every night he was pinched, squeezed, kissed, grabbed and groped as he brought drinks to tables.  Every night he came home with phone numbers.  More importantly, every night he got laid.  Girls would wait for him to get off work.  Sometimes they’d go home with him and often they’d use his van there in the parking lot.  The interesting thing is what he told me about these girls.  Most of them were in their twenties and had a boyfriend or husband.  He was fascinated by this and would ask them about their relationship.  Keep in mind these were attractive girls.  The typical profile of the boyfriend was he was a good looking, all-American guy.  He was a great guy with lots of friends and had a good job.  Basically, all these attractive women had nice, good looking boyfriends, but they were bored out of their minds.  Their boyfriends did nothing to spice up their lives.  No special events and the sex had become routine.  These women wanted some excitement in their lives and they weren’t getting it at home.”

This was quite a story and a question immediately came to my mind.  Did these boyfriends realize there was a problem simmering under the surface?  I asked Michael what he thought and he said, “Some men are pretty dense.  They don’t know how to recognize a problem or identify what it is or deal with it.  As far as the guy knows, things were good a year ago when they started living together.  He’s happy doing his things.  He has no reason to think she isn’t still happy like she was before.  The truth is he isn’t paying attention.  Problems in a relationship give off signs.  If a woman is unhappy with you or with the relationship or with herself, then symptoms or indications will be there.  Women will communicate their discontent.  Men need to receive that communication.  Then they need to interpret the signs of trouble…And, that can be tricky because often women aren’t even sure what’s bothering them.”

 

“Ah, the world of red flags,” I said, “I’ve lived in that world.  If experience counts for anything then I’m highly qualified to talk on this subject.”

 

Michael laughed and said, “Yes, this is an important topic and a lot of men don’t understand it.  Here’s a pop quiz for you.  What’s the purpose of dating, relationships and engagements?”  I thought about my answer but Michael interrupted.  “Time’s up.  The purpose of dating and relationships is to see if you want something more serious with this woman.  It’s to see if you want to go to the next level.  Most people don’t think of it that way.  They go to the next level because their partner wants to or because it’s convenient to do so.  They never analyzed what they were doing.  If you’re dating then the next level would be living together.  If you’re living together then the next level would be engagement and then marriage.  At each level you’re learning more about the woman and learning if she has the qualities you’re looking for.  And if she does, then that’s the reason you should go to the next level.  If she doesn’t have the qualities you want, then you should end it.”

 

I jumped in here.  “And one of the most important things a man can do when he’s learning about a woman is to watch for the red flags.”  Michael nodded his approval and I continued, “But then red flags have 2 purposes.  One would be to identify qualities or values which indicate this girl isn’t the girl for you…And therefore, you wouldn’t want to become more serious with her.  The other purpose would be to identify a problem in the relationship you need to fix.  Like the red flags those boyfriends ignored when their girlfriends became so bored they went out looking for a Chippendale’s waiter to shag in the back of a van.”

 

“Yes, exactly,” Michael said, “Some red flags are about problems which should be fixed and other red flags are an early warning that this girl isn’t for you and you need to end the relationship soon…”

 

“…And don’t think women aren’t looking for red flags.  I see an article written for women at least once a month about watching for the tell-tale signs…”

 

“…Okay, I’ll give you a good red flag.  Go on a trip with a girl and see how she reacts to the normal hassles of air travel like delays or bad seating.  Even better, go on a trip and make sure something goes wrong as a test.  It would be worth it to find out how the girl reacts.  Look at how well Stephanie dealt with the mixed up reservation and the rainy weather on our trip.  Compare that to this story.  I’d been out of school for a few years and I was dating a girl who was graduating.  She pressured me for a graduation trip.  So I booked a 4 day package to a Mexican resort…And I was glad to do it.  I looked forward to the trip as much as she did.  The flight and transfers all went perfectly and we arrive to the hotel at noon.  Check-in was at noon but the room wasn’t ready and wouldn’t be ready until 3pm.  I shrug my shoulders and figure we can walk down to the beach or have lunch or a drink in the bar.  I adapt and I’m enjoying myself at the bar watching the Indianapolis 500 live on TV.  She’s going to the front desk every 20 minutes asking if the room is ready yet, complaining and getting angrier every minute.  I have no idea if she was angry because I was watching the race on TV or if it was the hotel or the girl at the front desk or what.  All I knew was I’d given her this gift of a trip and she wasn’t making it fun for me.  She was in a sour mood over nothing.  I tried to console her.  I suggested we go down to the beach or to the pool so she could read the book she’d brought but she was determined to be angry and unpleasant to be with.  This wasn’t the first time I’d seen this spoiled, entitled attitude from her but this was the most dramatic example.  More and more I realized this girl wasn’t for me.”

 

Now it was my turn.  “Okay, I have a red flag story.  Remember Lori, the Virgo who didn’t like sex?  We’d been dating for quite a while and many, many times I’d cooked dinner for us.  My favorite, quick meal to prepare is stir fried chicken with vegetables and then mix in some Top Ramen noodles into the pan at the end.  Most of the work is in the preparation.  You chop up everything, then get the water boiling for the noodles and the skillet hot and then everything goes into the pans.  While the chicken and vegetables were frying I always added a little seasoning with a seasoning mix of herbs and spices I buy.  So this night I have everything going as usual and I’m almost ready to put the noodles into the stir fry, which is the last step.  Lori opens a cupboard, pulls down a big jar of garlic powder and dumps 2 or 3 tablespoons worth onto the stir fry and says, Here, you need some of this.  I’m in complete shock as I see her start to do this and yell, No! Don’t do that!  And then after it’s done I yell, Why did you do that?  What she did was completely inexplicable.  I’m totally confused by this and at the same time trying to somehow get the garlic powder out of the pan to save the meal.  But, the interesting thing about it all was her reaction…”

“…First of all, a normal person would’ve reacted with regret and apology.  Someone is preparing you dinner and then you do something to ruin the meal…A normal person would be apologizing and asking if there was a way fix it.  Well, that wasn’t Lori’s reaction.  As far as she was concerned, she did nothing wrong.  No, the only villain present was me because I’d yelled at her.  The first words out of her mouth were, Don’t yell at me!  Why are you yelling at me?  I was only helping.  At this point, I was focused on trying to save the meal and too confused by what had just happened to be thinking straight so I got caught up in her game and began trying to justify yelling when I was trying to stop what she was about to do.  It was later I figured out what this whole thing had been about.  The most telling clue was her state of mind as she was chastising me for yelling at her.  She wasn’t shocked and horrified I’d raised my voice.  No, she was smug and self-satisfied.  She had a look of accomplishment on her face.  She’d set out to prove I was a villain and now she had her proof…mission accomplished…”

 

“…Here’s what was really going on.  We’d been together for maybe 9 months.  We got along well on many levels but I think we’d both decided this was not a match made in heaven.  I was thinking in terms of ending our relationship, though I’d never said anything.  I think she was thinking the same thing.  I think what she did that night was a conscious act in order to cause a fight.  She wanted to break up with me but she had no obvious reason.  Her reason to break up was just a vague feeling we weren’t right for each other.  That’s a valid reason but she wanted something more.  She didn’t want to suffer the blame and guilt of being the one to want to break up with a good man.  She wanted me to be a villain.  She wanted to be able to tell her friends she broke up because I yelled at her and we had a big fight.  Then she’d have a good reason to break up and, most importantly, it wasn’t her fault.  Anyway, this was a giant red flag but a welcome one.  Once I fully understood what she was up to it made it a lot easier for me to suggest we go our separate ways, which I did a few weeks later.  She didn’t like I was the one to bring it up but she was on board with ending it.”

 

Now Michael said, “Another red flag is when a woman’s actions don’t make sense…when they’re not logical.  I’m trying to think of an example.  Say a guy has a girlfriend who lives in a nearby city.  They visit each other once or twice a month.  Now he has an opportunity to visit her this weekend for 3 days but she’s coming up with reasons why he shouldn’t come.  She’s a nurse and tells him they asked her to work some shifts this weekend.  He asks why she can’t say no, which he knows she can because this has happened before.  She says she needs to work the extra hours.  She needs the money to pay off some bills.  At this point, the guy is confused.  If she needs the money why has she never chosen to work extra shifts before?  Suddenly this weekend she wants to work.  And if their relationship was strong and seeing him was a top priority for her, wouldn’t she leave this weekend open and work extra shifts next weekend?”  Her behavior makes no sense unless she doesn’t want her boyfriend to visit her that weekend.  Then her behavior makes perfect sense.  She doesn’t want her boyfriend to visit and this is a classic early warning red flag.  Something’s up and that guy had better be ready for whatever revelation is to come.”

 

“I’ve seen plenty of those kinds of red flags,” I said, “Being too busy to spend time with you, breaking dates and flirting with other men in front of you are all in that category.  A girl getting drunk or smoking when she knows you don’t like her to do those things would be another example.  Not necessarily going on dates but getting together with some guy who’s just a friend is a classic red flag.  Sometimes these red flags come out of the blue, but usually they’re accompanied by an inkling you have deep down inside that something has changed.  We want to ignore those little behaviors because it seems so petty to worry about such little things.  And after all, she denies it means anything.  She tells you nothing has changed and everything is all right and it’s all in your head.  But, actions speak louder than words.  You’d better trust your instincts and figure out what the problem is.”

 

“Another red flag is when you aren’t being appreciated or complimented like you used to be.  When things were good in the relationship she thanked you for every little thing you did.  You always got a big hug and a kiss for taking her out or helping her with a project or even little things like going for a walk or talking with her about something bothering her at work.  And along with the appreciation came compliments.  She complimented your looks or your clothes or your body.  She complimented you for how well you did things or how nice you were to her family.  In one sentence, you were her hero.  But now things have changed.  Absent is the appreciation for the things you do.  Absent are the compliments.  She no longer calls you handsome.  She no longer thanks you for washing her car.  This is a major red flag and her attitude about you has changed.  Most likely she doesn’t treat you like her hero anymore because she no longer sees you as her hero.  You’d better find out what’s up…”

 

“…In fact, I have a friend who experienced that in his marriage.  You know they say the odds of divorce are 50% higher when the woman is more educated than the man.  My friend, John, experienced that.  He’s a very smart guy and about as well rounded as they come, but he has no interest in the corporate world or a prestigious career.  He’s more down to earth and enjoys sports and the outdoors.  He became a teacher which doesn’t pay as much but gave him the freedom and time off he values.  John is also an accomplished craftsman.  He used his summers off to remodel houses and then sell them.  When he met his wife she admired John for his intelligence and his talents.  She constantly told him how he was the best thing in her life.  After they were married she got a master’s degree and moved into a prestigious career.  It didn’t happen overnight but slowly it started affecting her that John didn’t have an impressive title or career.  She was embarrassed when John told people at a party he built houses.  She asked him to tell people he was a teacher instead.  John told me he woke up one day and realized he was no longer her hero.  She didn’t compliment him anymore.  She didn’t care to see the work he did remodeling a house.  Things had changed and he knew it.  A year later they were divorced when she accepted a job in another city.”

 

“You know,” Michael said, “Besides the importance of red flags when you’re in a relationship, red flags can be useful when you’re getting to know someone.  For example, if you start dating someone and there’s an absence of appreciation or compliments then this could mean she’s a spoiled, self-centered girl who feels entitled to be taken out and treated like she’s special.  It doesn’t occur to her to thank people for doing things for her…She expects it.  She doesn’t compliment people because she only thinks about herself.  Another reason she may not give you thanks and appreciation for taking her out is because she really isn’t interested in you that much and doesn’t want to encourage you.  She doesn’t want you to feel good being with her.”

 

“And another red flag for identifying a girl who you may not want to continue dating is when you start hearing her talk about villains in her past.  She talks about horrible ex-boyfriends or relationships.  Remember the story of Myrna and Dan?  After Myrna left Dan she told her next boyfriend how awful Dan was.  A year later that boyfriend called Dan to say he now knew the truth about Myrna.  Remember the story of my uncle and Stella?  When he first met Stella she painted this horrible picture of her ex-husband.  Soon my uncle found out it was Stella who created all the trouble in every relationship she was in.”

“Red flags which identify spoiled, self-centered, entitled girls are very important because there’s no future with those girls.  You just don’t want that in your life.  Here’s a good example.  I was dating this girl, Tania.  She lived nearby and often came over to spend the evening watching TV or eat dinner with me.  In fact, I cooked for her often and did lots of favors for her when she needed help.  So one evening she came over and wanted me to take her out to dinner.  I politely and calmly explained to her I couldn’t go out that night because I had a presentation to do the next day.  I explained I was a little stressed out about it and needed to prepare for it.  Okay, in my world a normal person would ask if there was anything they could do to help, perhaps cook dinner…especially since I’d cooked for her countless times and helped her out whenever she had a problem.  But that wasn’t the world Tania lived in.  She got angry and defensive with me for saying no to her.  I was surprised by her attitude and stuck to my guns.  I told her I had work to do.  She got even angrier and more defensive.  It turned into a fight about how hard she worked and she deserved a night out.  She got around to money issues.  It got ugly but the most telling thing she said came after the argument had progressed to the point of her being irrational.  She claimed I thought she was selfish and called her selfish.  It was interesting because her behavior and attitude were definitely selfish but I’d never called her selfish or even brought up the topic of her being selfish.  The only point I’d ever made was whenever she had a problem I was there to help and support her with my time and money.  Now I had something I needed to do.  I wasn’t asking for her time or help.  I was simply expecting her to support me with her understanding.  But she kept claiming I’d called her selfish.  It became obvious to me she was very sensitive about being selfish.  I think she’d been told she was selfish many times in her life and probably in situations just like this one.  She’d probably heard it from her parents and her brothers and every other boyfriend she’d ever had.  Anyway, Tania was a selfish, spoiled girl and everything was about her.  Her behavior that evening shouted it out clearly for anyone to see.”

 

Now I had a classic story to tell.  “Here’s a red flag which is actually insulting because it’s so dishonest.  Men really have to beware when a girl starts predicting the emergence of problems…not because of her of course…She’s predicting you or some other force will cause the problems.  She’s simply concerned because she doesn’t want to become a victim.  This happened to me when I was in college.  I had a girlfriend, Anna, during my junior year.  This was a good relationship and Anna is a good person so I’m not trying to pick on her here but this is how it happened.  In February she suggested we save up and take a trip to the tropics when classes ended in June.  This turned into a joint effort and we were enthusiastically working together on it.  I had a part time job and she also got some part time work to help finance our adventure.  We were both totally committed to the trip.  I had details which needed to be ironed out like time off from work and family commitments but everything was falling into place.  Then 6 weeks before the trip…and just before we bought our tickets…out of the blue Anna sits me down and expresses concern I’m going to pull out of the trip at the last minute and leave her hanging.  I was confused by this concern of hers because I’d never wavered from 100% commitment to the trip.  I remember asking her what it was in our past which made her think I wouldn’t follow through on what I said I would do.  Of course, at this time in my life I was young and stupid and didn’t know anything about women…other than I liked them…So I dismissed Anna’s concern as just that, concern.  I thought the trip meant a lot to her and she simply wanted reassurance all would go as planned…”

 

“…So we book the trip and everything is set.  A few days before we leave Anna tells me she wants to go on the trip alone.  She’d just turned 21 and wanted to spread her wings and experience the freedom of being an adult.  I immediately thought back to that conversation where Anna’s concern was in reality her wishful thinking.  You know, I really liked Anna.  I didn’t get mad at her.  I told her she’d really put me in a tough position.  I’d made arrangements with work and school yadda, yadda, yadda.  I have to give Anna credit.  She said, I know.  I’m sorry…I just didn’t know how to tell you before.  I was dying inside at the time but I told her I adored her and she should do what she wanted…I’d be there when she got back.  And I was.  And we stayed together through the summer.  She transferred to another school in the fall, which had been her plan for over a year.  So that’s one story of a woman predicting problems.  I have a similar story that’s a hum dinger…”

 

“…Some friends of my parents were the perfect family.  Roger and Diane had 3 kids in their young teens.  The kids were good students, athletic and every parents dream.  Roger was a successful lawyer and everyone loved him.  Diane was cute as a button and happy with her kids and all the things in her life.  They seemed to have it all, a perfect marriage and perfect kids.  Then, as happens to people who have it all, they sold a house they’d owned for many years in the Bay Area and made a ton of money.  Now here’s where the story gets interesting.  My mom is telling me about their good fortune with the real estate deal and she adds Diane said she hoped all the money wouldn’t change things in the marriage.  I remember asking my mom how $650,000 could cause problems in a marriage.  My mom suggested maybe Diane just wanted to play down the importance of money.  Well, 2 months later I find out Diane is in a full on affair with some tennis instructor.  He’s her soul mate and she’s never been so fulfilled.  I immediately recalled that seemingly innocuous thing Diane had said about her fear good fortune might adversely affect the marriage.  2 years later they were divorced.  I wonder what Diane said to Roger when he found out about the affair.  I wonder if she told him it was all because of that darn money.”

Michael was impressed with those stories and said, “Now those stories should go down in red flag history and I love the category:  Women predicting the emergence of problems which have nothing to do with them.  There’s another category of red flags which is very similar.  That’s when a woman sits you down and with drama announces there’s a problem in the relationship.  This announcement usually comes out of the blue and begins with the classic phrase, We need to talk.  In the history of man, no good has ever come from those 4 words.  Now occasionally a woman may have a valid issue and she brings it up out of concern for the long term health of the relationship.  An example of this might be when you haven’t been out in over a month and she misses doing things together.  And, if it’s true you haven’t been out together, then bless her heart.  She was honest and straightforward.  She told you what she wanted without games and did so for the health of the relationship…”

 

“…However, often these dramatic announcements are a smoke screen for something else on her mind.  And, it’s easy to tell when that’s the case.  The big clue is when the problems she starts discussing are contrived.  Another clue is when the blame for these problems is being leveled at you and she doesn’t share in the blame.  An example of this might be when she’s complaining you never go out together, yet that’s not the case.  You take her out every weekend except the last two when you asked her to go to movies and she declined once because she was too busy and the other time she was at a baby shower with her girlfriends.  You’d made yourself available and put out the effort.  If you hadn’t gone out, it was because of her.  Now you have two red flags.  First, she’s breaking dates to do other things.  Second, she’s announcing problems in the relationship and blaming them entirely on you.  Something is up and you’d better find out what her real issue is.”
