Now I thought of another big category of red flags and asked Michael about when a girl is a master manipulator.  He became animated and said, “Oh boy, here we go.  Everyone applies some pressure occasionally but sometimes you run into people who’re relentless in their pursuit of getting what they want from others…And, when it’s a girl my advice is to run the other way.  Like you called them, the master manipulator has many different techniques and sometimes they’ll shamelessly go from one method to the next.  When one doesn’t work on you they try the next one.  One technique is the broken record where they just ask you again and again.  They think they can wear you down.  Another technique is selective memory where they get you to agree to something reasonable but then later their memory of the conversation changes.  This one’s pretty serious because they’re lying and taking you for a fool.  Another technique is bullying.  That’s where, if they don’t get their way, you’re the bad guy.  They ask for something completely unreasonable which is their responsibility and when you can’t or won’t do it, you’re this horrible person.  An example would be asking you to take their car in to be serviced.  They had time to do it over the weekend but neglected to do it.  Now they’re asking you to do it during your work hours and you say no.  They try to bully you into doing it by portraying you as the bad person in the story.  Your best response to the bullying is, Nice try…”

 

“…Another manipulating technique is to butter you up.  That’s where they complement you and then ask for a favor.  Now if this is just another method used by a master manipulator then simply return the compliment and say no.  But, I have to admit when Stephanie butters me up, she gets whatever she wants…But, she’s not a manipulator.  She pampers me and showers me with affection and I reward her with whatever she wants…which is usually just to be pampered and given affection in return.  A symbiotic relationship in its purest form…”

 

“…The last manipulating technique is one of the most dangerous, guilt.  As you know, guilt is a woman’s number 1 tool.  They learn to use it as a child when they give that sad little face to their daddy and they perfect its use as they grow up.  It’s a powerful tool because men are so susceptible to it.  There’s this sneaky little bitch I work with, Christina.  She tries to use guilt or whatever she can to manipulate people.  She’d be dangerous if she were pretty.  Anyway, a few weeks ago the auditors were in town and I was helping them in my department one morning.  They broke for lunch and were asking about nearby restaurant options.  She overheard this and said to me, You’re leaving them on their own?  You aren’t going to take them to a restaurant?  I looked at her, laughed and said, What’s this, guilt trip 101?  Nice try.  Whatever it is you’re after, it won’t work.  The other women standing there had to walk away because they were laughing so hard.  They deal with all of her games and hate her…”

“…There’s something else.  Women have an interesting relationship with guilt.  On the one hand, they use it as their #1 tool.  But, on the other hand, they’re loath to ever accept it.  They have this built in guilt avoidance which is so overpowering they’ll avoid guilt to the point of their own detriment.  Let me give you a good example.  This has happened to me and I’ve seen it happen to friends.  A woman is in a relationship and, for whatever reason, has decided to move on.  She was initially attracted to this guy.  She started a relationship with him, but now she realizes he’s not what she’s looking for.  And, there’s nothing wrong with that.  In this case, it’s completely logical to end the relationship.  It’s good for both of them to move on.  So, she wants to end the relationship but that becomes a problem for her because she doesn’t want to accept the implied guilt which goes with being the person to break up.  She doesn’t want to be the bad person.  So, instead of doing the logical and appropriate thing, she does the illogical thing and stays in the relationship.  She’d rather stay with the guy for another 6 months living in turmoil and frustration if that’s what it takes to build a case against him.  She wants to be able to walk into a court of law and prove beyond a shadow of a doubt it’s his fault they’re breaking up.  She’d rather do that than simply tell him he’s a nice guy but she wants to move on…I accept responsibility for the break up…”

 

“…And from the guy’s standpoint, he has two choices.  One choice is to suffer through the next 6 months of criticism and accusations and fights…all designed to paint him as the villain.  Regrettably, most men follow this course.  The other choice is what any logical man would do.  He does a quick business analysis of the relationship and realizes it’s time to cut his losses and end it.  So he says to the girl, Fine, let’s break up.  I take full responsibility and I’ll sign your release form absolving you of any guilt so you can show it to all your friends.  And he moves on.  She then spends the next 6 months telling everyone how awful the guy was and proudly declares she was guiltless.”

 

Michael’s phone rang.  It was Stephanie.  As I watched him talk I wondered if you could do a study of how good a relationship was based on the facial language a person gives off when they talk to their spouse on the phone.  I watched Michael smile and laugh with Stephanie.  I could see he was anxious to see her.  I could imagine the face of a guy in a bad marriage wincing and frowning with a disappointed look because his wife had tracked him down.  I asked what Stephanie had said.  I heard him say something about guilt in the conversation.  Michael laughed and said, “I told Stephanie we were here talking.  She gets off work soon and asked if I wanted to come over.  I played with her a little and said I was kind of tired.  So she used her cute, little, sympathy voice and asked if I was going to leave her all alone.  So I asked if she was trying to make me feel guilty.  She paused and said, Yes, is it working?  How do I say no to that kind honesty?”

 

Michael thought for a moment and then said, “You know Stephanie’s conversation brings up an important point.  We’ve been talking about red flags and guilt and manipulating people.  The truth is we all manipulate people to one extent or another when we want something.  We do it at work and at home and with our parents.  We do it at the store when we’re nice to a sales person to get their help finding something.  I suppose you could say Stephanie manipulates me every day, but she does it by being sweet and affectionate.  She pampers me.  She showers me with affection.  She brings me cookies and ice cream when we watch TV…And by doing those things she has a loyal boyfriend, a loving relationship and harmony in her life...”

 

“…Now consider the poor guy whose wife or girlfriend gets everything by nagging and bitching at him.  If he couldn’t come to see her, she’d get angry and start yelling at him.  Why would that guy want to go over to see her?  He’s not going to get affection when he arrives.  He’ll be yelled at for being late.”  Michael paused and started laughing then continued, “One time I was listening to Dr. Laura in my car.  A woman called in and immediately started bitching and griping about her husband.  The woman went on for about 2 minutes.  Finally, the woman paused to hear what Dr. Laura had to say about her awful husband.  Dr. Laura said, If you raised a golden retriever puppy the way you treat your husband, you’d end up with a vicious dog biting your ankles.  Of course your husband stays away from the house on weekends.  Who would want to come home to a woman who only yells at him?  I almost had to pull off to the side of the road I was laughing so hard.”

 

Now my phone rang.  As I pulled it out I guessed it was Kim.  I gave Michael a surprised look.  It was Kristin.  I mouthed to Michael that Stephanie must have mentioned I was here.  After I hung up Michael asked for details.  I told him, “Kristin asked if I’d eaten and when I said yes she asked if I’d like to come over for a snack and watch some TV.  I said I’d love to.”  Michael and I cleaned up our plates and I headed over to see Kristin.

 

***************************

 

Wednesday Michael and I got together after work.  It was his turn for the food and he was cooking a spaghetti dinner for us.  When I walked in, it smelled delicious and was almost ready.  We sat down and enjoyed the garlic bread, salad and wine.  He asked me about going over to Kristin’s Monday evening.  I told him, “We had a nice time.  She’d warmed up some zucchini nut bread which we had with tea while we watched TV.  We watched How I Met Your Mother and then Two and a Half Men.  Both episodes were hilarious and we laughed a lot.  She kept asking me if men were really that stupid.  I assured her we were.  She enjoyed that I made fun of men.  The mood was comfortable and she started snuggling up against me while we were watching.  Then she said she was a little cold and got a blanket to cover us up.  I think it was an excuse to snuggle closer because she did and pretty soon we were kissing.  She’s a sensual girl.  The first kiss took a couple of minutes to develop and when we finally touched lips we were trembling.  We didn’t go beyond kissing and I left kind of early.  I asked her when we could go out again.  She said her classes began again on Tuesday so she’d be busy all week but could go out Friday after work like we’ve done before.  I said yes.”

 

Then Michael asked, “We talked the other day about red flags.  I’m curious.  Have you gotten any red flags from Kristin?”

 

I thought for a second and then said, “No, I haven’t seen anything I don’t like and I’ve seen a lot I do like.  She’s going to school and working.  She put studying for exams ahead of socializing.  She’s studying finance.  Those are all things to be admired.  She’s a passionate kisser and I like that.  Also, upon further inspection, her body looks better every time I see her.  She was wearing some sweat pants Monday night and her butt looked great in them.”  I paused and then said, “Something else.  I like that she’s going slowly with me.  I know that’s hypocritical after enjoying my wild ride with Kim but realistically do you want to fall in love with a girl who jumps into bed that fast with a guy?  If she gives it up that freely to you then how many guys came before you and is she the kind of girl to stay faithful?  And speaking of Kim, I called her Tuesday to ask if she wanted to get together.  She said she’d like to go out Wednesday, tonight.  Then today she called and cancelled.  She said things had come up and said she’d call me later in the week.  I wish I hadn’t fallen for Kim.  She really has me wanting what I can’t have.  The contrast is interesting.  Kim with her games has me hooked.  A smart man would get hooked on Kristin.  We’ll see what happens.”

 

Then I asked about Stephanie.  Michael smiled and said, “We had a nice time and a nice conversation.  She was telling me how she’s never been happier.  Then she asked me why I was single and available when we met.  I chose my words carefully on that question.  Didn’t you say to never make comparisons?  The reality is no girl ever compared to Anita since that ended but I’ll never bring that up so I told Stephanie I’d never met someone like her…Which is the truth.  But then I asked her the same question.  To me Stephanie is a catch and I’m surprised she was single and available when we met.  She gave me the same answer.  She said it was hard to meet someone who was just being himself.  Everyone was put on.  She also said I’m the first guy she’s met who appreciated her wit and intelligence.  Probably most men just saw her as a waitress and were trying to hook up.  They never bothered to get to know her.  Stephanie is a diamond in the rough.  I saw that the first night we met her.”

I wanted to change the subject back to what we were talking about last Monday so I brought that up and asked Michael if sex wasn’t one of the major red flags?  Michael said, “Oh yes, a change in the quality or frequency or intensity of sex is a sign something is happening.  Maybe it’s because she’s losing interest.  Maybe the guy’s losing interest.  You need to find out the cause.”  Michael paused, laughed a little and then continued, “Remember the story of Dan and Myrna, the bitch from hell?  I remember him telling me they never had sex after they were married.  I asked him when the sex stopped.  I assumed he’d say after a few months.  I’ll never forget him telling me it stopped on the honeymoon.  He told me she wouldn’t have sex on the honeymoon.  I was stunned.  I’d never heard anything like that.”

 

Now I jumped in and said, “Okay, I don’t mean to pull rank here but I am somewhat of an expert on this topic of limited sex in a relationship.  You could say I studied under a few girls who schooled me in living without sex.  And of course, I did my doctoral thesis on sexual frustration during the year I was with Lori the Virgo.  Let me summarize how the life cycle of those relationships went.  I begin a relationship and everything is new and exciting.  The sex is good in the beginning.  She’s enthusiastic and even initiates making love as often as I do.  Then her interest in sex fades.  We continue to make love but not as often and she’s not so enthusiastic anymore.  It becomes an activity for my pleasure and not hers.  The excuses for not having sex become more frequent.  At this point, I begin to lose interest in sex with her because she’s no longer an active participant.  It’s less than satisfying to make love to a girl who isn’t enjoying it…”

 

“…Now at a point, I’ve begun to go to bed and simply roll over and go to sleep.  It’s gotten old trying to cuddle with her only to be rejected.  Now she’s perfectly content not to have sex but she’s keenly aware I want sex all the time yet I’m no longer trying to have sex with her.  She may even have caught me masturbating in the shower or in bed when I thought she was sleeping.  She knows I’m horny and I’m not getting what I want and she knows I’m no longer trying to get what I want from her.  On some level she knows I find her inadequate as a partner.  She now goes into her guilt avoidance mode, like you talked about the other day.  Her rationale is as follows.  We’re not having sex and it can’t possibly be her fault.  That would make her guilty.  She must transfer the blame and therefore, it must be my fault…”

 

“…So now she begins her campaign to blame me for us not having sex.  She announces we’re not having sex anymore as if this is really bothering her.  She tells me it’s because I’m anti-romantic.  She says it’s because I don’t like foreplay and don’t know how to touch her.  She acts like she’s the one who wants sex and I’m the one who doesn’t.  Of course, any fool could poke holes in her logic.  If she wants sex then why does she tell me not to touch her when I try to cuddle?  If she wants sex then why isn’t she initiating it?  Why isn’t she seducing me in bed touching me or going down on me to get me aroused?  And that question is ridiculous because I fall asleep with a hard-on and wake up in the morning with one so arousing me wouldn’t be difficult…”

 

“…And the excuses become ridiculous.  She uses so many she loses track of what she said and when.  At one time Lori the Virgo told me my penis was too big and it hurt her.  Months later she told me the reason she didn’t have an orgasm was because my penis was too small.”

 

Michael enjoyed my rant and we exchanged some of the excuses we’d heard from girlfriends.  Then he said, “Sooner or later women will fall back on this one and you’ll hear them say, All you care about is sex.  Or, Is sex all you ever think about?  Don’t fall for this distraction.  Don’t ever apologize for enjoying sex.  Your reply should be, Darn right. I love sex.”

 

We’d finished the meal and began to clear the plates.  I pointed out to Michael that in our conversation of problems and red flags we hadn’t yet discussed something which seemed to accompany every relationship I’d had when it entered a problem phase and that was the criticism which was usually unfounded and usually illogical.  Michael made some dramatic gestures and said in a low tone, “Are you sure you want to go there?  Because once we open that door, there’re many dark corners and closets where a myriad of confusing motives are hiding.  When a woman begins criticizing you, it’s probably the mother of all red flags.”  I laughed at Michael’s introduction and told him to bring it on.  I could take it.  Michael took a deep breath and began, “The important thing to learn about criticism is usually her literal criticism is NOT her real agenda.  You saw this with Lori the Virgo criticizing you for being anti-romantic yet she didn’t like or want foreplay.  Her real agenda was to transfer the blame from her to you.  Another typical example would be if a girl starts criticizing her boyfriend for not loving her enough and not giving her enough affection.  That’s not the case but nevertheless the boyfriend steps up his efforts to shower her with affection with his actions and words.  But then she starts criticizing him about other things and now the boyfriend can’t seem to do anything right.  A month later, seemingly out of the blue, the girlfriend breaks up with the guy and is seeing another man…”

“…What happened here?  The girl was saying, You don’t love me.  You don’t give me enough affection.  Those are the words she spoke but here’s what she was REALLY saying, I want to break up with you, but you’ve been good to me and I feel guilty leaving a good relationship so, I’ll find things wrong with you to relieve me of guilt and justify my leaving…”

 

“…So that’s what makes criticism so tricky.  Usually the words she says are not what she really means.  This brings us to Cardinal Rule #1:  Never take what a woman says literally.”  I interrupted here because I thought it was:  Never stay where you are not appreciated.  Michael clarified that was the Cardinal Rule of Life.  This was Cardinal Rule #1 for dealing with women.  Of course, I immediately understood the wisdom of Cardinal Rule #1 but Michael went on to explain it.  “What this Cardinal rule means is a man always has to be asking himself what a woman really means and what she really wants.  And doing that can be difficult…”

 

“…Probably your first step is to ask yourself what her motive is.  In the above example, her motive was to break up with the guy and he didn’t recognize that so the break up came as a surprise.  Lori the Virgo’s motive was to deflect guilt for her low interest in sex.  In a way the man has to become a mind reader.  He’ll have to use his powers of analysis and interpretation to fully understand the motives behind the criticism…”

 

“…Now, we both know no one’s perfect and most of us deserve our share of criticism for things we need to improve upon.  But, I don’t go through life criticizing people.  With people I care about, I offer constructive criticism in the form of a suggestion and I give it in a positive tone.  I do the same at work with co-workers who need to improve for the good of our output.  What I’m saying here is there’re ways to give criticism which are positive in their tone and positive with their intention.  On the other hand there’re ways to give criticism which are negative in their tone and the intention seems more towards tearing a person down than helping them improve.  That’s the first bit of analysis and interpretation a man must do when a woman starts criticizing him…”

 

“…Men are human and imperfect.  Will there be times when a man receives valid criticism?  Yes, of course.  So let’s say a man is being criticized for never taking his girlfriend out.  Upon analysis, the man realizes she’s right.  He hasn’t taken her out for 2 months.  He also recognizes the tone of her criticism was positive and it appears her intention was to spend more time with him.  So he’d better wake up and start taking his girlfriend out more often, spending more time with her and letting her know he cares about her.  If he doesn’t, then the tone of her criticism is going to turn negative and her intention may change too…But all of that’s simply common sense and most of us can figure that out.  What we really want to talk about is when there’s no validity to the criticism or the tone is negative or you suspect she has some ulterior motives for criticizing you…”

 

“…A relatively benign form of this is when a woman criticizes you for one thing when she’s really concerned about something else.  For example, your girlfriend is nagging you for not taking her out enough but you suspect it’s something else which is bothering her because you take her out often.  In reality, she’s mad at you because you’ve been flirting with other women.  So why doesn’t she just come out and say that?  Well, maybe she doesn’t want to admit she’s jealous or maybe she’s trying to bring it up in a round-about way.  Maybe she’s feeling insecure with you because of the flirting and wants some reassurance, which going out with you more would give her…”

 

“…So, this is a legitimate complaint.  She just didn’t communicate it well to you and you were left to be a mind reader to figure out what the real problem was.  What do you do if you can’t read her mind?  In this example, you’d say to her, Listen, you know I take you out a lot, so what’s really bothering you?  If you’re aware enough to suspect jealousy is the real issue, then you could say, Is this because I was talking to that girl last night at the party?  Her reaction to that question will probably tell you if you’ve found the real problem.  Then you need to stop flirting if you value the relationship with your girlfriend.”

 

Now I thought of an example and I asked, “What about when a girl criticizes you to test you?  It’s one of the ways a woman tries to walk all over you.  She criticizes you to see how far she can push you and to test you and see your response.  This has happened to me a few times.  One time I was with a girl and we’d only dated a few times.  I was driving her and her friend to the mall.  We came to a four way stop.  I waited for it to be my turn and started out but so did another car.  We both stopped and I waved the other car to go ahead.  The friend starts criticizing me and asked why I’d let the other car go ahead because I had the right of way.  I told her I was just being polite.  She kept criticizing me.  At that I laughed and said, So you’re criticizing me for being polite.  She kept at it so this time I looked at my date and said in a joking way, Am I going to have to come back there?  Don’t make me come back there.  I was making fun of her and that seemed to settle the friend down.  It was interesting though.  I had to wonder why that girl started testing me.  Was that something the two friends routinely did when they met men?  Also, it was obvious to me I’d have been toast if I’d become irritated or defensive and said with an angry tone something like, Oh, do you want to drive?  I suppose you could do better?  It would’ve been like sharks smelling blood in the water.  That girl would’ve gone after me like a Great White.”

Michael laughed and said, “You’re exactly right.  If you’d reacted they would’ve smelled weakness and gone in for the kill.  And I think there’re girls who routinely do that when they meet men.  They probably go out to bars and find insecure men to taunt just for sport.  In a way, I can’t blame them.  They’re mostly picking on those guys who show up to a bar acting like a big shot but are 100% phony.  Anyway, it’s good you passed her test.”

 

“There’s another type of criticism which is serious trouble because the motive behind it is to create problems in the relationship and/or most likely to break up with you.  The woman is unhappy with you or the relationship and she begins criticizing you for most anything.  The content of the criticism really doesn’t matter.  What does matter is her intention is to paint you as an unworthy person…”

 

“…The way this usually begins is you no longer receive compliments you used to receive for your looks or your love-making or your accomplishments.  You’re no longer appreciated for the nice things you do for her.  And, this is usually the harbinger of a constant level of criticism.  At first, this criticism may appear or sound like something you’ve heard in the past.  What makes this different is it’s accompanied by the absence of compliments and appreciation and usually accompanied by some undesirable behavior.  This behavior may include changes in your sex life or she’s not spending as much time with you or she may even be enjoying interest from other men.  Red flags are going up like it’s May Day in Moscow…”

 

“…And, what’s this criticism for?  It really doesn’t make any difference.  It’s very picky and unfounded.  Suddenly, you’re being criticized for anything and everything, regardless of whether it has any merit.  One day you eat too fast and the next day you eat too slowly.  You talk too much.  You don’t talk enough.  In other words you can never please her and she’s bound and determined to criticize you no matter what you do…”

 

“…And usually the motive behind all of this is simply that she’s trying to pick a fight in order to cause problems which will ultimately lead to a break up.  She feels guilty she’s the one giving up on the relationship so she starts finding things wrong with you or hoping for a fight so the break up can be your fault.  It makes no difference that her criticisms are irrational and unfounded.  She just wants to make trouble…”

 

“…Actually, sometimes even when you’re innocent, you’re guilty.  Sometimes a woman creates in her mind an image of what she wants you to be when she meets you.  She falls in love with the image and not who you really are.  When you don’t live up to the image which she created, she blames you.  You’re guilty of not being the Ken doll she had in mind when she met you…”

 

“…A similar situation happens when a woman enters a relationship because she thinks it’ll make her feel good.  She thinks having a relationship will make her happy or solve all her problems.  Of course, her problems are because of her job or her finances or her health or from deep inside her psyche so when the relationship doesn’t solve all her problems or make her happy she blames you.  You’re guilty of not making her happy…but that’s no one’s job but her own.  She won’t look inward for the solutions though, it’s easier to blame you.”

 

“So what exactly should a guy do when he starts getting criticism with a negative tone, a bad intention and ulterior motives?  First, here’s what not to do.  Don’t accept the premise of the criticism and don’t become defensive.  If she says you eat too slowly, you don’t want to say, Gee, I’ll try to eat faster, and you don’t want to become defensive and say, I do not eat too slowly.  If you acknowledge any credibility to her complaints, you’ve simply encouraged her to continue.  What you want to do is simple.  You laugh and say, What’s your problem?”

 

“If you laugh off her criticism like you should, it’ll catch her off guard.  This kind of thing has probably worked for her in the past and so this has her a little flustered.  There’s a good chance she’ll fall back on saying, I’m just kidding.  Gee, I was only trying to bust your chops.  Don’t you have a sense of humor?  Don’t fall for this diversion.  If her criticism has been frequent and negative in its tone, then there’s a motive for it and you’d better find out what it is.”

 

“Here’s another important tip.  Let’s say you receive some criticism…and there’s some validity to it so you start explaining yourself.  You say, Gee, really?  I’ll try to do better.  I just haven’t been myself lately.  I have all these deadlines coming up and the big game is Saturday and my parents are mad at me and I just have a lot on my mind.  I hope you can understand.  This is a big mistake to start answering her, explaining yourself or asking to be understood.  This could be Cardinal Rule #2.  Don’t ever expect a woman to understand your problems.”

 

  1. You shouldn’t have any problems.

  2. Asking for understanding is a sign of weakness.

  3. You won’t get any understanding anyway, so don’t ask for it.

 

If you do hear valid criticism, then improve yourself.  But, don’t do it with words.  Make the improvements with your actions.

 

Michael’s phone rang and it was Stephanie.  They talked for a few minutes and then he put his hand over the phone and told me to call Kristin.  I gave him that confused look like I wanted to know why.  He gave the trust me look and said to call just to say hi.  So I called Kristin and told her I just wanted to say hi and asked how her classes were coming.  Michael, or Stephanie, had been right.  I could hear in her voice she loved getting the call.  She told me she was looking forward to Friday and we said goodbye.

 

When Michael got off his phone he explained to me that Kristin didn’t say anything to Stephanie.  It was Stephanie who could tell Kristin would like to get a call.  I told Michael that Stephanie had been correct.  It was obvious in Kristin’s voice.  Then we talked about Friday night.  We needed to think of something to do with the girls which would be fun.  They got off work too late for dinner or a movie…

 

Then I got back to what we’d been talking about.  I posed a question to Michael.  We’d talked about red flags and problems in relationships and we’d only talked a little about how to deal with them.  I wanted to know if there was more advice about dealing with red flags and problems.  Michael thought for a while and then began, “Some of the problems we talked about were little things and fixable.  We also talked about some serious problems or serious differences which are probably terminal to a relationship or they should be.  So I’m going to presume we aren’t talking about marriage.  If a guy is married and has some of these problems then he’s screwed because he married the wrong girl or got married when he wasn’t ready to take on that responsibility.  So let’s presume we’re talking about relationships which include dating or living together or engagement.  So we’re talking about times when both the man and the woman are learning more and more about the other and should be deciding whether they want to continue with that person.  In other words, breaking up is an option…”

 

“…I point out that breaking up is an option because often a person is faced with behavior or values which they don’t like and they really need to ask if they want to deal with those things their entire life.  That person isn’t going to change when they say, I do…”

 

“…So, I’ll get back to your question about dealing with these problems.  Actually, you did a good job when that girl was testing you from the back seat of your car.  You didn’t get angry.  You didn’t get defensive.  You didn’t raise your voice.  You didn’t react and you didn’t accept her premise.  You simply laughed and shrugged it off.  That’s the best thing to do.  That friend of your date was playing her games and you ignored them.  You’ll never win if you let yourself get pulled into those games.  They’re too good at it.  As long as you choose not to play, you’re safe.”

 

“Your biggest liabilities in an argument are your pride and your anger.  Women know this and are very cunning in their efforts to get you angry.  They know if they can get you angry they can win so they’ll use criticism or behavior which is insulting.  They’ll go after your insecurities.  They’ll hurt your pride.  All of it done to get you angry.  I work with a friend, Randy, and he told me this story.  He was having an argument with his wife and she’d gotten angry.  She was getting angry because her position in the argument had no logic or truth so her tactic was to try to deflect attention from the facts.  She wanted him to get angry and he wouldn’t.  It was making her furious that he remained in control and so she kept pushing.  He said she was trying to get him so angry and frustrated he’d yell at her.  She wanted him to yell at her so she could say, Ah ha!  You yelled at me.  That proves you’re an unstable, bad person and I’m the innocent victim in all this.  In her mind, she’d win the argument if he raised his voice towards her because then the facts of the argument wouldn’t matter…only that he’d lost his temper.”

 

“There’s a pathology many people have when they just have to be right.  These people never have a conversation to learn what’s true.  They only have arguments to prove their position is correct.  There’re a couple of guys at work who’re like this.  One time, we were at a retreat out in the country and they started disagreeing on exactly which direction was true north.  The disagreement got comical listening to these two idiots telling the other he was wrong.  The amazing thing to me was neither of them had an interest in learning where true north was.  It seems to me normal people are interested in seeking knowledge and would be looking for a compass in order to learn.  People who only care that they’re right are difficult to be around, block themselves from learning and something more.  Thinking you know everything usually gets you in trouble.  Take your job in finance.  The people who think they know for sure the market direction are the ones who hold on to loosing positions…”

 

“…Anyway, women have been known to have this pathology that they have to be right and it can be difficult to live with a woman like that.  I had a girlfriend who was a wonderful girl in every respect except she had one flaw which drove me crazy.  She had to be right about everything.  Here’re some classic examples.  One time she put a ceramic plate in the oven to heat up a cold dinner.  I told her the plate wasn’t oven proof and it would break if she put it in the oven.  She laughed off my warning and told me she did it all the time.  Ten minutes later we heard a snapping sound and sure enough the plate had broken into 3 pieces.  As I opened the oven to inspect the damage, I didn’t gloat.  I didn’t say I told you so.  I simply started picking up the pieces and I began explaining the difference between ceramic and oven proof plates.  She said, Oh, you just have to be right, don’t you?  And what do I say to that logic?  It’s not that I have to be right.  It’s that I was right and the proof was in my hands.  But what concerned me the most was her apparent lack of curiosity or willingness to learn from this.  From her attitude I was reasonably sure she’d stubbornly keep putting my plates into the oven to try to prove she was right until all my plates were broken…”

“…Any time I had a decision to be made, she would demand I take her advice on the choice.  I’m not talking about a joint decision like choosing which movie to see.  I’m talking about a choice which involves only me and I’m the one who has to pay for it and pay the consequences if it’s the wrong choice.  In these situations, I came to learn if I didn’t take her advice I’d suffer hearing things like, You only do what you want to do, don’t you?  You never listen to me, do you?  You think you’re the only one who knows anything.  Because of suffering these accusations and because I was trying to be sensitive to her needs and include her in my decisions, there were times I went with her choice…against my better judgment.  And, are you ready for the punch line?  When her advice or her choice turned out to be wrong…And, in some cases not just wrong but a disaster I ended up paying for with my time and money…She said, You’re not blaming me for this are you?  No one forced you to do that…”

 

“…How exactly does anyone deal with that?  If you do what you think is right, you suffer the consequences of listening to her call you stubborn.  If you go against your own better judgment and take her advice, you risk the consequences of a bad decision blowing up in your face.  You can’t win.  Of course, the correct course of action is to follow your better judgment.  You have to do that.  It’s your money at risk and you’re the one who’ll take responsibility for a bad decision.  You can explain this to a girl but she’ll probably just resent you more for pointing out she doesn’t have any power or skin in the game with regard to this decision.”

 

“So once again, we come to your question of how to deal with problems in a relationship.  We’ve emphasized the importance of remaining calm and rational.  You won’t benefit in any way from getting angry.  When you get negative criticism, you’re best served by laughing and asking what her problem is…”

 

“…What do you do when it’s more than words?  What do you do with bad behavior?  You can talk it out with her and again you have to remain calm and rational.  Your tone must be positive and your goal must not be to chastise her but rather to build understanding and respect.  If the girl sincerely cares about you and the relationship is important to her then these conversations will be effective and strengthen the relationship…”

 

“…What if talking with her and explaining your position isn’t effective?  What if a rational explanation of your position turns into a fight and she becomes defensive and begins blaming you.  In this case you have to ask if this is the kind of person you want to be with.  People are capable of improving.  They can lose weight and get in shape.  But, people and their values don’t change.  If a person is selfish to the point of being insensitive to others, then they’re not going to change by talking it out with them.  People are who they are and going through life trying to change them is to live your life in frustration.  This is what we do when we’re younger.  We believe in Santa Claus and we believe people will change and everything will work out.  As we grow up we learn to choose the kind of people we prefer to be around instead of hoping people change into what we want them to be…”

 

“…So if someone does bad behavior…as we all do from time to time…and they don’t come to you and apologize and make amends then this is a person who only thinks of themselves.  Even worse, what if someone is doing bad behavior on a recurring basis?  Do you want to go through life trying to deal with this behavior, fighting it or thinking it’ll change if you just figure out the perfect way to react to it?  Here’s the reality check:  They don’t respect you and don’t consider your feelings or your position.  Every choice they’ll ever make will be based on one thing…what’s good for them and what they want to do in that moment.  They’ll use you, take what they need from you and throw you away when you’re no longer of use to them…”

 

“…I divide problems in a relationship into two categories, those which are fixable and those which are terminal.  My favored criteria to decide where a problem falls is to ask yourself if this is something you want to live with your entire life.  My solution for the terminal problems is to admit they’re terminal and break up.  I know that sounds harsh.  Most people want to hang in there.  They want to work it out.  In other words, they hope the person will change or they think they can compromise their values or ignore a girl’s values they don’t respect.  I think this is a mistake.  It sounds admirable and hopeful and optimistic but I think it’s a mistake.  Consider this:  The logical progression of every relationship is marriage and the longer you stay with a girl, the harder it is to break up.  So if you don’t want to marry these problems, then you really only have one choice.  Remember, problems don’t go away by getting married…They get worse.”

I don’t think I’d ever heard or read a person taking such a tough stance about dealing with problems in a relationship.  I told him that but also said that as tough a stance as it was, it was also pragmatic and realistic.  Michael responded, “Yeah, some people would accuse me of running away from problems instead of working them out.  My response would be to ask if they’re working out problems or are they trying to impose their ways and change another person to be what they want.  But that’s all theory and philosophy.  Let’s look at the practical application of my approach.  My last girlfriend is a nice person.  We got along pretty well.  We didn’t have problems like we’ve been talking about.  But, I broke up with her and when I did many people questioned that.  My friends and my parents all saw a very nice girl and didn’t understand why I would break up with her.  Well, she just wasn’t the girl of my dreams.  There were enough little issues and differences in our values and goals which made me want more.  It didn’t make her bad.  It just meant she wasn’t a perfect fit for me.  And, look at the results of that break up.  I met Stephanie and I couldn’t be happier.  I haven’t yet seen a red flag with Stephanie.  I’m sure one will show up but I just don’t think I’m going to wake up one day and find Stephanie has morphed into her evil step sister.  I know her well enough to know we’ll work out any problems or differences we have together…”

 

“…And speaking of practical application of my approach, let me ask you a question…And, I’m not trying to be provocative here…You haven’t been dating Kim very long but you’ve been dating her long enough to see a lot of things you like.  You like her energy and her positive attitude.  You like your sexual compatibility.  You both enjoy an active life style.  You like her personality and always have interesting conversations.  Considering all of that, she’s a keeper.  But, that’s not the entire story.  She doesn’t seem to be looking for a relationship which is going to tie her down.  She breaks dates and wants the freedom to be out of touch for days at a time.  There’s nothing we can criticize her for.  She’s never lied to you or treated you poorly.  So, let me ask you this.  Are you ready to spend the rest of your life with Kim?”

 

I nodded with understanding at Michael’s questions and said, “We have negative connotations of breaking up.  Most everyone sees it as failure and a last resort.  You’re saying when two people aren’t a fit or when they aren’t getting what they want then breaking up is best for everyone.  You’re saying it was a positive thing for you because you met Stephanie.  Your ex-girlfriend is better off finding someone who’s more like her…”

 

“…Now, your question about Kim is provocative.  You’re right.  I’m attracted to most everything about Kim.  It would be very easy for me to hang on to her for a long time and all the while trying to make it work.  We don’t know for sure about her but it appears she wants to be single and have her freedom.  I want a real relationship.  It would be wrong to chase after Kim.”

 

It was late.  We talked about ideas for Friday night and then I headed home.

 

*************************

 

Friday night Michael and I met at Applebee’s around 9pm to wait for Stephanie and Kristin to get off work.  We’d done some investigating and found a night club which played Latin music and had dancing.  When we told the girls about it, they couldn’t contain themselves.  We got there around 10pm and it wouldn’t get crowded until later.  But they had a big group Salsa dance lesson which started when we arrived, which was perfect for the four of us.  We all learned enough to enjoy ourselves practicing and dancing for a couple of hours.  Both girls loved going out dancing.  I enjoyed it too and dancing with Kristin became more and more sensual as the night went on.  I sensed that for Kristin dancing with me was more than a fun activity.  She was feeling sensual being touched and held.  We left around 1am and even though it was late Kristin asked if I’d like to come over for a bit.  She fixed us some tea and we talked.  It was late and I could tell this was one of those times it was better to leave before she had to kick me out.  At the door she said she had to study and work all weekend but she’d like some exercise on Saturday and if that worked for me maybe we could get out and do something together.  Of course, this was music to my ears so I said yes and we decided to go for a jog along the river.  I told her I’d come by for her around 10am.

 

*********************

 

Sunday afternoon, Michael and I got together and played some tennis.  It was an epic day and we took full advantage.  We played until 4pm and then went over to our favorite Mexican restaurant to eat and relax.  We got our table outside under an umbrella.  Michael asked how Kristin liked the dance club we went to.  I gave a smile and said, “She loved going there.  Kristin seems quiet and cautious when you meet her.  I think there’s a passionate girl underneath her shy exterior.  She started to let go when we were dancing.  We were both getting aroused…”

 

“…But, I have more to tell.  Friday after the club she asked if I wanted to get some exercise with her the next day.  She wanted to take a study break in the morning.  So we went for a jog.  You should’ve seen Kristin in her running tights.  Her butt looked amazing.  Do you think she wore those to get my attention?  It worked.  Anyway, we came back to her apartment and I came in just for a minute to have some juice.  I asked what class she was studying for.  She’s studying finance, which is my field.  She said the class was investing…stocks, bonds and mutual funds.  She’s really interested in it but knows nothing about it.  I asked to see her book and she asked if I knew anything about investing.  I told her that’s what I do.  I’m an investment analyst.  She was totally surprised and said she didn’t know because I’d only said analyst and never spoke about investing.  I explained I don’t like to talk about it because when I do, one of two things happens.  Either people want investment advice which I’m uncomfortable giving or they think I’m going to try to sell them an annuity and I don’t want people to think I see them as a source of commissions.  I’m on a salary.  I don’t work for commissions…”

 

“…Anyway, one thing led to another and I spent the day being her private tutor explaining stocks and bonds and mutual funds.  We went on her computer and I showed her different stock quotes and then we looked up a mutual fund and found the stocks which make up that fund.  She was in heaven and understood it all so much better than reading about it in a book because she could see real life examples.  I enjoyed it too because what I do is considered the most boring thing on earth by most people.  It’s fun finding someone who’s fascinated by it all.   Something else happened too.  Kristin and I totally connected.  She wouldn’t let me leave until she had to go to work at 3pm.  She fixed us lunch.  We had a great time together…”

 

“…And, that’s not all.  In the middle of our study session my phone rang and it was Kim.  She asked me if I wanted to do something later.  Now, of course, Kristin can tell it’s another woman.  Women know these things.  For a moment my mind went blank but I recovered and told Kim I was busy and couldn’t do anything later.  Then we had some small talk and we said goodbye.  I could tell it rattled Kristin a little but also she was happy because I’d said no.  The interesting thing is Kristin has my attention now and not Kim.  I could’ve gotten together with Kim but I didn’t want to jeopardize what seems to be happening with Kristin.”

 

Michael was fascinated with this shift which had occurred over the last week from Kim to Kristin.  He wanted to know how and when this had happened.  I took a breath and then began, “Honestly, I didn’t know a shift had been made until I got that phone call from Kim.  I was talking to her and my gut reaction was I’d rather be with Kristin and I didn’t want to try playing them both and risk screwing it up.  As far as Kim goes, a week ago I was waiting for her call and caught up in the strategy to play it just right with her.  Looking back, I think a lot of my attraction to her was because she’s such a free spirit.  She’s like this elusive butterfly I couldn’t have, which made me want her all the more.  Over the last week I was asking myself about the negatives.  She completely called the shots as far as when we saw each other.  Also, we got along on many levels and we had a great sexual connection but it was sex without the emotional connection.  Now when I was only dating Kim I didn’t notice those negatives.  We got along.  We had fun and the sex was amazing…”

 

“…Okay that brings me to Kristin.  She wants the same kind of relationship I do.  We both want to be with someone.  Over the last week we went to a movie, went out dancing, went jogging and had our study date.  An emotional connection emerged.  During the study date, we both knew that yes, we want to be with each other and find out where this goes.  And, when that happened I realized what was missing with Kim and why I wanted to be with Kristin…”

 

Michael nodded his head and absorbed all of this.  Then he said, “This is interesting that in the end an emotional connection was what you were looking for and you knew it when you found it.  But one more question, how much of your choice was based on the jogging tights?”  We both laughed because we both knew there’s an element of truth to that.  Then I told Michael I’d been thinking about our discussions.  We’d just begun to talk about relationships breaking up.  I told him I had a lot of experience with break ups but most of my experiences were painful.  I asked if he had any painful break ups.  We compared notes for a while on traumatic break ups as teenagers and then he said, “Most people have the wrong perceptions about breaking up.  They see it as a failure so they avoid it and then waste even more time with the wrong girl.  And, the real tragedy is avoiding a break up can ultimately lead to a bad marriage.  Breaking up can be so difficult for some people it’s easier for them to get married to the wrong person than it is to break up…That’s frightening…”

“…There must be hundreds of reasons why people break up.  Can you imagine running a survey of why people break up?  Can you imagine all the answers?  To me there’s only one reason to break up and all the rest of it is superfluous.  All the emotions about what one person did or the other person did distract people from the one important reason.  You should break up when you know the other person isn’t a match for you…”

 

“…Of course, it’s a lot easier to think logically when you’re the one deciding to break up.  When someone breaks up with you, it’s horrible.  But trying to hang on and save a relationship is also a horrible experience.  Consider the guy who postpones the inevitable break up.  His girlfriend doesn’t return the energy and interest he gives to her.  His feelings aren’t reciprocated.   He wants to talk about the relationship and she doesn’t.  He constantly feels insecure.  The longer this goes, the worse it gets.  Logically, we know it’s foolish to suffer through this yet men do because they don’t want to let go.  They don’t want to take defeat.  The best thing to do is also the hardest thing to do and that’s to realize it’s time to go.”

 

“I have another Marcial story.  We shared a house in college one year.  He had a friend, Ted, who’d been married for 7 years.  The three of us played tennis and Ted was often over at our house.  It seemed like Ted’s marriage was drifting apart and then one day he came over and said his wife announced she wanted to separate.  She was getting ready to move in with a girlfriend.  Over the next month, we saw Ted a lot.  He went on and on about the marriage and his wife and all the problems but he was hopeful everything would work out.  Finally one day, Marcial sat him down to give him some advice.  I should mention here Marcial has a presence about him which says listen to this guy because he knows.  So when Marcial gave Ted this advice, he listened.  Here’s what Marcial said.  He said, In my life I’ve probably seen 200 relationships end and I’ve never seen a relationship go down this far and come back to life…Oh, maybe you’ll reconcile for a few months but 6 months from now you’ll be separated again and living in limbo.  Listen to me.  You’re getting divorced.  You need to accept that, deal with it and move forward.  You have the rest of your life to live.  I remember listening to Marcial that day and being stunned with the clarity of his advice.  Like I said, Marcial had a way of commanding attention and he got Ted’s attention that day.  Ted took the advice and let the break up run its natural course.  Ted and his wife worked, more or less, together on the divorce and probably saved a lot of money and certainly saved a lot of time and aggravation.”

 

“Later that day Marcial expanded on what he’d told Ted.  He said he’d never seen a situation where the girl wanted to break up and the guy saved the relationship by saying the right words or doing the right things.  He said maybe the guy could delay the break up because it would be unreasonable for the girl not to accept his efforts to appease her.  He gave an example of a girl who wants to break up so she starts criticizing her boyfriend.  She tells him they never do anything fun.  So, the guy takes her to Disneyland for 3 days.  What can the girl say now?  She’s boxed herself into a corner with a specific criticism he can easily respond to.  But a month later she’ll have another reason to break up and another reason after that.  The guy’s just wasting his time and energy putting off the inevitable.”

 

Michael’s story about Ted reminded me of a close friend I had in high school.  “Let me tell you about my friend, Dave,” I said, “Dave went out with his wife, Tammy, all through high school.  Dave and Tammy were constantly fighting but somehow they stayed together and then got married.  Five years later the inevitable happened.  Tammy left Dave and sued for divorce.  Dave had a difficult time dealing with all of it and didn’t want to divorce.  He couldn’t accept it and tried to reconcile.  Finally, he took her to see a marriage counselor.  The councilor met with them several times and then met with Dave alone.  The councilor told him, you don’t need a marriage counselor.  You need a divorce councilor.  Your marriage is over.  The councilor got Dave to accept that.

 

Michael continued the theme and said, “Breaking up is hard to do.  It’s hard on everyone.  People are filled with feelings of failure and guilt and heartbreak and loneliness and loss…All the worst feelings people can experience.  But I think people make it much harder than it has to be.  For example, why does a break up have to be someone’s fault?  The girl and the guy start pointing fingers and it all spirals down into a life and death fight to determine who’s at fault.  They’re determined to prove their lover is the worst person on earth.  This approach to a break up is ridiculous.  Usually both of them are good people.  They just aren’t a good match for each other.  It happens every day that people are initially attracted and then later decide they aren’t a perfect match.  There’s nothing wrong with getting to know someone and then realizing you don’t want to marry them…And it doesn’t make either one of them a bad person.”

“We grow up with a concept where you fall in love, get married and stay together for eternity and anything short of that makes you a complete failure.  Either you get it right the first time or you must live in shame.  I admire people who get it right the first time.  They’re either smart or lucky…probably both.  But here’s my question:  What’s so wrong with falling in love, being together in a relationship and then the relationship runs its course so both parties move on enriched by their time together?  It doesn’t have to be a loss for either of them.  Look at all the changes which can happen to good people while they’re in a relationship.  People grow and change and change careers.  They go to school.  They develop different desires in where to live.  They have different family plans and plans for children.  There’re numerous options in our lives and any one of them could cause a relationship to run its course.  There’s no reason to look at a decision to separate as a failure.”

 

“This topic of breaking up is a reminder of the importance of having control over your life.  If you have your job and your career and your finances and your house in order then you’re strong and prepared for set-backs.  If a man is in control of his life then he’s better prepared for a break up and will come out the other end in better shape.”

 

I interrupted here and said, “Let me tell you a story about a friend I work with, Gary.  We became very close when he went through his divorce.  He had a tough time.  He married his high school sweetheart who was his first girlfriend.  They had two kids and he was a happy man.  Then he gets dumped for the first time in his life at age 39.  Imagine that…I was dumped 10 times before I left college.  I’m a seasoned veteran.  Poor Gary was devastated.  A lot of his self-esteem and identity came from the approval of his wife and being a family man.  Now, all of that was gone.  When it first happened he felt like he had nothing left.  The only thing that saved Gary was he had his job and his career and his finances in order.  Without that I don’t know how he would’ve survived.  He told me he finally got over her by telling himself over and over that she didn’t love him so why should he love her.  He said his ideal woman was a girl who loved him above all else.  His ex-wife no longer fit that ideal.”

 

Michael’s phone rang.  I heard him tell Stephanie where we were.  Then he started laughing.  He looked at me and said Stephanie wants to know why we aren’t at Applebee’s eating.  He hung up and I asked him why we didn’t ever go there to eat.  He said he loves eating there but two guys keeping an eye on their girlfriends while they work is kind of pathetic.

 

Now my phone rang.  Michael looked at his watch like he was timing how long it would take for Stephanie to tell Kristin what I was doing.  Kristin said she called to thank me again for being her tutor.  We chatted some and I asked when we could have another study date.  There was a pause and then she invited me over that evening.  She’d finished a long assignment in her class and would love it if I could go over it with her.  I asked if she wanted to email it to me.  There was a pause and she said she wouldn’t be able to feed me then.  I told her I’d love to come over and we set a time when she’d be home from work.

 

Now Michael said, “The story about your friend, Gary, you just told is a good one and a good example.  He was devastated but he fell back on his strengths and came through it.  I’m sure it took him a long time to get over a 25 year relationship but he was moving forward while his broken heart healed.  Many men are just not capable of dealing with the emotions which come from a woman rejecting them.  Last week I heard on the morning news about some woman who woke up at 3am and found a car burning in her driveway.  I’m trying to imagine how that happened and then the news report goes on to say the police suspect the jilted lover of the woman’s house guest.  That’s not going to look good on his resume.  Some time ago I read in the paper an article about a man dying of a self-inflicted gunshot.  I read down the article and found the shooting took place in the front yard where the man’s ex-wife resided.  The article said there’d been marital problems for years and the man had prior arrests for domestic battery, blah, blah, blah.  Like I said, some men don’t deal with break ups well…”

 

“…Now, those are extreme examples I just gave but even the most secure guys can have a tough time accepting a break up.  Let me tell you some of the things I’ve seen men do.  This first story is common.  A girl wants to break up with a guy and for valid reasons…not that she needs to have any reason.  This is America.  She has the right to choose who she dates.  Anyway, the girl wants to break up but she doesn’t want to hurt the guy’s feelings so she doesn’t want to come out and list her reasons and make the guy feel like a total looser.  She’s trying to be gentle about it.  She starts laying little hints like she needs to spend more time with her friends or she needs to find herself.  She chooses to spend less time with him.  She never answers his calls now.  They always go to voice mail.  The guy doesn’t get the hint and even worse when he’s with her he drives her crazy with questions about them and analyzing their relationship.  The poor girl is trying not to hurt his feelings while at the same time let him know it’s over, but he still isn’t getting it.  Then the girl starts dating another guy.  Now the old boyfriend is really feeling the pain of rejection.  The discussions about their relationship now become arguments.  He’s demanding she see him so she can explain what’s going on.  He’s meticulously comparing her actions to the things she told him and accusing her of lying.  The old boyfriend becomes obsessed with analyzing what happened and now the girl dreads talking to him.  She just wants him to leave her alone.”

 

“Here’s another one I’ve seen.  I actually heard these words come out a man’s mouth.  He was devastated because his girlfriend said she wanted to break up.  I was talking with him and trying to gently tell him it was over but he just couldn’t accept it.  After 30 minutes of telling me about their issues and also of listening to my advice, he paused for a minute and then said, Maybe she’s saying she wants to break up to test my love.  She wants to see if I’ll fight for her.  Then he looked at me and asked what I thought.  I told him that was the song of the truly desperate.  He didn’t even hear what I said.”

 

“I’ve also known men to hang around during and after a break up like a puppy dog.  They think if they stand there looking forlorn and tell her how much they love her, that this girl will take them back.  In all fairness, the reason a guy does this is because when his girlfriend broke up with him she said, I want us to be friends.  The poor guy is clinging to straws so he interprets this to mean she wants to spend time with him, do things together, be best friends and someday become lovers again.  But, that’s not what she wants.  She wants him to think well of her.  She wants him to leave as a friend and then she wants to be free to date other men.  When she says, Let’s be friends, what she really means is, I don’t want to hurt your feelings and I don’t want you to hate me, but I want you out of my life.  Now, I understand when a guy’s losing his girlfriend, he’s dealing with a broken heart, he’s clinging to any hope there might be and he doesn’t want to admit defeat.  He’s probably not thinking very clearly but he’s being pathetic and again, the girl just wants him to leave her alone.  She’s already moved on.”

 

I had a classic story to share about guys not dealing well with break ups so I interrupted and said, “Let me tell you about my friend, Carl, from college.  Carl is a very easy going guy, he’s a smart guy and he’s a good athlete.  He does have one trait, however, which can be a little irritating to his friends and that is he tends to put out the minimum amount of effort in whatever he’s doing.  This was the case with a girlfriend he had, Ruth.  Ruth is a very sweet girl.  She’s tall, attractive and has a nice athletic build.  None of us understood why Ruth was with Carl because he completely took her for granted and even expected her to pay half for movies or going out to eat.  To all his friends Carl would say he could take her or leave her but she was convenient until something better comes along.  Now, I think Ruth was kind of a late bloomer.  I think she was skinny and awkward growing up and was used to being ignored.  Maybe she just felt lucky to have a boyfriend and that’s why she stayed with Carl.  But now she’d blossomed into a pretty girl and more and more men were paying attention to her.  Carl went home for 3 months during the summer break and during that time Ruth started dating another guy.  Carl found out about this but was confident he’d have her back when he returned in the fall.  It didn’t work out that way.  When Carl returned, Ruth told him she didn’t want him back.  She said she left him because he took her for granted and treated her so poorly…She told him the truth…”

“…Well, the girl Carl could take or leave suddenly became the most important thing in his life.  He tried to correct his past sins by showering her with attention.  He confessed his love to her and even spoke of marriage.  He cried to all of us how he couldn’t live without her.  His whole life became a ritual of penance to prove his sincerity to her.  None of his friends had any sympathy for him.  Ruth was ambivalent towards him and ignored his gestures to win her back.  It was all fascinating and educational to watch how a man who could care less about a girl one day suddenly devotes his life to her the next.”

 

Michael appreciated that story and added, “Losing a girlfriend is one thing but dealing with losing her to another man is about the worst blow a man can take.  That can be devastating to the best of us and I’m not sure there’s any good way to deal with it…Maybe immediately start dating a super model…”

 

“…Here’s another story.  Actually, this guy dealt with the break up fairly well.  He wasn’t happy about it but he accepted it and moved on.  This was in college where he had the displeasure of running into his “ex” more often than he’d like to.  Anyway, two of us were sitting with Steve.  Steve’s girlfriend had broken up with him like a week before and he was telling us about it.  I have to give him kudos because he took it like a man and ended it.  But now he’s telling us how he’d just run into her on campus.  He said, I saw her and just walked by.  I didn’t say a thing.  He said this with complete conviction and self-satisfaction.  There was a brief silence and then the other guy sitting with us said, Boy…that showed her.  We about fell out of our chairs laughing and finally Steve started laughing too.  Like I said, Steve handled that break up really well but when he ignored his ex on campus like that, it just showed her how much she’d hurt him.  I guess that’s what he wanted her to know.  But, is that what he really wants?  I think he should’ve stopped briefly to say hi and then walk on like he’s doing just fine.  Showing people you’re hurt or angry seldom works to your advantage.”

 

“When a girl breaks up with you, it’s difficult not to get mad or show emotions.  But the more you do that the more you demonstrate to her you’re hurt and the more you prove to her she really matters to you.  If you don’t want her to have the satisfaction of knowing you care for her deeply, then bite your tongue and walk away without telling her off.  Your indifference will hurt her more than anything you could say.”

 

“You know they say the best revenge is living well so if a girl’s hurt you and you want to get back at her for it, the best thing you can do is to move on and have a good life.  In that last example Steve’s girlfriend wanted to break up and he did the right thing.  He left and let her realize the permanence of her decision.  Now if he really wants to get back at her and make her regret losing him, he should rebound, be happy and thrive.”

 

“I hesitate to even mention my next thought because I doubt any man’s ever actually done this.  It’s a strategy which could be used if you suspected your girlfriend is about to break up with you.  Here’s how it would go.  If you sense your girlfriend wants to break up with you, then you do yourself and your self-esteem a favor and break up with her first.  It sounds harsh but keep in mind you’re doing her a favor too.  It’s what she wants and you’re avoiding a lot of trouble for both of you.”  I interrupted here and told Michael he could call that strategy the Pre-emptive Break up.  He liked that and continued.  “Ironically, if your girlfriend really is thinking of breaking up, this may be the best chance you have for getting her back.  Remember, women are drawn to what they can’t have so the idea of losing you may just get through to her.”

 

“I think the most important thing for men to remember is when a girl wants to break with you, there’s really not much you can do about it.  You’ve pretty much already lost the game so you’re not going to come out ahead no matter what you do.  If you hang on thinking you can somehow renew her love for you, it’s not going to happen and you’re going to get dumped anyway.  If you accept your fate and say, Fine, let’s break up, she’ll say it’s your fault for the break up because you’re not willing to fight for her love.  So which is the best approach?  It all comes back to the Cardinal Rule of Life, Never stay where you are not appreciated.  That means accept your fate, move on, make the necessary adjustments to make yourself a better man and then find a girl who does appreciate you.”

 

We’d been mostly talking about when a girl wants to break up with the guy so I asked Michael if he had any advice for the guy who wants to break up with the girl.  He thought about that and said, “If you want to end a relationship, the most important thing is to think of yourself and not her.  Don’t stay with her just to spare her feelings.  It’s not fair to either of you.  Realistically, why would she want to be with someone who no longer loves her?  It’s best to end it soon and not let it drag on…”

 

“…Actually, that takes me back to another Marcial story.  You remember the advice he gave to his friend, Ted.  He told him he was getting divorced.  He needed to accept that, deal with it and move forward.  And Ted took that advice.  He told his wife he understood she wanted to get divorced and they should go ahead with that.  But, Ted was holding on to some anger and he was caught up in the blame game.  He’d come over to vent.  Marcial listened to this for a while and then he told him he was going to give him some more advice.  Marcial said, My advice to you is to end this marriage and end it high mindedly and cleanly on friendly terms and walk away.  He expanded on that and told Ted his anger would be the death of him.  He told him he’d give him 10 minutes to give his speech to us and he could tell us all the horrible things his wife had done and then that was it.  He told him to never give that speech again, especially to his wife.  I remember thinking what great advice it was and I loved his term, high minded.”

 

“And Marcial gave that same advice to me one time when I was breaking up with a girlfriend.  He told me, If you want out of this relationship, don’t ever blame it on her.  This would hurt her feelings needlessly.  Blame the break-up on yourself.  Tell her you are not good for her, you need space, you are not able to deal with a relationship or you have important things you have to do now in your life.  Whatever the reason you give, don’t blame her.  Don’t let it get ugly and end up in the gutter.  Keep it high minded.”

 

Michael and I sat back to enjoy the sunset.  Having a nice meal with a good friend on a beautiful summer day cost us about $12 each and that’s with the beer.  It was an inexpensive pleasure and probably under appreciated by many people.  We chatted some more about work and about the girls.  We agreed we were lucky guys…But I told Michael he’d helped me make my luck.

 

We headed home.  I wanted to take a shower and clean up before going over to see Kristin.  I spent some time choosing what I wore…not too nice and not to casual.  It had to be just right.

 

****************************

 

Movies recommended in this chapter:

 

500 Days of Summer





Chapter 10  Love and Marriage

Wednesday Michael came over after work.  I put together some meat, potatoes and salad for us and we sat down to catch up on what was new.  He asked about my study date on Sunday night.  “It went well on all counts.  We went over her class work and I helped outline the project she has to do for the class.  She really likes learning about investments so it’s fun mentoring an enthusiastic student.  She fixed a snack which we ate while we studied.  After about an hour with her class work she asked me to stay and talk.  She told me more about her family and asked me lots of questions.  She asked why I was single.  That was a clever way to ask about old relationships.  I told her I dated a lot in school and some since but I hadn’t had a serious relationship.  She basically told me the same story.  The conversation faded away and we started kissing.  We both became aroused to the point she had to stop herself.  It was time to go so we went to the door and had a long embrace.  We were both still aroused.  I made a little pelvic contact and she pushed back.  The best part was she looked at me and bit her lip when she did that.  Then she pushed me out the door…”

 

“…When I got home there was an email waiting for me.  She emails me at least once a day.  There’s always some class work related reason for the email but she adds personal messages too.  She doesn’t work tomorrow and wants me to come over.  I’ll probably take some food over so she doesn’t have to cook.  I really don’t know where she finds the time to work 30 hours a week and take classes.”  Michael asked me if I’d heard from Kim.  I said, “Yeah, I wasn’t quite sure what to do.  I felt like I should call her but I’m not sure if I want to go out with her.  But, I decided to call…to say hi and see how she’s doing.  If getting together came up I’d tell her this week was really busy but maybe next week.  So I called Monday and it was a pleasant conversation but she didn’t bring up getting together and neither did I.  We left it with one of those vague promises to see each other soon.  My best guess is I was a fling for her and our fling has run its course.  What do you think my fling with her was all about?”

 

Michael said, “Actually, I think you’re right.  From the very beginning Kim seemed like a free spirit who isn’t looking for anything serious and that’s exactly what she told you.  Maybe she falls in love easily and falls out of love just as easily.  It’s interesting she didn’t need to have the drama of a break up.  It’s not like you had a real relationship but often girls want to make out like there was a relationship so they don’t feel like a tramp sleeping around.  Kim seems content to just let your dating fade away.  Luckily, that was okay with you too.”

 

We talked some more about Kim and then I asked Michael what was new with Stephanie.  He said, “We’re doing great.  Stephanie is one of the funniest people I’ve ever known.  She’s always full of energy and always full of surprises.  Every day she does some little thing for me.  When I get home from work I find a note on my door or maybe once a week she brings lunch to my work and we go across the street to that little park and eat.  One time when she came to my office she picked up a vibe from a girl there that made her suspicious.  Later she told me, If that girl flirts with you, I’ll come down there and slap her silly.  The way she said it we laughed about it for days.  I think we bring the best out in each other.  I know she keeps me happy.”

 

I asked Michael if they’d talked about living together and he said, “The subject is coming up.  We’ve known each other for maybe 4 months now.  She’s ready to make the jump, but I want to be more cautious.  Of course, the day to day convenience and enjoyment of being together is a plus.  The economics make sense.  I just want to make sure we’ve given it enough time and it’s the right thing for us.”  I asked Michael if he thinks about marrying Stephanie.  He laughed at himself as he said, “I’ve wondered if I wanted to marry every girl I’ve ever dated.  One of my pathologies is to look at every girl I meet and wonder if she’s the one.  I think I spend too much time thinking about the perfect woman.  Sometimes I look back at girls I’ve known and think if I could take this girl’s body and this girl’s looks and this girl’s personality and this girl’s athleticism and this girl’s sexuality…then I’d have the perfect girl for me.”

 

I asked Michael how Stephanie matched up to his perfect composite girl.  He sighed and said, “She’s darn close to perfect for me.  I love everything about her.”  Michael thought for a bit and then said, “You know, if you took a hundred guys who were engaged and asked them why they were marrying their girlfriend, they’d probably all say, because I love her.  I’d like to know all the real reasons why they’re marrying their girlfriends.  Some of those reasons are probably pretty short sighted.  If you made a list of the right reasons to marry someone, what would be on that list?  I think it would be compatibility of values and personalities and similar interests, sexual compatibility…and you treat each other with respect.  When I consider marrying Stephanie, those are the criteria I think about…”

 

“…But considering the high failure rate of marriages, I don’t think a lot of men are thinking about those criteria first.  I think too often there’re other reasons men are marrying their girlfriend and they hope the important criteria work themselves out.  How many men marry a girl on looks alone?  Red flags are going off everywhere.  His friends and family are warning him but he marries her anyway because she’s beautiful and he wants a trophy wife.  He only starts thinking about things like values and how she treats him when it’s too late and he’s writing alimony checks…”

“…How about the man who marries a girl because it’s the best sex he’s ever had in his life.  Some guy spends his life with less than fulfilling sex.  Then he meets a girl, she climbs on top of him, rides him like a wild horse, cries out his name, has a screaming orgasm and collapses on his chest telling him it was the best she’s ever had.  He’d only dreamed about such things and now here it is.  Odds are good he’s never before heard his name during sex and he likes it.  And, I won’t disagree with good sex being a valid reason to marry someone…But it’s only 1 out of 5 reasons and if she’s a person he can’t get along with or someone who doesn’t share his values, then that great sex is going to come at a huge cost.  And, what if that great sex is because she’s a good actress and knows how to play a man?  When everything about a girl is right is when a man should start thinking about marriage…Not just after an hour of fantasy sex…”

 

“…Your experience with Kim is a good example. Should you marry Kim because you got along in the bedroom or should everything about her have to be right?”

 

“What about when a man marries a girl because of guilt?  Remember the woman’s two powerful weapons, pussy and guilt.  Men marry women for pussy and you better believe men get married out of guilt too?  Here’s what men hear from women.  We dated all through college…I could’ve dated other men.  I wasted my youth on you.  I have nothing because I sacrificed for you.  You owe me.  You can’t leave me now.  What will my family say?  I have no job because I took care of you.  How will I live?  If you leave me I’ll commit suicide…”
