“…And often women have every right to be using guilt on some guy.  The lesson here is for men to be very careful when a woman makes a commitment to you and makes changes in her life for you.  You don’t want to walk down that road unless you’re ready to follow it to its logical end, marriage.  And, if you begin on that road and then you see good reasons not to continue, then you need to end the relationship right away.  If you don’t, you’ll be dealing with guilt for a good reason.  You earned it.”

 

“I have a good one for you.  How about rejection as a reason to marry a girl?  We’ve touched on this but I don’t think I’ve ever articulated it quite like this.  Rejection evokes a stronger emotional reaction than love does.  It’s sad but true and your story about Carl and Ruth demonstrate the point.  Ruth gave her love to Carl and he felt nothing, but as soon as she rejected him he had a huge emotional response.  Unfortunately, it’s human nature.  If you really want to be successful in a relationship then reward love and walk away from bad treatment…”

 

“…Anyway, I have a story.  I’ve known Nick since high school.  He’s a lady killer.  Women are to die for him and it’s been that way since he was 10 years old.  He grew up excelling in all sports, competed in speed skating and then performed a barrel jumping act in ice shows.  He was a total stud in every way and a complete womanizer.  Traveling with the ice shows he literally had women in every city and every young girl who figure skated had a crush on him.  Every one of his conquests was in love with him even knowing he was a womanizer and cheating on all of them.  But then he started dating a famous performer in the ice shows.  Linda was beautiful, glamorous and confident.  She fell for Nick just like every girl but she laid down the law and told him if they were going to date then it had to be exclusive.  Linda knew his reputation.  That lasted for a little while but Nick was soon cheating on her and she found out.  Linda dumped him and told him it was over.  That wasn’t the first time a girl had gotten mad at Nick but they’d always taken him back.  This was the first time in Nick’s life he’d been dumped and rejected by a woman…”

 

“…Nick was devastated and he told Linda he’d do anything to get her back.  And that’s what he did.  Nick crawled and begged and promised and then they got married.  The marriage lasted for a few years but it wasn’t a relationship based on the right things.  Nick didn’t marry Linda because she was the girl of his dreams.  He married her to win her approval which she’d taken from him when she dumped him.  Imagine all those girls who’d fallen in love with Nick over the years only to be tossed aside for his next conquest.  They must have seen what happened and all said, Oh my God!  All I had to do was say no to him and he would’ve been mine.”

 

“Sadly, many men drift from having a girlfriend to having a wife without giving it much thought.  They climb on the girlfriend train and move comfortably down the tracks watching the scenery and enjoying the steady sex.  They wake up one day and there are tampons in their bathroom.  Before they know it invitations are being sent out.  Their fate is sealed.  They allowed themselves to be railroaded by a girl who gave it a lot of thought.  Every step along the way was carefully planned and implemented.  The woman planned all the changes she will make to shape her new life just the way she wants it...and this man will facilitate it all...”

 

“...Meanwhile men are approaching their wedding perfectly content in life.  They have their job, their friends, their free time and steady sex.  They're happy and have no interest in changes.  They assume marriage will just be a continuation of their happy, carefree life.  The wedding plans move forward.  These men are happy.  They have no idea about their girlfriend's vision of how their life should be...will be...”

 

“...A year later the man never sees his friends, has no free time and gets no steady sex. In addition, his job is no longer deemed adequate for his wife's lifestyle.  Now he's looking back at that moment just before the invitations were sent out and wishing he knew then what he knows now.  But, he has only himself to blame.  He never asked himself important questions.  He deferred the decision making to his girlfriend.  And, just like many, many decisions a man will make throughout his life, it should not be left to a woman.”

“Yikes, that paints a scary picture but often that’s how men “decide” to get married.  The line of least resistance is to just go along with their girlfriend’s plan.  It’s not that they made the choice to get married.  They just didn’t want to disappoint anyone so they never stood up and said no.  So if those are various reasons why men get married, give me a list of reasons women get married.”

 

“Well, the right reasons for a woman to marry are the same…similar values, personality, shared interests, sexual compatibility and you treat each other with respect.  Those are the right reasons, but women also get married for wrong reasons and short sighted reasons.  And they also play the risky game of hoping the important things will work themselves out later…”

 

“…You and Kristin are a perfect example.  You two shouldn’t think about marriage for another year…But, there’re a lot of good reasons right now for her to think about marriage.  She’s working full time, going to school and maintaining an apartment.  That’s a tough road to walk down and how many more years of that does she have?  If she marries you or lives with you her life gets a lot easier.  She can cut down her hours at work.  She can finish school quicker.  Her monthly expenses get cut in half.  She has you sharing the responsibilities and she has a good man taking care of her.  That’s a perfect quick fix for her.  And, it’s not bad for you either for the same economic reasons, but what will attract you most to marrying Kristin now is you’ll have that great feeling you get from being her knight in shining armor.  You get to make her life wonderful, which is what all of us men want to do.  We want to be the hero…”

 

“…And Kristen may, indeed, be the girl of your dreams but you won’t know that for sure without more time.  If you jumped ahead now because a quick fix for her and the prospect of waking up watching her put on those jogging tights is just too good to pass up, then you’ve put yourself on a course which becomes more and more difficult to change.  You need more time to make sure Kristin and you are good together in every way.”

 

Michael had explained my situation perfectly and I told him how easy it would be for me to jump into living with her or marrying her.  When men are falling in love they need a trusted friend to pull back on the reins so we have some perspective.

 

I was enjoying this topic.  I wanted to hear more about why women get married and Michael continued, “Sometimes it’s really very simple…And it’s a good and healthy reason.  Some girls, most girls have a plan for what they want and they simply need a man to provide it.  They want a house and kids and security.  There’s nothing wrong with that…Well, let me be more specific.  When a woman wants a house and kids and security and the man of her dreams to share it all with and she chooses a man she loves and respects and shares the same values and interests with, then all is right in the universe…”

 

“…It’s when a woman wants a house and kids and security and her only interest in the man is a guy to provide it for her.  That’s when things are wrong in the universe.  That’s the case of the single mom who used to live across from me.  She wanted those things and found some poor guy to give them to her.  There’s a song from the 70’s, At Seventeen, by Janis Ian.  The lyrics talk about beauty queens marrying young into what they need and then retire.  I’m sure people would think we were cynical for saying this but there’s a big difference between a girl who’s marrying a man and thinking about everything she has to give him and a girl who’s marrying a man and thinking about everything he can give her.  It’s the question of givers and takers.  The problem for most of us is we become blinded by our feelings for a girl and we really aren’t able to clearly see the answer to that question.  That’s where good friends and family come in.”

 

“Sometimes women marry for money and status.  It’s that simple.  They’re snobs and they want to live in a big house and drive a German car.  They’ll do what they have to do to get those things.”

 

“Happily, Stephanie doesn’t want a German car or a big house or to be a snob and that’s one of the many reasons I adore her.  Let me tell you about Stephanie.  She’s from a good, happy family with 6 kids.  No one in her family went to college but I think her parents are smarter than 90% of the people who did.  I know Stephanie is.  So here’s Stephanie.  She’s in her late 20s.  She has a good job and she lives comfortably but she’s not getting ahead like she wants to.  She’d like to go to school to become a medical technician, maybe to do sonograms.  She’d like to own a house someday.  She’d like to travel.  She’s trying to save for those things but it’s tough to do alone…”

 

“…And then I walk into her life.  If we got married her life would change overnight.  She could start saving to go to school.  I could support her while she’s in school.  Together it would be possible to invest and maybe buy a house.  We could travel.  For Stephanie, having her life change overnight is a darn good reason to get married.  For me, here’s the reason I’m thinking about marrying Stephanie.  I’ve known her maybe 4 months now and I honestly think when Stephanie thinks about marrying me, she’s thinking about what she can give me…her love, her devotion, her support and her affection.  The first night we met her she gave us a free desert.  Do you remember that?  She’s never stopped giving to me.  Every date we’ve ever been on Stephanie found a way to give something back to me.  A girl like Stephanie is the kind of girl you get serious about.”

 

At that moment Stephanie walked in the door with a piece of key lime pie in her hand.  Michael and I looked at each other wondering if she’d heard us talking.  She was so happy to show Michael what she’d brought him, but then she could tell she’d interrupted something.  She told Michael no key lime pie until he told her what we were talking about.  I stayed around talking with them for another 30 minutes and Stephanie told me all the nice things Kristin had said about me.  Then I took off.  I was anxious to get home and check my email.  Kristin had sent me one and attached a funny picture of Stephanie and her at work.  Life is good when you’re falling in love.

 

***************************

 

Friday Michael and I went out after work.  He asked about my study date with Kristin the night before.  I had a big smile when I said, “It went well.  I told her not to worry about dinner so I arrived with some food to prepare.  She sat at the kitchen counter while I prepared the food and asked questions about her assignment.  The dinner was a big hit and then we talked more about her class and things related to investing.  She wants to open an investment account so I showed her online the best companies to consider and we talked about what kind of mutual fund she should invest in.  That led to an analysis of the market and where the best values are at this time.  We were like two geeks at a Star Trek convention.  Actually, one thing I’m picking up about Kristin is she’s good with money.  She’s frugal and a saver…not a spender.  I like that.  Suddenly it’s after 10pm and I’m thinking it’s time to go.  Kristin had other ideas though.  She put me on the floor and told me I deserved a massage for the dinner and the help with her class.  She took off my shirt to massage my back and then I felt her rubbing her breasts up and down my back.  She’d taken off her shirt and bra.  No more clothes came off but she was as aroused as I was.  I went home happy.”

 

Michael and I chatted more about Kristin and Stephanie and all of their positive traits.  Then I brought up something I’d been thinking about.  We’d talked so much about the cautions one should take before jumping into marriage so I asked him what the potential problems are in marriage.  He nodded his head and said, “My brother told me something once I’ve never forgotten.  He told me not to get married if you’re about to become rich…or poor.  And, I remembered that advice a month ago when I met a guy from Miami.  He was in real estate and I was asking him about the bubble and crash.  He told me he had friends who were contractors.  They were making $30,000…a month during the boom years and living high.  Then it all ended overnight and these guys were struggling to stay afloat.  The interesting part of the story was the wives of the contractors weren’t sympathetic to the sudden financial hardships.  These contractors said their wives were furious with them.  They didn’t want to hear explanations or excuses.  They just wanted their allowance so they could continue living the lives to which they were accustomed.

“There’re mainly only two causes of problems which come up in marriages…money and sex.  It turns out couples fight about money twice as much as they fight about sex.  So what kind of money problems come up after people get married?  One is debt.  Suddenly one spouse finds out the other spouse came into the marriage with car loans, student loans and maxed out credit cards…and now those debts belong to both of them…”

 

“…Another huge problem is something you just mentioned.  It’s the conflict of personalities of spenders and savers.  If both spouses are spenders then they’ll enjoy themselves on the way to the poor house.  If they’re both savers then they’ll get along fine as they prosper.  If a saver marries a spender then they’ll both be miserable and constantly fighting…”

 

“…Then there’s the power play between the person who earns the money and the other spouse.  The money earner tries to dictate the spending priorities and the other spouse resents this.  That tug of war for control of the money can end a marriage…”

 

“…A marriage can be going along fine financially and then the children arrive.  Suddenly, there’re bills for food, clothing, medicine, school and a hundred other things.  The budget gets tight and now there’s stress which wasn’t there before…”

 

“…Here’s one no one thinks about until it happens.  You now have an extended family and may be expected to contribute to your in-law’s financial needs.  If you say no, your spouse may accuse you of choosing your family over hers.  This can drive a wedge down the middle of your marriage…”

 

“…This is just a short list of all the money problems which can befall a marriage.  Fixing these problems can be tough, if not impossible.  The best solution is to marry the right girl in the first place.  I think we talked about this before but don’t marry someone who hasn’t lived on their own.  The quickest lesson in economics is to be the person paying the rent and utilities and buying the food.  If a girl’s living on her own and responsible for her monthly budget, then she probably has a grasp on real life economics…”

 

“…Another thing you have to do before you marry a girl is find out if she has any debt, car loans, credit cards or student loans.  This becomes interesting though because it’s hard to get that information without asking your girlfriend.  If she gladly tells you about any debts she has and they’re minimal, then you have another good sign about her.  However, if she becomes defensive when you ask for this information then you have 2 red flags.  One red flag because she probably has debt and the second red flag is even bigger.  Any normal, honest and responsible girl would understand the reason to be open about her debt with someone who’s about to become tied for life to her financial position.  If a girl becomes defensive about this, then she probably has debt problems and for sure has personality traits you don’t want to be married to.  She’s a selfish, spoiled and entitled girl who sees marriage as a one way street where she shouldn’t be asked to give.  She only takes.  That defensive behavior is a huge red flag.  Run the other way…”

 

“…Finally, and most importantly, there’re 2 kinds of people in the world, spenders and savers.  You don’t want to marry a spender.  You must watch a girl’s spending habits for a long time to learn how she is with money.  Figure out if she’s a spender or a saver.  That’s the most important thing to your financial well-being as a couple.”

 

I jumped in here to comment about our girlfriends and their money sense.  I said, “You and I are doing pretty well with Stephanie and Kristin.  They both work and support themselves…Check.  They both enjoy simple forms of entertainment like renting a movie or cooking at home…Check.  When you went away for the weekend, Stephanie packed a lunch…Check.  Kristin is looking into opening an investment account and even has the $1,000 minimum in savings…Check.  Those are all good signs.”  Michael agreed and added that Stephanie grew up in a family which had to make ends meet so she knows how to stretch a dollar.”

 

Then my phone rang.  It was Kristin letting me know when they’d be off work.  The four of us were going to go out dancing again.  That had turned out to be a big success with the girls and they wanted to do it again tonight.

 

I hung up and Michael asked where we were in the discussion and then he said, “Oh yeah, Sex.  Sex is the other common cause of fights in a marriage.  We talked a lot about how the sex can change after a couple is married.  That can be a tough one to predict.  I think it’s important to pay attention to the girl’s desire for sex.  Does she ever initiate sex or is it always you?  Does she have orgasms?  Does she seek out different positions or activities she enjoys or only go along with what you want?  Does she enjoy sex or pretty much just go along because it’s what you want?”

 

I jumped in and said, “Every one of those questions would warn me about Lori the Virgo.  I look back and it’s so obvious.  I’m not sure I’d really understand the differences between women if I hadn’t been with Lori.  I can still remember kissing her and getting nothing in return.  When I kiss Kristin she starts trembling.”

I said to Michael money and sex can’t be the only causes of problems in a marriage and he said, “Those are the main ones.  The other problems which come up are usually things like personality traits which were obvious but you ignored them while you were dating.  These are things you don’t have to be a psychologist to recognize.  Anyone can recognize a selfish girl or a girl who gets defensive.  Girls who can’t admit when they’re wrong are no fun to be married to.  Mood swings and fits of anger aren’t something you want to live with but even worse, they can be a sign of depression.  If you marry a girl who’s an unhappy person, you now become the cause of her unhappiness.  That’s a heavy cross to bear…”

 

“…I just said you don’t need to be a psychologist.  I’m going to take that back.  You do need to become a psychologist and really take notice of a girl’s unpleasant behavior.  Don’t ignore it.  Don’t explain it away.  Don’t let her dismiss it and blame it on whatever outside force which caused her to act like a bitch.  The bottom line is you need to step back and look at a girl while you’re dating and ask yourself if she has personality traits which are warning signs of selfishness or anger or depression.  You really don’t want to be married to those kinds of personality disorders.”

 

Michael and I chatted some more about personality traits.  We recollected old girlfriends.  It was interesting how we remembered mostly the good things about them and often couldn’t remember why we’d broken up.  There must have been a reason at the time.

 

Later we headed over to Applebee’s to get Kristin and Stephanie.  They’d both been looking forward to going out dancing so they were happy girls.  We got to the club early enough to stake out a table and got in on the free salsa lesson.  We danced until after midnight and then headed home.  Kristin had been very affectionate dancing and asked me to come over for a while.  She gave me another massage and we took our level of arousal just a bit further than the night before.  When I got home there was an email waiting for me.  Life is good when you’re falling in love.

 

*******************

 

I spent most of the weekend with Kristin when she wasn’t working.  We went for a jog Saturday in the mid-morning.  I complimented her jogging tights.  She smiled.  She knew that was code for I like your butt.  Then she brought her laptop and books to my house Sunday morning.  I made us a smoothie and some breakfast and showed her a lot of investing resources I have on my computer.  We made a short list of mutual funds she may consider for her first investment and I showed her how to research their holdings and past performance.  She decided to make that her final project.  She was going to show her research of the funds, research of the current market conditions and then how she opened her account to buy the fund she chose.  I liked Kristin was taken with the practical aspect of investing.  Her text and the class were more oriented to the theoretical side, which is to be expected from an introductory course. 

 

Kristin left around 1pm.  She had things to do before she went to work.  I had lots of things to catch up on too.  I’d been devoting all my time to Kristin.  Later in the afternoon I asked Michael if he wanted to play some tennis and he was anxious to get together.  We played and then went over to our favorite Mexican restaurant.  There’s something about Mexican food which makes a Corona taste even better.  I told Michael about my weekend and asked what they’d been up to.  Michael said, “Actually, last night was pretty interesting.  Stephanie called me from work and asked if I’d come over to be with her after work.  I came over around 10pm and usually we watch TV or talk or play around, but last night she was quiet and just wanted to go to bed.  So we get to bed and she buries her face in my chest holding on to me and then I realize she’s crying.  I ask what’s wrong and she just completely falls apart.  She’s crying and holding on to me.  First, I was panicked that something had happened to her family and I asked but she shook her head no.  Stephanie is always happy and full of energy and I’ve never seen her in a mood of any kind.  She’s always so strong I’d forgotten she’s a woman and can be fragile…”

“…She didn’t want to tell me.  I used her line and told her if she didn’t tell me I’d slap her silly.  She looked at me and started to smile and then cried even more.  Now she was wrapping herself around me and wanted to be held closer…so I held her.  Finally, she looked up at me with those big, beautiful, puppy dog eyes and asked me if she was just a toy for me.  I was confused by this because I couldn’t think of a single thing in our history which would suggest that.  As this went on I put together what it was all about.  I’m the best thing that ever happened to her in her life.  With me she has prospects for a great future with lots of options which could come true…marriage, going to school, having a family, financial growth and all the things any person would want.  Without me she has a job and a car.  She’ll be treading water and any dreams she may have will be difficult to realize.  I think Stephanie started to realize how emotionally dependent on our relationship she’s become.  It’s everything for her and she feels vulnerable.  What if she lost that?  She’s scared of that happening…”

 

“…We talked for hours in bed and I learned some more.  As confident and happy as Stephanie is, she has insecurities about only being a high school graduate.  I think that’s why she wonders if she’s just a toy for me.  She looks at all the professional women with their college degrees in the offices where I work and wonders why I wouldn’t be with one of them.  She thinks I’d have more in common with them.  People marry someone from their own strata.  And those generalities are true…”

 

“…Stephanie is an extraordinary girl.  She knows more about life than any person I’ve ever met.  She has a spirit and energy which is infectious.  But, she just sees herself as this average, country girl with no education.  We talked and talked.  She apologized for being such a cry baby.  I told her I wasn’t going to leave her.  She needed reassurance I was serious about her.  Maybe…Maybe this is one of those ways women try to get something.  Stephanie wants us to move in together, which leads to engagement, which leads to marriage.  I don’t think this was a manipulation.  Her tears and emotions were real and Stephanie is too honest for that but she wants those things and she wants us to talk about them…”

 

“…Actually, this brings up another reason why a man may marry a girl…because he cares about her.  I care more about Stephanie than any girl I’ve been with and that’s a dangerous thing.  What if rationally and logically I decided Stephanie and I weren’t right for each other?  That’s not the case but what if it was?  My feelings for her would cloud my judgment.  I’d be in a horrible position caring too much about her to break up but reluctant to marry her.  Luckily, Stephanie is the girl of my dreams.  I can only pray Stephanie’s evil step sister doesn’t one day take over her body.”

 

“This brings up something else you and I are both dealing with.  When you date in high school it’s all good fun.  We have serious crushes on all those girls but it’s just dating.  When a girl gets close to 30 years old, dating is serious for her.  She’s not interested in someone wasting her time on a 6 or 12 month relationship that runs its course and then the guy moves on to the next girl.  She’s serious about finding a relationship she can build upon.  Men have to understand that and respect it.  If a man starts dating a 30 year old girl for more than 2 or 3 months, she’s going to start having expectations.  He has to figure out if he’s serious about this girl or not and act accordingly.”

 

I was riveted to Michael’s story because this was my situation too.  I said, “You know, Kristin is younger but I’m sure she’ll start building expectations after a few months just like any normal girl would.  We always hear people say things like Oh, we’re just dating…nothing serious.  Well, after a period of time dating becomes serious.  Sometimes that’s where the conflict begins.  The girl wants a commitment and a ring and a wedding date and the guy’s still content with nothing serious.  A couple who got along perfectly is suddenly fighting all the time.  You and Stephanie aren’t fighting but her break down tells you she’s now very serious and needs the reassurance of living together or a ring or a date or all of it.  And there’s nothing wrong with that.  It simply means she’s a normal girl who wants all the right things in her life.”

 

Michael and I talked a lot more about this topic and how it related to Kristin and Stephanie.  We wondered how many men even conceptualized all of this let alone talked about it.  We joked about how most men wake up one day and realize they’re engaged having no idea how it happened.

 

Then we continued on our recent conversations about the cautions a man has to take to make sure he marries the right girl, I asked Michael what happens when a guy marries the wrong girl.  He took a deep breath and said, “Do you have any friends who’ve gone through a divorce?  We’re not talking about an unpleasant hour in the dentist’s chair here.  I’ve watched friends lives go into hell on earth for 2 and 3 years and sometimes longer with custody battles.  They’re living with heart break and anger and the stress…And then sooner or later everyone steps up to the cash register and the man pays the bill.  I think Robin Williams said, Ah Divorce…From the Latin word meaning to rip your heart out through your wallet.  I really don’t think men have any idea what it’s like until they go through it and that’s why they’ll jump into marriage with reckless abandon.  It takes like 2 seconds to sign your name and it can take a lifetime to undo that signature.”

 

“…The sitcom, Two and a Half Men, captures a lot of the joys of divorce.  The ex-wife from hell is perfectly portrayed and her lawyer is called the blood sucking jackal.  It’s hilarious but you really pity the poor ex-husband.  There’s a great movie about divorce called, Bye, Bye Love.  That movie covers all the aspects including the effects on the kids.”

 

“Marrying the wrong person doesn’t always mean divorce.  Sometimes it just means your life isn’t what you’d hoped.  Have I ever mentioned Ian from work?  Ian is maybe 25 years old and is an all American boy.  He’s nice and smart and good looking.  He reminds me of Paul Rudd, the actor who always plays the good looking, nice guy role.  Ian is married and I met his wife at a Christmas party.  She’s a tall, strawberry blonde.  She’s maybe 5’10” and has a strong build.  She’s not skinny.  I remember she brought their newborn baby to the party.  I also got an impression of her.  She seemed smug to me.  Anyway, Ian is a PhD candidate and works in research.  I doubt he makes big money now but will make six figures in 5 years.  His wife doesn’t work.  She’s home with the baby.  When Ian first arrived at work all we heard about was his wife and the baby and the remodel projects he was doing on the house they’d just bought.  He seemed like your typical newly-wed who’d married his college sweetheart and I got the impression he thought he’d scored with a beautiful, tall, blonde…”

 

“…After about 3 months we started to hear a shift in the stories and his tone.  I was a little surprised he was telling these stories to me and also to some of the women in the office but after a while I realized he needed to talk to someone.  Here were the stories.  Ian came to work with a lunch he’d packed himself.  His wife wouldn’t make his lunch for him.  When he got home he cooked his own dinner and took over taking care of the baby because his wife had been with the baby all day and she had to rest.  In the evenings and every weekend Ian worked on the remodel projects.  His wife didn’t help because she had to take care of the baby.  The house needed a complete make-over with painting outside and inside, new bathrooms and kitchen and landscaping.  Ian worked like a dog on the house and all he heard from his wife were complaints that it was taking so long.  When Ian went to bed he had to be careful not to wake his wife, who was tired from taking care of the baby.  When Ian woke up to get ready for work he had to be careful not to wake his wife while he fixed his breakfast and made his lunch.  She needed her rest so she could take care of the baby.  The best story of all was when Ian told us he had to do his own laundry.  His wife did her laundry and the baby’s laundry but refused to do his at the same time.  She didn’t have time to do his laundry because she was too busy taking care of the baby.  Ian never got specific about sex but implied his wife was too tired from taking care of the baby to have sex…”

“…At first Ian told the stories with an understanding tone about his wife.  Then his tone changed to confusion as if he was asking if this was normal.  As time went on his tone changed to frustration, and anger.  The women in the office were in complete disbelief.  I didn’t know what to say.  Privately, the women in the office and I agreed that Ian was screwed.  He can’t get divorced.  With his house and baby he can barely make ends meet.  There’s no way he could support his wife and baby in the house and also live in an apartment…”

 

“…Here’s a good question.  Do you think Ian’s wife demonstrated those qualities before they got married?  It’s hard for me to believe she was a complete sweetheart before they were married and then turned into a bitch afterwards.  My suspicion is Ian chose his wife because she was tall, blonde and beautiful and nothing else mattered.  And, he ignored the obvious personality traits which predicted trouble…”

 

“…I don’t know if he’s come to the conclusion he married the wrong woman or not.  Ian is the kind of guy women look for.  He’s good looking and has a great career in front of him.  I’m sure he had lots of women to choose from.”

 

“I’m sure Ian went to the wedding thinking about all the good things he’d enjoy being married.” 

1.      He’d get steady sex.

2.      He’d have a partner to enjoy the good times with.

  1. He’d no longer be alone.

4.      He’d have the support of his loving wife when times were tough.

 

“Ian woke up months later married to a cold, nagging bitch and all the benefits of marriage never materialized.”

1.      Steady sex?      Nope.

2.      There are no good times…Only things for her to complain about.

  1. He wishes he were alone.

4.      She doesn’t want to hear about his problems.  She’s too tired from taking care of the baby.

 

“Let me ask you something.  Do you want to be a millionaire?  Of course, who doesn’t?  That’s a dream for all of us to get a good job in a good career, save and invest well and build wealth over the years.  If you marry the wrong person you’ll never be wealthy.  It’s that simple.  Let me repeat that.  If you marry the wrong person you’ll never be wealthy.  There’s a book, The Millionaire Next Door by Thomas Stanley and William Danko.  The authors gathered data from thousands of millionaires across the country and then did interviews and came to some amazing findings.  The average millionaire became wealthy with an average paying job, living in an average house and driving a 10 year old car.  He was also married to the same woman his entire adult life.”

 

“The authors came up with 7 rules for becoming a millionaire.  The first rule is to live below your means.  The book emphasizes wealth takes sacrifice, discipline and hard work.  It emphasizes frugality and delayed gratification.  The book also emphasized choice of spouse.”

 

“It’s also interesting the book cites stories of doctors who make $300,000 a year yet have a negative net worth because they live way above their means.  The authors say your career and job matter but it’s better to have an average paying job and live below your means than to make big money and spend it all.  Of course, no one can live below their means, save and invest unless their wife is like minded.  If that doesn’t put the importance of marrying the right woman into perspective, then nothing will.”

 

“Here’s another personality trait to look for in the woman you marry.  It’s good to marry someone who’s compassionate and charitable.  A woman who genuinely cares about people less fortunate is going to be a wife who takes care of her husband and family.  In addition she may involve herself with church charities or community programs helping underprivileged children, for example.  These are the kinds of activities which give people a fulfillment and happiness in life.  Compare that to the happiness derived from being a power shopper.  I have some friends, Dan and his wife Cathy.  When they travel for vacations they go to South America and help build houses in small pueblos and work in health clinics.  When you marry a woman who enjoys living for 2 weeks in squalor conditions because helping people makes her happy…well, you have a good woman who’ll be there for you in the good times and the bad.”

I’d been taking in all of Michael’s observations and advice.  Throughout I was reflecting on Kristin and seeing if she passed all of Michael’s tests.  Then I said, “You know, that’s not a bad test for a woman you’re thinking of marrying.  See if she wants to do some charitable work with you.  It would be easy to do locally.  A big test would be to travel like your friends, Dan and Cathy, do.  Michael, you’re on the verge of making a commitment with Stephanie.  If you’re true to your own advice you’ll plan a trip to Central America and see what happens in tough conditions.”  Michael started nodding his head and thinking.  He agreed and said he was going to talk to Dan about some local volunteer work they could do on a weekend.  The more he thought about it, the more he liked the idea.  He was confident Stephanie would respond well to it.  I told him to give me all the information he found because I wanted to do the same thing with Kristin during her next break in classes.

 

Michael and I talked some more about Stephanie and Kristin and marrying the right woman.  Then Michael said, “We’ve been talking about marriage and instead of talking about the beauty of two people sharing their lives together, I’ve been talking about the cautions a man should take.  Let’s see, I’ve covered women who spend their husbands money, the story of Ian and his wife, the horrors of divorce, unsupportive women, women marrying for money and…can you think of any others?”  I mentioned women who use guilt to force a man to marry them.  Michael said, “Thank you.  I must sound like the most paranoid, anti-woman, anti-marriage man on earth.  But I’m not anti-woman or anti-marriage.  What I am is an outspoken advocate for marrying the right woman for the right reasons at the right time in your life.”

 

Michael’s phone rang and Stephanie was letting him know when she’d be home from work.  I had a lot of things I wanted to prepare for Monday morning.  I needed to spend some time alone at home to catch up but I was hoping I heard from Kristin too.  That’s probably a sure sign of someone losing control of his heart strings.

 

*********************

 

Wednesday Michael and I got together for lunch.  We met at the little soup and sandwich shop near our offices.  First Michael told me he’d spoken to his friend, Dan.  This Saturday Dan and his wife were going to help with a Special Olympics activity.  It was going to be held at a high school track.  They needed people to help “coach” the special needs kids in the track and field events.  So Michael asked Stephanie and she was excited to do it.

 

Then Michael changed the subject and became animated.  He told me he had this great idea.  He said, “I got this idea.  We can make a fortune with a new video game, The Marriage Game.  Think about this.  All the time people are getting married and they have specific ideas about how they expect their life to be.  They know when they want to have kids and how many, they know which spouse will work, they know where they’ll live and how they’ll budget the money…They have their entire life mapped out.  But, here’s the crazy thing.  They’ve never compared their expectations with their fiancé’s expectations.  They just assume their boyfriend or girlfriend is on the same wave length as they are.  This is just a train wreck waiting to happen.  They get married and the wife is ready for kids and the husband doesn’t want kids.  The wife quits her job and the husband thought she was going to keep working.  They had no meeting of the minds, no agreements and no plan.  They loved each other and got along well as boyfriend and girlfriend.  Then they get married and suddenly there’re huge disagreements.  They had two different visions for how their life would be…”

 

“…So I figure you make a video game which couples can play together.  Questions and situations come up you each respond to.  The questions would be about having kids and who works and where you’ll live.  There could be situational questions to help determine if you’re a spender or a saver.  There could be those questions which determine personality types.  Supposedly, there’re matchmaker services which give extensive questionnaires to match you on many levels of compatibility.  This would be similar but in the form of a video game you play with your girlfriend.  If you get to the 7th level then you’re marriage worthy as a couple.”

 

I wasn’t sure if Michael was kidding or serious but he was right.  People should have a serious conversation to compare expectations before they get married.  I liked his idea and started brainstorming with him.  “Yeah, one of the situations could be the guy wants to play golf with his friends on Saturday morning.  The girl can decide to let him play but she loses points because then she doesn’t get to go to Home Depot to pick out paint for the bathroom.”  Michael liked that one and we had fun going back and forth with the different questions which could be in the game.  The one flaw we came up with was probably the fatal flaw…Every woman in America would want to play this game but very few men would go near it.

We finished our sandwiches and headed back to work.  I thought more about the content of Michael’s video game.  When should a couple have that conversation to learn what the other person expects in marriage with regard to kids and work and finances and where to live?  For example, Michael and Stephanie have been together for 4 months.  If he was to have that conversation with her now, she’d interpret it as a proposal.  I mean how could Michael bring that up and say, Oh, nothing.  I was just curious.  In my situation, I’d been dating Kristin for only a few weeks.  It would be a lot easier to ask her, in conversation, what her future plans were, if she wanted kids, if she wanted to continue working or where she wanted to live.  It would simply be an interesting conversation to get to know each other better.  It wouldn’t be interpreted as a guy getting ready to pop the question.

 

I also thought about how you get people to give honest answers.  Some men would just want to agree with their girlfriend so they won’t have an argument.  Many girls will ignore what the man says because all women assume they can change the man after they get married.  I remember seeing an episode of Cheers where Sam and Diane have a counseling session with this brilliant marriage therapist played by John Cleese.  The therapist asks Sam and Diane a series of questions.  They spend some time talking and then the therapist says, Okay, I’ve heard enough.  Here’s my diagnosis.  Not only should you two not get married, but you should never speak to one another ever again.  The funniest part of the episode was Diane trying to prove the doctor wrong.  And that’s the problem.  Once we develop feelings for a person we want to ignore those differences which will spell trouble in the future.

 

Of course, all of this had me thinking more and more about Kristin.  So far we seemed like a perfect match.  I wondered if I had the guts to play Michael’s video game with her.  What if we bombed out before reaching level 7?  Would I accept that and stop seeing her?  I wouldn’t want to.

 

*********************

 

Friday I called Michael and asked if we were on for after work.  I really wanted to talk to him.  We went to one of our favorite places and after we ordered I told Michael I needed some advice.  I said, “I’m totally falling for Kristin and I’m losing all caution to go slowly.  We call and email every day and I go over to see her almost every night.  It’s like I’m just handing her the keys to my heart.”  I paused and Michael commented what a good investment those running tights had been for Kristin.  We laughed a little and I said, “I can’t disagree.  I think she’s beautiful but it’s more than that.  Everything else is perfect too…her personality, her values, her interests, the way we treat each other, her passion and I’m addicted to the connection we’re developing.  I’m losing perspective because she’s all I think about.  You’ll probably slap me silly for saying this but I’m in love with her…or am I?  Is this love or is this infatuation?  Do I need to be more cautious and wait until I’ve known her longer?  Are there red flags everywhere but I’m too blind to see them?”

 

Michael nodded and smiled.  He thought for a few seconds and then said, “First of all, I haven’t seen any red flags with Kristin.  If I had I’d tell you.  The times we’re together, I find her to be genuine and down to earth.  Secondly, I doubt this is an infatuation just because you were lonely and horny because you met Kristin and became attracted to her during the time you were having a wild fling with Kim.”  Now Michael paused searching for words and then continued, “You’re wondering if you’re in love with Kristin.  Defining love is…uh…a philosophical pool of quick sand.  How much has been written on the subject of love?  There’re books on how to fall in love.  There’re books on how to fall out of love.  There’re songs about its beauty and about its loss.  If there’re people who understand it, they’re wiser than I…”

 

“…Actually, I’ll tell you what.  The most intelligent thing I ever heard said about love was said by Jimmy Stewart in a movie, Shenandoah.  Let’s rent that movie and watch it with the girls.  I’m serious.  It’s that good.  In the movie, Jimmy Stewart is a Virginia farmer during the Civil War.  A young man has come to ask permission to marry Stewart’s daughter, Jenny.  Jimmy Stewart asks the young man why he wants to marry his daughter.  The young man says, Because I love her.  Stewart asks, But, do you like her?  The young man was confused with this question because he’d just said he loved Jenny.  Jimmy Stewart said loving someone and liking them are two different things.  He said if you love someone but don’t like them, your love can fade and turn to resentment.  Then Stewart said when he married Jenny’s mother he didn’t love her but he liked her.  After living and working next to her for a few years, he woke up one day and realized his respect for his wife had grown into love…”

 

“…I understand what you’re going through because I’ve been going through the same thing with Stephanie.  I adore her and I want to be with her all the time…And, that’s dangerous because when we fall in love we forget everything we learned about what women find attractive.  We start acting like complete fools.  We close our eyes and jump in with confessions of love and spoiling them with attention.  We know this is the wrong thing to do but we justify it all by convincing ourselves this girl is different.  The rules don’t apply to this girl…”

 

“…You’ve done this before and so have I.  I spent 4 years in high school learning every time I thought a girl was different, it turned out she wasn’t.  Unfortunately, the more you care about a girl the easier it is to start thinking with your heart.  You want to think she’s different.  She may even tell you she’s different.  But, she isn’t.  She’s a woman and always will be.  She’ll always be influenced by what women are attracted to and that’s a strong man, a man who is hard to get and a man they must seduce and compete for with all the other women who’re attracted to him…”

 

“…And, how do you do this?  You have to maintain your independence.  You have to have a life outside of Kristin.  You and I need to get together once or twice a week so they respect us.  You told me you’ve neglected your work and chores at home to be with Kristin every night.  You have to say no to her.  Even when it’s what you’d rather do, you have to say no and take care of your responsibilities.  You have to stand strong and not feel guilty for being responsible.  And that can be a good test for red flags.  When she asks you to come over and help her with her class and you say no, what will she say?  Remember we’re not talking about saying no because you want to go out drinking with the boys.  You’re saying no because you must be responsible and prepare a report for work the next day.  Okay, what will she say?  Will she get angry?  Will she try to manipulate you?  Will she expect you to do what she wants?  If she does, you need to run away from her as fast as you can.  That would mean she’s a spoiled, selfish, entitled little princess who expects everything to be done for her…”

 

“…On the other hand, if she completely understands, tells you she’ll miss you and even asks if there’s anything she can do to help, then this girl may have just reached level 7 in the marriage game.”

 

Michael’s phone rang.  He picked up, gave me a grin and said, “Yes, dear.  No, dear.  Whatever you say dear...”  Then he started laughing and explained to Stephanie we’d been joking around.  He got off the phone laughing and told me Stephanie was going to slap us both silly.

 

Michael and I talked some more about what love will do to a guy.  We told stories of past girls and all the mistakes we’d made.  Later the girls joined us.  Kristin and Stephanie had been friends from work but now were closer.  Each had had a positive effect on the other.  Stephanie’s energy and humor had rubbed off on Kristin and Kristin’s sacrifices to make it through school had inspired Stephanie to set her goals higher.  I remembered something Michael had said about relationships.  He said you want to be with someone not because they make you feel better but because they make you be a better person.

 

********************

 

Sunday afternoon Michael called and asked if I wanted to play tennis.  I told him I needed to finish a report I was doing for work.  He said he’d like to bring a pizza over later if that was okay.  He said he’d be over around 6pm.  When he arrived I’d just finished so we sat down to enjoy the pizza.  “I have a good story for you,” I said, “This morning Kristin called and asked if I wanted to come over and go do something.  I told her I’d love to but I wanted to clean this house and I had a report I needed to do for work.  Are you ready for this?  Kristin asked if she could come over and help me clean.  I told her she didn’t need to do that.  She said she wanted to.  She came over with some fruit and muffins she’d picked up at the store and fixed us a Sunday brunch.  Then she cleaned the kitchen and vacuumed while I cleaned the bathroom and ran the laundry.  And, she showed up wearing her jogging tights.  Now that shows the power of saying no to a girl.  Then she asked if she could stay here and study while I worked on my report.  She went out to her car and got her books and laptop and curled up on the sofa and read.”

Michael gave a big smile and said, “Kristin passed the test.  Congratulations.  That’s a major statement about Kristin’s quality.  Did you ever see the movie, A Bronx Tale?  Remember the test in that movie.  Sonny loaned his car to the kid to take on a date.  He told him when he picks her up to hold her door for her and then walk around the back of the car to his door.  If she leans over to unlock his door for him, it means she thinks about your needs.  It means she’s the one.”

 

We talked some more about Kristin and then Michael wanted to tell me about Saturday.  He said, “It was quite a day and very rewarding.  I’ve never been comfortable helping disadvantaged people.  This was much easier than I thought it would be but Stephanie was amazing.  She was completely natural and comfortable with those challenged kids.  Every one of them adored her.  Pretty soon she was like a rock star because all the kids wanted to be with her.  She was the most fun.  The organizers even came over and asked her if she taught special needs children.  And Stephanie is a good little athlete.  She was out there running and broad jumping and throwing the shot put to show the kids how to do it.  If a kid did something wrong or embarrassing she had that charm to make sure they didn’t feel badly and then show them how to do it better.  Stephanie always has fun no matter what we do.  We can be cleaning the bath tub and she’s laughing out loud.  But, I’ve never seen her have more fun…mainly because she felt so rewarded.  All the kids wanted to be with her…”

 

“…When we drove home she thanked me a hundred times for suggesting we volunteer.  Then she told me one of the organizers told her she has a gift and wants Stephanie to come to her office to talk with her.  That was quite a day for us.  You know I’ve always admired Stephanie but Saturday I realized what an amazing girl she is…”

 

I asked Michael if Stephanie had moved to level 7.  He said she had.  Then he thought for a while.  He was organizing his thoughts and choosing his words.  Then he said, “Stephanie is my best friend.  Our personalities fit together perfectly.  Our values are the same.  We have the same taste in music and movies and sports.  The way we treat each other is the way I always thought it should be.  She makes love to me and falls asleep holding me.  Stephanie is perfect…But, I’m afraid to take such a big step.”

 

We talked for a while about commitments and marriage.  We talked about the fears we naturally have of making a mistake with such an important decision.  I asked Michael if he could imagine leaving Stephanie.  He said he couldn’t.  I asked if he ever thought he’d find another girl like Stephanie.  He said not in a million years.  Then I asked him if Stephanie makes him be a better person.  He looked at me and said, “Yes, she does.”

 

We talked for an hour about different things and then Michael said he needed to get home.  He said he was going to plan a trip with Stephanie to visit her family.  She hadn’t been home to visit since before they’d met…not since Christmas and now it was summer.  He wanted to ask her about it tonight so they could start planning to get time off from work.  He said, “As much as I’ve heard about her family, I want to meet them.  I’m sure they want to meet me too.”  Michael took a deep breath and then said, “After all, you don’t just marry the girl.  You marry her family too.”

 

********************************

 

Movies referenced in this chapter:

Bye, Bye Love

Shenandoah

A Bronx Tale

 

Songs referenced in this chapter:

At Seventeen by Janis Ian

 

Books referenced in this chapter:

The Millionaire Next Door by Thomas Stanley and William Danko

 

TV shows referenced in this chapter:
